facts.value: First of four cumulative Ardougne Achievement Diary tiers; region-wide tasks around East/West Ardougne and Yanille, reward claimed from Two-pints.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Easy tier.

facts.label: length
facts.value: 10 tasks (from notes text; the row's own length field is recorded as "??")

facts.label: start
facts.value: Two-pints, Flying Horse Inn, East Ardougne.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: 2,500xp, usable on any skill level 30+ (stated directly in rewards.xp).

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Ardougne cloak 1: unlimited teleports to Ardougne Monastery.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Double death runes when trading cats to West Ardougne civilians.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Noted drops from Jubsters and Frogeels in Creature Creation.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Teleport to the Rune Essence mine via Wizard Cromperty (right-click).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Pickpocket a cake from an Ardougne market stall (5 Thieving).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Sell silk to the Silk trader for 60 coins (offer 120 first, then counter at 60; he refuses to deal for ~10 min after you've stolen from his stall).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Use the altar in East Ardougne's church while below full prayer.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Start (not finish) the Fishing Trawler minigame.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Enter the Combat Training Camp north of West Ardougne (needs Biohazard).

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Have Tindel Marchant identify a rusty sword; 1/100 chance it's worthless, requiring a fresh sword to retry.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Pull the Ardougne Lever to the Wilderness.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: View Aleck's Hunter Emporium in Yanille.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Check insured pets with Probita (right-click).

notes: 10 easy tasks, doable in any order, claim reward from Two-pints once all are done: teleport to the Rune Essence mine via Wizard Cromperty (right-click); pickpocket a cake from an Ardougne market stall (5 Thieving); sell silk to the Silk trader for 60 coins (offer 120 first, then counter at 60 - he refuses to deal for ~10 min after you've stolen from his stall); use the altar in East Ardougne's church while below full prayer; start (not finish) the Fishing Trawler minigame; enter the Combat Training Camp north of West Ardougne (needs Biohazard); have Tindel Marchant identify a rusty sword (1/100 chance it's worthless, requiring a fresh sword); pull the Ardougne Lever to the Wilderness; view Aleck's Hunter Emporium in Yanille; check insured pets with Probita (right-click).

facts.value: White Knight Master rank (required for task 2) itself requires a Black Knight kill score of 1300, per reqs.other.

facts.label: mechanics

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Buy a White 2h Sword from Sir Vyvin for 1,920 coins (needs Wanted! plus White Knight Master rank).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Dig up 3 or more magic roots from a Falador magic tree you felled yourself.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Perform a skillcape or quest cape emote atop Falador Castle (needs all quests done or 99 in a skill).

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Jump the strange floor in Taverley Dungeon while taking no damage.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Mix a Saradomin brew atop Falador east bank, reached via the agility course.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Defeat the Giant Mole; required for this tier (from reqs.other, not reflected in reqs.quests or the task list).

notes: Elite tier, 6 tasks: craft 252 Air runes at once from essence (Runecraft, tiered lower with Raiments of the Eye pieces); buy a White 2h Sword from Sir Vyvin (needs Wanted! + White Knight Master rank, 1,920gp); dig 3+ magic roots from your Falador magic tree you felled yourself; perform a skillcape/quest cape emote atop Falador Castle (needs all quests or 99 in a skill); jump the strange floor in Taverley Dungeon taking no damage; mix a Saradomin brew atop Falador east bank (climb via the agility course). Claim from Sir Rebral in the White Knights' Castle courtyard; prior tiers must be claimed first.

name: Sheep Herder
summary: Free a quarantined flock of colour-coded sheep for Councillor Halgrive by herding, poisoning, and incinerating them near East Ardougne.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Novice-difficulty quest with no skill or quest requirements; can be started immediately.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Given by Councillor Halgrive outside the East Ardougne church.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Get a plague jacket, plague trousers, and a cattleprod from Doctor Orbon, then prod one sheep of each colour (blue, yellow, green, red, each penned in its own quadrant north-west of the north Ardougne bank) through the gate into the enclosure.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Feed the penned sheep poisoned sheep feed until they die, then burn the corpses on the incinerator before reporting back to Halgrive.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: No skill xp reward; grants 3,100 coins and 4 quest points, generous for a Novice-difficulty quest.

notes: No skill or quest requirements at all -- can be done immediately. Get a plague jacket/trousers and cattleprod from Doctor Orbon, then prod one sheep of each colour (blue, yellow, green, red -- each in its own quadrant of the pen north-west of the north Ardougne bank) through the gate into the enclosure, feed them poisoned sheep feed until they die, then burn the corpses on the incinerator and report back. No skill xp reward, just 3,100 coins, but unusually generous at 4 quest points for a Novice-difficulty quest.

name: Sleeping Giants

items: 20 free inventory slots for the crate search

unlocks: Access to the Giants' Foundry minigame

start.location: Giants' Plateau, east of Al Kharid

summary: Repair the giant workshop machines and forge one sword for Kovac to unlock the Giants' Foundry minigame.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Very short intro quest.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Given by Kovac at Giants' Plateau, east of Al Kharid.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Repair three broken machines in the giant-sized workshop -- a polishing wheel, a grindstone, and a trip hammer -- using scavenged parts.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Forge one sword from start to finish: choose the three correctly-shaped mould pieces (forte, blade, tip), pour molten metal from the crucible, cool and heat the preform on the waterfall and lava pool, then alternate trip-hammer, grindstone, and polishing-wheel passes while watching a heat gauge.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Working the sword outside the correct heat band lowers its quality.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks access to the Giants' Foundry minigame.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Grants 6,000 Smithing xp on completion.

notes: Short intro quest that unlocks the Giants' Foundry minigame; only real requirement is 15 Smithing. Repair 3 broken machines inside the giant-sized workshop (polishing wheel, grindstone, trip hammer) using found parts, then forge one sword start to finish: pick the 3 correctly-shaped mould pieces (forte/blade/tip), pour molten metal from the crucible, cool/heat the preform on the waterfall and lava pool, and alternate trip-hammer -> grindstone -> polishing-wheel passes while watching the heat gauge (working outside the right heat band lowers sword quality).

summary: Reforge V's shield to clear a basilisk-market gauntlet, then defeat Typhor and Jormungand on the Island of Stone.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Master-difficulty quest, considered a quest-cape gate.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Given by Brundt the Chieftain in the Longhall, Rellekka.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium length.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Investigate a basilisk attack in Rellekka, get framed and exiled, then gather three components in any order to reforge V's shield: a Fremennik shield, Lunar glass with an enchanted V's sigil, and a Polishing rock from a geyser puzzle at Mountain Camp.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Wield the reforged V's shield and grind basilisk kills to 100% favour in the market; Monstrous Basilisks count 10% and ordinary Basilisks count 3.33% toward that total.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: After reaching 100% market favour, sail to the Island of Stone, solve a rune code-door puzzle, then fight Typhor (level 218) followed by the endboss Jormungand (level 363, weak to crush, two avoidable special attacks, pray Magic).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: V's shield must remain equipped throughout the Island of Stone fights.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants V's shield, access to the Island of Stone, Basilisk Knight as an assignable Slayer task, the Fremennik honorific 'Dräpare', and the ability to craft a Neitiznot faceguard.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Grants 50,000 Slayer xp, 50,000 Crafting xp, and 30,000 Runecraft xp.

notes: Master-difficulty quest-cape gate. Skill reqs alone are steep (65 Crafting/60 Slayer/60 Smithing/60 Fishing/55 Runecraft, 60 Mining boostable), and the quest chain is deep: The Fremennik Isles -> The Fremennik Trials; Lunar Diplomacy -> Lost City + Rune Mysteries + Shilo Village -> Jungle Potion -> Druidic Ritual; Mountain Daughter; Heroes' Quest -> Dragon Slayer I + Lost City + Merlin's Crystal + Shield of Arrav -- roughly 10 prior quests deep before you can even start. Walkthrough: investigate a basilisk attack in Rellekka, get framed and exiled, then gather 3 components in any order (a Fremennik shield from dagannoth/market NPC drops or free from Bardur with a Ring of charos(a); Lunar glass + an enchanted V's sigil made via Lunar Isle/Astral Altar/smithing; a Polishing rock from a geyser puzzle at Mountain Camp) to reforge V's shield. Return to Rellekka, wield V's shield and grind basilisk kills to 100% in the market (Monstrous Basilisk=10%, Basilisk=3.33%), then sail to Island of Stone, solve a Mastermind-style rune code-door puzzle, and fight Typhor (218) then the endboss Jormungand (363, weak to crush, 2 avoidable specials, pray Magic) while keeping V's shield equipped throughout.

summary: Settle a sibling rivalry between Jatizso and Neitiznot's kings, then kill the level 122 Ice Troll King.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Medium-length quest involving a sibling rivalry between the kings of Jatizso and Neitiznot.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Given by Mord Gunnars at the northernmost dock in Rellekka.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Must be able to kill a level 122 Ice Troll King, and separately fight through 10 rock-throwing ice trolls in a multicombat area (Protect from Missiles recommended).

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Complete a jester-outfit fetch task and a tax-collection errand for Jatizso's king, then repair two rope bridges on Neitiznot for spymaster Mawnis.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Keep Protect from Missiles active while repairing the Neitiznot rope bridges -- troll damage stacks and is only applied once the repair animation finishes.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Craft or buy yak-hide armour and a Neitiznot shield as tribute, fight through the ice trolls, then melee down the high-Magic/Ranged-defence Ice Troll King.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants the Helm of Neitiznot (re-buyable for 50,000gp if lost), access to arctic pine trees, a runite rock with 7 coal rocks near Neitiznot, the Jatizso mine (up to adamantite), roughly 20,000 coins picked up during the quest, craftable yak-hide armour and Neitiznot shields, and the Ice Troll King added to Nightmare Zone.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Grants 5,000 Construction xp, 5,000 Crafting xp, 10,000 Woodcutting xp, and a choice of 10,000 xp in a combat skill, given twice.

notes: Sibling rivalry between the kings of Jatizso and Neitiznot. Needs The Fremennik Trials done, 20 Construction (boostable), and the ability to kill a level 122 Troll King plus 10 ice trolls in a multicombat zone. Do a jester-outfit fetch task for Jatizso's king (tribute of ores), run a tax-collection errand around Jatizso for the king (upsets a black-market NPC you can later square with a bribe), repair 2 rope bridges on Neitiznot for spymaster Mawnis (keep Protect from Missiles on -- damage from trolls stacks and only applies once the repair animation finishes), then craft/buy yak-hide armour and a Neitiznot shield as tribute, before fighting through 10 ice trolls (multicombat, use Protect from Missiles) to reach and melee down the Ice Troll King (high Magic/Ranged defence, so bring melee).

facts.label: mechanics

Comedic frog-politics plot: rig a pond election by distracting the rival yellow frogs (chop the orange tree near them) and sabotaging their lily pad while they're not looking, help nominate a candidate, crack a chest combination ('NALIA') in Marcellus's house, plant a plushy in capybara dung to frame a scapegoat, then kill the 1hp joke-boss Cuthbert (killable via recoil damage on a pure non-combat account) before the final vote. Reward is a new hardwood Farming patch, not the usual quest-cape xp haul.

Investigate odd behaviour in Witchaven by interviewing 3 townsfolk, find a hidden dungeon behind a false wall with a locked 'imposing door', scan it with the Commorb to get a torn transcription, then recover and repair 2 more page fragments (one from the mayor's desk, one solved via a sea-slug-glue matching puzzle on the Fishing Platform). summary: Woo a royal sibling and broker a peace treaty for King Vargas to unlock the Miscellania kingdom-management minigame.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Medium-length gate quest into the Miscellania kingdom-management minigame.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Given by King Vargas at Miscellania Castle.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Woo either Princess Astrid or Prince Brand through several rounds of dialogue (gifts and emotes) until they call you 'dear', then ferry an anthem and treaty back and forth between the two royal siblings.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Advisor Ghrim fixes a bad anthem; forge a giant pen from an iron bar via blacksmith Derrik.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Grind favour from 25% to 75% by buying flowers, raking herbs and flax, mining coal, chopping maples, or fishing near the docks; world-hopping regrows flower/herb weeds faster.

notes: Gate quest into the Miscellania kingdom-management minigame. Requires the Heroes' Quest chain (Shield of Arrav, Lost City, Merlin's Crystal, Dragon Slayer I) plus The Fremennik Trials. Choose to woo either Princess Astrid (give flowers, dance emote, later a bow, then blow-kiss emote, then a ring) or Prince Brand (give flowers, clap/cheer emote, a cake, then blow-kiss emote, then a ring) through several rounds of agreeable dialogue until they call you 'dear'. Ferry an anthem and treaty back and forth between the two royal siblings (advisor Ghrim fixes a bad anthem), forge a giant pen from an iron bar via blacksmith Derrik, then grind favour from 25% to 75% by buying flowers / raking herbs+flax outside the castle / mining coal / chopping maples / fishing near the docks (world-hopping regrows weeds faster) before reporting back to Vargas.

notes: Novice puzzle quest, only 10 Construction required. Climb the tower fixing 3 broken machines with scavenged parts: a pressure/valve-and-lever pipe puzzle, a simpler pipe-fitting puzzle, and a cage-building puzzle where each of 4 sides needs specific horizontal/vertical piece sizes placed in order. Afterward, answer the Homunculus's back-and-forth dialogue quiz picking consistently either all 'Logic' or all 'Magic' answers to finish the questline and unlock the Creature Creation minigame. A raw chicken + raw swordfish let you optionally kill a 'swordchick' afterward for a Medium Ardougne diary task.

Get a Commorb device from Tiffy/Sir Amik, meet informant Lord Daquarius in the Black Knights' Base inside Taverley Dungeon after killing a Black Knight, hand 20 essence to the Mage of Zamorak at the Chaos Altar, then use the Commorb to 'scan' and chase fugitive Solus Dellagar across roughly 7 wiki-listed locations in sequence (Rellekka, Musa Point, Wizards' Tower, Dorgesh-Kaan, Ardougne, Champions' Guild, etc. -- exact set/order varies) -- this leg alone can take 20-30+ minutes with no way to skip Savant's cutscene dialogue. Finish with an instanced kill of Solus in the rune essence mine.

name: A Kingdom Divided

unlocks: The book of the dead
Two antique lamps (10,000 xp each to any skill level 40+, only one if the Architectural Alliance lamp was already claimed before 10 Jan 2024)
Ability to set respawn point to Kourend Castle (via Asteros Arceuus)
24 new spells unlocked in the Arceuus spellbook (all combat spells except Ghostly Grasp/Inferior Demonbane, all utility spells except Resurrect Crops/reanimation spells)
Ability to fight the boss Yama

summary: Investigate Sophia Hughes' disappearance across Kourend, defeat the Judge of Yama and Xamphur, and win the support of all five Great Kourend Houses.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Investigate Councillor Sophia Hughes' disappearance for Commander Fullore and Martin Holt, tracing her trail through Kourend to a final confrontation and gaining favour with all five Houses.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Fights the level-168 demon Judge of Yama mid-quest (dodge fire waves like Galvek, melee him in the gaps; he keeps an overhead prayer up the whole fight).

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Fights the spirit boss Xamphur (level 239): Protect from Magic, kill summoned hands, dodge a falling-hand mechanic similar to Vorkath.

facts.label: kills
facts.value: Also requires defeating a level-91 Barbarian Warlord and a level-132 assassin during the quest.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Includes multiple puzzle sequences: a stone-pile numeric code, a statue-panel matching puzzle, and brewing a shielding potion from a sulphur potion + volcanic sulphur for the Doors of Dinh.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants the book of the dead, respawn-point relocation to Kourend Castle (via Asteros Arceuus), 24 new Arceuus spellbook spells (nearly all combat and utility spells), and access to fight the boss Yama.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards two antique lamps of 10,000xp each (any skill level 40+) — only one lamp if the Architectural Alliance miniquest's own lamp was already claimed before its removal on 10 Jan 2024.

notes: Investigate the disgraced Councillor Sophia Hughes' escape for Commander Fullore and Martin Holt. Search a house for a receipt, question Tomas Lawry and the Lovakengj pub owner Fuggy, then a cutscene at Veos's boat in Port Piscarilius drops you straight into a fight with the level-168 demon Judge of Yama (dodge fire waves like Galvek, melee him in the gaps, he prays overhead the whole fight). Pickpocket Istoria and search a case in the Arceuus Library for Rose's diary and a hidden note, then chase Rose's trail: solve a stone-pile numeric code at the Forthos Ruins, fight off an assassin and melt an ice panel with a fire spell at the Settlement Ruins, and match statue-panel order at The Legless Faun in Piscarilius, ending at a shack trapdoor near the Farming Guild. Head into Molch's Lizard dwelling to see Khat B'alam, retrieve a lizardman egg from Kebos Swamp (a level-75 lizardman brute attacks but doesn't need to be killed) to trade for a key, then fight the spirit boss Xamphur (239, Protect from Magic, kill summoned hands, dodge falling-hand mechanic Vorkath-style). Visit all five Kourend house Lords/Ladies at their home locations, then again at Xeric's Lookout to gather their support. Finish by brewing a sulphur potion (defence potion + volcanic sulphur) enchanted by Kaal-Mej-San at Mount Karuulm into a shielding potion for the Doors of Dinh, defeat a level-91 Barbarian Warlord in the Kourend Woodland, collect a declaration from Jorra in the Graveyard of Heroes (kill a level-132 assassin), craft a dark essence block (pickaxe+chisel a dense runestone near the Dark Altar, use on the Dark Altar) to trade Mori for a dark nullifier, retrieve a Shayzien journal from a lockpicked chest in the Hosidius Vinery, then report back to all five Lords and Commander Fullore at Xeric's Lookout for the final cutscenes.

name: A Night at the Theatre

facts.label: overview

refs.title: A Night at the Theatre/Quick guide

notes: The Mysterious Stranger wants Lady Verzik Vitur humiliated. Kill a vyrewatch below the Theatre for a key, unlock a gate and loot a coffin for Ranis' head, then head to a cave north of Darkmeyer to find an egg sac guarded by venomous spiders (bring anti-venom/antipoison) — get sulphuric acid from Daer Krand in the Sisterhood Sanctuary (south of Slepe graveyard) and pour it on the egg sac for strange spider eggs (no need to kill the spiders). With a ghostspeak amulet or Morytania legs 2+, visit the Nature Spirit at the Nature Grotto (fairy ring BIP), then cross to the island east of Mort Myre Swamp (north of the Shades of Mort'ton entrance) to kill the level-302 hespori and chop it for hespori bark. Deliver both trophies to the Mysterious Stranger, then complete one Theatre of Blood raid (any mode; entry mode recommended, skippable if you already have a completion) and report back to finish.

refs.title: A Tail of Two Cats/Quick guide

notes: Sequel to Gertrude's Cat: Bob the cat has gone missing. Get Hild to enchant a catspeak amulet with 5 death runes, then use it to Locate Bob repeatedly across a list of world-hoppable spawn spots (western Varrock anvil and Draynor Manor garden are the fastest camp spots). Visit Gertrude and Reldo in Varrock Palace library to learn Bob's lineage, then travel to Sophanem to consult the Sphinx (desert-region travel, needs desert clothing or waterskins). Do a set of fetch-quest chores for Unferth at his house (grow potatoes, make the bed, light the fireplace, feed him cake+milk, cut his hair with shears) before a final disguise-and-cutscene sequence with the Apothecary in Varrock to cure Bob's "sickness" (white doctor/nurse outfit required).

start.location: Ver Sinhaza, outside the Theatre of Blood entrance

summary: Infiltrate Ver Sinhaza, forge the Ivandis flail, and defeat the vampyre lord Ranis Drakan as the fourth Myreque chain quest.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Fourth Myreque chain quest: infiltrate Ver Sinhaza, forge the Ivandis flail, and defeat vampyre lord Ranis Drakan.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Fights Ranis Drakan (level 233, 3 phases including summoned vyrewatch and a berserk finale) — only the Ivandis flail deals real melee damage to her.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Notably harder than its stated skill requirements suggest — 60+ melee and 43+ Prayer are recommended for the Ranis Drakan fight despite the quest's low formal level reqs.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants the Ivandis flail (damages vyrewatch) and Drakan's medallion (unlimited Theatre of Blood teleports).

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards a tome of experience granting 2,500xp to any 3 skills level 35+.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required to start both Sins of the Father and A Night at the Theatre.

notes: Myreque chain quest 4: infiltrate Ver Sinhaza via steam-vent parkour to spy on the vampyres, brew truth potions to interrogate a captive, forge the Ivandis flail (emerald sickle + enchant + chain), then fight Ranis Drakan (level 233, melee-only damage via the flail, 3 phases including summoned vyrewatch and a berserk finale). Notably hard boss relative to the quest's skill reqs -- 60+ melee and 43+ Prayer recommended despite low stated requirements.

name: Alfred Grimhand's Barcrawl

unlocks: Access to the Barbarian Outpost Agility course (required for Scorpion Catcher + Horror from the Deep)
Barbarian guard teaches how to auto-smash empty potion vials on the last dose
required for the Kandarin Medium diary

summary: Visit ten named pubs across Gielinor and report back to the Barbarian Guard at the Outpost gate to complete this pub-crawl miniquest.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Visit ten named pubs across Gielinor in any order, picking the barcrawl dialogue option at each, then report back to the Barbarian Guard at the Outpost gate.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants access to the Barbarian Outpost Agility course, required later for Scorpion Catcher and Horror from the Deep.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The Barbarian Guard teaches auto-smashing empty potion vials on their last dose.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for the Kandarin Medium Achievement Diary.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Reward xp is unknown/unrecorded in held text ("??") — this miniquest has no combat and needs no items beyond travel means.

notes: Simple pub-crawl miniquest: talk your way past a Barbarian Guard into the Outpost, then visit 10 named inns/bars across Gielinor in any order (Blue Moon Inn Varrock, Jolly Boar Inn, Rising Sun Inn Falador, Rusty Anchor Inn Port Sarim, Karamja Spirits Bar Musa Point, Dead Man's Chest Brimhaven, Flying Horse Inn Ardougne, Forester's Arms Seers' Village, Blurberry Bar Tree Gnome Stronghold, Dragon Inn Yanille), picking the barcrawl dialogue option at each. Return to the same Barbarian Guard at the Outpost gate to finish. No combat, no items needed beyond travel means.

name: Animal Magnetism

unlocks: Ava's attractor (Ranged<50) or accumulator (Ranged>=50) - auto-collects ranged ammo
Ability to trade Ava for her devices
Ballistic attractor schematic if Pandemonium completed

summary: Complete a four-stage fetch quest for Ava to build her Ranged-ammo-collecting device across three item-gathering stages.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Four-stage fetch quest for Ava: gather ingredients across three item-gathering stages (amulet/chickens, magnet, undead-tree twigs) to build her ranged-ammo-collecting device.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants Ava's attractor (below 50 Ranged) or accumulator (50+ Ranged), which auto-collects fired ranged ammo, plus the ability to trade Ava for further devices later.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: The Ballistic attractor schematic variant is only available if Pandemonium has also been completed.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Stage 3 requires solving an orb puzzle (toggle off all but the 2nd/5th/9th orbs left-to-right) before combining the solved notes with a button and leather.

notes: 4-stage fetch quest for Ava's device. Stage 1: talk to Ava, travel to undead chicken farm west of Ectofuntus, talk Alice/Malcolm loop, get crone-made amulet from old crone near Slayer Tower, give to Malcolm, buy 2 undead chickens w/ ecto-tokens. Stage 2: bring chickens + iron bars + hammer to Ava, get selected iron from Witch in manor, forge at Rimmington mine (face north), return magnet to Ava. Stage 3: cut undead tree (not dead tree) outside manor w/ mithril axe, see Turael in Burthorpe for Blessed axe (needs mithril axe + holy symbol), cut undead tree again for twigs, give to Ava, solve orb puzzle (toggle off all but 2nd/5th/9th left-to-right), combine solved notes + button + leather, hand in. Core reward is the Ava's device line feeding Ranged training.

name: Another Slice of H.A.M.

items: Magic or Ranged gear (melee unusable on 2 of the fight's enemies)

req_items.name: Magic or Ranged gear
req_items.note: melee cannot damage 2 of the fight's enemies

unlocks: The ancient mace
Ability to buy Goblin Village teleport spheres
Free access to the Dorgesh-Kaan-Keldagrim train system

summary: Excavate artefacts for Tegdak, fight through a goblin ambush and Sigmund, and rescue Zanik from the H.A.M. plot.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Archaeology-themed goblin quest: excavate artefacts for Tegdak, uncover a H.A.M. plot, and fight through an ambush and Sigmund to rescue Zanik.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: The goblin-village ambush pits you against a level-30 H.A.M. Mage and level-30 H.A.M. Archer that can only be damaged with Ranged or Magic — melee doesn't work on them.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Final fight is against Sigmund (level 64) — save the ancient mace's special attack until after he activates Protect from Melee, since the special disables his prayer for the rest of the fight.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants the ancient mace, the ability to buy Goblin Village teleport spheres, and free access to the Dorgesh-Kaan-Keldagrim train system.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Riding the train to Keldagrim after the quest also completes a Lumbridge & Draynor Diary hard task.

notes: Archaeology-themed goblin quest: dig up 6 artefacts in a cave south of Dorgesh-Kaan's middle level and clean them on a specimen table for Tegdak, which uncovers another H.A.M. plot. Teleport to the Goblin Village with Zanik following, then fight through an ambush killing a level-30 H.A.M. Mage and level-30 H.A.M. Archer with Ranged/Magic only (a spare crossbow+bolts spawns nearby if unequipped). Sneak past goblin guards in the Lumbridge Swamp Caves via a scripted stealth sequence with a goblin-sergeant escort, then fight Sigmund (level 64) at the end -- save the ancient mace's special attack until after he activates Protect from Melee/prayer, since the special disables his prayer for the rest of the fight. Untie Zanik to finish; optionally ride the train to Keldagrim afterward for a Lumbridge & Draynor Diary hard task.

Gated A Kingdom Divided

summary: REMOVED (10 Jan 2024): historically required reaching 100% favour in all five Great Kourend houses to unlock Xeric's Heart teleport access.

facts.label: removed
facts.value: Permanently removed from the game on 10 January 2024 (Children of the Sun update) along with the entire Kourend Favour system — this miniquest can no longer be started or completed on any account; the wiki page documents legacy content only.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Historically required reaching 100% favour in all five Great Kourend houses via each house's favour activities (library assistance, farming/ploughing, sulphur mining, fishing-crane repair, infirmary/crime-fighting).

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Historically granted access to the Xeric's Heart teleport on Xeric's talisman (itself gated behind the hard Kourend & Kebos Diary) and a 10,000xp lamp (any skill level 40+) from Hosa.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Was a hard prerequisite gate for A Kingdom Divided while live.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Mining 42 was only relevant if the Lovakengj volcanic-sulphur favour activity specifically was chosen among the five houses' activities — not a universal skill gate.

notes: REMOVED FROM THE GAME on 10 January 2024 (Children of the Sun update), alongside the entire Kourend Favour system -- this miniquest can no longer be started or completed by any account, live or otherwise; the page documents legacy content only. When it existed: task was to reach 100% favour in all 5 Great Kourend houses (Arceuus/Hosidius/Lovakengj/Piscarilius/Shayzien) via each house's favour activities (library assistance, ploughing/fertiliser, sulphur mining, fishing-crane repair/sandworm hunting, infirmary/crime-fighting), each of which also had its own house-specific sub-quest that granted a favour bump. Given the removal, do not schedule this as live-game work; retained here only because the depth:04 bundle explicitly named it.

name: At First Light

unlocks: Access to Master Tier Hunters' Rumours

summary: Help injured Guild Hunter Fox with a poultice and assist Atza in Civitas illa Fortis for the Hunters' Guild, with no combat required.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Fetch quest for the Hunters' Guild: help injured Guild Hunter Fox with a poultice, assist Atza in Civitas illa Fortis, and fix a cat bed, all reported back to Guildmaster Apatura.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: No combat required anywhere in the quest.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants access to Master Tier Hunters' Rumours.

notes: Speak to Guildmaster Apatura and Guild Scribe Verity in the tree-basement of the Hunters' Guild, then get a toy mouse from Guild Hunter Wolf and use it to distract Guild Hunter Kiko so you can check his cat bed. Go help injured Guild Hunter Fox south-east of the guild near the mine: pick a leaf from a leafy bush near him, pick a second leaf at Locus Oasis, and catch 2 embertailed jerboas with a box trap for their tails, then combine a tail with the leaves into a makeshift poultice and give it to Fox. Visit Atza near the southern gate of Civitas illa Fortis, set up a pile of equipment (hammer available nearby) for her, and receive trimmed fur in return. Return the trimmed fur/report to Fox, deliver his report to Guild Scribe Verity, fix Kiko's cat bed with the spare jerboa tail, and report to Guildmaster Apatura to finish. No combat required.

Barehanded fishing at harpoon spots
Firemaking a log with a bow (skill-gated by log tier)
Pyre ship funeral firemaking method (needs chewed bones from mithril dragons, level 304)

Barbarian herblore: combine roe into an attack potion for a barbarian potion

summary: Complete six independent skill lessons taught by Otto Godblessed at his Baxtorian Falls grotto, in any order.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Six independent skill lessons taught by Otto Godblessed (fishing, farming, firemaking, smithing, herblore techniques), completable in any order by reporting success back to him after each.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Barbarian heavy-rod fishing (dissect leaping fish for roe) and barehanded fishing at harpoon spots.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Firemaking a log directly onto a bow (skill-gated by log tier), plus the advanced pyre-ship funeral method using chewed bones from mithril dragons (level 304).

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Bare-handed seed planting (no dibber) and pot-smashing sapling planting.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: One-handed spear/hasta smithing at the barbarian anvil.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Barbarian herblore: combine roe into a 2-dose attack potion for a barbarian potion.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Barbarian-series unlocks feed directly into later content: Scorpion Catcher, Horror from the Deep, and Rag and Bone Man II's mithril-dragon-farming bank access.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Reward xp is unknown/unrecorded in held text ("??").

notes: Six independent skill lessons taught by Otto Godblessed, doable in any order, each ending by reporting success back to him: (1) heavy-rod fishing - search the barbarian bed for a rod, catch a leaping fish, dissect it for roe; (2) barehanded fishing - catch at any harpoon spot with no tool; (3) firemaking with a bow - use logs on the bow per a level table, then (advanced) kill mithril dragons for chewed bones and build/light a funeral pyre ship; (4) farming - bare-hand-plant a seed, then plant a sapling by smashing its pot; (5) smithing - forge one bronze spear then one bronze hasta at the barbarian anvil, asking Otto between each; (6) herblore - use roe on a 2-dose attack potion to brew a barbarian potion. Unlocks are used throughout later Barbarian-series content (Scorpion Catcher, Horror from the Deep, Rag and Bone Man II bank access via mithril-dragon farming).

name: Bear Your Soul

skills: Agility 70/80 (boostable) only if using the agility shortcut to the Key Master instead of a dusty key or Key master teleport

summary: Locate a randomly-spawning book in Arceuus Library, then dig up and repair a damaged soul bearer via Aretha and the Key Master.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Find-the-book miniquest: locate a randomly-spawning book in Arceuus Library, then dig up and repair a damaged soul bearer via Aretha and the Key Master in Cerberus' Lair.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The triggering book ('The Journey of Souls') spawns on a random bookshelf in Arceuus Library each playthrough — no fixed location.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Lost soul bearers are freely re-obtainable afterward by digging the Arceuus church crypt again — no need to repeat the full quest.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants the soul bearer, used to store and collect soul runes.

notes: Find-the-book miniquest: the target book spawns on a random bookshelf in Arceuus Library each playthrough (a third-party client's book-search helps narrow it down); reading it points to Aretha at the Soul Altar, who sends you to dig in the Arceuus church crypt for a damaged soul bearer, then to the Key Master in Cerberus' Lair (Taverley Dungeon) to repair it. Lost soul bearers are freely re-obtainable by digging the crypt again.

name: Below Ice Mountain

unlocks: 2,000 coins
Access to the Ruins of Camdozaal
Flex emote
Ability to make a steak sandwich

start.location: path south of Ice Mountain, north of Falador (3002,3436)

summary: Reunite archaeologist Willow's old crew through three recruitment side-tasks, then defeat the level-25 Ancient Guardian at Camdozaal.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Reunite archaeologist Willow's old crew — Checkal, Marley, and Burntof — via three separate recruitment side-tasks, then break into the sealed Ruins of Camdozaal together.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Recruiting each NPC needs a distinct mini-task: training with Atlas plus a Flex emote for Checkal, crafting a steak sandwich for Marley, and winning rock-paper-scissors for Burntof.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Final encounter is the level-25 Ancient Guardian — can be fought directly, or bypassed by mining its 4 corner structural pillars instead of combat.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants 2,000 coins, access to the Ruins of Camdozaal, the Flex emote, and the ability to make a steak sandwich.

notes: Archaeologist Willow needs her old crew reunited to break into sealed ruins west of Ice Mountain. Recruit Checkal in Barbarian Village: train with Atlas in The Long Hall tavern, then use the Flex emote on him with cooked meat/beer from the tavern tables in hand. Recruit Marley at the yew trees in Edgeville (must speak to him first), then get the steak sandwich recipe from the cook at Varrock's Blue Moon Inn and craft one (knife on cooked meat with bread in inventory) to bring back to Marley. Recruit Burntof at Falador's Rising Sun Inn by buying him a beer and winning/playing a round of rock-paper-scissors. With all three recruited, meet Willow at the Ruins of Camdozaal entrance west of Ice Mountain; after the entry cutscene, defeat the level-25 Ancient Guardian (fight it directly, or mine its 4 corner structural pillars instead of combat) to finish.

name: Beneath Cursed Sands

unlocks: Access to the Tombs of Amascut raid
Keris partisan (claimable free from Selim outside Al Kharid Palace if inventory was full)
Circlet of water — untradeable helm that acts as a waterskin (claimable free from the High Priest in Sophanem if inventory was full)
Fairy ring code AKP on a small island south-west of the necropolis
Jaltevas teleport option on the Pharaoh's sceptre (via Commune on the necropolis obelisk)

summary: Investigate Menaphos's lockdown for spy Maisa through the Ruins of Ullek and the necropolis, defeating the Champion of Scabaras and the Menaphite Akh.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Investigate Menaphos's lockdown for spy Maisa, working through the Ruins of Ullek and the necropolis to defeat the Champion of Scabaras and the Menaphite Akh.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Fights the level-174 Head Menaphite Guard at the Jaltevas Pyramid entrance — it deals 1/3 max HP through Protect from Melee, so that prayer doesn't fully block it.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Fights the level-379 Champion of Scabaras (alternates magic/melee) — stay at range, destroy its 20hp Shadow Rift quickly, kill its poison-swarm summon fast, and avoid its 4-tile Shadow Burst attack.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Final fight is the level-351 Menaphite Akh (melee) — dodge her lightning-bolt twitch attack by standing behind or beside her, and kill her summoned Menaphite Shadow quickly or it can swap places with her.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The Ruins of Ullek section includes a passcode-locked chest (1118513), a scarab-emblem pillar placement puzzle, timed lever traps in a maze, and an urn-matching Riddle of the Tomb keyed to each player's own emblem clues.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants access to the Tombs of Amascut raid, a free Keris partisan (from Selim, if inventory was full when offered), a free Circlet of water waterskin-helm (from the Sophanem High Priest, if inventory was full), fairy ring code AKP, and a Jaltevas teleport option on the Pharaoh's sceptre.

notes: Investigate Menaphos's lockdown for the Al Kharid spy Maisa at her campsite east of Sophanem (reachable after quest-start via fairy ring AKP or a Communed Pharaoh's sceptre). Defeat the level-174 Head Menaphite Guard at the blocked Jaltevas Pyramid entrance without Protect from Melee (it deals 1/3 max HP through the prayer). At the Ruins of Ullek, light a furnace with coal, dig up a passcode-locked chest (1118513), craft and place a scarab emblem on a pillar (facing pincers-up), then fight through two level-119 Scarab Mages and disable timed lever traps in a corridor maze. Solve the Riddle of the Tomb by matching per-player emblem clues (item/god order) to the correct urns, then grab a rusty key from the spirit's room. Use ranged to fight the level-379 Champion of Scabaras (alternates magic/melee — stay at range, destroy its 20hp Shadow Rift fast, kill its poison-swarm summon quickly, avoid its 4-tile Shadow Burst). At Nardah, feed a crocodile at the River Elid to retrieve a Lily of the Elid, then help Zahur balance her chemistry-heat puzzle (max the middle/right bars, halve the right, lower the middle to finish) for a pox cure to deliver to the High Priest in Sophanem. Finally return to the necropolis and defeat the level-351 Menaphite Akh (melee; dodge her lightning-bolt twitch attack by standing behind/beside her; kill her summoned Menaphite Shadow quickly or it can swap places with her) to complete the quest.

summary: Sequel to Dwarf Cannon: recover Dwarven lore-book pages, forge a gold helmet, and defeat an Arzinian Avatar variant for Dondakan.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Direct sequel to Dwarf Cannon: help Dondakan the Dwarf recover Dwarven lore-book pages, forge a gold helmet, and defeat an Arzinian Avatar for the schematic reward.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Final battle is against one of three Arzinian Avatar variants (chosen by your highest combat stat) within an 8-minute time limit; mining 15 gold ore in the arena beforehand lowers the boss from level 125 to level 75 (mining 6 keeps it at 125).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Removing the gold helmet mid-fight aborts the Arzinian Avatar attempt — it must stay equipped throughout.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants the Rune pickaxe, the Gold helmet, access to the Arzinian Mine (dwarf banks a 20%/10%-with-Charos cut of gold ore mined there), and a ring of wealth teleport to Dondakan's rock.

notes: Direct sequel to Dwarf Cannon. Cross the river to Dondakan via the Dwarven Ferryman, then collect 3 Dwarven lore book pages in the Dwarven Mine (kill scorpions, search mine carts, mine any non-Adamantite/Coal rock). Make a golden cannonball (gold bar smelted at a furnace) and fire it at Dondakan's rock to get a Schematic; combine it with base schematics from the lore book and further schematics from the Dwarf Engineer, then smith a gold helmet. Final battle: equip the gold helmet + best pickaxe, mine 6 or 15 gold ore inside the arena (15 lowers the boss from level 125 to 75), then fight one of three Arzinian Avatar variants (chosen by your highest combat stat) within an 8-minute time limit -- removing the helmet mid-fight aborts the attempt. Talk to Dondakan again to finish.

start.location: eastern coast of Feldip Hills (fairy ring code AKS)

summary: Craft ogre arrows for Rantz, lure a chompy bird with bloated toads, then kill and cook it using his ogre bow.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Craft ogre arrows for Rantz the ogre, obtain bloated toads to lure a chompy bird, then kill and cook it yourself using his ogre bow.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Effectively requires the ability to defend against level-64 wolves and level-53 ogres, though this isn't a formal skill/quest gate.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Fill ogre bellows from swamp bubbles and use them on a swamp toad to make bloated toads (holds up to 3), which lure a chompy bird when dropped where Rantz points.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants the Ogre bow, the ability to fletch ogre arrows, and the ability to cook chompy birds to earn bowman hats.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Mourning's End Part I, Recipe for Disaster (Lumbridge Guide & Skrach Uglogwee subquests), Zogre Flesh Eaters, and the Western Provinces Diary.

notes: Intermediate quest requiring the ability to defend against level 64 wolves and level 53 ogres. Talk to Rantz the ogre; chop achey trees south-west of him, knife the logs into ogre arrow shafts, feather them, chisel 4 wolf bones (dropped by nearby wolves) into arrowtips and attach them, then hand the finished ogre arrows to Rantz. Ask him all his dialogue options, enter the cave north of him, talk to Fycie and Bugs and unlock their chest for ogre bellows; fill the bellows from swamp bubbles south-west and use them on a swamp toad for bloated toads (can hold up to 3). Drop a bloated toad where Rantz points, wait for a chompy bird, then take the ogre bow from him and kill the chompy bird yourself; pluck it for raw chompy meat. Return to the cave, gather the ingredients Fycie/Bugs ask for, cook the chompy on the spit-roast north of Rantz, and hand it in to finish. Required for: Mourning's End Part I, Recipe for Disaster (Lumbridge Guide & Skrach Uglogwee), Zogre Flesh Eaters, Western Provinces Diary.

notes: Sneak a gas mask (via pigeon + bird feed distraction) past the mourner watchtower into West Ardougne to retrieve Elena's distillator, fetch touch paper from the Rimmington chemist, hand plague-sample vials to 3 of the chemist's henchmen around a campfire and collect the processed samples back, then smuggle the sample and paper through the guarded Varrock fence disguised in a priest gown to deliver them to Guidor, and finally report to King Lathas in Ardougne Castle.

summary: Infiltrate the Black Knights' Fortress disguised as a knight to eavesdrop on their plans and sabotage their secret weapon with a cabbage.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Infiltrate the Black Knights' Fortress disguised as a knight to eavesdrop on their plans and sabotage their secret weapon with a cabbage.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Requires 12 Quest Points and the ability to dodge/evade level-33 Black Knights — no kills are actually required.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Sabotage is done by jamming the secret weapon's works with the cabbage (do not eat it) rather than through combat.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants 2,500 coins.

notes: Requires 12 quest points and the ability to dodge/evade level 33 Black Knights (no kills required). Disguised in an iron chainbody + bronze med helm, infiltrate the Black Knights' Fortress, push a hidden wall and navigate ladders to eavesdrop at a grill, then bluff past a guard's chat to reach the dining hall, push another hidden wall, and jam the secret weapon's works with a cabbage (do not eat it) before reporting back to Sir Amik Varze.

name: Bone Voyage

unlocks: access to Fossil Island (disembark via the Junior Navigator)
Fossil Island note book

summary: Gather a sawmill agreement, a bone charm, and a potion of sealegs, then crew a ship to Fossil Island for Curator Haig Halen.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Help Curator Haig Halen arrange a sea voyage to Fossil Island by gathering a sawmill agreement, a bone charm, and a potion of sealegs, then crewing the ship yourself.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Requires visiting the Apothecary in Varrock twice with 2 vodka + marrentill potion (unf) to brew a potion of sealegs for the Lead Navigator.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Ends with a ship-steering minigame: click the arrow opposite the shown tilt direction and adjust sail height for stability until the progress bar fills.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Attempting to open the Woodcutting Guild's east gate is enough to satisfy the sawmill-proposal step even without meeting the guild's own entry requirements.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants access to Fossil Island (disembark via the Junior Navigator) and the Fossil Island note book.

notes: Talk to Curator Haig Halen to start. Visit the canal barge north of the Digsite (barge foreman), then the sawmill operator at the Lumber Yard, carry a sawmill proposal to the Woodcutting Guild's sawmill operator (just attempting to open the guild's east gate is enough if you lack entry requirements), then bring the signed agreement back to the Lumber Yard. Board the barge, talk to the Lead Navigator, then Jack Seagull at the Rusty Anchor Inn in Port Sarim, reboard, talk to the Odd Old Man at the limestone mine north of the digsite for a bone charm. Bring 2 vodka + marrentill potion (unf) to the Apothecary in Varrock twice for a potion of sealegs; give it to the Lead Navigator and the bone charm to the Junior Navigator. Steer the ship minigame (click the arrow opposite the tilt direction shown, adjust sail height for stability) until the progress bar fills to arrive at Fossil Island and finish.

summary: Sabotage a rival pirate ship in Bill Teach's crew and win a naval battle to unlock Mos Le'Harmless.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Board Bill Teach's ship and fight a scripted sea battle against a rival pirate vessel.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Cut the enemy ship's sail rigging, blow up its powder barrel, and patch your own ship's leaks during the battle.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Plunder the enemy ship's cargo for loot, then repair and fire your own cannon to finish the fight.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Fire canister rounds first to kill 3 pirates, then cannonballs to hole the enemy ship 3 times to win.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for The Great Brain Robbery.

notes: Board Bill Teach's ship and sabotage an enemy pirate vessel: cut their sail rigging, blow up their powder barrel, patch your own ship's leaks, plunder their cargo for loot, then repair and fire your cannon (canister rounds first to kill 3 pirates, then cannonballs to hole their ship 3 times) to win the sea battle.

name: Children of the Sun

unlocks: Access to Varlamore via the Quetzal Transport System and fairy rings

summary: Investigate a Varlamore delegation in Varrock by tailing a suspicious guard and unmasking four disguised bandits.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Varlamore's opening quest: investigate a Varlamore delegation that has arrived in Varrock.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Covertly tail a suspicious guard through Varrock without being spotted, using nearby buildings for cover.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Identify and mark four bandits disguised as guards around Varrock Square by their appearance, weapons, or stance.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: No combat or skill requirements.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for the rest of the Varlamore quest line.

notes: Varlamore's opening quest. A Varlamore delegation has arrived in Varrock; speak to Noah or Alina about it, then covertly tail a suspicious guard through Varrock without being spotted, ducking behind buildings (Lowe's Archery Emporium, the building south of it, Old Man Yarlo's house, near Aubury's Rune Shop) to stay hidden. Report to Sergeant Tobyn at the castle wall, then identify and mark four bandits disguised as guards by their distinctive appearance/weapons/stance around Varrock Square, and report back to the sergeant to finish. No combat or skill requirements — this quest unlocks the rest of the Varlamore quest line.

facts.label: overview

facts.label: mechanics

facts.label: items-note

notes: Talk to Veos (found at the northernmost dock of Port Sarim, south of the pub) to travel to Port Piscarilius and start; he gives an enchanted scroll, use a feather on it for an enchanted quill. Visit each of the 5 Great Kourend districts and speak with a named store owner about their district (e.g. Jennifer in Shayzien, Munty in Lovakengj). Return to Veos, then travel near the Dark Altar (Games necklace to Wintertodt Camp then run, or fairy ring code CIS) and activate the mysterious orb. Return to Veos to finish.

summary: Fit four coloured cogs into their matching spindles in the dungeon beneath the Clock Tower to restart it.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: No skill or quest requirements; the wiki's own 'requirements' line is a survivability note about running past level 53 ogres, not a level gate.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Find 4 coloured cogs in the dungeon beneath the tower and fit each into its matching spindle one at a time (can't carry more than one cog at once).

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: The black cog must be cooled with a bucket of water or ice gloves before it can be picked up.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: The red cog is guarded by ogres; the white cog requires poisoning dungeon rats with rat poison and a food trough to unhinge a gate.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: No xp is granted; the only reward is a flat 500 coins.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Fills out the Ardougne Diary prerequisite set alongside Plague City and Sheep Shearer.

notes: No skill/quest gate; the wiki's own 'requirements' line is just 'ability to run past level 53 Ogres' -- not a level check, a survivability note for the red-cog room. Find 4 coloured cogs in the dungeon under the tower and fit each into its matching spindle one at a time (can't carry more than one cog at once): blue cog sits in a rat cell; black cog needs a bucket of water or ice gloves to cool it before picking up; red cog is guarded by ogres; white cog requires poisoning dungeon rats (rat poison + food trough) to unhinge a gate. Reward is a flat 500 coins, no xp. Fills out the Ardougne-diary prereq set alongside Plague City and Sheep Shearer.

summary: Infiltrate the penguins' Iceberg outpost as Zookeeper Larry's spy to uncover their escape plan.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Only quest in the Penguin series -- Zookeeper Larry suspects the zoo's penguins are planning an escape and enlists you to investigate.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Watch a cutscene of a unique 3-emote penguin greeting sequence and memorize it -- it's needed again later (guessable if forgotten, max 24 tries).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Craft a Clockwork penguin suit at a crafting table 3, then test the memorized greeting on zoo penguins to learn of their escape plan.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Trade a raw cod or wear a ring of charos with penguins disguised as sheep to learn the secret phrase 'do not trust the walrus'.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Trade swamp tar and 5 feathers to black-marketeer Noodle for a fake KGP ID, then infiltrate the Iceberg outpost using the password 'cabbage'.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Escape an Icelord cage by defeating 1-3 level 51 Icelords, each granting 40 Attack xp.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Making Friends with My Arm.

notes: Only quest in the Penguin series. Zookeeper Larry is paranoid about penguins; build a bird-hide lookout on the Iceberg (oak plank + spade on a snow patch) and watch a cutscene of a unique-per-player 3-emote penguin greeting sequence (memorize it -- 8 possible emotes, needed later). Craft a Clockwork suit at a crafting table 3 (plank + silk + clockwork mechanism). Test it on zoo penguins using the memorized emote greeting (guessable if forgotten, max 24 tries) to learn they're planning to escape by flying; find more penguins disguised as sheep in Fred the Farmer's Lumbridge pasture, get a secret phrase ("do not trust the walrus") by trading a raw cod or wearing a ring of charos. Gather swamp tar + 5 feathers to trade black-marketeer Noodle for a fake KGP ID, then infiltrate the Iceberg outpost with the password "cabbage", complete the Penguin Agility Course, and steal a cowbell + craft Penguin Bongos (mahogany plank + leather) to distract the guards Ping and Pong and open the war room. Get caught by Pescaling Pax and thrown in an Icelord cage (level 51, kill 1-3, each grants 40 Attack xp) to escape, then report the penguin invasion plan back to Larry.

summary: Sneak through the Sophanem dungeon labyrinth and defeat the Giant Scarab to open a secret route into Menaphos.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Sequel to Icthlarin's Little Helper: sneak into Menaphos through the Sophanem dungeon labyrinth.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Navigate floor/wall traps, crusher blocks, sand pits, and scarab pit-traps using a tile-marker path (green=direction, yellow=step carefully, red=warning); one trap drops you into a multi-combat area.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Kill the level-191 Giant Scarab boss, which can spawn mages/riders and extinguishes bullseye lanterns.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Shuttle between Maisa and Osman to arrange a second, secret entrance into Menaphos.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Optionally grab the Keris dagger for the Desert hard diary by killing a locust rider with it before teleporting out.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Two bankable lamps grant 7,000xp each, chosen from Attack, Strength, Defence, Hitpoints, Ranged, or Magic.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Beneath Cursed Sands and the Desert hard diary.

notes: Sequel to Icthlarin's Little Helper: sneak into Menaphos through the Sophanem dungeon labyrinth. Talk to the High Priest, then Jex in Sophanem's NE building, descend and follow the tile-marker path (green=direction, yellow=step carefully, red=warning) through floor/wall traps, crusher blocks, sand pits and scarab pit-traps to a ladder -- keep Protect from Magic up and auto-retaliate off; one scarab trap drops you a level into multi-combat. Search Kaleef's corpse, read the parchment, then shuttle between Maisa (west) and Osman (Al Kharid, then Sophanem's gates) to arrange a second, secret entrance. Re-enter via that route and kill the level-191 Giant Scarab boss (safespottable near Kaleef's body with Protect from Missiles if maging/ranging, but it can spawn mages/riders and extinguishes bullseye lanterns -- bring a tinderbox). Talk to Osman, optionally grab Keris for the Desert hard diary (don't teleport out if going for that task; return and kill a locust rider with it), then return to the High Priest to finish. Gates Beneath Cursed Sands and continues the desert quest series toward Desert Treasure II.

unlocks: Permission to use the Cook-o-matic 100 (reduces burn chance on some foods)

summary: Gather an egg, a bucket of milk, and a pot of flour for the Cook to bake the Duke's birthday cake.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Novice tutorial-tier quest: gather 3 ingredients for the Duke's birthday cake, all obtainable for free during the quest.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Get a bucket from the castle cellar and milk a dairy cow in the Lumbridge cow field; get an egg from the chicken farm north-west of the cow field; get an empty pot from the Cook and mill flour at Mill Lane Mill.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Recipe for Disaster; also counts as an Easy Lumbridge & Draynor Diary task (bake bread on the Lumbridge castle kitchen range).

notes: Novice tutorial-tier quest, all 3 ingredients are free and gettable during the quest with zero GE use. Talk to the Cook about the Duke's birthday cake; get a bucket from the castle cellar and use it on a dairy cow in the Lumbridge cow field for milk; get an egg from the chicken farm north-west of the cow field; get an empty pot from the Cook, pick grain outside the Mill Lane Mill, climb to the top floor and use the grain on the hopper, pull the lever, then scoop flour from the bin downstairs with the pot. Hand all 3 items to the Cook to finish. Required for: Recipe for Disaster; also an Easy Lumbridge & Draynor Diary task (bake bread on the Lumbridge castle kitchen range).

summary: Help Arhein get Catherby's by-laws changed so a duck can be used to chart ocean currents.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Third Sailing-skill quest: help Arhein test whether a duck can track ocean currents, blocked by Catherby bureaucracy.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Fill in form cr-4p for Councillor Catherine using charcoal; the quiz answers are arbitrary but must be repeated consistently later.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Buy a mayoral fishbowl and tiny net from Harry, fish a new Mayor of Catherby from an aquarium, and get it a mayoral chain from Arhein.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Sign form 7r4-5h with the new mayor to change the by-law, then track an ocean current near the Obelisk of Water with the current duck.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Needs a boat docked at Catherby to complete the final leg.

notes: Third Sailing-skill quest. Arhein wants to test whether a duck can track ocean currents but is blocked by Catherby bureaucracy. Fill in form cr-4p for Councillor Catherine using charcoal (answers are arbitrary but must be repeated later -- picking the first option every time is simplest). She reveals the by-law needs mayoral approval, and the current mayor died -- Catherby has had 25 consecutive fish mayors. Buy a mayoral fishbowl and tiny net from Harry (50 coins) and fish a new Mayor of Catherby from an aquarium, then get it a mayoral chain from Arhein. Redo the form quiz for Catherine (only wrong answers need repeating), sign form 7r4-5h with the new mayor, and return it to change the by-law. Take a boat out east near the Obelisk of Water, find a ripple, and use the current duck on it to track the current to shore near Holgart, then return the duck to Arhein to finish. Needs a boat docked at Catherby for the final leg.

summary: Follow one of three randomized ghost sequences across Gielinor to earn the full ghostly robes set.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: After talking to Valdez, the game randomly assigns one of 3 fixed ghost sequences (hinted via a location clue); you must follow that sequence's 5 ghosts at that sequence's specific locations only.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Several sequence stops are in deep, dangerous Wilderness (up to level 56), such as Rogues' Castle or Edgeville Dungeon.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Ghostly robe pieces are individually reclaimable for free afterward by revisiting your assigned sequence's ghosts.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: A 10,000xp lamp (any skill level 50+) plus 10 kudos are awarded from Historian Minas in Varrock Museum upon completion.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Gates The General's Shadow.

notes: After Valdez, the game randomly assigns one of 3 fixed sequences (told via a location hint -- 'pirates and a shipwreck'=Seq1, 'north-east'=Seq2, 'south-east'=Seq3) and you must follow that sequence's 5 ghosts (Rennard/Kharrim/Lennissa/Dhalak/Viggora) in order at THAT sequence's specific locations only -- the same ghosts exist at all 3 sequences' spots but only respond in your assigned one. Multiple stops are deep/dangerous Wilderness (up to level 56, e.g. Rogues' Castle in Seq1, Edgeville Dungeon in Seq3). Robes are individually reclaimable for free afterward by revisiting your sequence's ghosts.

facts.label: overview

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Steal a servant uniform to infiltrate Villa Lucens through the Head Butler, then question the four nobles and search the wine cellar for clues.

facts.label: mechanics

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Accuse and fight Adala (level 49, innocent), then later accuse and fight Naiatli on stage to close the case.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Post-quest: claim masks/tray from the Head Butler and a needle from the Costumer; search the SW fountain for an icon to use on the NE Aldarin statue for a bonus reward.

notes: Murder-mystery on the island of Aldarin. Break into the house north of Patzi via the south window (wait for the guard to move) to steal a servant uniform, then wear it to enter Villa Lucens through the Head Butler (who holds your gear). Talk to the four nobles (Constantinius, Cozyac, Pavo, Xocotla) and Patzi, then search the wine cellar for clues (antique wine, injured man, jugs, box, stool, pottery, Livius' body, wine storage). Talk to guards Stradius/Hutza, pickpocket all six suspects, inspect the physical evidence (wine labels, threatening note, shipping contract, drinking flask), then accuse and fight Adala (level 49) -- she's innocent. Climb the Loose rocks shortcut, go backstage, question the Costumer, search the poisoned crate/secret bookshelf passage/stained costume rack, then accuse and fight Naiatli on stage to close the case. Post-quest: claim masks/tray from the Head Butler, needle from the Costumer, pickpocket Constantinius for a chest key (opens the cellar's fancy chest), search the SW fountain for an icon, and use it on the NE Aldarin statue for a bonus reward.

summary: Help Denulth find a hidden route up Death Plateau to investigate troll raids on Burthorpe.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Novice quest that opens the troll questline: help Denulth investigate troll raids on Burthorpe.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Give Harold an Asgarnian ale for an IOU note with a combination (a Blurberry special auto-wins the dice bet, otherwise gamble repeatedly over 60gp until he goes bankrupt).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Exchange a certificate, climbing boots, and iron bar for spiked boots, then trade 10 unnoted bread + 10 unnoted trout + the spiked boots to Tenzing for a map.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Walk the back route around the mountain to reach Denulth and finish, ignoring the Death Plateau warning message on the main path.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Troll Stronghold.

notes: Novice quest, opens the troll questline. Talk to Denulth about the troll raids on Burthorpe; ask Eohric on the 1st floor of the castle then Harold at the Burthorpe bar about the missing guard. Give Harold an Asgarnian ale (a Blurberry special auto-wins the dice bet, otherwise gamble over 60gp repeatedly until he goes bankrupt) for an IOU note with a combination; use the combination on the 5 coloured balls at the castle to unlock a door. Talk to Saba in the cave north-west of Burthorpe about another route up the plateau; talk to Tenzing, then Dunstan for a certificate, and exchange the certificate + climbing boots + iron bar for spiked boots. Give Tenzing 10 unnoted bread + 10 unnoted trout + the spiked boots for a map, walk the back route around the mountain (ignore the Death Plateau warning message on the main path), and talk to Denulth with the combination to finish. Required for: Troll Stronghold.

summary: Help Zanik infiltrate the H.A.M. Hideout, revive her with tears of Guthix, and defeat Sigmund.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Follow Kazgar through a hole under Lumbridge kitchen to Mistag, then help Zanik infiltrate the H.A.M. Hideout.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Must defeat level-50 Sigmund with melee or magic.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Position Zanik out of guard sightlines and lure each of 4 guards into her line of fire one at a time to sneak into the Hideout.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Travel to Juna in the Chasm of Tears and collect 20 tears to revive Zanik after she is killed.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Fight and kill 3 Senior Guards (safespottable via Zanik), then Sigmund (level 50, uses protect-missiles in phase 2, safespottable behind boxes).

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Ironmen should pickpocket H.A.M. members instead of gathering robe sets -- better odds during the quest.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Directly extends the H.A.M. Hideout thieving spot with post-quest loot-chest access.

notes: Must beat level-50 Sigmund with melee or magic. Follow Kazgar through hole under Lumbridge kitchen to Mistag, bring 2 sets of H.A.M. robes to Zanik in Lumbridge cellar (ironmen: pickpocket H.A.M. members instead, better odds during quest). Zanik follows you on a Lumbridge town tour (talk to Cook/Duke/Hans/random Man-or-Woman/Bob/Father Aereck/Lumbridge Guide/a goblin/shopkeeper) then infiltrate the H.A.M. Hideout in disguise (full robes), talk Johanhus, find a hidden trapdoor, and use a multi-step guard-sniping stealth sequence: position Zanik out of guard sightlines, lure each guard into her line of fire one at a time (4 guards total) using wait/say-now dialogue commands. Get arrested by a senior guard, pick the cell lock to escape, find Zanik dead and take her body. Travel to Juna in the Chasm of Tears (via Tears of Guthix path from Lumbridge kitchen cellar - mine a blockage, hop a stone between waterfalls, or games-necklace teleport if that quest is done) and collect 20 tears to revive Zanik in a cutscene. Return to the castle cellar, go to the chicken farm near the water wheel, hide Zanik in an empty crate, descend a trapdoor in full H.A.M. robes to fight and kill 3 Senior Guards (safespottable via Zanik) then Sigmund (level 50, uses protect-missiles in phase 2, safespottable behind boxes). Smash the drilling machine and exit south. Directly extends the H.A.M. Hideout thieving spot with post-quest loot-chest access.

summary: Investigate an undead uprising in Varrock and confront the Fitzharmon descendant behind it.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Long-chain endgame Varrock quest: investigate an undead uprising and confront a hidden threat to Varrock led by Dimintheis.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Follow a forest trail of clues to a dungeon, kill armoured zombies and bottle their red mist to investigate the uprising.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Get a chaos core from Chaos Golems at Ruins of Camdozaal and combine it with a mined barronite deposit at the Sacred Forge to imbue a barronite ore.

facts.label: mechanics

notes: Long-chain endgame Varrock quest gated behind six other quests plus 55 Smithing/52 Hunter. Talk to Elias in the Jolly Boar Inn, follow a forest trail of inspectable clues east to a trapdoor dungeon, kill armoured zombies and bottle their red mist (3 before meeting Arrav, 3 more after) to investigate the undead uprising. Report to Elias at Varrock Palace, then head to Ruins of Camdozaal, get a chaos core from Chaos Golems and combine it with a mined barronite deposit at the Sacred Forge to imbue a barronite ore. Finally receive the Shield of Arrav from Captain Rovin in Varrock Palace, dig through Reldo's scrolls/records to identify the Fitzharmon descendant among six named Varrock NPCs, then confront Dimintheis in south-east Varrock to trigger the finale cutscene.

notes: No skill/quest requirements; must be able to defeat a level 27 demon (Delrith) aided by dark wizards, using Silverlight in melee. Give Aris 1gp, then collect three keys to Silverlight from three Varrock Palace NPCs: Captain Rovin (talk, NW tower 2F), a rusty key found via bucket-of-water-on-drain then a manhole dungeon crawl, and Wizard Traiborn at the Wizards' Tower in exchange for 25 un-noted bones. Return the keys to Sir Prysin, wield Silverlight, fight through dark wizards at Varrock's south entrance, then banish Delrith with the incantation Aris/Wally gave earlier (randomized per player, re-askable from Aris if forgotten).

summary: Collect four elemental diamonds from separate dungeons and place them in the pyramid to reach Azzanadra.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Master-tier quest: collect four diamonds (Smoke, Shadow, Ice, Blood) from separate mini-dungeons, then place them in the pyramid's obelisks to reach Azzanadra.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Smoke diamond: light 4 torches in the Smoke Dungeon before they burn out, then kill Fareed (ice gloves or water spells needed to damage him).

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Shadow diamond: kill Damis in both forms underground (safespottable, drains prayer fast).

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Ice diamond: kill 5 ice trolls then Kamil using only fire spells.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Blood diamond: kill Dessous by pouring blessed blood on his tomb in the Morytania Graveyard.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: The final pyramid run races through 4 trapped floors to reach Azzanadra.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Recipe for Disaster (Culinaromancer), Curse of the Empty Lord (partial completion), Hopespear's Will, The Frozen Door, hard tiers of Desert/Varrock/Morytania diaries, elite tiers of Ardougne/Wilderness/Lumbridge/Desert diaries, and Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire.

notes: Master-tier quest, the classic 4-diamond fetch. Talk to Asgarnia Smith at Bedabin Camp, get a scroll translated by Terry Balando at the Digsite Exam Centre, buy Eblis a beer at the bandit-camp pub, deliver the listed items to Eblis to learn of the four diamonds, then collect them in any order: Smoke diamond (light 4 torches in the Smoke Dungeon before they burn out, unlock the central chest, kill Fareed -- ice gloves or water spells needed to damage him); Shadow diamond (pick 3 locks on a chest in the bandit camp, give the gilded cross to Rasolo for the ring of visibility, then kill Damis in both forms underground -- safespottable, drains prayer fast); Ice diamond (feed a Troll child a sweet, kill 5 ice trolls then Kamil using only fire spells, wear spiked boots to climb an ice ledge, melt 3 frozen trolls with a fire spell); Blood diamond (get a silver pot blessed on Entrana, mix spice+garlic powder into a pot of blood from Malak in Canifis, pour it on Dessous's tomb in the Morytania Graveyard and kill him -- alternating protect-melee/protect-range each tick avoids most damage). Place all 4 diamonds in the pyramid's obelisks, then race through 4 trapped floors to reach Azzanadra. Flattened per depth:02 convention: Troll Stronghold's own requirement Death Plateau is folded into reqs.quests directly. Required for: Recipe for Disaster (Culinaromancer), Curse of the Empty Lord miniquest (partial completion only), Hopespear's Will miniquest, The Frozen Door miniquest, Hard tiers of Desert/Varrock/Morytania diaries, Elite tiers of Ardougne/Wilderness/Lumbridge/Desert diaries, Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire.

summary: Defeat the four Forgotten Four bosses to loot their medallions and confront the Empty Lord's legacy at the Ancient Vault.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Grandmaster capstone: defeat the four 'Forgotten Four' bosses to loot medallions and place them in the Ancient Vault's statues before the finale.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Vardorvis: defend Kasonde from stranglers for 3 minutes, brew a strangler serum, then kill Vardorvis and Kasonde himself.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Perseriya: solve 3 randomized puzzle rooms to burn 3 ships, then fight the Leviathan with Ranged and Shadow spells.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Duke Sucellus: solve gate-code puzzles while dodging Jhallan, then defeat the sleeping Duke via a mushroom-poison mechanic.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: The Whisperer: navigate the Shadow Realm managing a sanity meter, then fight a magic-weak boss with frequent prayer switches.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Finale: fight a Mysterious Figure, then Sliske's four wights one after another (Forsaken Assassin, Ketla the Unworthy, Kasonde the Craven, Persten the Deceitful), each with distinct dodge mechanics, before a final cutscene with Azzanadra.

refs.title: Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire
Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire/Quick guide

notes: Grandmaster capstone: the Ancient Vault hub has 4 empty statues, one per medallion looted from the 'Forgotten Four' bosses guarding fragments of the fallen Empty Lord's empire. Familiar Faces: solve a mural/mining intro at the Digsite then kill the Ancient Guardian (shield regens unless meleed; dodge falling rocks by avoiding shadowed tiles) and solve a totem/cell golem puzzle. Vardorvis: sail to the Stranglewood, defend Kasonde from strangled for 3 minutes with barricades/satchels, brew a strangler serum from gathered herbs, kill Vardorvis (slash weapon, dodge axes/root splats/prayer-disabling darts) then Kasonde himself (ranged/kite, avoid poison splats, out-DPS his healing). Perseriya: through Guardians of the Rift's Scar, solve 3 randomized puzzle rooms (nerve-combination circuits, cerebral-orb maze, rune-nerve crafting) to burn 3 ships, then fight the Leviathan with Ranged+Shadow spells (prayer-switch by attack colour, stun and hit its back). Duke Sucellus: escape a prison cell, solve gate-code puzzles (chest passwords/combinations) while dodging Jhallan, then defeat the sleeping Duke via a mushroom-poison mechanic and dodge his ice-spike/acid/eye-gaze attacks behind pillars. The Whisperer: navigate the Shadow Realm of Lassar's undercity collecting shadow-torch schematics and keys while managing a sanity meter, then fight a magic-weak boss with frequent prayer switches. After all 4 medallions are placed, the finale ("The Desert Treasure") has you fight a Mysterious Figure, then Sliske's four wights one after another (Forsaken Assassin, Ketla the Unworthy, Kasonde the Craven, Persten the Deceitful -- each with distinct dodge mechanics) before a final cutscene with Azzanadra.

At Doric's house forge a bow-sword from a mithril 2h sword + bow string. Give it to the monk, put the orb he provides into a large pouch, then run the pouch through the Wilderness Abyss to the Law Altar and on to the Entrana church to trigger a swap cutscene. Search the resulting dead monk back at Paterdomus, return to Entrana's high priest, then finish at Falador Park with Sir Tiffy Cashien.
