summary: Assemble a 3-piece map, sail to Crandor, and slay the level 83 dragon Elvarg with an anti-dragon shield.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Classic quest to assemble a 3-piece map, sail to Crandor, and slay the dragon Elvarg to earn a rune platebody and mid-game unlocks.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Talk to the Guildmaster at the Champions' Guild.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Elvarg is a level 83 dragon fought on Crandor; an anti-dragon shield must be equipped (antifire potion recommended).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The 3 map pieces are gathered independently: Melzar's requires clearing a maze in Rimmington and killing a monster sequence ending in a level-82 lesser demon; Thalzar's needs silk+lobster pot+unfired bowl+wizard's mind bomb on a magic door in the Dwarven Mine; Lozar's is bought from or telegrabbed off Wormbrain in Port Sarim's jail.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: A boat to Crandor is bought/repaired from Klarense using 3 planks, 90 steel nails, and 2,000 coins, then sailed to Crandor via Ned in Draynor Village.

notes: Classic quest, gates the Rune platebody and a huge amount of mid-game content. Get Melzar's, Thalzar's, and Lozar's map pieces to Crandor in any order: Thalzar's requires using silk+lobster pot+unfired bowl+wizard's mind bomb on a magic door in the Dwarven Mine; Melzar's requires clearing his eponymous maze in Rimmington, killing a sequence of monsters (zombie rat, ghost, skeleton, zombies, Melzar the Mad, then a level-82 lesser demon) to collect keys; Lozar's is bought from or telegrabbed off Wormbrain in Port Sarim's jail. Combine the three pieces, buy/repair a boat with 3 planks+90 steel nails+2,000 coins from Klarense, sail to Crandor via Ned in Draynor Village, and kill the level-83 dragon Elvarg with an anti-dragon shield equipped (antifire potion recommended). Report back to Oziach in Edgeville to finish.

summary: Grandmaster sequel to Dragon Slayer I: gather 4 dragon key pieces then fight the Assault and a 4-phase Galvek.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Grandmaster sequel to Dragon Slayer I: solve a Fossil Island jigsaw map, gather 4 dragon key pieces, then fight through the Assault against dragon waves and a 4-phase Galvek.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Talk to Alec Kincade outside the Myths' Guild.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Very Long

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Grandmaster

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Final boss Galvek is a 4-phase fight requiring full dragonfire protection and phase-specific dodges (fire traps, stat-drain beam, wave-gaps, earth-bind projectiles).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The Assault stage is a checkpointed defence against escalating dragon waves (green/blue, then black/steel/brutal red, then mithril/adamant/rune, the last two needing antidote++ and insulated boots), preceded by rallying Varrock/Falador/Ardougne or Kandarin rulers in 'A World United'.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: 4 dragon key pieces are gathered independently across Karamja (colour-coded maze avoiding a golem's melee/ranged/magic), Morytania (locator orb dowsing in Mort Myre Swamp), Ungael (kill Vorkath, then a lever/timed dungeon puzzle), and Kourend (statue-placement riddle in a crypt), then reforged at the Ancient Cavern with Fire Wave/Surge on 3 dragon heads.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Notes state this also requires having started the pyre-ship part of the Barbarian Training miniquest for Ancient Cavern access -- not listed in the row's structured reqs.quests array.

notes: Grandmaster sequel to Dragon Slayer I; also requires having started the pyre-ship part of the Barbarian Training miniquest for Ancient Cavern access. Investigate a mural under Crandor, then find and solve a 24-piece jigsaw map on Fossil Island, build a rowboat to Lithkren, and read a skeleton's diary. Use a Catspeak amulet to find Bob the Cat and have the Sphinx enchant permanent cat-speech. On Lunar Isle, brew a dream potion and fight Robert the Strong inside Bob's dream (melee/crush-only fight, hide behind pillars to dodge his special). Collect 4 dragon key pieces: Karamja (navigate a trapped jungle-temple maze avoiding golem melee/ranged/magic by colour), Morytania (craft a locator orb and dowse for a buried piece in Mort Myre Swamp), Ungael (kill Vorkath, then solve a lever/timed-run dungeon puzzle), Kourend (statue-placement riddle in a skeleton-filled crypt). Reforge the key at the Ancient Cavern by casting Fire Wave/Surge on 3 dragon heads, then open the Lithkren doors. Rally Varrock/Falador/Ardougne (or Kandarin) rulers for 'A World United', then 'The Assault': defend a ship from a boarding raid, fight through checkpointed waves of increasingly dangerous dragons (green/blue, then black/steel/brutal red, then mithril/adamant/rune -- the last two have hazardous specials needing antidote++/insulated boots), and finish with a 4-phase Galvek fight requiring full dragonfire protection and phase-specific dodges (fire traps, stat-drain beam, wave-gaps, earth-bind projectiles).

summary: Nurse Cyrisus back to health, then fight four powerful monsters without prayer inside his dream.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Escort the ailing seer Cyrisus to full health, then fight four powerful monsters without prayer inside his dream to become the new Dream Mentor.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Talk to Cyrisus (the fallen man) in a small cave in the Lunar Isle mine.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Master

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Fight in sequence without prayer: The Inadequacy (level 343, hardest, magic recommended), The Everlasting (223, safespottable), The Untouchable (274, safespottable), and The Illusive (108, burrows and relocates).

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: All teleports including ring of life are disabled inside the Dream Hall; the only exit is the north lectern, and dying here is a real hardcore-ironman-ending death.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Raise Cyrisus's health/spirit/armament stats to 100% by feeding him 3 food types in a repeated 7-7-6 order, talking positively, and guessing his ideal armour set at the bank (confirmed via NPC Contact), before brewing a dream potion (goutweed + water-filled dream vial + a crushed hammered-astral-rune reagent).

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Dragon Slayer II, While Guthix Sleeps, Hard Desert Diary, Hard Kourend & Kebos Diary, and Elite Varrock Diary.

notes: Master-difficulty Fremennik/Lunar quest, ability to defeat 4 powerful monsters without prayer required. Find Cyrisus collapsed in the Lunar Isle mine and raise his health/spirit/armament stat bars: feed him 3 food types in the same repeated order (7-7-6 split) for health, talk positively to raise spirit, and guess-and-check his ideal armour set via the bank ('Birds-Eye' Jack) confirmed with the NPC Contact spell (set varies by dominant combat style: melee/ranged/magic). Once all stats hit 100%, escort him to the Oneiromancer near the Astral Altar, brew a dream potion (goutweed + water-filled dream vial + a crushed hammered-astral-rune reagent), then enter the Dream Hall and light the brazier to fight, in sequence and without prayer, The Inadequacy (343, hardest, magic recommended), The Everlasting (223, safespottable west of the lectern), The Untouchable (274, safespottable), and The Illusive (108, burrows and relocates). The lectern at the north of the arena is the ONLY way to leave -- all teleports including ring of life are disabled, and dying here is a real hardcore-ironman-ending death. Return to the Oneiromancer afterward (keep the seal of passage) to finish.

notes: Novice quest that literally unlocks the Herblore skill. Talk to Kaqemeex at the stone circle north of Taverley, then talk to Sanfew upstairs in the Taverley herb shop. Bring the four raw meats down to the Cauldron in Taverley Dungeon and use each on it to enchant them, return the enchanted meats to Sanfew, then talk to Kaqemeex to finish. Required for: A Kingdom Divided, A Taste of Hope, Barbarian Herblore, Eadgar's Ruse, Fairytale II - Cure a Queen, Grim Tales, Heroes' Quest, Jungle Potion, Recruitment Drive, Shades of Mort'ton, Song of the Elves, The Dig Site, Watchtower.

facts.value: Short novice quest that unlocks the dwarf multicannon by repairing Captain Lawgof's fence, retrieving dwarf remains, and reassembling a broken cannon for the Black Guard.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Talk to Captain Lawgof south of the Coal Trucks, north-west of the Fishing Guild.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Novice

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Repair 6 bent railings with a hammer, retrieve dwarf remains from a tower, find the goblin Lollk hiding in a crate south-east of the Fishing Guild, then reassemble the multicannon in the correct tool order (hook on spring, pliers on safety switch, tooth tool on gear) before travelling to the Dwarven Black Guard camp for Nulodion's notes and a cannonball mould.

notes: Short novice quest that unlocks the dwarf multicannon, one of the most impactful early combat-XP tools in the game. Repair 6 bent railings on the west/south fence for Lawgof with a hammer, retrieve dwarf remains from a tower, then find the goblin Lollk hiding in a crate deep in a cave south-east of the Fishing Guild. Reassemble a broken multicannon in the correct tool order (hook on spring, pliers on safety switch, tooth tool on gear), then travel to the Dwarven Black Guard camp to get Nulodion's notes and a cannonball mould before returning to Lawgof to finish.

quests: Troll Stronghold (which itself needs Death Plateau)

Trollheim Teleport spell
Scroll of redirection can retarget a Teleport to house tablet to Trollheim

summary: Trick troll cook Burntmeat with a drugged parrot and a truth potion to get goutweed for Eadgar and Sanfew.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Steal and drug a parrot to trick troll cook Burntmeat into revealing his goutweed recipe, and brew a truth potion to unmask his lie, so Eadgar on Trollheim can obtain goutweed for Sanfew.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Talk to Sanfew on the upper floor of the Herbalist shop in Taverley.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Intermediate

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Steal and drug a parrot (vodka + pineapple chunks) from Ardougne Zoo, hide it near the troll prison rack, and supply grain/logs/tinderbox/raw chicken/pestle-and-mortar plus a dirty robe from Tegid so Eadgar builds a fake man to trade Burntmeat for the storeroom key.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Brew a troll truth potion (troll thistle near Eadgar's cave, dried and ground into an unf ranarr potion at Herblore 31) so Burntmeat can't lie about the goutweed recipe, then dodge patrolling troll guards via safespots to grab the goutweed.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants the Trollheim Teleport spell as an alternative route to Trollheim, alongside the Agility 44/47 Burthorpe shortcut routes.

notes: Intermediate. Sanfew sends you to Eadgar atop Trollheim for goutweed, but troll cook Burntmeat won't hand it over without a 'human' to cook. Eadgar's trick: steal and drug a parrot (vodka+pineapple chunks) from Ardougne Zoo, hide it near the troll prison rack, then supply grain/logs/tinderbox/raw chicken/pestle-and-mortar plus a dirty robe from Tegid (Taverley lake) so Eadgar builds a fake man; separately brew a troll truth potion (find a troll thistle near Eadgar's cave, dry+grind it, mix into an unf ranarr potion at Herblore 31) so Burntmeat can't lie about the recipe. Trade the fake man to Burntmeat for the storeroom key, dodge patrolling troll guards via safespots to grab one Goutweed (grab 2 extra if planning Dream Mentor/Dragon Slayer II), hand it to Sanfew. Resolves the Trollheim-teleport branch of unlock-gwd (steer item 133, boots-vs-tele choice) by granting the Trollheim Teleport spell as an alternative to the Agility 44/47 Burthorpe-route shortcuts.

rewards.xp: 2,500 Hunter; 2 QP

start.location: Ardougne Zoo, south-west near the scorpion cage (2606,3264)

summary: Solve a 3-feather puzzle and disguise as an eagle to rescue huntsman Nickolaus near Eagles' Peak.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Help zookeeper Charlie find missing huntsman Nickolaus near Eagles' Peak, solving a 3-feather puzzle and disguising as an eagle to rescue him and learn to trap ferrets.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Talk to Charlie at Ardougne Zoo, south-west near the scorpion cage.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Novice

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Collect three feathers to open the eagle door to Nickolaus: bronze (defeat a net trap via four corner winches), silver (Hunter 27 to read a pedestal trail to a hidden tunnel, then deal with a level 13 kebbit), and gold (solve a bird-seed/lever puzzle moving eagle statues and wing-gates).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Craft two eagle costumes (10 eagle feathers + swamp tar + yellow dye + 50 coins at Varrock's Fancy Dress Shop) to disguise as eagles and pass the guard eagle.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Unlocks the Eagle transport system and is required for At First Light and the Medium Desert/Fremennik/Western Provinces Diaries, plus a partial Medium Kourend & Kebos Diary task.

notes: Novice Hunter-skill quest, no series. Charlie the zookeeper needs help finding huntsman Nickolaus, missing near Eagles' Peak west of the Tree Gnome Stronghold. Find his tent, pick up a bird book that drops a metal feather, and use it to open the mountain-top door into Eagles' Peak Dungeon. Shout to Nickolaus, trapped by a giant eagle, then craft two eagle costumes (10 eagle feathers + swamp tar + yellow dye + 50 coins at Varrock's Fancy Dress Shop) so you can both disguise as eagles. To open the eagle door to Nickolaus you first need three feathers: bronze (defeat a net trap via four corner winches), silver (Hunter 27 to read a pedestal trail leading to a hidden tunnel, then kill or threaten a level 13 kebbit), and gold (solve a bird-seed/lever puzzle moving eagle statues and wing-gates through a specific lever/feeder sequence, reset lever available if you mess it up). Use all three feathers on the eagle door, wear the disguise to pass the guard eagle, then learn to catch ferrets from Nickolaus (no box trap needed for this part) and deliver a ferret to Charlie at Ardougne Zoo to finish.

start.npc: none (search the bookcase in the house south-west of Seers' Village bank)
start.location: house south-west of Seers' Village bank (2716,3481)

summary: Find the hidden underground Elemental Workshop, mine/slay an earth elemental for ore, and smith an elemental shield.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: First Elemental Workshop-series quest: find a hidden underground workshop, mine and slay an earth elemental for ore, and smith an elemental shield.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Search the bookcase in the house south-west of Seers' Village bank (no NPC).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Very Short

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Turn the east and west water controls green and pull the lever to start the water wheel, fix the bellows with leather+needle (Crafting 20), then mine an elemental rock (Mining 20) to spawn and kill a level 35 earth elemental for elemental ore.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Fill a stone bowl with lava for the furnace, smelt the ore with 4 coal per bar (Smithing 20), and smith an elemental shield at the workbench with the slashed book in inventory.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Elemental Workshop II, and grants partial completion of an easy Kandarin Diary task.

notes: First Elemental Workshop-series quest. Search a bookcase south-west of the Seers' Village bank for a battered book and read it to start. Slash it with a knife to get a battered key, then use the key on an odd-looking wall in the northern anvil-icon building to enter the underground Elemental Workshop. Turn the east then west water controls green and pull the lever to start the water wheel; fix the bellows with leather+needle (Crafting 20) and pull its lever. Mine an elemental rock (Mining 20) to spawn and kill an earth elemental (level 35) for elemental ore (kill extras now to save time for Elemental Workshop II / a Mind shield later). Fill a stone bowl with lava and use it on the furnace, then smelt the ore with 4 coal into elemental metal (Smithing 20) and smith an elemental shield at the workbench with the slashed book in inventory.

start.npc: none (search the bookcases in the Exam Centre's library)
start.location: Exam Centre library, south of the Digsite (3366,3335)

summary: Retrieve schematics inside the Elemental Workshop and run elemental metal bars through a crane/press/fan sequence.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Second Elemental Workshop-series quest: retrieve schematics inside the Elemental Workshop, run elemental metal bars through a crane/press/tank/fan sequence, and craft a mind helmet or mind shield.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Search the bookcases in the Exam Centre's library, south of the Digsite (no NPC).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Collect crane and lever schematics, craft a crane claw from a metal bar, connect a junction box's pipes per the schematic, and find 3 randomly-placed cogs plus a pipe piece to fix a broken pipe and set cog sizes on the wind tunnel pins (small upper-left, medium lower-left, large right).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Run each elemental metal bar through the crane-lava-press-water tank-fan sequence to produce a primed bar (twice for a Mind shield), then drain a bar into a primed mind bar at the extractor gun (temporarily lowers Magic by 20 per bar powered) before smithing the mind helmet, or mind shield if the slashed book from Elemental Workshop I is also carried.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Medium Kandarin Diary.

notes: Second Elemental Workshop-series quest. Find a beaten book with a scroll in the Exam Centre library south of the Digsite, read both, then head to the Elemental Workshop (same entry as EW1). Search machinery in the water elemental room for a hatch key (must have read the scroll first), and smelt 2-3 elemental ore into elemental metal bars beforehand. Downstairs, collect crane and lever schematics, craft a crane claw from one bar, connect a junction box's pipes per the lever schematic's clue, and find 3 randomly-placed cogs + a pipe piece in crates to fix a broken pipe and set cog sizes on the wind tunnel pins (small upper-left, medium lower-left, large right). Run each elemental metal bar through the crane-lava-press-water tank-fan sequence via a cart-advancing lever to produce a primed bar (do this twice for a Mind shield). One level further down, drain a bar into a primed mind bar at the extractor gun/hat (temporarily lowers Magic by 20 per bar powered). With the beaten book in inventory, smith the primed bar into a mind helmet to finish; a second powered bar plus the slashed book from Elemental Workshop I in inventory instead makes a mind shield.

start.location: North-east of the Desert Quarry, south of Bandit Camp (3191,2926)

summary: Build a statue to enter Lazim's buried temple, solve elemental corner puzzles, and earn the Camulet.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Build a statue to enter Lazim's buried temple, then solve puzzles across three floors -- sigil collection, four elemental-themed corner puzzles, and a boneguard/wall-patch finale -- to earn the Camulet.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Talk to Lazim north-east of the Desert Quarry, south of Bandit Camp.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Firemaking 45 is listed in reqs.skills but the row's own notes state it is not actually needed in practice (only used to identify brazier fuel by inspection; trial-and-error works without it).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Bottom floor: chisel a fallen statue for 4 limbs, collect M/R/K/Z sigils by walking outer hallway rooms (limbs auto-consumed per door), then place all 4 sigils in the centre room.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Middle floor: mould soft clay + carved 5kg granite into a new pedestal head, then solve 4 corner puzzles (feed food to Pentyn, cast a wind spell on the furnace, light 6 braziers with correct fuel per item, cast a fire spell on the fountain) to pass the magic barrier.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Top floor: cast Crumble Undead on a boneguard, Protect-from-Melee-tank while talking to a second boneguard, then patch a wall with 3 chiselled sandstone pieces to finish.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Needs enough Prayer points for roughly 5 seconds of Protect from Melee to survive a boneguard conversation on the top floor.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Standard spellbook must be active -- it is needed to cast the Fire/Wind spells and Crumble Undead used in the puzzles.

notes: Firemaking 45 is listed as a requirement but is not actually needed in practice (only used to identify brazier fuel by inspection; trial-and-error works without it). Build a statue for Lazim from 32kg sandstone base + 20kg sandstone body (chiseled down from bigger pieces), pick a head design, carve it from a 5kg granite piece, hand it over and fall into a buried temple. Bottom floor: chisel a fallen statue for 4 limbs, collect M/R/K/Z sigils by walking the outer hallway rooms (limbs auto-consumed to open each door), place all 4 sigils in the center room. Middle floor: mold soft clay + carved 5kg granite into a new stone head for the pedestal (triggers cutscene), then solve 4 corner puzzles - feed food to Pentyn (NW), cast a wind spell on the furnace (NE), light 6 braziers with correct fuel per item (SE), cast a fire spell on the fountain (SW) - to pass the magic barrier. Top floor: cast Crumble Undead on a boneguard, then Protect-from-Melee-tank while talking to a second boneguard, patch a wall with 3 sandstone pieces (chisel each into place) to finish. Region-opener quest, no known deeper dependency; unlocks quick desert-temple teleport infrastructure via Camulet.

summary: Two-trip Entrana quest to build and fly a hot air balloon for Auguste, unlocking the balloon transport network.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Two-trip Entrana quest to help balloon-maker Auguste build and fly a hot air balloon, unlocking the balloon transport network.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Talk to Auguste on Entrana.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Explicitly a hard prerequisite for Monkey Madness II's Gnome Stronghold route.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Trip 1 gathers papyrus/candle/dye/silk/sand-filled sacks around Entrana for the balloon frame; trip 2 (after leaving to gather logs and willow branches, since axes can't be brought to Entrana) attaches 12 willow branches and 10 regular logs.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Piloting is a 3-screen minigame of dropping sandbags, burning logs, pulling coloured ropes, and holding a set number of 'relax' prompts to control altitude/direction; crashing costs 10 more logs and a restart from Auguste.

notes: Two-trip Entrana quest that unlocks the balloon transport network -- explicitly a hard prerequisite for Monkey Madness II's Gnome Stronghold route. Trip 1: gather papyrus/candle/dye/silk/sand-filled sacks around Entrana to help Auguste build a balloon frame. Trip 2 (after leaving to gather logs/willow branches, since axes can't be brought to Entrana): attach 12 willow branches and 10 regular logs to the balloon. Piloting is a 3-screen minigame of dropping sandbags, burning logs, pulling coloured ropes, and "relaxing" a set number of times per prompt to control altitude/direction -- crashing just costs 10 more logs and a restart from Auguste. Optionally stockpile extra noted logs (oak/willow/yew/magic) in log storage to unlock further balloon routes post-quest.

summary: Carry the Mage of Zamorak's scrying orb to 3 of 5 rune essence mine entry points to open the Abyss.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: The Mage of Zamorak hands over a scrying orb; visit 3 of 5 possible rune essence mine entry points so it records readings, then return to open the Abyss as an essence-mining and teleport hub.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Talk to the Mage of Zamorak, who roams level 4-7 Wilderness along the River Lum near Edgeville; the second conversation happens at the Zamorak chaos temple hut in Varrock, south-east of Aubury's.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Very Short

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Any Saradomin or Guthix items must be unequipped (inventory is fine) before talking to the Mage of Zamorak or he refuses to speak.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Feeds into Enter the Abyss's own required-for list: Devious Minds, Temple of the Eye, partial Wanted!, and the Wilderness Easy Diary.

notes: Unequip any Saradomin/Guthix items before talking to the Mage of Zamorak or he refuses to speak (inventory is fine, no need to bank). Core task: he hands over a scrying orb, then you travel to the rune essence mine from 3 of 5 possible start points (Aubury/Varrock, Wizards' Tower basement, East Ardougne, Brimstail's cave, or Wizards' Guild) so the orb records readings, then return to Varrock to finish. This is the gap-filler the alch-fodder/rune-supply chain needs: it opens the Abyss as a fast essence-mining + teleport hub feeding into Enter the Abyss's own required-for list (Devious Minds, Temple of the Eye, partial Wanted!, Wilderness Easy Diary).

notes: Requires only the ability to survive a level 22 skeleton. Explore the spooky Draynor Manor for Veronica's missing fiance Ernest: gather poison and fish food to make poisoned fish food, solve a lever/door puzzle in the basement to fetch an oil can, dig up a key from the compost heap outside, retrieve a pressure gauge from a poisoned fountain, and grab a rubber tube from a skeleton-guarded room, then deliver all the parts to Professor Oddenstein to turn Ernest back from a chicken.

name: Ethically Acquired Antiquities

unlocks: 5,000 coins

summary: Investigate a Fortis museum theft, following witnesses to Varrock Museum to expose curator Haig Halen.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Investigate a theft from the Fortis Grand Museum, following a trail of witnesses and evidence to Varrock Museum's storeroom to expose curator Haig Halen.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Inspect the empty display case in the Grand Museum, Civitas illa Fortis.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Follow a witness chain (Regulus Cento, a Trader Crewmember after a sail-repair favour for Artima, Trader Stan's crew, Betty at the Port Sarim rune shop) to Varrock Museum, pickpocket curator Haig Halen for the storeroom key, search the NE storeroom crates, then confront him with the correct dialogue picks.

notes: Investigate a theft from the Fortis Grand Museum. Inspect the empty display case, talk to Curator Herminius, then question museum visitors until one mentions a suspicious man. Follow the trail: Regulus Cento by the quetzals, then a Trader Crewmember (do Artima a sail-repair favour first to get his help), then Trader Stan/crew at Port Sarim, then Betty at the Port Sarim rune shop for a note. Head to Varrock Museum, ask Curator Haig Halen about Xerna's Diadem, pickpocket him for the storeroom key, search the crates in the NE storeroom, then confront and shame Haig Halen with the correct dialogue picks (the in-depth guide lists the exact options). Return to Herminius at the Fortis museum to close the case.

facts.value: Medium

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Final fight is a level 111 Tanglefoot in Zanaris, beatable with limited weaponry using Protect from Melee or by flinching around mushrooms; must be defeated with the Magic secateurs equipped.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Nature Spirit's own Priest in Peril / Restless Ghost requirements are inherited/flattened into this quest's own reqs.quests rather than listed as a separate Nature Spirit sub-chain.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Confirm membership with 5 of the Group of Advanced Gardeners at farming patches (any except Ayesha in Al Kharid or the Kourend ones), then give Malignius Mortifer a Draynor skull (dug from a Draynor Manor grave) for a list of 3 randomly-assigned items to collect, bringing a filled druid pouch to protect perishables from ghasts.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Do not pickpocket Martin the Master Gardener when first talking to him, or he locks you out for 10 game-minutes.

notes: Must be able to defeat a level 111 Tanglefoot with limited weaponry (may be flinched). Nature Spirit's own Priest in Peril/Restless Ghost requirements are inherited/flattened. Talk to Martin the Master Gardener (do not pickpocket him or you're locked out for 10 game-minutes), then speak to 5 of the Group of Advanced Gardeners at farming patches (any except Ayesha in Al Kharid or the Kourend ones) confirming membership, then report back to Martin. Travel to Zanaris (dramen/lunar staff), talk to the Fairy Godfather, then Fairy Nuff for a symptoms list, then Zandar Horfyre atop the Dark Wizards' Tower. Give Malignius Mortifer (NW of Port Sarim rune shop) a Draynor skull (dug from a grave at Draynor Manor) to get a list of 3 randomly-assigned items to collect (bring a filled druid pouch to protect perishables from ghasts), then take them plus a ghostspeak amulet to the Nature Spirit in the Nature Grotto (Mort Myre Swamp) for Magic secateurs. Return to Zanaris, squeeze through a gap near the Cosmic Altar, fight and kill the level 111 Tanglefoot (Protect from Melee or flinch around mushrooms) with Magic secateurs equipped, take the Queen's secateurs, and talk to the Fairy Godfather to finish.

name: Fairytale II - Cure a Queen

skills: Thieving 40 (boostable: no, needed to pickpocket the Fairy Godfather, not needed just to unlock fairy rings)

rewards.xp: 3,500 Herblore, 2,500 Thieving; 2 QP
unlocks: Antique lamp: 2,500xp in any skill over level 30
Access to the fairy ring network (usable partway through the quest, before full completion)
Access to Fairy Fixit's Fairy Enchantment shop
required for Hopespear's Will miniquest
6 Medium diary tasks needing fairy ring travel (Kandarin ALS; Ardougne AIR/BIS; Fremennik CIP; Lumbridge & Draynor DIS; Kourend & Kebos CIR)

summary: Decode Fairy Nuff's certificate and brew a magic essence potion from a star flower and gorak claw powder.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Decode Fairy Nuff's ransacked-grotto certificate, learn the fairy ring network, then brew a magic essence potion from a star flower and gorak claw powder to revive the poisoned Fairy Queen.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Talk to Martin the Master Gardener at the pig pen in Draynor Village/Market.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Decipher Nuff's certificate via the Cosmic Altar's rune temple sign in Zanaris, then use a 4-code fairy ring sequence (AIR, DLR, DJQ, AJS) with the certificate in inventory to reach the Fairy Queen's hideout.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Pickpocket the Fairy Godfather (Thieving 40, no boost) for the Queen's secateurs, then brew a magic essence potion from a star flower (grown on the CKP cosmic plane, Farming 49) and gorak claw powder (from level 145 Goraks on the DIR gorak plane, Herblore 57 to combine).

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Access to the fairy ring network opens partway through the quest, before full completion; required for the Hopespear's Will miniquest and 6 Medium diary tasks needing fairy ring travel (Kandarin ALS; Ardougne AIR/BIS; Fremennik CIP; Lumbridge & Draynor DIS; Kourend & Kebos CIR).

notes: Second Fairy Tale-series quest. Wait for Martin's crops to fail again (~5.5 min logged-in time), then investigate in Zanaris: find Fairy Nuff's ransacked grotto and pick up Nuff's certificate, decipher its fairy-symbol code by reading the Cosmic Altar's rune temple sign in Zanaris. Get permission from the Fairy Godfather to use fairy rings and learn the network from the Co-ordinator/Fairy Fixit. Use a 4-code fairy ring sequence (AIR, DLR, DJQ, AJS) with Nuff's certificate in inventory to reach the Fairy Queen's hideout, where Fairy Nuff explains the dying queen needs her stolen magic secateurs back. Pickpocket the Fairy Godfather (Thieving 40, no boost) for the Queen's secateurs, deliver them to Fairy Nuff, then brew a magic essence potion from a star flower (grown on the CKP cosmic plane, Farming 49) and gorak claw powder (killed from level 145 Goraks on the DIR gorak plane, Herblore 57 to combine). Use the potion on the Fairy Queen to revive her; she ultimately decides to let the Godfather keep the throne for now given his numerical advantage.

name: Family Crest

After the Family Pest miniquest: buy unlimited copies of all 3 gauntlets for a one-off 500,000gp
Chronozon fightable in the Nightmare Zone

summary: Reforge the family crest via Dimintheis's 3 sons, then defeat level 170 Chronozon to finish.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Track down Dimintheis's 3 estranged sons to reforge the family crest, each rewarding a set of steel gauntlets, then defeat level 170 Chronozon to complete the crest.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Talk to Dimintheis in south-eastern Varrock.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Experienced

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Chronozon is a level 170 demon fought in the Wilderness section of Edgeville Dungeon, safespottable, defeated by landing all 4 elemental Blast spells before finishing him off.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Caleb (Catherby) wants 5 cooked fish types for the cooking gauntlets; Avan (near Al Kharid's Gem Trader) needs 2 'perfect' gold ore mined from the hellhound room in Witchaven Dungeon via a lever-sequence puzzle, then smithed/crafted into a ruby ring and necklace, for the goldsmith gauntlets; Johnathon (Jolly Boar Inn) needs a poison cure for the crest piece's location.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Family Pest, Defender of Varrock, Legends' Quest, Recipe for Disaster (Freeing Sir Amik Varze), and the Elite Kandarin Diary.

notes: Experienced quest requiring defeat of level 170 Chronozon (safespottable, fought in the Wilderness). Talk to Dimintheis about his family's lost crest, then track down his 3 sons: Caleb in Catherby (feed him 5 cooked fish types for the cooking gauntlets); Avan at the scorpion mining pit near Al Kharid's Gem Trader (mine 2 'perfect' gold ore from the hellhound room in Witchaven Dungeon via a lever-sequence puzzle, smelt and craft a ruby ring and necklace, hand them over for the goldsmith gauntlets); Johnathon at the Jolly Boar Inn near Varrock (give him a poison cure) for the last crest piece's location, then fight Chronozon (Edgeville Dungeon's Wilderness section) by landing all 4 elemental Blast spells on him before finishing him off any way. Assemble the crest and return it to Dimintheis to finish. Required for: Family Pest, Defender of Varrock, Legends' Quest, Recipe for Disaster (Freeing Sir Amik Varze), Elite Kandarin Diary.

name: Family Pest

unlocks: Ability to own the steel gauntlets AND all three of its upgraded forms (chaos gauntlets, cooking gauntlets, goldsmith gauntlets) simultaneously in unlimited amounts -- get replacements free from Dimintheis or any of his sons at any time

summary: Pay Dimintheis 500,000 coins after his 3 sons decline a favour, unlocking unlimited free gauntlet replacements.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Visit Dimintheis's three sons, who each turn down a favour, then pay Dimintheis 500,000 coins so he'll hand out unlimited free replacement gauntlets.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Talk to Dimintheis in the south-east Varrock closed-off area.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The row's own notes call this a 'pure dialogue miniquest' rather than a quest; card_kind is recorded as quest here only because the enum has no separate miniquest value -- treat as a miniquest downstream.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Visit Avan (Al Kharid mine), Caleb (Catherby), and Johnathon (Jolly Boar Inn) in any order, each of whom declines to help with a comic non-problem, before returning to Dimintheis for the real ask.

notes: Pure dialogue miniquest, no combat/skilling: visit Dimintheis's three sons in any order (Avan at Al Kharid mine, Caleb in Catherby, Johnathon at the Jolly Boar Inn) who each turn down help with a comic non-problem, then return to Dimintheis, who reveals the family isn't actually rich and asks for 500,000gp instead. Hand over the coins to finish. The real payoff is the gauntlet-duplication unlock, since normally only one gauntlet type can be held/enchanted at a time.

name: Fight Arena

rewards.xp: 12,175 Attack, 2,175 Thieving; 2 QP
unlocks: 1,000 coins
Khazard armour set
Bouncer accessible in Nightmare Zone

start.location: west of Ardougne Monastery, north-east corner of Gnome Maze (2566,3199)

summary: Infiltrate General Khazard's prison in Khazard armour and fight through a scripted arena sequence to free Lady Servil's family.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Free Lady Servil's kidnapped family from General Khazard's arena by infiltrating the prison in Khazard armour and fighting through a scripted enemy sequence.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Talk to Lady Servil, west of Ardougne Monastery at the north-east corner of the Gnome Maze.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Take Khazard armour from a chest south of Ardougne Monastery to enter the prison, buy Khali brew from the Khazard barman to get the Head Guard drunk, then steal his cell keys to free Sammy Servil.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Scripted arena fight sequence: level 63 Khazard ogre, level 44 Khazard scorpion, then level 137 Bouncer (hellhound) -- all safespottable behind a skeleton, or beatable with Protect from Melee. The optional level 142 General Khazard fight can be skipped via the door's quick-escape.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for The General's Shadow and While Guthix Sleeps.

notes: Third quest in the Mahjarrat series. Lady Servil's family has been kidnapped by General Khazard to fight in his arena. Get Khazard armour from a chest in a house south of Ardougne Monastery and wear it to be let into the prison. Free Sammy Servil after buying Khali brew (5 coins) from the Khazard barman to get the Head Guard drunk and steal his cell keys. Fight through a scripted sequence in the arena: level 63 Khazard ogre, level 44 Khazard scorpion, level 137 Bouncer (hellhound) -- all safespottable behind a skeleton, or use Protect from Melee. The optional level 142 General Khazard fight can be skipped via the door's quick-escape. Return to Lady Servil to finish.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Novice quest unlocking a shortcut through White Wolf Mountain by winning a fishing contest for two dwarves.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to either dwarf (Austri or Vestri) guarding the mountain's tunnel entrances; starting from the west side is faster on foot.

facts.label: required-for

notes: Novice quest gating a shortcut through White Wolf Mountain. Talk to either dwarf guarding the mountain's tunnel entrances (starting on the west side is faster on foot). Grab garlic from a house south-west of the Seers' Village bank if needed, take a spade to McGrubor's Wood and dig up 3 red vine worms from the vine just west of fairy ring ALS. Head to Hemenster, buy a fishing rod from Grandpa Jack if you don't have one, enter with the fishing pass, put garlic in the north pipes (finish the dialogue), pay Bonzo 5 coins and start fishing once the Sinister Stranger clears the pipes. After winning the contest, bring the trophy back to the dwarf to finish. Required for: Recipe for Disaster (Freeing the Mountain Dwarf), Forgettable Tale..., Between a Rock..., Land of the Goblins.

summary: Investigate the Red Axe by befriending a drunk dwarf, brewing kelda ale, and exploring a minecart tunnel with puzzles.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Part 2 of the Rise of the Red Axe series; investigate the Red Axe organisation through an extremely drunk, kebab-obsessed dwarf informant.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Commander Veldaban at the Dwarven Black Guard headquarters in Keldagrim.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Requires growing kelda seeds in the Kelda Hops Patch (15-20 minutes) then brewing them into kelda ale at the East Keldagrim pub vat (another 15-20 minutes).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Involves exploring a closed-off minecart tunnel through 6 rooms, including 3 sliding-block cart-track puzzles and a room requiring 3 Red Axe documents to be read.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: A dwarven stout must be delivered to Khorvak as part of the investigation.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants 2 Dwarven stout (m).

notes: Part 2 of the Rise of the Red Axe series (after The Giant Dwarf). Investigate the Red Axe via an extremely drunk, kebab-obsessed dwarf: ply him with beer, fetch a random item requested by a rowdy dwarf, deliver a dwarven stout to Khorvak, then grow kelda seeds in the Kelda Hops Patch (15-20 min real-time wait) and brew them into kelda ale at the East Keldagrim pub vat (another 15-20 min wait) -- plan filler activities like High Alch around the waits. Explore a closed-off minecart tunnel through 6 rooms, including 3 sliding-block cart-track puzzles and a room requiring you to read 3 Red Axe documents. Finish by drinking a beer and eating a kebab back at the eastern pub after reporting to Veldaban.

length: Long (must wait for several crops to grow)
summary: Trade grown crops with 5 NPCs for rare seeds, move two garden statues, then present the finished garden to Ellamaria and King Roald.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Long farming-focused quest to earn seed upgrades from 5 NPCs and beautify the garden east of Varrock Palace for Ellamaria.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Ellamaria, then pass a quiz from the Wise Old Man in Draynor while wearing the ring of charos.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Grow seeds for 5 NPCs to trade up to rarer seeds: onions for Lyra (orchid seeds), cabbages for Kragen (snowdrop seeds), marigolds for Elstan (delphinium seeds), a White Tree cutting for Dantaera, and a rune-dust plant cure for Brother Althric and Bernald (rose and vine seeds).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Requires moving two statues by trolley: the Lumbridge king statue east across the bridge, and the Falador Saradomin statue north through a gated cutscene.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: The ring of charos(a) must be thrown down the Edgeville monastery well then retrieved with a fishing rod for Brother Althric's task.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants an activated ring of charos(a) for cheaper NPC services and extra dialogue, plus an apple tree seed, acorn, 5 guam seeds, and a 4-dose compost potion.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: The white tree grown in the player's personal garden yields fruit that restores about 8% run energy and regrows over time.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Long because most steps are gated by farming grow-timers of roughly 10-15 minutes once seeds are finally planted.

notes: Long quest, mostly farming-timer-gated. Talk to Ellamaria then the Wise Old Man in Draynor (wearing ring of charos) to pass a quiz. Grow seeds for 5 NPCs to trade up to rarer seeds: Lyra (west of Port Phasmatys, fairy ring AJQ) grows onions for orchid seeds; Kragen (north Ardougne, fairy ring BJR) grows cabbages for snowdrop seeds; Elstan (south Falador) grows marigolds for delphinium seeds; Dantaera (Catherby) needs a cutting from the White Tree on Ice Mountain grown in a filled plant pot; Brother Althric (Edgeville monastery) needs the ring of charos(a) thrown down the well then retrieved with a fishing rod, plus rune dust used on a plant cure for diseased vines, for rose seeds; Bernald (behind Burthorpe pub) similarly needs a plant-cure/rune-dust vine cure via Alain at the Taverley tree patch, for vine seeds. Then move two statues via trolley (Lumbridge king statue east across the bridge to the garden's east plinth; Falador Saradomin statue north through the gate, cutscene, to the south plinth). Finally plant everything with compost/supercompost in the garden (10-15 min grow, cannot die), talk to Ellamaria, then King Roald (ring of charos(a) must be equipped or he won't follow) for the finishing cutscene.

Feeds into Ratcatchers and other cat-line quests.

notes: Kourend/Kebos Lowlands quest. Speak to Gordon and Mary about their missing livestock, use a pot of flour on the southern gate to lay a scent trail south past the bridge and west into the Kebos Lowlands mine cave, kill the level-82 Headless Beast there. Craft a fake head to fool them: soft clay knifed into a clay head, fur+needle/thread into a fur head, red dye for a bloody head, then mount it on the wall with two planks and six nails. Report back to Gordon to finish.

summary: Dye goblin mail to help two goblin generals settle on a new armour colour.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: No-requirement, no-combat quest to help two goblin generals settle a dispute over a new armour colour.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Collect 3 sets of goblin mail from crates around Goblin Village or goblin drops, dye one orange (from red+yellow dye) and one blue (from woad leaves via Wyson the gardener and Aggie in Draynor), leaving the third plain.

facts.label: required-for

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants a gold bar.

notes: No skill/quest requirements, no combat. Goblins are debating a new armour colour. Collect 3 sets of goblin mail from crates around Goblin Village (or off goblin drops), make blue dye (woad leaves from Wyson the gardener + Aggie in Draynor) and orange dye (combine red+yellow dye from Aggie), dye one mail orange and one blue, leave the third plain. Show the generals orange (rejected), then blue (rejected) -- they settle on the original plain brown. This is the real gate on the RFD 'Freeing the Goblin generals' subquest.

facts.value: Glod quakes to drain Prayer and heals himself during the fight.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants a Dwarven helmet (equip in the Dwarven Mines for a Hard Falador Diary task) and makes Glod accessible in Nightmare Zone; required for the Hard tier of the Falador Diary.

notes: Master quest, no series, heavy fairy-tale references. Collector Sylas wants two rare items for his magic beans: a feather from the griffin Grimgnash on White Wolf Mountain (tell him an escalating violent bedtime story via specific dialogue options or take 20% current HP damage) and a helmet from dwarf Rupert, imprisoned in a tower between Goblin Village and Ice Mountain. Climb the tower's crumbling east wall (Thieving 58) to reach Rupert, then climb his lowered beard (Agility 59) to talk to him; Princess Miazrqa will free him only if you retrieve her pendant, stolen by a mouse and hidden in the Witch's House basement behind an electric-shocking gate (leather gloves avoid 17 damage) and a piano puzzle (play notes EFEDCAEGA). Shrink with a herblore-brewed potion (Herblore 52) to enter the mouse hole, navigate nail-wall ladders past level 95 mice to the pendant, then trade it to Miazrqa for Rupert's helmet. Give both items to Sylas for magic beans, plant them near Taverley's tree patch, then climb the fast-growing beanstalk (Agility 59, no followers allowed) to fight Glod, a level 118-to-138 cloud giant weak to Magic who quakes to drain Prayer and heals himself. Loot includes a golden goblin and gems; use the spare shrinking potion on the beanstalk then chop it down (Woodcutting 71) and return to Sylas for the reward.

name: Haunted Mine

unlocks: ability to make the Salve amulet (undead combat bonus)
shortcut to the crystal outcrops
access to Tarn's Lair
Treus Dayth accessible in Nightmare Zone
required for Lair of Tarn Razorlor + Morytania medium/hard diary + Ardougne elite diary

summary: Retrieve a glowing fungus from the Abandoned Mine and defeat Treus Dayth to access Tarn's Lair.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Short quest to descend through the Abandoned Mine in south-western Morytania and defeat Treus Dayth.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to the Zealot in the Abandoned Mine.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Pickpocket the Zealot's key, solve a lever/points puzzle to route a minecart carrying a light-giving glowing fungus, then open a water valve to access a lift.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Fight Treus Dayth, level 95 but notably harder than the level suggests.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: The fight against Treus Dayth is not a safe death fight, since the glowing fungus is lost on death; ranged or magic with Protect from Missiles is effective.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants the ability to make the Salve amulet, a shortcut to the crystal outcrops, and access to Tarn's Lair; makes Treus Dayth accessible in Nightmare Zone.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Lair of Tarn Razorlor, the Morytania medium/hard Achievement Diary, and the Ardougne elite Achievement Diary.

notes: Pickpocket the Zealot's key, descend through the Abandoned Mine's cave levels, solve a lever/points puzzle to route a minecart carrying a light-giving glowing fungus, retrieve the fungus, open a water valve to access a lift, then fight Treus Dayth (level 95 but notably harder than the level suggests -- ranged/magic + Protect from Missiles recommended, not a safe death fight since the fungus is lost on death) before cutting a crystal outcrop to finish.

Carnillean armour if siding with Ceril (lower defence than iron platebody)

5 Kudos claimable from Historian Minas

summary: Choose a side in a dispute between Ceril Carnillean and the Mahjarrat Hazeel over stolen family armour.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Second Mahjarrat-series quest, novice tier, with a good/evil branch similar in structure to Temple of Ikov.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Ceril Carnillean in his south-western house in East Ardougne.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Head to the cave south-east of the Ardougne clock tower and talk to Clivet to learn about the Mahjarrat Hazeel, then choose to side with Ceril or Hazeel.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Siding with Ceril requires turning 5 sewer valves in specific directions to reach the hideout raft and killing the level 13 Alomone for Carnillean armour.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Siding with Hazeel requires poisoning Ceril's cooking range (which instead kills the dog Scruffy), then helping Butler Jones steal a restoration scroll from a locked chest in Ceril's house.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants 2,000 coins, plus 5 extra and Carnillean armour if siding with Ceril, or Hazeel's mark cosmetic if siding with Hazeel; also 5 Kudos claimable from Historian Minas.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Secrets of the North; the side chosen also has a minor effect on that quest.

notes: Second Mahjarrat-series quest, novice, with a good/evil branch (same structure as Temple of Ikov). Ceril Carnillean says cultists stole his family armour; head to the cave south-east of the Ardougne clock tower and talk to Clivet to learn about the Mahjarrat Hazeel. Choose a side: siding with Ceril means refusing Clivet, turning 5 sewer valves in specific directions to reach the hideout raft, killing Alomone (level 13) for Carnillean armour, then reporting to Ceril for a fake ending -- search the upstairs cupboard for Hazeel's mark and poison and show Ceril to fire the butler for the real reward. Siding with Hazeel means accepting Clivet's offer, poisoning Ceril's cooking range (accidentally kills the dog Scruffy instead), getting Hazeel's mark from Clivet, turning the same 5 valves, then helping Butler Jones steal a restoration scroll from a locked chest in Ceril's house and giving it to Alomone to begin the resurrection. Choice also has a minor effect on Secrets of the North.

Ice gloves (obtainable in-quest) or smith's gloves(i)

start.location: Entrance to Heroes' Guild, between Taverley and Burthorpe (2904,3511)

summary: Complete 3 cooperative fetch chains with a second-player partner to earn Heroes' Guild access for Achietties.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Medium quest requiring a second player as a cooperative partner to complete 3 independent fetch chains for Achietties at the Heroes' Guild.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Achietties at the entrance to the Heroes' Guild, between Taverley and Burthorpe.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Cannot be soloed; requires a partner from the opposite Shield of Arrav gang for a cooperative candlestick-theft sequence in Brimhaven.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Lava eel chain: mix blamish snail slime with harralander(unf) for blamish oil, oil a fishing rod, then fish and cook a lava eel near the blue dragons in Taverley Dungeon.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Master thieves' armband chain: the Black Arm partner and Phoenix partner each navigate Brimhaven password puzzles to trap Grip, loot 2 candlesticks, and trade one to their gang leader for the armband.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Fire feather chain: kill the level-111 Ice Queen inside White Wolf Mountain for Ice Gloves, then kill the Firebird near the Law Altar on Entrana for its feather.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants Heroes' Guild access, the ability to purchase and wield the dragon battleaxe and dragon mace, and use of the Fountain of Heroes to recharge amulets of glory.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Hard-gates Legends' Quest.

notes: Requires a second player as a partner from the OPPOSITE Shield of Arrav gang (Black Arm vs Phoenix) for a cooperative candlestick-theft sequence in Brimhaven - cannot be soloed. 3 independent fetch chains converge at Achietties: (1) Lava eel - get blamish snail slime from Gerrant in Port Sarim, mix into harralander(unf) for blamish oil, oil your fishing rod, travel to Taverley Dungeon (get dusty key from Velrak after killing the Jailer, or use 70 Agility shortcut, or 60 Sailing route via Charred Dungeon) and fish+cook one lava eel near the blue dragons. (2) Master thieves' armband - Black Arm partner talks Trobert in Brimhaven (password "Four leaved clover"), gets Miscellaneous key from Grip, passes it to Phoenix partner who navigates Brimhaven's food shop/secret wall panel (password "Gherkin" with Straven in Varrock) to lock Grip in a room; Black Arm partner then kills/traps Grip, loots 2 candlesticks from a chest, trades 1 to the Phoenix partner - each turns in a candlestick to their own gang leader (Katrine/Straven) for the armband. (3) Fire feather - get Ice Gloves by killing the level-111 Ice Queen inside White Wolf Mountain (ladder/tunnel maze north side), then travel to Entrana and kill the Firebird near the Law Altar for its feather. Hand in all three (eel, armband, feather) to Achietties. High spine value: hard-gates Legends' Quest and unlocks dragon weapon purchase infrastructure.

facts.value: Short experienced-tier miniquest to retrieve a strange icon guarded by the six Barrows Brothers for the Strange Old Man.

facts.value: Start by talking to the Strange Old Man, who wanders the Barrows east of Mort'ton.

facts.label: boss

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Loot the icon from the Barrows reward chest after all six brothers are defeated; at least 6 free inventory slots are needed or all six brothers must be redone to retrieve it.

notes: Short experienced-tier miniquest. The Strange Old Man asks you to retrieve a strange icon guarded by the six Barrows Brothers — defeat all six, then loot the icon from the Barrows reward chest (keep at least 6 free inventory slots or you must redo all six brothers to get it), and return it to him to finish. Wiki lists 'Required for completing: Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire' (Twisted Tale/lore cross-reference, not a normal prerequisite chain). Promoted into this batch from a stalled wave3:morytania-prayer-pvm queue entry per DEPTH_BACKLOG Band C guidance.

name: Holy Grail

skills: Attack 20 (boostable: no; not a hard quest requirement, needed to wield Excalibur)

rewards.xp: 11,000 Prayer, 15,300 Defence; 2 QP
unlocks: Access to the Fisher Realm
King Arthur picture unlocked for Construction wall decoration
Black Knight Titan accessible in Nightmare Zone
required for King's Ransom

summary: Defeat the Black Knight Titan and find the Fisher King's missing son to retrieve the Holy Grail for King Arthur.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Second Camelot-series quest to find the Holy Grail for King Arthur and help the ailing Fisher King.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to King Arthur in Camelot.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Visit Entrana (weapons and armour must be deposited for the monks), get a holy table napkin from Galahad, and grab two magic whistles from a room in Draynor Manor.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Blow a whistle at the guard tower north of the Brimhaven goldmine to enter the Fisher King's Realm.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Fight the Black Knight Titan, a level 120 monster with +1000 magic and ranged defence that is flinchable; the killing blow must be dealt with Excalibur or he heals to full.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Get a magic gold feather from King Arthur and follow it to Goblin Village to find Percival hiding in sacks in the east house, then give him a magic whistle.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Return to the Fisher Realm, now also reachable via fairy ring code BJR, to find Percival has become king, then take the Holy Grail and return it to King Arthur.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants access to the Fisher Realm, a King Arthur picture for Construction wall decoration, and makes the Black Knight Titan accessible in Nightmare Zone.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for King's Ransom.

notes: Second Camelot-series quest. King Arthur sends the player after the Holy grail; Merlin points to Entrana and Sir Galahad. Visit Entrana (weapons/armour must be deposited for the monks) and meet a mysterious crone who explains a Fisher King needs help. Get a holy table napkin from Galahad west of McGrubor's Woods, then grab two magic whistles from a room in Draynor Manor. Travel to Brimhaven, head to the guard tower north of the goldmine, and blow a whistle to enter the Fisher King's Realm. Kill the Black Knight Titan (level 120, +1000 magic/ranged defence, flinchable) but the killing blow must be dealt with Excalibur or he heals to full. Ring the grail bell to meet the ailing Fisher King, who asks you to find his son Percival. Get a magic gold feather from King Arthur, follow it to Goblin Village, and find Percival hiding in sacks in the east house; give him a magic whistle. Return to the Fisher Realm (fairy ring code BJR works now) where Percival has become king, then take the Holy grail from upstairs and return it to King Arthur.

items: Ring of visibility or ring of shadows

req_items.name: Food and potions
req_items.note: needed to defeat 5 enemies while unequipped (no armour, weapons, or magic)

unlocks: Prayer xp only (1,250/2,500/5,000/10,000/20,000 per priest bone, cannot be pyre-boosted or Sinister-Offering-converted)

start.location: crypt of the Goblin Temple, reached via Goblin Cave east of the Fishing Guild

summary: Rekill 5 unequipped goblin high priests and bury their bones at Yu'biusk to free their souls for Hopespear.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Short sequel quest to Land of the Goblins, freeing 5 goblin high priests' souls for the ghost prophet Hopespear.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Hopespear's ghost in the crypt of the Goblin Temple, reached via Goblin Cave east of the Fishing Guild.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Drink the goblin potion to enter the temple disguised, then equip the ghostspeak amulet and ring of visibility only after entering the crypt, or risk being expelled.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Rekill each of the 5 goblin high priests completely unequipped (no weapons, spells, or armour, though jewellery is fine) and collect their bones before summoning the next, since old bones despawn.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Priests fought: Snothead (level 32), Snailfeet (56), Mosschin (88), Redeyes (121, stat-draining), and Strongbones (184, hardest, summons skoblins, drains stats, and hits up to 18).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Players can restock and bank between fights; the goblin potion must be re-drunk to re-enter, but the ghostspeak amulet does not need to be reapplied.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Finish by using fairy ring code BLQ to reach Yu'biusk and burying the collected bones, which can be done in batches.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Prayer xp is granted per priest bone (1,250/2,500/5,000/10,000/20,000) and cannot be pyre-boosted or converted via the Sinister Offering.

notes: Sequel to Land of the Goblins. Drink goblin potion to enter the temple disguised, THEN equip ghostspeak amulet + ring of visibility inside the crypt (equipping too early gets you expelled). Hopespear the ghost prophet asks you to free 5 goblin high priests' souls by rekilling them completely unequipped (no weapons/spells/armour -- jewellery like ghostspeak/ring is fine, but Morytania legs are not) and collecting each one's bones before summoning the next (old bones despawn otherwise). Priests: Snothead (32, SW), Snailfeet (56, SE), Mosschin (88, NW), Redeyes (121, NE, stat-draining), Strongbones (184, N, hardest -- summons skoblins, drains stats, hits up to 18; recommended 70+ melee/Prayer for Piety or Chivalry). Can restock/bank between fights (goblin potion re-needed to re-enter, ghostspeak not). Finish by fairy-ringing to Yu'biusk (code BLQ) and burying the collected bones (can be done in batches).

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Short quest to fix the lighthouse's light mechanism and rescue the missing keeper Jossik from its basement.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Larrissa outside the Lighthouse, north of Barbarian Outpost.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Fix the light mechanism with swamp tar, tinderbox, and molten glass, then break into the basement past a rune-and-weapon-sacrifice wall puzzle.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Kill a level-100 Dagannoth (safespottable), then the level-100 Dagannoth Mother.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The Dagannoth Mother cycles her damage-type weakness roughly every 18 seconds through all 4 elemental spell schools plus ranged and melee.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants a damaged prayer book of choice (fills into the Holy/Unholy book or Book of Balance), access to the Lighthouse general store and Dagannoth caves, and makes the Dagannoth Mother accessible in Nightmare Zone.

facts.label: required-for

notes: Fix the lighthouse's light mechanism (tar/tinderbox/glass) then break into its basement past a rune-and-weapon-sacrifice wall puzzle to find the missing keeper Jossik. Kill a level-100 Dagannoth (safespottable), then the level-100 Dagannoth Mother who cycles her damage-type weakness every 18s through all 4 elemental spell schools plus ranged/melee -- bring every attack style.

start.location: Camp west of the Agility Pyramid (3315,2849)

summary: Help the Wanderer investigate a haunted pyramid with your cat to earn access to the city of Sophanem.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Medium quest requiring a cat to help the Wanderer investigate a pyramid and open access to Sophanem.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to the Wanderer at the camp west of the Agility Pyramid.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Requires defeating a level-91 Possessed Priest plus one randomly-assigned level 75-81 elemental guardian, determined by which canopic jar the player is given.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Once inside the pyramid there is no teleporting out; it contains aggressive level-84 mummies and poison scarabs, and a pit must be jumped at 20%+ run energy.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Solve a golden-bird door puzzle to trigger the first flashback; dropping the cat is required to answer the Sphinx's riddle or the cat is lost.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The quest runs through 4 flashback sequences: retrieving the correct canopic jar and killing its guardian, preparing embalming materials and a holy symbol, then using the holy symbol on a sarcophagus and defeating the Possessed Priest.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants the Catspeak amulet, access to the city of Sophanem, and magic carpet rides between Pollnivneach, Sophanem, and Menaphos.

notes: Must own a cat and beat a level-91 Possessed Priest plus one randomly-assigned level 75-81 elemental guardian (Apmeken/Crondis/Scabaras magic, or Het melee - determined by which canopic jar you're given). Give the Wanderer a tinderbox+waterskin(4), enter the pyramid (no teleporting out once inside - aggressive lvl-84 mummies + poison scarabs, use minimap-click nav to dodge traps, jump the pit at 20%+ run energy). Solve a golden-bird door puzzle to trigger flashback 1. Drop your cat to answer the Sphinx's riddle (answer: 9) or lose the cat, then see the High Priest. Flashback 2: re-enter pyramid, take the correct canopic jar from the west room, kill the guardian that spawns, take the jar out (crossing the pit twice), then return and drop the jar back before leaving. Prep for the ceremony: give the Embalmer linen+salt+sap, give the Carpenter willow logs for a holy symbol. Flashback 3: use the holy symbol on a sarcophagus in the east room, kill the level-91 Possessed Priest, talk High Priest. Flashback 4: talk High Priest again in the temple to finish. Post-quest: equipping the catspeak amulet and asking your cat about "being confused" unlocks bonus lore cutscenes. High spine value: opens Sophanem/Menaphos region access.

name: In Search of Knowledge

Extra pages found after completion sell to Logosia for 1,000gp each

start.location: South-east end of Forthos Dungeon, outside Sarachnis' lair (1840,9926)

summary: Farm Forthos Dungeon monsters for tattered pages to fill 3 tomes for Brother Aimeri and Logosia.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Medium quest with no hard skill or quest gate, requiring high combat only as a recommendation, to fill 3 tomes with pages found in Forthos Dungeon.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to the injured Brother Aimeri at the south-east end of Forthos Dungeon, outside Sarachnis's lair.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Kill Forthos Dungeon monsters (Sarachnis, red dragon, undead druid, baby red dragon, temple spider) for a drop table of tattered sun/moon/temple pages; 4 of each are needed to fill 3 tomes found in the dungeon's Temple Library.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Hand the filled tomes to Logosia in the Arceuus Library to finish.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants a Lamp of Knowledge for 10,000xp in any skill at least level 40; extra pages found after completion sell to Logosia for 1,000gp each.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: A parenthetical claim of a 'prayer-book unlock' reward for this quest circulating in an earlier queue payload is not substantiated anywhere on the quest's own wiki page (Details/Walkthrough/Reward/Trivia); the only reward is the one-time 10k xp lamp.

notes: No hard skill/quest gate -- high combat is only recommended. Core loop: kill Forthos Dungeon monsters (Sarachnis 1/5, red dragon 1/10, undead druid 1/20, baby red dragon 1/25, temple spider 1/30) for a drop table of tattered sun/moon/temple pages, need 4 of each to fill 3 tomes found in the dungeon's Temple Library, then hand the filled tomes to Logosia in the Arceuus Library. Designed to piggyback on spider/zombie/red-dragon/Sarachnis Slayer tasks as a passive grind. NOTE: the depth:04 queue payload's parenthetical '(prayer-book unlock)' for this quest is NOT substantiated anywhere in the quest's own page (Details/Walkthrough/Reward/Trivia) -- no prayer-book, spellbook or altar unlock is mentioned; the only reward is the one-time 10k xp lamp. Treated as an unverified/likely-wrong brief claim, not repeated as fact here.

name: In Search of the Myreque

unlocks: access to the Hollows + fairy ring dls

required for In Aid of the Myreque

start.location: Hair of the Dog tavern, Canifis

summary: Deliver steel weapons to the Myreque resistance and defeat a Skeleton Hellhound in Canifis.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Short quest, Myreque chain part 1, delivering steel weapons to the resistance hideout in Canifis.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Vanstrom Klause at the Hair of the Dog tavern in Canifis.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Deliver steel weapons from Cyreg Paddlehorn's boat at Mort'ton to the Myreque hideout, then answer a spelling-checked lore quiz to get in.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Safespot-kill a level-97 Skeleton Hellhound before talking your way out.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants access to the Hollows and fairy ring code dls; makes the Skeleton Hellhound spawn in Nightmare Zone.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for In Aid of the Myreque.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Vanstrom Klause, met at the tavern, is later revealed as the villain of the Myreque chain; this is his first appearance.

refs.title: In Search of the Myreque/Quick guide

notes: Myreque chain quest 1: deliver steel weapons from Cyreg Paddlehorn's boat at Mort'ton to the Myreque hideout, answer a lore quiz (spelling-checked) to get in, hand over weapons, then safespot-kill a level 97 Skeleton Hellhound before talking your way out. Vanstrom Klause at the tavern is later revealed as the villain -- first appearance in the chain.

unlocks: required for Shilo Village
required for My Arm's Big Adventure (Tai Bwo Wannai Cleanup work)
required for Tai Bwo Wannai Trio
