start.location: his house, north-east of Tai Bwo Wannai

summary: Gather five jungle herbs around Tai Bwo Wannai and Karamja for Trufitus, showing each herb to him before hunting the next.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Trufitus at his house north-east of Tai Bwo Wannai.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short quest.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Show Trufitus each new herb before searching for the next: snake weed, ardrigal, sito foil, volencia moss, and rogue's purse.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Herb spots: snake weed in marshy vines SW of the village, ardrigal from palm trees NE near Harpie Bug Swarms, sito foil on scorched earth south of the village, volencia moss at rock veins SE, rogue's purse searched from cave walls NW (not a Jogre drop).

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Harpie Bug Swarms (level 46) and Jogres (level 53) are encountered while gathering herbs.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Grants 775 Herblore xp.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Shilo Village, My Arm's Big Adventure's Tai Bwo Wannai Cleanup portion, Tai Bwo Wannai Trio, Zogre Flesh Eaters, and several Karamja Diary tasks.

notes: Gather 5 jungle herbs around Tai Bwo Wannai/Karamja for Trufitus's potion, showing him each new herb before hunting the next: snake weed (marshy vines SW of the village), ardrigal (palm trees NE, near Harpie Bug Swarms), sito foil (scorched earth south of the village), volencia moss (mine rocks SE) and rogue's purse (fungus on cave walls NW, must be searched from the wall, not looted from a Jogre kill).

name: King's Ransom

unlocks: Antique lamp: 5,000 xp to any skill 50+
Knight Waves Training Grounds access
Chivalry prayer (needs Defence 60/70 Prayer via Knight Waves completion)
Piety prayer (same, via Knight Waves)

summary: Clear Anna of murder charges, free Merlin from Camelot's dungeon, and free King Arthur from a statue at Black Knights' Fortress.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Gossip outside Sinclair Mansion.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium-length quest with three linked storylines.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Break into Sinclair Mansion for evidence, then clear Anna of murder at the Seers' Village courthouse by presenting three witness testimonies to the Judge.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Freeing Merlin requires solving a 4-tumbler lock puzzle in his cell (raise each tumbler until it shows green).

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Entering Black Knights' Fortress requires full black armour and an Animate rock scroll (bought from Wizard Cromperty in Ardougne) to animate King Arthur's statue with granite.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Deep prerequisite chain that effectively also requires Merlin's Crystal, Murder Mystery, Rune Mysteries, and the Druidic Ritual line to be completed first.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks the Knight Waves training ground, which leads to the Chivalry and Piety prayers.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Grants 33,000 Defence xp and 5,000 Magic xp.

notes: Deep prerequisite quest chain (effectively requires Merlin's Crystal, Murder Mystery, Rune Mysteries, Druidic Ritual line too). Flow: at Sinclair Mansion, break in via smashed east window, grab scrap paper + address form + black knight helm from bookcase, give all 3 to guard then talk Gossip. Travel to Seers' Village courthouse for Anna's Trial: call Dog Handler (ask about poison), Butler (ask about dagger), Maid (ask about night of murder + thread) as witnesses via the Judge to get her declared not guilty. At Camelot Castle statue: cutscene, then free Merlin from his cell (reach vent, Telekinetic Grab a guard's hair clip or take a knight's lockpick, solve a 4-tumbler lock puzn - raise each tumbler until it shows green, keep raising the rest), climb to top floor and pick the round purple box. At Black Knights' Fortress (west of Edgeville): get an Animate rock scroll from Wizard Cromperty in Ardougne first, wear full black armour to enter through the south sturdy door, push the north wall, climb down, free King Arthur from his statue using granite, give him the bronze med helm + iron chainbody, then report to King Arthur at Camelot's round table to finish. Big spine value: unlocks Knight Waves -> Chivalry/Piety prayers, a broadly-used combat prayer tier.

name: Knight Waves

unlocks: Chivalry prayer (needs 60 Prayer & 65 Defence to use)
Piety prayer (needs 70 Prayer & 70 Defence to use)
Ability to set your death/respawn point to Camelot

start.location: Top floor of Camelot Castle, via the south-western tower ladder (2758,3507); the training ground itself is 'Camelot training grounds'

summary: Fight through 8 increasingly tough knights in a melee-only training ground unlocked by King's Ransom.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to the Squire on the top floor of Camelot Castle, reached via the south-western tower ladder.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Long.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Fight 8 knights (level 110-127) in sequence inside a melee-only room; each hit drains a specific combat stat.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Progress saves on death or teleport, resuming at the knight that killed you; leaving via the door resets progress to wave 1.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: No Prayer, Ranged, or Magic is allowed in the fight room.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Grants 20,000 xp each to Attack, Strength, Defence, and Hitpoints.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks the Chivalry prayer (needs 60 Prayer and 65 Defence) and the Piety prayer (needs 70 Prayer and 70 Defence), plus setting your respawn point to Camelot.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Listed on the Miniquests page's 'one-off content without miniquest designation' table rather than the main miniquest list, and gated by a quest (King's Ransom) rather than being a miniquest itself.

notes: Not a Quest-List miniquest -- it's a members-only Activity/training-ground gated by a quest (King's Ransom), listed on the Miniquests page's 'one-off content without miniquest designation' table (Length=Long there) rather than under 'List of miniquests'. Fight 8 increasingly tough knights (level 110-127) in sequence, Melee-only (no Prayer/Ranged/Magic allowed in the room), each draining a specific combat stat on hit; progress is saved on death/teleport (resume at the knight that killed you) but resets to wave 1 only if you leave via the door. A flinch-behind-the-training-dummy strategy is the commonly cited safe/cheap method; Guthan's set or straightforward gear-and-food both also work at 65+ combat stats. Confirmed safe death for solo Hardcore Ironman (not safe for Hardcore Group Ironman).

name: Lair of Tarn Razorlor

items: 100 coins per use of the lair's banker Odovacar (optional)

req_items.name: coins
req_items.note: per use of the lair's banker Odovacar

unlocks: Tarn's diary
Salve amulet (e) (20% Attack+Strength vs undead; combine the diary with a salve amulet, repeatable)
counts for a Medium Morytania diary task (kill a terror dog here)

start.location: Haunted Mine area, west of Burgh de Rott, south of the Zealot (3424,9661 instance)

summary: Fight through a trap-filled dungeon to reach and defeat Tarn, the sequel dungeon to Haunted Mine.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Enter directly at the Haunted Mine area, west of Burgh de Rott and south of the Zealot.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Navigate floor, wall, and log traps plus receding-wall pits by searching to disarm them (log traps re-arm if you fall) to reach Tarn's chamber.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: A Slayer ring teleport goes straight to Tarn's Lair, skipping the trap gauntlet entirely.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Tarn transforms into a level-69 mutant with two level-110 terror dogs and nearby level-53 animated armour; only Tarn needs to be killed, and he continuously drains prayer.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: After the mutant form dies, Tarn becomes an easier level-69 ghost; poison carries over between his forms.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Grants 5,000 Slayer xp.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks Tarn's diary, which can be combined with a salve amulet to make a salve amulet (e) (20% Attack and Strength bonus vs undead), and counts toward a Medium Morytania Diary task (killing a terror dog here).

notes: Dungeon-crawl sequel to Haunted Mine. Fastest entry is a Slayer ring teleport straight to Tarn's Lair (skips the whole trap gauntlet); otherwise walk in via the Haunted Mine cave and navigate floor/wall/log traps and receding-wall pits (search to disarm; log traps re-arm if you fall) to reach a banker (100gp/use, last restock point) then on to Tarn's chamber. Fight: Tarn transforms into a level-69 mutant with two level-110 terror dogs and nearby level-53 animated armour; Protect from Melee, ignore the dogs/armour (only Tarn needs to die), watch for continuous prayer drain. After the mutant form dies he becomes an easier level-69 ghost (poison carries over between forms). Read Tarn's diary in the next room to finish; enchant salve amulets with it afterward at leisure.

name: Land of the Goblins

items: Black dye (or black mushroom + empty vial + pestle and mortar)

req_items.name: Black dye
req_items.note: or black mushroom + empty vial + pestle and mortar

unlocks: Access to the Goblin Temple prayer-recharge altar
Access to Yu'biusk via fairy ring code BLQ
Ability to buy plain-of-mud spheres (teleport to Goblin Cave)
Ability to make goblin potions

summary: Infiltrate the Goblin Temple disguised as a goblin, steal six coloured keys, and defeat goblin bosses to learn the location of Yu'biusk.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Grubfoot in the Dorgesh-Kaan mine.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Disguise as a goblin with a Makeover Mage transformation and a brewed goblin potion to infiltrate the Goblin Temple, free Zanik from her cell, and pass High Priest Bighead's lore quiz.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Steal 6 coloured keys from priests by repeatedly re-dyeing a goblin mail and pickpocketing; the white key needs a side trip to Hemenster's Fishing Contest area to catch a whitefish for Aggie.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Unlock the crypt with all 6 keys and defeat 4 named goblin-ancestor bosses (Snothead, Snailfeet, Mosschin, Redeyes), then the level-184 Strongbones (skoblins present; use Protect from Magic).

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Finish by returning to Oldak in Dorgesh-Kaan's South Dungeon, dialling combination 9-4-1, and opening the strange box in Yu'biusk.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Grants 8,000 xp each to Agility, Fishing, Thieving, and Herblore.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks the Goblin Temple prayer-recharge altar, fairy ring access to Yu'biusk (code BLQ), purchasable plain-of-mud spheres (teleport to Goblin Cave), and the ability to make goblin potions.

notes: Endgame of the goblin questline (Zanik's destiny to find Yu'biusk). Disguise as a goblin using a Makeover Mage transformation + a goblin potion (toadflax unf + pharmakos berries) to infiltrate the Goblin Temple, free Zanik from a prison cell using a Dorgesh-kaan sphere, then pass High Priest Bighead's goblin-lore quiz. Steal 6 coloured keys (black/white/yellow/purple/orange/blue) from priests by repeatedly re-dyeing a goblin mail and pickpocketing -- requires a side trip to Hemenster's Fishing Contest area to catch a whitefish for Aggie in exchange for the white mail. Unlock the crypt with all 6 keys and defeat 4 named goblin-ancestor bosses (Snothead, Snailfeet, Mosschin, Redeyes) then the level-184 Strongbones (skoblins present, Protect from Magic) to learn Yu'biusk's location. Return to Oldak in Dorgesh-Kaan's South Dungeon, dial combination 9-4-1 on his machine, and open the strange box in Yu'biusk to finish.

name: Learning the Ropes

rewards.xp: N/A (starting gear + tutorial only)
unlocks: Access to the Mainland
Lumbridge Home Teleport spell
Starting gear bundle (bronze axe/pickaxe, tinderbox, small fishing net, bronze dagger/sword, wooden shield, shortbow+25 bronze arrows, runes, bucket, pot, bread, 25 coins)

summary: Complete Tutorial Island's eight instructor stations to reach the mainland; the universal starting quest for every new account.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to the Quest Guide on Tutorial Island.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Very Short.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Walk through 8 instructor buildings in order: Gielinor Guide, Survival Expert, Master Chef, Quest Guide, Mining Instructor, Combat Instructor, Account Guide, Brother Brace, Magic Instructor.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: The Combat Instructor stop includes killing 2 level-3 giant rats.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Character customization happens first; Ironman Mode restrictions (if applicable) are chosen with the Ironman tutor near the end, before boarding the boat to the mainland.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks access to the mainland, the Lumbridge Home Teleport spell, and a starting gear bundle (bronze axe/pickaxe, tinderbox, small fishing net, bronze dagger/sword, wooden shield, shortbow with 25 bronze arrows, runes, bucket, pot, bread, 25 coins).

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: This page IS Tutorial Island itself; no separate Quick guide subpage exists -- both titles resolve to the same page/section map.

notes: This IS Tutorial Island itself -- no separate /Quick guide exists (search resolves to the identical page/section map for both titles). Walk through 8 instructor buildings in order (Gielinor Guide, Survival Expert, Master Chef, Quest Guide, Mining Instructor, Combat Instructor -- kill 2 level-3 giant rats here, Account Guide, Brother Brace, Magic Instructor) doing character customization first. Ends by choosing Ironman Mode restrictions with the Ironman tutor if applicable, then stepping onto the boat to the mainland. Every new account starts here; it is the universal quest-chain root.

name: Legends' Quest

items: 1 of each cut gem up to diamond (sapphire/emerald/ruby/diamond/opal/jade/red topaz)
Soul rune, mind rune, 2 law runes, earth rune

req_items.name: Cut gems up to diamond
req_items.note: one each of sapphire, emerald, ruby, diamond, opal, jade, red topaz

xp.choice: 30,000 xp x4 picks (any of Attack/Defence/Strength/Hitpoints/Prayer/Magic/Woodcutting/Crafting/Smithing/Herblore/Agility/Thieving, repeats allowed)

unlocks: Legends' Guild access + cape of legends shop
Ability to wield dragon square shield
Skills necklace / combat bracelet chargeable at Legends' Guild or Fountain of Rune
Kharazi Jungle access + 79 Agility vine shortcut
Replacement dark daggers from Radimus Erkle
Rune spear / Dragon spear / Shield left half added to Gem drop table
Nezikchened added to Nightmare Zone

summary: Master-tier capstone quest through Kharazi Jungle, ending in defeating the level-187 demon Nezikchened for a choice of skill xp.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to the Legends' Guards just outside the Legends' Guild.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Long.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Master-difficulty; one of the deepest prerequisite chains in the game.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Must be able to defeat the level-187 demon Nezikchened, fought up to 3 times across the quest; he has high magic resistance and drains prayer to 1/10 at the start of the fight.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Map Kharazi Jungle for Radimus, summon Gujuo, then progress through cave puzzles: enchant a golden bowl, place gems by colour on carved rocks, and douse and bind Ungadulu before fighting Nezikchened.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Grow a Yommi tree from seeds, brew a bravery potion, then choose a short 'evil' path (kill Viyeldi) or long 'good' path (cast Holy Force on Zekin) through a second cave, fighting 3 named skeletons for crystal pieces on either path.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Carving and using the Yommi totem triggers up to 4 unavoidable melee hits (up to 72 damage total) while Nezikchened respawns after each of the 3 skeleton fights; pray Protect from Melee.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Reward is a choice of 30,000 xp, 4 times, in any of Attack/Defence/Strength/Hitpoints/Prayer/Magic/Woodcutting/Crafting/Smithing/Herblore/Agility/Thieving (repeats allowed).

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Deep prerequisite chain requiring Family Crest, Heroes' Quest (incl. Shield of Arrav, Lost City, Merlin's Crystal, Dragon Slayer I), Shilo Village line, Jungle Potion/Druidic Ritual line, Underground Pass, Biohazard, Plague City, Waterfall Quest, and 107 quest points.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks Legends' Guild access, the dragon square shield, chargeable skills necklace/combat bracelet, Kharazi Jungle's 79 Agility vine shortcut, and adds Nezikchened to the Nightmare Zone.

notes: Master-difficulty capstone quest, one of the deepest prereq chains in the game (Family Crest, Heroes' Quest incl. Shield of Arrav/Lost City/Merlin's Crystal/Dragon Slayer I, Shilo Village line, Underground Pass line, Waterfall Quest, 107 QP). Must be able to beat a level-187 demon (Nezikchened, fought up to 3x). Flow: talk Radimus Erkle at the guild, travel to Kharazi Jungle south of Shilo Village, chop through and map all 3 jungle sections with Radimus notes, get bullroarer from a Jungle forester, summon Gujuo, crawl into Mossy rock cave (50 Agility). Smith a golden bowl (2 gold bars + hammer), have Gujuo enchant it (42+ Prayer, can fail), fill with sacred water from the pool, squeeze through a bookcase crevice, lockpick a gate, smash 3 boulders, force a 2nd gate (50 Str), place gems on carved rocks by color to open a marked wall (Soul/Mind/Earth/Law/Law rune order) and get the binding book. Douse Ungadulu's fire with the bowl, bind him with the book, fight and kill Nezikchened (prayer drained to 1/10 at fight start; ranged/melee+protect-melee recommended, he has high magic resist). Get Yommi tree seeds from Ungadulu, water them, brew a bravery potion (ardrigal + snake weed + vial water) for a branching "evil" (short, kill Viyeldi for a glowing dagger) or "good" (long, cast Holy Force on Zekin) path through a second cave to restore the water source, fighting 3 named skeletons (lvl 106/100/92) for crystal pieces either way. Grow+carve a Yommi totem from the restored tree, use on an old totem pole, survive 4 unavoidable melee hits (up to 72 dmg total, pray Protect Melee) while Nezikchened respawns after each of 3 skeletons, get gilded totem from Gujuo. Hand gilded totem + notes to Radimus at the guild, pick 4 skills for the 30k xp reward, finish. Very high spine value as a QP/skill-chain gate for later quests + the quest-cape line.

name: Lost City

items: Woodcutting axe (must be un-blessed and banked before Entrana)

req_items.name: Woodcutting axe
req_items.note: must be un-blessed and banked before entering Entrana

unlocks: Access to Zanaris
Ability to buy/wield dragon longswords and dragon daggers (60 Attack)
Ability to make cosmic runes
Permanent Puro-Puro portal in Zanaris' wheat field
Tree spirit fightable in the Nightmare Zone

summary: Kill a level-101 Dramen Tree Spirit on Entrana (no armour allowed) to make a dramen staff and reach Zanaris.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to the warrior camped in north-western Lumbridge Swamp.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Chop (not chop-down) the tree near the warrior to summon Shamus, then bank your axe and all armour/weapons before sailing to Entrana with a Monk of Entrana (jewellery may be kept).

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Fight through 2 Greater Demons in the Entrana cave, then kill the level-101 Dramen Tree Spirit, which is safespottable behind the southern fungus.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Kill zombies in the cave for a bronze axe drop, then chop the Dramen tree and use a knife on a branch to make a dramen staff; cutting extra branches now saves later trips for Freeing Sir Amik Varze and The Fremennik Trials.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Reward xp is not itemized in the held data.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Fairytale I - Growing Pains, Heroes' Quest, Lunar Diplomacy, Freeing Sir Amik Varze (Recipe for Disaster subquest), Lumbridge & Draynor Medium/Hard Diary, and Slayer Master Chaeldar.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks access to Zanaris, the ability to buy/wield dragon longswords and daggers (60 Attack), cosmic rune crafting, a permanent Puro-Puro portal in Zanaris' wheat field, and the Tree spirit as a Nightmare Zone boss.

notes: Intermediate quest requiring the ability to kill a level 101 Tree spirit with limited gear (Entrana bans armour). Talk to the warrior camped in Lumbridge Swamp about Zanaris, chop (not chop-down) the nearby tree to summon Shamus and talk to him. Bank your axe and all armour/weapons before sailing to Entrana with a Monk of Entrana (jewellery is fine to keep). Head to the cave entrance, fight through 2 Greater Demons, kill zombies for a bronze axe drop, then kill the level 101 Dramen Tree Spirit (safespottable behind the southern fungus) and chop the Dramen tree; use a knife on a branch for a dramen staff (cutting a few extra branches now saves trips for Freeing Sir Amik Varze and The Fremennik Trials later). Teleport back, wield the staff, and enter the tool shed in the centre of Lumbridge Swamp to finish. Required for: Fairytale I - Growing Pains, Heroes' Quest, Lunar Diplomacy, Freeing Sir Amik Varze (Recipe for Disaster subquest), Lumbridge & Draynor Medium/Hard Diary, Slayer Master Chaeldar.

name: Lost Lover

unlocks: None (lore-only per the Miniquests page's own "Lore" table)

start.location: Tlati Rainforest, Varlamore (Fabia by the southern magic trees west of Kastori; Xilo by the river bank west of the Crypt of Tonali lift)

summary: Resolve a miscommunication between Fabia and Xilo about where they were meant to meet in the Tlati Rainforest.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to either Fabia or Xilo in the Tlati Rainforest, Varlamore (Fabia by the southern magic trees west of Kastori; Xilo by the river bank west of the Crypt of Tonali lift).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Each NPC describes the same rendezvous tree differently and is confused why the other never showed; talk to whichever one you did NOT speak to first.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Talking to the second NPC makes her leave to join the other, resolving the miscommunication and completing the miniquest.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: No skills, quests, or items are required; reward xp is not stated in the held data.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: No unlock -- lore-only, per the Miniquests page's own table.

notes: Tiny two-NPC errand: speak to either Fabia (thinks she was to meet Xilo by 'the magic tree') or Xilo (thinks she was to meet Fabia by 'the special tree') -- each is confused because they described the same rendezvous tree differently. Then speak to whichever of the two you did NOT talk to first; she leaves her spot to go join the other, resolving the miscommunication and completing the miniquest. No requirements, no combat, no reward beyond flavour lore per the Miniquests page's own table (Requirements/Length/Unlock all listed None/Short/None).

name: Lunar Diplomacy

req_items.name: Elemental talismans or tiaras
req_items.note: all 4 elements, or Abyss access instead

unlocks: Lunar spellbook access
Lunar Isle + Lunar equipment shop
Astral Altar access
Seal of passage
50 astral runes
Me becomes a Nightmare Zone boss

summary: Multi-stage Lunar Isle quest crafting lunar equipment and clearing the Dream World to unlock the Lunar spellbook.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Lokar Searunner at Rellekka's westernmost dock.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Long.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Sail to Lunar Isle with Captain Bentley by talking to 5 crew NPCs in a strict order and finding 5 hidden symbols with a lit lantern around the ship.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: On Lunar Isle: kill Suqah for a tooth, brew a waking-sleep potion, charge a Dramen staff at all 4 elemental altars in order to make a Lunar staff, then craft full lunar gear (helm, torso, trousers, gloves, boots, ring) from mined lunar ore and Suqah hides.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Enter the Dream World and defeat Me (level 79, safespottable); no gear switching is allowed once inside and you cannot teleport out.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Before the Me fight, complete 6 mini-puzzles at coloured platforms: dice-sum guessing, number-sequence pattern, a 20-log chopping race, a memorize-and-cross stepping-stone maze, a hurdle race, and copy-the-emote.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Grants 5,000 xp each to Magic and Runecraft.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks the Lunar spellbook, Lunar Isle and its equipment shop, the Astral Altar, a seal of passage, 50 astral runes, and adds Me as a Nightmare Zone boss.

notes: Long multi-stage quest gating the Lunar spellbook (key teleport/utility infrastructure). Get seal of passage from Brundt the Chieftain, sail to Lunar Isle with Captain Bentley (talk to 5 crew NPCs in strict order, then find 5 hidden symbols with a lit lantern around the ship). On Lunar Isle: kill Suqah (safespottable, lvl 111) for a tooth, brew a waking-sleep potion for Baba Yaga/Oneiromancer. Charge a Dramen staff at all 4 elemental altars (air/fire/water/earth, in order) to make a Lunar staff, hand to Oneiromancer. Mine+smelt lunar ore into a lunar helm; solve Pauline Polaris name-guess dialogue; kill more Suqah for a special tiara + 4 hides, tan hides, craft full lunar gear (torso/trousers/gloves/boots); dig up blue flowers west of the two bridges for the lunar ring; give the Oneiromancer all lunar gear then get it back. Enter the Dream World: light ceremonial brazier with waking-sleep-soaked kindling, then complete 6 mini-puzzles at colored platforms (dice-sum guessing, number-sequence pattern, 20-log chopping race, memorize-and-cross a Dream Puffs stepping-stone maze, hurdle race, copy-the-emote) before fighting and killing Me (level 79, safespottable, no gear switching allowed once in the dream, can't teleport out). Return via lectern to Lunar Isle, hand in seal of passage to Oneiromancer to finish.

name: Mage Arena I

items: Runes or a powered staff to fight Kolodion
Knife/slash weapon/Wilderness sword to cut webs

req_items.name: Knife, slash weapon, or Wilderness sword

unlocks: Choice of one god cape (Saradomin/Zamorak/Guthix) plus the matching god staff, chosen by praying at a god statue after defeating Kolodion

Ability to unlock each god spell (Claws of Guthix/Flames of Zamorak/Saradomin Strike) for use outside the arena by casting it 100 times inside the arena; other god staves can be bought from the Chamber guardian for 80,000gp each to unlock the other two spells the same way
Gates Mage Arena II and the Wilderness Hard Diary

start.location: Mage Arena bank, level 53 Wilderness (2538,4716); reach via Edgeville or West Ardougne wilderness lever to the Deserted Keep, then north and west, cutting two webs

summary: Defeat Kolodion through five shifting forms in the Wilderness to choose a god cape and staff.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by reaching Kolodion at the Mage Arena bank in level-53 Wilderness, via the Edgeville or West Ardougne wilderness lever to the Deserted Keep, then north and west while cutting two webs.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Kolodion shifts through 5 forms (himself, ogre, frost spider, ghost, level-112 black demon); all forms after the first take magic-only damage, so Protect from Magic tanks the fight.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Roughly 50-100 spell casts (fewer with Iban Blast) clear all 5 forms at 60+ Magic.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: The arena is deep Wilderness (level 53-56), carrying PKer risk; enabling PK Skull Prevention and the hidden attack option is recommended before entering.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Choosing a god cape (Saradomin, Zamorak, or Guthix) with matching staff after defeating Kolodion; each god spell (Claws of Guthix, Flames of Zamorak, Saradomin Strike) is separately unlocked for outside use by casting it 100 times inside the arena, with the other two staves buyable from the Chamber guardian for 80,000gp each.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The 100-cast god-spell unlock grind is the larger time cost, not the Kolodion boss fight itself.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Gates Mage Arena II and the Wilderness Hard Diary.

notes: Solo boss-form gauntlet, not a puzzle miniquest: Kolodion shifts through 5 forms (himself -> ogre -> frost spider -> ghost -> level 112 black demon), all magic-only damage after the first form, so Protect from Magic tanks the fight. ~50-100 spell casts (fewer with Iban Blast) clears all forms at 60+ Magic. The 100-cast god-spell unlock afterward is the real time cost, not the boss fight itself. Deep Wilderness (level 53-56) throughout -- PKer risk, and 'PK Skull Prevention' setting plus hidden attack option are recommended before entering.

name: Mage Arena II

skills: Must have cast Claws of Guthix, Flames of Zamorak and Saradomin Strike each 100 times inside the Mage Arena (the Mage Arena I unlock grind) before this miniquest can start
items: Air, fire and blood runes to cast the god spells
Knife/slash weapon/Wilderness sword to cut webs

req_items.name: Knife, slash weapon, or Wilderness sword

unlocks: Ability to imbue a god cape into its (i) variant via Kolodion, using one remains item from the matching follower: Justiciar's hand (Zachariah/Saradomin), demon's heart (Porazdir/Zamorak), ent's roots (Derwen/Guthix)
Extra imbued capes obtainable later by re-killing a follower for another remains, or buying a base cape from the Lost Property shop first

start.location: Mage Arena bank, level 53 Wilderness (2538,4716), same as Mage Arena I

summary: Track and kill three wandering Wilderness followers to imbue a god cape at Kolodion.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by returning to Kolodion at the Mage Arena bank in level-53 Wilderness.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: An enchanted symbol works like a Hot/Cold clue to locate one of three wandering deep-Wilderness followers (Porazdir, Justiciar Zachariah, Derwen) at a time; each can only be damaged by its own matching god spell.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: The followers hit hard (up to 43) with both magic and melee, and despawn if not killed fast enough.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Recommended loop: locate a follower with the symbol, bank/gear up at Ferox Enclave, kill it and grab the remains, hand the remains to Kolodion, then repeat for the other two.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Deep Wilderness carries high PKer risk; dying to a player loses your cape and awards the killer 60,000gp.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Follower spawn locations rotate roughly every 45 minutes of Wilderness time, per player.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Imbues a god cape into its (i) variant using the matching follower's remains (Justiciar's hand for Saradomin, demon's heart for Zamorak, ent's roots for Guthix); extra imbued capes can be made later by re-killing a follower for another remains, or buying a base cape from the Lost Property shop first.

notes: Track-and-kill loop, not a fixed dungeon: an enchanted symbol acts like a Hot/Cold treasure-trail clue to locate one of three wandering deep-Wilderness followers (Porazdir, Justiciar Zachariah, Derwen) at a time; each can only be damaged by its own god spell, hits hard (up to 43) with both magic and melee, and despawns if not killed fast enough. Recommended loop: locate with the symbol -> bank/gear up at Ferox Enclave -> kill and grab the remains -> hand in to Kolodion -> repeat for the other two. Spawn locations rotate every ~45 minutes of Wilderness time, per-player. High PKer risk in deep Wilderness; dying to a player loses the cape (killer gets 60,000gp).

name: Making Friends with My Arm

summary: Escort My Arm to Weiss to court Snowflake, sneaking into the walled troll town and fighting Mother to secure fire-making secrets.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Burntmeat in the Troll Stronghold kitchen.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Travel by boat to the hidden troll town of Weiss, then sneak, mine, and climb past hostile trolls through a stealth section with a checkpoint and a stepping-stone dodge puzzle.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Fake the Wise Old Man's death with a built coffin to fool Mother.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Fight through Don't Know What (level 163) and Mother (level 198); Mother must be doused with a water bucket to make her vulnerable via the Fire of Domination.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Grants 10,000 Construction xp, 40,000 Firemaking xp, 50,000 Mining xp, and 50,000 Agility xp.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Deep prerequisite chain (My Arm's Big Adventure, Eadgar's Ruse, Druidic Ritual, Troll Stronghold, Death Plateau, The Feud, Jungle Potion, Swan Song, One Small Favour, Rune Mysteries, Shilo Village, Garden of Tranquillity, Creature of Fenkenstrain, Priest in Peril, The Restless Ghost, Cold War, Romeo & Juliet); is itself required for Secrets of the North.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks access to the Salt Mines, the ability to build fire pits, a disease-free herb patch in Weiss, and a teleport-room portal to Troll Stronghold via salt.

refs.title: Making Friends with My Arm/Quick guide

notes: Escort My Arm by boat to the hidden troll town of Weiss to court Snowflake: sneak/mine/climb into the walled town past hostile trolls (a stealth section with a checkpoint and a stepping-stone dodge puzzle), fake the Wise Old Man's death with a built coffin to fool Mother, then fight through Don't Know What (163) and Mother (198, must douse the Fire of Domination with a water bucket to make her vulnerable) before securing Weiss's fire-making secrets.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Research three independent leads in any order: dig up a buried chest near Castle Wars with a silver merchant's key, trade a ghost in Port Phasmatys a sapphire amulet for a scroll, and pass a trivia quiz with the warrior Dron in Rellekka.

facts.label: overview

notes: Research 3 independent historical leads for the outpost's curator Jorral, in any order: dig up a buried chest near Castle Wars with a silver merchant's key, trade a ghost in Port Phasmatys a sapphire amulet for a scroll, and pass a lengthy trivia quiz with the warrior Dron in Rellekka. Report all 3 findings back to Jorral, then relay them to King Lathas.

name: Meat and Greet

unlocks: Access to Emelio's Kebab Shop

summary: Investigate a missing spice delivery for Emelio and prepare a test kebab, fighting a Dire Wolf Alpha and the Colosseum Minotaur.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Emelio at his home in Outer Fortis.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Talk to the Spice Merchant in the Bazaar about a missing delivery and open a mystery box with code 2546, then visit Alba north of Ortus Farm.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Fight the level-113 Dire Wolf Alpha in the wolf den west of the farm; it is multicombat, attacking with melee and ranged simultaneously, so bring food.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Fight the level-193 Minotaur in Fortis Colosseum for Lelia, switching Protect from Melee/Magic on 'Moo!'/'Moooo!' voice cues (a double-moo signals a faster attack).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Deliver a specific mix of meat, salad, spice, and sauce portions to make a test kebab before the Minotaur fight.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Grants 8,000 Cooking xp.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks access to Emelio's Kebab Shop.

notes: Varlamore/Civitas illa Fortis quest. Talk to Emelio then the Spice Merchant in the Bazaar about a missing delivery (open the mystery box with code 2546). Visit Alba north of Ortus Farm, then fight the level-113 Dire Wolf Alpha in the wolf den west of the farm (multicombat, melee+ranged simultaneously -- bring food). Return, deliver a specific mix of meat/salad/spice/sauce portions to make a test kebab, then fight the level-193 Minotaur in Fortis Colosseum for Lelia -- watch for "Moo!"/"Moooo!" voice cues to switch Protect from Melee/Magic (the double-moo signals a faster attack). Finish by reporting to Emelio.

name: Merlin's Crystal

rewards.xp: 6 QP; Prayer/Defence not granted here (see Holy Grail for those); 1000xp lamp in any skill 20+ via Historian Minas kudos reward
unlocks: Excalibur (required for Holy Grail)
Honorary Knight of the Round Table title
5 Kudos claimable

summary: Free Merlin from Morgan Le Faye's castle by sparing Sir Mordred and gathering the items to summon and defeat Thrantax the Mighty.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to King Arthur in Camelot.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Ask King Arthur, then Sir Gawain and Sir Lancelot, for leads, then sneak into Keep Le Faye by hiding in crates near the Catherby docks.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Fight Sir Mordred (level 39) on the top floor but do not kill him or interrupt the cutscene when Morgan Le Faye begs for his life -- sparing him gives the recipe for freeing Merlin.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Gather bat bones from giant bats, make a black candle (bucket of wax plus the candle maker in Catherby), and pass the Lady of the Lake's bread-to-a-beggar test at the Port Sarim jeweller's to get Excalibur; read the chaos altar inscription in Varrock's Zamorakian chapel for the magic words.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Light the candle and drop (not bury) the bat bones on the garden symbol at Camelot to summon Thrantax the Mighty (level 92); getting the incantation wrong causes him to attack.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Reward includes 6 quest points and a 1,000xp lamp in any skill 20 or higher via Historian Minas kudos, but does not directly grant Prayer or Defence xp (those come from Holy Grail).

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks Excalibur, required for Holy Grail, the Honorary Knight of the Round Table title, and 5 claimable Kudos; gates into Heroes' Quest and Holy Grail.

notes: First Camelot-series quest. Ask King Arthur then Sir Gawain and Sir Lancelot in Camelot for leads, sneak into Keep Le Faye by hiding in crates near the Catherby docks, fight Sir Mordred (lvl 39) on the top floor but do not kill him or interrupt the cutscene when Morgan Le Faye begs for his life -- sparing him gets the recipe for freeing Merlin. Gather bat bones (kill giant bats), a black candle (bucket of wax + candle maker in Catherby), and Excalibur (pass Lady of the Lake's bread-to-a-beggar test at the Port Sarim jeweller's). Read the chaos altar inscription in Varrock's Zamorakian chapel for the magic words. Back at Camelot, light the candle and drop (not bury) the bat bones on the garden symbol to summon Thrantax the Mighty (level 92, do not get the incantation wrong or he attacks), grant your boon to free Merlin, then use Excalibur on the crystal in the south-east tower. Gates into Heroes' Quest and Holy Grail.

name: Misthalin Mystery

unlocks: Uncut ruby
Uncut emerald
Uncut sapphire

start.location: SE corner of Lumbridge Swamp, near the fishing spots (3236,3155)

summary: Solve a whodunit inside a manor south of Lumbridge Swamp by collecting three coloured gem keys and unmasking the killer.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Abigale or Hewey at the SE corner of Lumbridge Swamp, near the fishing spots.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Reach the manor by boat, empty a rainwater barrel for the manor key, then solve three room puzzles for coloured gem keys: slash a painting for the ruby key, play D-E-A-D on a piano for the emerald key, and enter a gem sequence (Sapphire-Diamond-Zenyte-Emerald-Onyx-Ruby) in a fireplace for the sapphire key.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Use all three keys on the final blue door, then dodge the hidden killer's knife throws by aiming a mirror at their wardrobe.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Fight and unmask Abigale as the killer to finish.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Grants 600 Crafting xp.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks an uncut ruby, uncut emerald, and uncut sapphire.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: No requirements to start.

notes: No requirements. A whodunit inside a manor reached by boat south of Lumbridge Swamp: empty a rainwater barrel for the manor key, then solve three room puzzles for coloured gem keys (slash a painting for the ruby key, play D-E-A-D on a piano for the emerald key, enter a gem sequence Sapphire-Diamond-Zenyte-Emerald-Onyx-Ruby in a fireplace for the sapphire key). Use all three keys on the final blue door, dodge the hidden killer's knife throws by aiming a mirror at their wardrobe, then fight and unmask Abigale as the killer.

name: Monkey Madness I

unlocks: 10,000 coins
3 diamonds
ability to buy/wield the Dragon scimitar
full access to Ape Atoll (except bank/javelin store, unlocked after Monkey Madness II)

Jungle Demon fightable in Nightmare Zone

summary: Infiltrate Ape Atoll to craft an m'speak amulet and monkey greegree, then defeat the level-195 Jungle Demon.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to King Narnode Shareen at the Grand Tree.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Long.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Glider to Gandius for monkey bones, deliver the royal seal to G.L.O. Caranock, talk to Daero, then solve a sliding puzzle (or pay Glough roughly 200k coins to skip it) before sailing to Ape Atoll via Crash Island.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: On Ape Atoll, sneak past guards (escape jail if caught), find Garkor, gather monkey dentures and an m'amulet mould, then trade them plus a gold bar to Zooknock for an enchanted bar, and combine it with a wall-of-flames and ball of wool into an m'speak amulet.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Trade 5 bananas for a monkey talisman, return to Zooknock with the talisman and monkey bones for a greegree, then use a Karamjan greegree at Ardougne Zoo to smuggle a monkey to Awowogei's audience chamber via Kruk and the Elder Guard, retrieving the 10th squad sigil from Garkor.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Must be able to defeat a level-195 Jungle Demon (Protect from Magic recommended) to finish.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Reward xp is a player choice of 35,000 or 20,000 in each of Attack, Defence, Strength, and Hitpoints, told to Daero.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks 10,000 coins, 3 diamonds, the ability to buy/wield the Dragon scimitar, full Ape Atoll access except bank/javelin store, permanent monkey greegree use at Ape Atoll and Ardougne Zoo, and adds the Jungle Demon to the Nightmare Zone.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Not found in the held steps_quests.jsonl index; steps_quests_id is null.

notes: Must be able to defeat a level 195 Jungle Demon at the end. Speak to King Narnode, glider to Gandius, get monkey bones, deliver the royal seal to G.L.O. Caranock, get Narnode's orders, talk to Daero (multiple dialogue branches to teleport locations), solve a sliding puzzle (or pay Glough to skip it, ~200k coins), fly to Crash Island via Waydar, convince Lumdo to sail to Ape Atoll. On Ape Atoll: sneak past guards (get arrested/escape jail if caught), find Garkor, gather monkey dentures and an m'amulet mould from crates, fall into a cave, then head to the Ape Atoll Dungeon to meet Zooknock and trade dentures+mould+gold bar for an enchanted bar, then combine bar+wall-of-flames+ball of wool into an m'speak amulet. Visit the monkey child's house (avoid the Monkey's Aunt), trade 5 bananas for a monkey talisman, then return to Zooknock with a talisman+monkey bones for a greegree. Use the Karamjan greegree at Ardougne Zoo to smuggle a monkey to Awowogei's audience chamber via Kruk and the Elder Guard, retrieving the 10th squad sigil from Garkor. Final battle: equip the sigil, kill the Jungle Demon (Protect from Magic recommended), return to King Narnode to finish. Optional RFD (Freeing King Awowogei) prep: collect extra bones/talismans for all 4 greegree types (Karamjan/Ninja/Zombie/Gorilla) while in the dungeon/garden.

summary: Grandmaster five-chapter quest infiltrating Glough's dungeon and Ape Atoll to fight Kruk, Kob, Keef, and Glough.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to King Narnode Shareen at the Grand Tree.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Very Long.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Grandmaster-tier, 5-chapter quest.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Chapter II: infiltrate Glough's dungeon via the Agility Route or the web-slashing Tanking Route to reach and kill Kruk (level 207).

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Chapter III: defeat Kob (level 185) and Keef (level 178), sneak into Ape Atoll disguised as Assistant Le Smith, and play the Monkey Gear Solid stealth minigame back to Kruk's dungeon.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Chapter V: a 3-phase final battle against Glough (level 431) using combat-style-dependent tanking or safespotting per phase.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Grants 80,000 Slayer xp, 60,000 Agility xp, 50,000 Thieving xp, and 50,000 Hunter xp.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks 2 Duke training tokens worth 50,000xp each, the royal seed pod (unlimited Grand Tree teleports up to level 30 Wilderness), Crash Site Cavern (demonic gorillas) access, Ape Atoll bank and Javelin Store access, a new glider spot direct to Ape Atoll, the heavy ballista, and dragon javelin tip drops from dragons.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Deep prerequisite chain (Enlightened Journey with the balloon route to Gnome Stronghold, The Eyes of Glouphrie, Recipe for Disaster's Freeing King Awowogei subquest, Monkey Madness I, The Grand Tree, Tree Gnome Village, Troll Stronghold, Death Plateau, Watchtower).

notes: Grandmaster-tier, 5-chapter quest. Chapter I: report to King Narnode about Glough's escape. Chapter II: infiltrate Glough's dungeon via the Agility Route or the Tanking Route (web-slashing) to reach and kill Kruk (level 207). Chapter III: defeat Kob (185) and Keef (178), sneak into Ape Atoll disguised as Assistant Le Smith, and play the Monkey Gear Solid stealth minigame back to Kruk's dungeon. Chapter IV: major combat/story chapter (6933B walkthrough). Chapter V: 3-phase final battle strategy against Glough (431) with combat-style-dependent tanking or safespotting per phase. Very high spine value -- unlocks the heavy ballista and Crash Site Cavern demonic gorillas, both major mid-late-game combat gates.

summary: Infiltrate Mourner HQ in stolen gear, poison West Ardougne's food stores, and free the caged gnome to unlock Lletya.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Sneak into West Ardougne Mourner HQ disguised as a Mourner to sabotage the city's food supply and open elf relations.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Must defeat a level 11 Mourner at the Arandar gate to loot mourner gear; its attacks reduce all stats including HP to 20.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Combat stat drain from the Mourner fight is severe -- a ring of dueling trip to the Ferox pool of refreshment is recommended afterward.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Clean the bloody mourner top with Tegid's stolen soap and mend the trousers with silk and bear fur via Oronwen.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Re-dye 4 sheep herds using the fixed device's Aim-and-Fire mode, then poison 2 of West Ardougne's food stores with a barrel of toxic powder.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks Mourner HQ basement, the village of Lletya, an elf teleport crystal, and full mourner clothing.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Sets up Mourning's End Part II and its Death Altar unlock chain.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 40,000 Thieving xp and 25,000 Hitpoints xp.

notes: Also requires ability to beat a level 11 Mourner with combat stats set to 20 each (kills all stats incl. HP to 20 on contact -- ring of dueling to Ferox pool of refreshment recommended afterward). Flow: talk Eluned then Arianwyn in Lletya, kill a Mourner at the Arandar gate and loot its 7-piece outfit. Clean the bloody mourner top with Tegid's stolen soap + bucket of water at Taverley. Mend trousers with silk+bear fur via Oronwen in Lletya (buy dyes here). Don full mourner gear, enter West Ardougne Mourner HQ, free/befriend the caged gnome downstairs with feather+leather+magic log+toad crunchies. Re-dye 4 sheep herds (Feldip Hills or Castle Wars swamp toads dyed via ogre bellows) using the fixed device's Aim-and-Fire mode on the coloured sheep north of East Ardougne. Make a barrel of toxic powder (naphtha + rotten apple mush via sieve, cooked on a range not fire) and poison 2 of West Ardougne's food stores, then report to Essyllt and Arianwyn to finish. Sets up Mourning's End Part II (teleport crystal) and unlocks the Death Altar chain via that sequel.

name: Mourning's End Part II

req_items.name: Death talisman/tiara, catalytic talisman/tiara, Runecraft cape, OR 50 junk items
req_items.note: any one of these satisfies the requirement; 50 junk items substitutes for ironmen without talismans

unlocks: Crystal trinket (re-enter Temple of Light after the quest)
Access to the Death Altar
Death talismans obtainable via Guardians of the Rift
Death runes craftable during Guardians of the Rift
Access to Dark Beasts
Dark Beasts teleport added to Slayer ring menu

summary: Solve the Temple of Light's mirror puzzle and charge an enchanted crystal at the Death Altar to finish Mourning's End.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Chip and enchant a black crystal, then solve the Temple of Light's mirror puzzle to reach and charge the Death Altar.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: No hard skill requirement, but 65+ combat, 75-91 Agility, and 43+ Prayer are strongly recommended for the shadow-infested temple.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: A 3-floor light/mirror puzzle across 5 numbered treasure chests repositions hand mirrors and coloured crystals to route a light beam.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Ironmen without a death talisman must instead collect 50 junk items to open the Death Altar route.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks the Death Altar, Death talismans/runes via Guardians of the Rift, Dark Beasts access, and a Slayer ring teleport to Dark Beasts.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 60,000 Agility xp.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Direct sequel to Mourning's End Part I; grants a crystal trinket for re-entering the Temple of Light afterward.

notes: No hard skill requirement, but 65+ combat, 75-91 Agility (higher = fewer obstacle failures), and 43+ Prayer are strongly recommended for the shadow-infested temple. Flow: in mourner gear, get the new key from Essyllt, traverse a cave to reach the Temple of Light, chip a piece off a black crystal and have Arianwyn enchant it. Re-enter the temple and solve a 3-floor light/mirror puzzle across 5 numbered treasure chests plus a final Death Altar puzzle -- each chest step repositions hand mirrors and coloured crystals (yellow/cyan/blue/fractured) to route a light beam to unlock the next chest, ultimately opening a path to the Death Altar. Ironmen without a death talisman must instead collect 50 junk items to open the Death Altar route. Charge the enchanted crystal at the Death Altar, then use it on the black crystal to finish and return to Arianwyn.

name: Murder Mystery

unlocks: 2,000 coins

start.location: around Sinclair Mansion, north of Seers' Village and Camelot (2741,3562)

summary: Investigate old man Sinclair's death: match fingerprints, question suspects, and identify the poisoner to report to a guard.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Investigate Sinclair Mansion's murder by dusting fingerprints and questioning suspects to identify the poisoner.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Started by talking to any guard around Sinclair Mansion, north of Seers' Village and Camelot.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: No requirements; a short Kandarin-region quest.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Dust silver items in two thread-colour-matched suspects' bedrooms with flour and flypaper to match a fingerprint to the dagger.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Ask Gossip and the Seers' Village pub poison salesman who bought poison, then confront and confirm the matched suspect's motive.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 1,406 Crafting xp.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Rewards 2,000 coins.

notes: No requirements (Kandarin-region quest, included in this bundle's spine). Old man Sinclair is dead; find flypaper and a dagger inside the mansion, use flour+flypaper on silver items from two thread-colour-matched suspects' bedrooms to dust prints and match one to the dagger. Ask Gossip outside and the Seers' Village pub poison salesman who bought poison, confront the matched suspect about their motive, then search the item they claim to have used the poison on to confirm before reporting to a guard.

name: Natural history quiz

unlocks: 28 unique Varrock Museum kudos (2 per display x 14 displays)
note: if this is your first-ever Slayer xp, you lose the free enchanted gem on your first Slayer assignment and must pay 1gp for it instead

start.location: basement of the Varrock Museum, centre of the room

summary: Answer 3 questions correctly at each of 14 Varrock Museum creature displays to earn Hunter and Slayer xp.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Answer multiple-choice questions at 14 Varrock Museum creature displays to earn xp from Orlando Smith.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Started by talking to Orlando Smith in the basement of the Varrock Museum.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Each display needs 3 correct multiple-choice answers sourced from that display's lecture or wiki text; only 3-of-several need to be right, so guessing is viable.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 1,000 Hunter xp and 1,000 Slayer xp.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: If this is the player's first-ever Slayer xp, the free enchanted gem is lost on the first Slayer assignment and must instead be paid for at 1gp.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks 28 unique Varrock Museum kudos (2 per display across 14 displays).

notes: Talk to Orlando Smith to start, then click the plaque in front of each of 14 creature displays (North room: Lizard/Battle tortoise/Dragon/Wyvern; East room: Snail/Snake/Sea slug/Monkey; South room: Kalphite Queen/Terrorbird; West room: Penguin/Mole/Camel/Leech) and answer 3 multiple-choice questions correctly per display -- answers come from that display's own Natural historian's lecture or the wiki page text itself, and a good approach is simply guessing since only 3-of-several need to be right. Once all 3 for a display are answered correctly its questions are replaced by the creature's common+scientific name. Return to Orlando after all 14 displays are done for the xp reward.

name: Nature Spirit

unlocks: Access to Mort Myre Swamp and the Nature Spirit altar (prayer restore plus temporary +2 boost)
Ability to fight ghasts
3 meat pies + 3 apple pies (unless rotted by ghasts)
Fire of dehumidification (after Making Friends with My Arm)

start.location: basement of the Paterdomus Temple by the River Salve

summary: Retrieve a mirror and journal for the ghost Filliman, then give the nature spirit a sickle to fight ghasts in Mort Myre Swamp.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Help the ghost Filliman recover his memory, then receive a druid pouch from the nature spirit to fight ghasts.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Started by talking to Drezel in the basement of the Paterdomus Temple by the River Salve.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Find a mirror under a bench's washing bowl for Filliman, then search the grotto tree for his journal.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Solve a 3-stone puzzle: mushroom on the light-brown stone, used spell card on the grey stone, stand on the orange stone.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Use the sickle's Bloom ability on swamp plants to fill a druid pouch, then use the filled pouch to make ghasts attackable and kill three of them.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks Mort Myre Swamp, the Nature Spirit altar (prayer restore plus temporary +2 boost), and the ability to fight ghasts.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for Fairytale I - Growing Pains, In Search of the Myreque, Recipe for Disaster, While Guthix Sleeps, and Easy Morytania Diary.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 3,000 Crafting xp, 2,000 Defence xp, and 2,000 Hitpoints xp.

notes: Intermediate quest, direct Priest in Peril sequel. Talk to Drezel for pies, run south through Mort Myre Swamp (must enter via the gate or Filliman won't appear) to the Nature Grotto north-west of Mort'ton, wear the ghostspeak amulet and talk to Filliman, find a mirror under a bench's washing bowl and use it on him, then search the grotto tree for his journal and hand it back for a druidic spell. Return to Drezel to be blessed, cast the spell next to a rotting log in the swamp, bring the mushroom you pick to Filliman's camp, solve his 3-stone puzzle (mushroom on the light-brown stone, used spell card on the grey stone, stand on the orange stone), enter the hole under the tree and give the nature spirit your silver sickle for a druid pouch. Use the sickle's Bloom ability on swamp plants to fill the pouch, use the filled pouch on ghasts to make them attackable and kill three of them, then return to the nature spirit to finish. Required for: Fairytale I - Growing Pains, In Search of the Myreque, Recipe for Disaster (Lumbridge Guide subquest), While Guthix Sleeps, Easy Morytania Diary.

name: Observatory Quest

skills: Agility 23, Ranged 24, Strength 28 (all boostable) plus a crossbow and mith grapple -- only needed post-quest to build the permanent Observatory shortcut, not to complete the quest itself

unlocks: A random constellation reward, assigned once per account and fixed thereafter: pool includes combat-skill xp (~875 in one combat stat, pure-account protected from assignment below level ~3 for most XP signs post-Nov 2018), runes, an amulet of defence, a black 2h sword, a maple longbow, weapon poison, or a super strength(1) -- exact assignment is per-account RNG, not player choice
Permanent Agility shortcut through the Observatory dungeon once Agility 23/Ranged 24/Strength 28 (boostable) + crossbow + mith grapple are available

start.location: Observatory Reception Room, north of Castle Wars (2441,3187)

summary: Deliver 3 building materials to the Observatory professor and retrieve a stolen lens mould to view the rebuilt telescope.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Supply materials to rebuild the Observatory telescope and retrieve a stolen lens mould from the dungeon below.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Started by talking to the Observatory professor in the Observatory Reception Room, north of Castle Wars.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Hand in 3 planks, a bronze bar, and molten glass as 3 separate deliveries, each fully finished before the next can begin.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: The Observatory Dungeon has goblins guarding a kitchen key chest and a sleeping guard to bypass or kill.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Viewing the rebuilt telescope grants a fixed, account-locked constellation reward chosen once per account by RNG.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The exact constellation reward (combat xp, runes, an amulet of defence, a black 2h sword, a maple longbow, weapon poison, or a super strength(1)) is assigned per-account and cannot be chosen.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Building the permanent Observatory dungeon shortcut afterward needs Agility 23, Ranged 24, Strength 28 (all boostable), a crossbow, and a mith grapple -- not needed to complete the quest itself.

notes: Fetch-quest structure: give the professor 3 planks, a bronze bar, and molten glass (3 separate hand-ins, must fully finish each before continuing), then retrieve a stolen lens mould from the goblin-infested Observatory Dungeon below (goblin kitchen key from a chest, a sleeping guard to bypass/kill), craft the new lens from the mould + molten glass, and view the rebuilt telescope to receive your (fixed, account-locked) constellation reward. An unlit antipoison spawns near the dungeon entrance if you didn't bring one.

summary: Complete a long chain of favours across the map, then fight Slagilith and a dwarf gang to finish Petra's storyline.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Chase down a long favour-for-favour chain of NPCs to repair a weather vane and reforge a sharpened axe for Yanni Salika.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Slagilith (level 92) reduces damage by 67% unless a pickaxe is equipped or magic is used; fought via an animate rock scroll on a sculpture.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: A group of dwarf gang members (levels 44/48/49) must be fought in a multicombat area.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Brew a Guthix rest tea for Sanfew and smith 3 weather vane parts on an anvil from bronze, iron, and steel bars.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Long.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks two 10,000xp experience lamps (skill must be level 30+), a steel key ring, pot lid crafting, Guthix rest tea, and a gnome glider route to Feldip Hills.

notes: A long fetch-quest chain of favours-for-favours across the map; also requires killing Slagilith (level 92, 67% damage reduction unless a pickaxe is equipped or magic used) and a group of dwarf gang members (levels 44/48/49, multicombat). Agreeing phase: Yanni Salika -> Jungle forester (blunt axe) -> Brian in Port Sarim (sharpen axe) -> Aggie in Draynor -> Johanhus Ulsbrecht in the H.A.M. Hideout -> Fred the Farmer -> Seth Groats (chicken farm) -> Horvik in Varrock (give 3 steel bars) -> the Apothecary -> Tassie Slipcast (Barbarian Village) -> Hammerspike Stoutbeard (Dwarven Mine) -> brew a Guthix rest tea for Sanfew (Taverley herblore shop) -> Captain Bleemadge (White Wolf Mountain gnome glider) -> Arhein (Catherby docks) -> Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight (Seers' Village) -> search a sculpture in the Goblin Cave SE of the Fishing Guild -> Wizard Cromperty (NE Ardougne) -> Tindel Marchant (Port Khazard) -> Rantz (Feldip Hills, mattress) -> Gnormadium Avlafrim (cut/replace gems in landing lights, crushed gems cost 500gp each to replace, up to 2). Completing phase: report back to Gnormadium and Rantz; give Tindel the mattress; give Cromperty iron oxide; use an animate rock scroll on the sculpture to fight and kill Slagilith, free Petra, and finish her dialogue; repair Phantuwti's weather vane (3 parts smithed on an anvil with bronze/iron/steel bars) and deliver the forecast to Arhein then Bleemadge then Sanfew; kill Hammerspike's 3 gang members then make a pot lid (via Tassie's clay) for the Apothecary; deliver pigeon cages via Horvik, Seth Groats, Johanhus, Aggie, Brian, then the Jungle forester; finally return to Yanni Salika with the sharpened-axe red mahogany to finish.

unlocks: Access to the Sailing skill
Access to The Pandemonium (ship)
A raft

summary: Salvage a shipwreck, build a raft cargo hold, and deliver cargo to Port Sarim to unlock the Sailing skill.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Salvage a shipwreck and deliver raft cargo between Port Sarim and The Pandemonium to unlock Sailing.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Started by talking to Will and Anne at the south docks of Port Sarim.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Helm a ship south through the Bay of Sarim, trimming sails on wind gusts, then deploy a salvaging hook east of the Karamja Shipyard.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Build a cargo hold on a raft with the hammer and saw, then load cargo from the ledger table with empty hands and no weapons equipped.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks the Sailing skill, access to The Pandemonium ship, a raft, a 25 sawmill coupon, 2 repair kits, and a spyglass.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 400 Sailing xp.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

notes: The Sailing-skill unlock quest. Accept a shipwreck-salvage job from Will/Anne, helm a ship south through the Bay of Sarim to east of the Karamja Shipyard (trim sails on wind gusts for speed), deploy the salvaging hook. At the Pandemonium Pub talk to 'Squawking' Steve Beanie and Ribs about the map, then get a raft from Junior Jim in exchange for a salvaged cup; build a cargo hold on the raft with the hammer+saw. Final leg: sail the raft north through Mudskipper Sound/Bay of Sarim to Port Sarim, load cargo from the ledger table (must have empty hands, no weapons), sail back and deliver it. Talk to Steve Beanie to finish.

name: Perilous Moons

items: Combat gear for multiple PvM encounters

unlocks: Access to Neypotzli and the Moons of Peril
Lesser nagua Slayer task assignment
Completion counts as a kill of the Moons of Peril, allowing Lunar Chest loot

summary: Build 3 dungeon camps, locate a hidden NPC by dowsing, and defeat all 3 Moons of Peril in Neypotzli.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Investigate the stirred ancient bosses beneath Cam Torum and defeat all 3 Moons of Peril.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Blue Moon (crush-weak; weapon-freeze and brazier-relight specials), Blood Moon (slash-weak; raining-blood pools and blood-jaguar specials), and Eclipse Moon (stab-weak; rotating shield and clone-facing specials) are each fought in their own chamber.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Build 3 camps in the dungeon's corner areas using building supplies, then dowse hot/cold with infused water and earth talismans to locate the hidden NPC Eyatlalli (location varies per player).

facts.label: combat
facts.value: A Sulphur Nagua guards the entrance and must be killed to enter the city.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Stand in a rotating safe-damage circle throughout each Moon fight; damage taken during their specials still counts.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks access to Neypotzli and the Moons of Peril, and a Lesser nagua Slayer task assignment.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Completion counts as a kill of the Moons of Peril, allowing Lunar Chest loot.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 40,000 Slayer xp, 5,000 Runecraft xp, 5,000 Hunter xp, and 5,000 Fishing xp.

notes: The dwarven town Cam Torum's underground temple Neypotzli has stirred 3 sealed ancient bosses. Kill a Sulphur Nagua guarding the entrance, enter the city, and meet Jessamine, Attala, Zuma. Build 3 camps in the dungeon's corner areas (Ancient Prison, Streambound Cavern, Earthbound Cavern) using building supplies. Get water and earth talismans from Attala, have them infused (magic shop's Nahta, then the blacksmith), then dowse hot/cold with both talismans to locate the hidden NPC Eyatlalli (location varies per player). Gather 3 ritual items around the dungeon (moonlight grub paste, bream scales from fishing, a trapped moss lizard's tail) and start the ritual. Fight all 3 Moons of Peril in their own chambers: Blue Moon (crush-weak; weapon-freeze and brazier-relight specials), Blood Moon (slash-weak; raining-blood pools and blood-jaguar specials), Eclipse Moon (stab-weak; rotating shield and clone-facing specials) -- stand in a rotating safe-damage circle throughout, damage during specials still counts. Return to the Antechamber and speak to Eyatlalli to finish.

name: Pirate's Treasure

rewards.xp: None
unlocks: Access to One-Eyed Hector's Treasure Chest (gold ring, emerald, 450 coins)

start.location: Northernmost dock at Port Sarim, outside The Rusty Anchor (3052,3252)

summary: Smuggle rum for Luthas, retrieve a key from Wydin, and dig up One-Eyed Hector's treasure in Falador Park.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Smuggle rum through Karamja and trade it for a key to unlock a pirate treasure buried in Falador Park.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Started by talking to Redbeard Frank at the northernmost dock at Port Sarim, outside The Rusty Anchor.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: A level-4 Gardener guards the dig site in Falador Park and must be defeated or evaded.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Fill the OUTSIDE banana crate (not the bedroom one) to smuggle Karamjan rum, then trade it to Redbeard Frank for a key that opens a chest in Varrock's Blue Moon Inn.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Very Short.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: No xp reward.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks access to One-Eyed Hector's Treasure Chest (gold ring, emerald, 450 coins).

notes: Classic F2P-adjacent quest, needs a level-4 Gardener defeated or evaded. Sail to Karamja (30 coins), buy Karamjan rum, gather 10 bananas, and get a job with Luthas the banana farmer smuggling rum in a banana crate (fill the OUTSIDE crate, not the bedroom one). Return to Port Sarim, get a white apron from Gerrant's Fishy Business, work for Wydin to retrieve the rum from the crate, and trade it to Redbeard Frank for a key. Use the key on a chest upstairs in Varrock's Blue Moon Inn to read a pirate message pointing to Falador Park. Dig there (kill/evade the Gardener) to unearth the treasure casket.

name: Plague City

items: Hangover cure (or chocolate dust + bucket of milk + snape grass to make one)
Picture of Elena (found in-quest, inside Edmond's house)

summary: Sneak into plague-locked West Ardougne, cure Bravek's hangover, and free Elena from the barred plague house.

facts.label: overview

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Soften the mud behind Edmond's house with buckets of water, dig under the wall, then use rope on the blocked pipe grill to enter West Ardougne.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Cure Bravek's hangover with chocolate dust, bucket of milk, and snape grass to get a warrant past the Mourners guarding the plague house.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Very Short.

facts.label: unlock

notes: Edmond's daughter Elena vanished into plague-locked West Ardougne. His wife Alrena makes a gas mask from dwellberries; soften the mud behind the house with 4 buckets of water then dig under the wall with the spade, use rope on the blocked pipe grill to get through. Inside, show Jethick Elena's picture, learn from the Rehnison family she was taken to the barred plague house. Cure Bravek's hangover (mix chocolate dust + bucket of milk + snape grass) to get a warrant past the Mourners guarding that house, find and free Elena, then return to Edmond via the manhole. One of the Ardougne-diary prereq quests alongside Sheep Shearer and Clock Tower.

facts.label: overview

facts.value: Rewards 1,406 Prayer xp.

notes: Novice quest, Morytania gateway. Talk to King Roald about the temple losing contact; head to the temple east of Varrock by the river, kill the Temple Guardian dog in the basement (safespottable, immune to Magic). Return to Roald, then bring 25 unnoted essence + a bucket + a weapon back: climb to the top floor and talk to the imprisoned monk Drezel, kill a Monk of Zamorak on the 1st floor for a golden key, use it on the correct monument downstairs, fill the bucket from the well there, get it blessed by Drezel, pour it on the coffin to free him, then hand over the remaining 50 essence total to finish. Required for: Animal Magnetism, Creature of Fenkenstrain, Desert Treasure I, Ghosts Ahoy, Haunted Mine, His Faithful Servants, Making History, Nature Spirit, Rum Deal, Shades of Mort'ton, Wanted!.

name: Prince Ali Rescue

req_items.name: Yellow dye
req_items.note: or 2 onions + 5 coins to make it

rewards.xp: None
unlocks: 700 coins
Free passage through the Lumbridge-Al Kharid toll gate forever
Access to the Sorceress's Garden Thieving minigame (members)
Al Kharid Palace rear window shortcut (70 Agility, members)

summary: Craft a disguise and a bronze key to break Prince Ali out of Lady Keli's jail in Draynor.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Break Prince Ali out of a jailhouse using a crafted disguise and a smelted bronze key.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Started by talking to Chancellor Hassan in Al Kharid palace.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Combine a disguise wig, skin paste (ashes, flour, water, redberries), and yellow dye into a disguise.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Take a key impression from Lady Keli, smelt a matching bronze key with a bronze bar, then use the rope on Lady Keli to bypass her.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Level-26 jail guards must be talked past using 3 beers for Joe rather than fought.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks 700 coins, free passage through the Lumbridge-Al Kharid toll gate forever, the Sorceress's Garden Thieving minigame (members), and an Al Kharid Palace rear window shortcut (70 Agility, members).

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: No xp reward.

notes: Prince Ali has been kidnapped by Lady Keli; Hassan hires you to break him out. Osman sends you to Ned in Draynor Village for a disguise wig (3 balls of wool) and to Aggie for skin paste (ashes+flour+water+redberries) and yellow dye, then combine into a disguise. Get a key impression from Lady Keli at the jailhouse east of Draynor and smelt a matching bronze key with a bronze bar at any furnace. Talk your way past the level-26 jail guards with 3 beers for Joe, use the rope on Lady Keli, unlock the cell with the smelted key, and hand the wig/paste/pink skirt to Prince Ali so he can disguise himself out. Report back to Hassan to finish.

25 sawmill coupon (oak plank)

summary: Deliver cargo to The Pandemonium, forge a crowbar with Thurgo, and pry open a sealed crate at sea.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Deliver ledger cargo to The Pandemonium and forge a crowbar to pry open a sealed crate offshore.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Started by talking to 'Squawking' Steve Beanie at The Pandemonium pub.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Fetch a crate of pirate looty from the Port Sarim ledger table and deliver it via the cargo hold.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Bring a steel bar, hammer, and redberry pie to Thurgo, who forges a crowbar to pry open a sealed crate found by sailing to the north-west island off The Pandemonium.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Drinking the recovered fish bladder stout can spawn an optional level 14 drink troll, skippable by returning to the buoys first or disembarking.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks the ability to chart forgotten drinks, a 25 sawmill coupon (oak plank), and unlimited crowbars from the crate of crowbars.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 1,000 Smithing xp, 800 Sailing xp, and 1 Quest Point.

notes: Second Sailing-skill quest. Steve Beanie asks you to fetch a crate of pirate looty from the Port Sarim ledger table, sail it back to The Pandemonium in your cargo hold, and deliver it. He then wants a special key -- take steel bar, hammer and a redberry pie to Thurgo (hut south of Port Sarim) who forges a crowbar. Test the crowbar on a sealed crate found by sailing to the north-west island off The Pandemonium; pry it open from your vessel for a bottle of fish bladder stout, drink it (spawns an optional level 14 drink troll, skippable by returning to the buoys first or disembarking), then return to Steve and open the crate behind him to finish.

summary: Kill 8 low-level creatures for bones, soak them in vinegar, and boil each at the Odd Old Man's hut.

facts.value: Kill a giant rat, unicorn, bear, ram, goblin, big frog, monkey, and giant bat for their bones -- all reachable on a walk from Varrock through Lumbridge Swamp to Karamja.

facts.label: mechanics

facts.label: items-note

facts.label: required-for

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

refs.title: Rag and Bone Man I/Quick guide

notes: No requirements. Kill 8 low-level creatures for their bones (giant rat, unicorn, bear, ram, goblin, big frog, monkey, giant bat -- all reachable on a walk from Varrock through Lumbridge Swamp to Karamja), buy vinegar from Fortunato in Draynor to soak them, then boil each in a pot over a fire at the Odd Old Man's hut. Buying 27 extra vinegar jugs now (35 total) saves a trip if planning Rag and Bone Man II next; direct prerequisite for that sequel quest.

Kill 27 varied monsters worldwide (bats, undead cows, werewolves, ghouls, zombies, moss giants, jackals, desert lizards, vultures, ice giants, mogres, snakes, jogres, baby blue dragons, trolls, rabbits, basilisks (needs mirror shield), dagannoths, fire giants, terrorbirds, wolves, ogres, zogres, etc.) for their bones, then repeat the vinegar-soak-and-boil process from part I 27 times at the Odd Old Man's hut. Ironmen/leaguers should avoid starting until ready since quest-specific bones can litter drop tables across five regions while active.

facts.value: Started by talking to Gertrude, west of Varrock.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Sneak the cat past guards in an Ardougne mansion, poison cheese to lure rats from a Varrock warehouse, and smoke a rat hole in Keldagrim with a lit weed pot.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Learn animal charming from Ali the Snake Charmer in Pollnivneach using an 8-note sequence: D G E F# B(+octave) C# A.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks the Rat pole, training overgrown cats into wily/lazy cats, naming your cat, and access to the Rat Pits with a minigame teleport.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium.

notes: Requires Icthlarin's Little Helper and Gertrude's Cat complete, plus The Giant Dwarf started for Keldagrim access. Your cat catches rats through five vignettes across the map: sneak it past guards in an Ardougne mansion to nab 6 rats, poison cheese to lure rats from a Varrock warehouse's holes, smoke a rat hole in Keldagrim with a lit weed pot, meet Felkrash in Port Sarim's rat pits, then learn animal charming from Ali the Snake Charmer in Pollnivneach (an 8-note sequence: D G E F# B(+octave) C# A) to finish.
