summary: Ten-chapter umbrella quest: unlock the feast, free 8 frozen council members in any order, then defeat the Culinaromancer for full Chest access.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Umbrella chain of 10 chapters: Another Cook's Quest opens the feast hub, 8 council-member chapters can be done in any order, then Defeating the Culinaromancer is the finale.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts with the Cook in the Lumbridge Castle kitchen.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Very Long across the full 10-chapter chain.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Several chapters require defeating high-level bosses with Prayer disabled.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Full completion opens the Culinaromancer's Chest (kitchen weaponry, banking chest, food store, Barrows gloves), Mogre Camp, Evil Chicken's Lair, and the Ape Atoll Teleport spell.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: The 20000 xp reward can be placed into any skill already above level 50.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: The finale chapter needs 175 total Quest Points, combining RFD's own 9 subquest QP with the flattened prerequisite-quest list.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: No single combined gather list -- items are per-chapter; see each RFD subquest row for its own req_items.

notes: Umbrella quest of 10 chapters: start with Another Cook's Quest to unlock the feast/dining-room hub, then free 8 frozen council members (each its own mini-subquest with its own reqs, feeding them a food item) in any order, then Defeating the Culinaromancer as the finale once all others + the flattened prereq-quest list above are done. Each chapter is contributed as its own questdb: row (recipe-for-disaster-*) with per-chapter reqs/rewards; this row covers the whole-chain totals only.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Boss gauntlet order: Agrith-Na-Na, Flambeed (needs ice gloves), Karamel (weak to fire spells below max level), Dessourt (weak to stab/magic), Gelatinnoth Mother (colour-cycling elemental weakness), then the Culinaromancer himself.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: No Prayer allowed against any of the six bosses.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Player can leave and restock supplies between each boss fight without losing progress.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Triggers automatically in the Lumbridge Castle dining room after freeing all 8 council members; no NPC to talk to.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants full Culinaromancer's Chest access, including Barrows gloves.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: The 20000 xp reward can be placed into any skill already above level 50.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Needs 175 total Quest Points before it can be attempted.

notes: Finale chapter. After freeing all 8 council members, a cutscene sends the player through a portal into a boss gauntlet with no Prayer allowed: Agrith-Na-Na (146), Flambeed (149, needs ice gloves), Karamel (136, weak to fire spells cast below max level), Dessourt (121, weak to stab/magic), Gelatinnoth Mother (130, colour-cycling elemental weakness) and finally the Culinaromancer himself (75, hits hard but near-zero Defence). Can leave and restock between each boss without losing progress.

facts.value: Medium.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Cooking 25 is a wiki recommendation, not an enforced requirement to start or complete the chapter.

notes: Talk to Evil Dave's mother Doris in Edgeville, then to Dave himself in the basement; hellrats stole the spices. Use your cat to catch hellrats for 4 spice colours, feed Dave test stews one dose at a time to work out each colour's correct 1-3 dose amount by trial and error, then make the final perfectly-spiced stew and deliver it to Dave in Lumbridge without eating it.

summary: Pass 3 trivia quizzes with Wizard Traiborn to enchant milk, egg and flour, then bake a cake for the frozen Lumbridge Guide.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Take milk, egg and flour to Wizard Traiborn in the Wizards' Tower and pass 3 short trivia quizzes (identify an NPC from a prior quest, answer a general knowledge question, pass an item memory test) to get each ingredient enchanted, then bake and give the cake to the frozen Lumbridge Guide.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by inspecting the Lumbridge Guide in the Lumbridge Castle dining room.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: 2500 Cooking xp and 2500 Magic xp.

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Lumbridge Guide

notes: Take the milk/egg/flour to Wizard Traiborn in the Wizards' Tower and pass 3 short trivia quizzes (identify an NPC from a prior quest, answer a general OSRS knowledge question, pass a memory test on shown items) to get each ingredient enchanted, then bake and give the cake to the frozen Lumbridge Guide.

name: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Mountain Dwarf

skills: ability to defeat a level 13 Icefiend (only if lacking ice gloves)
quests: Recipe for Disaster - Another Cook's Quest

req_items.name: Gloves
req_items.note: ice gloves are the best cooling option, but any gloves work if using an alternate cooling method

unlocks: Buy further dwarven rock cakes from Rohak for 100 coins or raw ingredients
further Culinaromancer's Chest access

summary: Get Rohak drunk on Asgoldian ale, hand him cake ingredients, then cool the resulting dwarven rock cake before delivering it to the frozen dwarf.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Learn the Asgoldian ale recipe from a Rising Sun Inn barmaid in Falador, get Rohak (the dwarf's father, under White Wolf Mountain) drunk on Asgoldian ales, hand him the ingredients for a dwarven rock cake, then cool the hot cake before delivering it to the frozen dwarf.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The hot dwarven rock cake can be cooled instantly with ice gloves, by killing an icefiend while carrying it, or by banking it and waiting.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: A level 13 Icefiend fight is only needed as a cooling method if ice gloves are not already held.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by inspecting the frozen dwarf in the Lumbridge Castle dining room.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: 1000 Slayer xp and 1000 Cooking xp.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants the ability to buy further dwarven rock cakes from Rohak for 100 coins or raw ingredients.

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Mountain Dwarf

notes: Learn the Asgoldian ale recipe from a Rising Sun Inn barmaid in Falador, get Rohak (the dwarf's father, under White Wolf Mountain) drunk on Asgoldian ales, hand him the ingredients for a dwarven rock cake, then cool the hot cake (ice gloves cool it instantly; otherwise kill an icefiend while carrying it, or bank it and wait) before delivering it to the frozen dwarf.

name: Recruitment Drive

items: None required, but you MUST have an empty inventory and nothing equipped to be allowed into the testing grounds

req_items.name: None
req_items.note: inventory must be completely empty and nothing equipped to enter the testing grounds

unlocks: Initiate armour access (free sallet, then buy cuisse 8,000gp / hauberk 10,000gp / full set 20,000gp from Sir Tiffy Cashien)
Gaze of Saradomin: choice to respawn at Falador instead of Lumbridge on death, toggleable via Sir Tiffy Cashien
Initiate title in the Order of Temple Knights -- gates Wanted!

start.location: 2nd floor (UK), western tower of the White Knights' Castle, Falador (2959,3336, plane 2)

summary: Clear 5 of 7 randomized puzzle rooms in a row (always including a Sir Leye fight) to become an Initiate of the Order of Temple Knights.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: After Sir Amik sends you to Sir Tiffy Cashien in Falador Park, clear 5 of 7 possible randomized puzzle rooms in a row, always including a Sir Leye fight; failing any room restarts the 5-room gauntlet from Falador.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Sir Leye fight is mandatory in every playthrough; use the warhammer or fight unarmed, since any bladed weapon on the killing blow fails the room.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Non-combat rooms are randomized logic/memory puzzles: river-crossing animals, a missing-statue memory test, a multi-step chemistry door puzzle, a riddle-based combination lock, multiple-choice riddles, and an 8-second stand-still test.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Sir Amik Varze on the 2nd floor of the western tower, White Knights' Castle, Falador.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short, but the randomized 5-room gauntlet must be cleared without a single failure.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants Initiate armour access (free sallet; cuisse, hauberk and full set purchasable from Sir Tiffy Cashien), the Gaze of Saradomin death-respawn toggle, and the Initiate title in the Order of Temple Knights.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Gates the Wanted! quest and the Medium Falador Diary.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Reward xp is 1000.5 in each of Prayer, Herblore and Agility, plus 3000 coins and 1 Quest Point, as held in source data.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The .5 xp fractions in the reward figures are as recorded in the held source row -- unverified against the wiki.

notes: First quest in the Temple Knight series. After Sir Amik sends you to Sir Tiffy Cashien in Falador Park, you must clear 5 of 7 possible randomized puzzle rooms in a row (order and selection random per playthrough) -- always including a Sir Leye fight (use the warhammer or fight unarmed; any bladed weapon on the killing blow fails the room). Other rooms are logic/memory puzzles (river-crossing animals, missing-statue memory test, multi-step chemistry door puzzle, riddle-based combination lock, multiple-choice riddles, an 8-second stand-still test). Failing any room sends you back to Falador to restart the 5-room gauntlet from scratch. Short quest but gates Wanted! and the Medium Falador Diary, so it's a real progression choke point despite its low difficulty rating.

unlocks: 15,000 coins
Access to the elven kingdom of Tirannwn and the Arandar overpass
Wield the dragon halberd (60 Attack/30 Strength)
Charter a ship to, or dock a boat at, Port Tyras (66 Sailing)
Iorwerth camp teleport scrolls
Zul-Andra teleport scrolls
BJS fairy ring code
Attempt the Gwenith Glide (Barracuda Trial)

summary: Craft a barrel bomb through the Underground Pass and fire it from a catapult to destroy Lord Iorwerth's target for King Lathas.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Return through the Underground Pass to Iban's Temple exit, travel west through Isafdar meeting Idris then Lord Iorwerth at the Iorwerth Camp, track the Elf Tracker to find and kill a Tyras guard for a crystal pendant, gather coal-tar barrels, sulphur, quicklime and naphtha ingredients, distill naphtha at the Rimmington chemist's still, assemble a barrel bomb, carry it to the catapult north of Tyras Camp, bait the guard with cooked rabbit and fire the bomb, then return to King Lathas.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Must defeat a safespottable level 110 Tyras guard.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Naphtha distillation at the Rimmington chemist's still is a careful tar/pressure/coal-feed minigame.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to King Lathas on the 1st floor of Ardougne Castle.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Long.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants 15000 coins, access to the elven kingdom of Tirannwn and the Arandar overpass, the dragon halberd (60 Attack/30 Strength to wield), Port Tyras charter/docking at 66 Sailing, Iorwerth camp and Zul-Andra teleport scrolls, the BJS fairy ring code, and the Gwenith Glide (Barracuda Trial) attempt.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Direct prerequisite for Roving Elves and the rest of the Mourner/elf storyline.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Agility 56 avoids a slower alternate trap route but is not a hard start gate for the quest.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: 13750 Agility xp.

notes: Agility 56 is only needed to avoid a slower alternate route through the traps (not a hard start gate); must also defeat a safespottable level 110 Tyras guard. Flow: return through the Underground Pass (west West-Ardougne entrance) to Iban's Temple exit, run west through Isafdar meeting Idris then Lord Iorwerth at the Iorwerth Camp, track the Elf Tracker to find/kill the Tyras guard for a crystal pendant, collect coal-tar barrels + sulphur, gather quicklime/naphtha ingredients and distill naphtha at the Rimmington chemist's still (careful tar/pressure/coal-feed minigame), assemble a barrel bomb (quicklime+sulphur+naphtha+cloth), carry it back through the Underground Pass to the catapult north of Tyras Camp, bait the guard with cooked rabbit and fire the bomb to destroy Lord Iorwerth's target, then return to King Lathas to finish. Directly prerequisite for Roving Elves and the rest of the Mourner/elf storyline.

name: Rogue Trader

quests: Icthlarin's Little Helper (only needs to be STARTED, for Sophanem access)
Gertrude's Cat (prerequisite of Icthlarin's Little Helper)
items: 3 dyes of the same colour OR 3 animal pelts of the same kind (cowhide, grey wolf fur, snakeskin, bear fur, or wool -- not balls of wool) to unlock either Menaphite clothing or the Desert outfit
100 coins, optional, to bribe the street urchin for defensive blackjacks

req_items.name: Dyes of the same colour
req_items.note: OR 3 animal pelts of the same kind (cowhide, grey wolf fur, snakeskin, bear fur, or wool -- not balls of wool); either set unlocks Menaphite clothing or the Desert outfit

req_items.name: Coins
req_items.note: only needed to bribe the street urchin for the defensive blackjack option

unlocks: Ali Morrisane's rune stock (opened via casket runedoku puzzle / Strength force / Thieving pick on a small casket, puzzle-only on the large casket)
Offensive or defensive blackjack stock (switchable later)
Menaphite clothing or Desert outfit stock (switchable later, cosmetic-only variants of Desert clothing)
Discounted magic carpet network fares: 100gp (75gp with ring of charos (a)), down from 200gp/100gp

summary: Complete 3 independent errand branches for Ali Morrisane (runes, blackjacks, clothes) in any order to unlock his full Discount Wares stock.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Not an official miniquest -- the wiki classifies it as one-off training/trade-unlock content, not a Quest List entry. Three independent branches unlock separate parts of Ali's shop and can be done in any order: Runes (solve, force or pick two lockable rune caskets; the large casket only accepts the runedoku puzzle), Blackjacks (talk to the Blackjack seller in Pollnivneach and pick offensive or defensive style), and Clothes (give Siamun in Sophanem 3 matching dyes or pelts).

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Ali Morrisane in Al Kharid.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short if fully prepared, Medium otherwise.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Runes branch opens Ali's rune stock; Blackjacks branch opens offensive or defensive blackjack stock (switchable later); Clothes branch opens Menaphite clothing or Desert outfit stock (switchable later, cosmetic-only); all three together give discounted magic carpet fares (100gp, or 75gp with ring of charos (a), down from 200gp/100gp).

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Icthlarin's Little Helper only needs to be started, not completed, for the Sophanem access this quest needs.

notes: Not an official miniquest -- the page itself says it does not appear in the quest list; wiki classifies it as one-off 'training or trade unlock' content, not a Quest List miniquest. Three independent errand branches, doable in any order, each separately unlocking a piece of Ali's shop: (1) Runes -- solve/force/pick two lockable rune caskets (small casket allows any method, large casket ONLY the runedoku puzzle); (2) Blackjacks -- talk to the Blackjack seller in Pollnivneach, pick offensive (take 2 hits) or defensive (bribe an urchin) style; (3) Clothes -- give Siamun in Sophanem 3 dyes or 3 matching pelts. All three done = full access to Ali's Discount Wares.

name: Romeo & Juliet

unlocks: 5 quest points only -- no xp/item rewards listed on the page

summary: Relay a letter between Juliet and Romeo, then brew and deliver a sleeping potion so the two can meet in the crypt.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Relay a letter from Juliet (upper balcony of the mansion west of Varrock's west bank) to Romeo in the square, fetch cadava berries (growing west of the south-east Varrock mine) for the Apothecary to brew a sleeping potion, give the potion to Juliet, then tell Romeo to meet her in the crypt.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Romeo in Varrock Square.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: No requirements to start; reward is 5 Quest Points only, no xp or item rewards listed.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Prerequisite quest feeding into Defender of Varrock's requirement chain.

notes: No requirements. Classic go-between quest: relay a letter from Juliet (upper balcony of the mansion west of Varrock's west bank) to Romeo in the square, then fetch cadava berries (grow west of the SE Varrock mine) for the Apothecary near the west bank to brew a sleeping potion. Give the potion to Juliet and tell Romeo to meet her in the crypt to finish. Prerequisite quest feeding into Defender of Varrock's requirement chain.

name: Roving Elves

items: Glarial's pebble (obtained in-quest if not kept from Waterfall Quest)

req_items.note: obtained in-quest if not kept from Waterfall Quest

unlocks: Crystal shield OR crystal bow with 500 charges (player choice)
Moss Guardian becomes accessible in the Nightmare Zone
Isafdar painting for the Quest hall (required for the hard Western Provinces Diary)

summary: Befriend Islwyn, plant a consecration seed grown from a Moss Guardian kill in the Waterfall Dungeon's final room, and choose a crystal weapon reward.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Track down and befriend Islwyn (world-hop if not present), bank combat gear, use Glarial's pebble on Glarial's tombstone north-west of the Fishing Guild, kill the Moss Guardian for a consecration seed, travel through the Waterfall Dungeon (raft, rope, rope, door) to plant the seed in the final room, then report back to Islwyn for the reward choice.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Level 84 Moss Guardian must be fought unarmed under heavy item restrictions (flinchable).

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Agility 56 is waived entirely if travelling to the elven woods by charter ship instead of on foot.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Islwyn in the Elven woods of Isafdar.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Reward choice of a crystal shield or a crystal bow with 500 charges; also makes the Moss Guardian accessible in the Nightmare Zone and grants the Isafdar painting for the Quest hall (required for the hard Western Provinces Diary).

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: 10000 Strength xp.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Directly sets up Mourning's End Part I's teleport crystal once Eluned is then spoken to.

notes: Agility 56 is waived if travelling to the elven woods by charter ship instead of on foot. Must also be able to defeat a level 84 Moss Guardian unarmed under heavy item restrictions (flinchable). Flow: track down and befriend Islwyn (world-hop if not present), bank combat gear, use Glarial's pebble on Glarial's tombstone NW of the Fishing Guild, kill the Moss Guardian for a consecration seed, then travel through the Waterfall Dungeon (raft+rope+rope+door) to plant the seed in the final room and report back to Islwyn for the reward choice. Directly sets up Mourning's End Part I's teleport crystal if Eluned is then spoken to.

Bosun Zarah hireable as crewmate at Sailing 80
Enhanced rewards from Managing Miscellania
ability to dock at Etceteria at Sailing 65

summary: Repair a broken lift, cross hazards to collect a burnt diary, then defeat the Giant Sea Snake to end the Miscellania-Etceteria conflict.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: As regent, investigate thefts threatening war between Miscellania and Etceteria; talk to citizens on both sides, get a signed scroll from King Vargas to access the dungeon under Miscellania Castle, talk to the dwarf Donal, then repair a broken lift using an engine, 5 coal, 3 beams, 3 pulley beams and 2 ropes assembled in a specific order.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Deeper in the dungeon: swing across water with a rope, cross slippery rocks with a plank while avoiding steam vents and falling rocks, and collect 5 pages of a burnt diary from fire remains, reading it before the final crevice.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Instanced fight against the Giant Sea Snake, level 149, 100 HP, poison hits up to 9 -- antipoison strongly recommended.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Advisor Ghrim in Miscellania Castle.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants 20000 coins, Bosun Zarah as a hireable crewmate at Sailing 80, enhanced Managing Miscellania rewards, and the ability to dock at Etceteria at Sailing 65.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: 5000 Agility, 5000 Slayer, 5000 Hitpoints xp, plus 1 Quest Point, as held in source data.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Second quest in the Miscellania series; requires Throne of Miscellania, which itself flattens Heroes' Quest, Shield of Arrav, Lost City, Merlin's Crystal, Dragon Slayer I and The Fremennik Trials.

notes: Second Miscellania-series quest. As regent, investigate thefts threatening war between Miscellania and Etceteria; talking to citizens on both sides reveals soldiers demanded tithes and were refused. King Vargas sends you to the Sailor at the docks, who mentions visiting children; get a signed scroll from Vargas to access the dungeon under Miscellania Castle. Talk to the dwarf Donal, then repair a broken lift using an engine + 5 coal, 3 beams, 3 pulley beams and 2 ropes assembled in a specific order. Deeper in, swing across water with a rope, cross slippery rocks with a plank (avoid steam vents and falling rocks), and collect 5 pages of a burnt diary from fire remains, reading it before the final crevice. Meet the runaway Fremennik-trial children (a Scooby-Doo parody) who admit to staging the whole conflict, then kill the Giant Sea Snake (level 149, 100 HP, poison hits up to 9 -- antipoison strongly recommended) in an instanced boss room. Collect the heavy box it drops, then deliver a letter from Queen Sigrid to King Vargas to finish. reqs.quests is the fully flattened chain from the page's own Details template (Throne of Miscellania requires Heroes' Quest which requires Shield of Arrav+Lost City+Merlin's Crystal+Dragon Slayer I, plus The Fremennik Trials).

summary: Grow blindweed for the Braindeath Island brewery, then defeat an evil spirit and a fever spider to finish a batch of Unsanitary Swill.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Talk to Pirate Pete then Captain Braindeath (decline his money) to start; grab a blindweed seed, plant it in the south-eastern patch, feed the grown blindweed into the brewery hopper, fill a bucket with stagnant water and add it, fish 5 sluglings near the farm patch and add them, then pull the lever.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Must kill a safespottable level 150 Evil spirit and a safespottable level 49 fever spider, adding the spider's corpse to the hopper.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Davey must bless the wrench (needs 47+ Prayer) before it can be used on the shaking brewing control.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Pirate Pete at the dock north of Port Phasmatys.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants the holy wrench (extra prayer points from prayer potions while held) and access to Braindeath Island.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: 7000 xp each in Fishing, Prayer and Farming.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Zogre Flesh Eaters' own Big Chompy Bird Hunting to Jungle Potion to Druidic Ritual chain is inherited and flattened into this quest's requirements.

notes: Must be able to kill a level 150 monster (safespottable). Zogre Flesh Eaters' own Big Chompy Bird Hunting/Jungle Potion->Druidic Ritual chain is inherited/flattened. Talk to Pirate Pete then Captain Braindeath (decline his money) to start; grab a blindweed seed. Plant blindweed in the south-eastern patch, wait for it to grow, feed it into the brewery hopper. Fill a bucket with stagnant water north of the island and add it to the hopper. Fish 5 sluglings near the farm patch and add them, then pull the lever. Get Davey to bless your wrench (needs 47+ Prayer) and use it on the shaking brewing control, then kill the level 150 Evil spirit (safespottable). Kill a fever spider (level 49, safespottable) and add its corpse to the hopper. Collect the finished 'Unsanitary Swill' and deliver it to Captain Donnie among the protesters, then report back to Captain Braindeath to finish.

name: Rune Mysteries

unlocks: ability to mine rune essence
an air talisman
lamps/books of knowledge from random events can go into Runecraft
5 Kudos (claim from Historian Minas, Varrock Museum)

summary: Deliver an air talisman between Sedridor and Aubury for Duke Horacio to unlock Runecraft and rune essence mining.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Duke Horacio gives an air talisman; take it to Archmage Sedridor in the Wizards' Tower basement, who sends it to Aubury in his Varrock rune shop as a research package, Aubury swaps it for notes to carry back to Sedridor, who explains Runecraft basics and returns the talisman.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Duke Horacio on the 1st floor of Lumbridge Castle.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short; no requirements or items needed.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Opens rune essence mining and Runecraft entirely; grants an air talisman, the ability to send lamps/books of knowledge from random events into Runecraft, and 5 Kudos claimable from Historian Minas at the Varrock Museum.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: The single most-required prerequisite quest on the wiki; directly gates Aubury's essence-mine teleport (Varrock Diary Easy task) and Sedridor's essence-mine teleport (Lumbridge & Draynor Diary Easy task).

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Reward xp amount is unknown ('??' in held data) -- not stated in the source row.

notes: No requirements or items needed -- easiest of the orphan gates. Duke Horacio gives an air talisman; take it to Archmage Sedridor in the Wizards' Tower basement, who sends it on to Aubury in his Varrock rune shop as a research package; Aubury swaps it for notes to carry back to Sedridor, who then explains Runecraft basics and returns the talisman. The single most-required prereq quest on the wiki: opens rune essence mining/Runecraft entirely, and directly gates Aubury's essence-mine teleport (Varrock Diary Easy task) and Sedridor's essence-mine teleport (Lumbridge & Draynor Diary Easy task).

name: Sacred Bone Burner

items: Five unnoted dragonstones, OR 100 (baby)dragon bones for a half-restore / 200 for a full restore (lava dragon bones count double, superior dragon bones count triple toward the 200 total)

req_items.name: Unnoted dragonstones
req_items.note: jumps faith straight to the 100-point mark instead of gradual bone offerings

req_items.name: Draconic bones (babydragon/dragon/lava dragon/superior dragon)
req_items.note: 100 bones for a half-restore or 200 for a full restore; lava dragon bones count double and superior dragon bones count triple toward the 200 total

rewards.xp: ?? (burner grants +300% Prayer xp on eligible bone/ash offerings once faith reaches 100+ points, permanently)
unlocks: The Sacred Bone Burner's boosted-xp offering effect
a temple key that permanently unlocks the remaining gates/shortcut in Forthos Dungeon

summary: Offer draconic bones or dragonstones to Olbertus's cursed burner to raise its faith level and unlock a permanent Prayer xp boost.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Olbertus's burner/shrine sits under a curse; offer eligible draconic bones (babydragon, dragon, lava dragon, superior dragon) to raise its 'faith level' from 0 toward 200 points, or give 5 unnoted dragonstones up front to jump straight to the 100-point mark.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Faith thresholds give flavour messages at 0/50/100/150/200; the 'Check-Faith' option on the burner shows current progress at any time.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts by talking to Olbertus in Forthos Dungeon.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: At 100 faith the burner grants +300% Prayer xp on offered bones/ashes, permanently; at 200 faith a temple key appears that permanently unlocks the dungeon's remaining gates/shortcut.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The xp boost itself has no further threshold beyond the 100-point mark; not on the official quest list -- classified as a one-off restoration errand/miniquest.

notes: One-off restoration errand, not on the quest list. Olbertus's burner/shrine sits under a curse; offer eligible draconic bones (babydragon, dragon, lava dragon, superior dragon) to raise its 'faith level' from 0 toward 200 points (giving 5 unnoted dragonstones up front instead jumps you straight to the 100-point mark). Faith thresholds give flavour messages at 0/50/100/150/200; at 100 the burner starts granting +300% Prayer xp for offered bones/ashes; at 200 a temple key appears, permanently unlocking the dungeon's remaining gates/shortcut, though the xp boost itself has no further threshold beyond 100. Use the 'Check-Faith' option on the burner to gauge progress at any time.

name: Scorpion Catcher

req_items.name: Dusty key
req_items.note: obtainable during the quest from the jailer; alternative: Agility 70 (boostable) bypasses the shortcut without needing the key

rewards.xp: 6,625 Strength; 1 QP
unlocks: Thormac can enchant a battlestaff into a mystic staff for 40,000 coins

start.location: top floor of Sorcerer's Tower (2701,3405, plane 3), south-east of Ranging Guild

summary: Catch Thormac's three escaped scorpions from Taverley Dungeon, Edgeville Monastery, and Barbarian Outpost, then return them.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Thormac's three rare Kharid Scorpions have escaped his tower; he provides a scorpion cage to recapture them.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Thormac on the top floor of Sorcerer's Tower (2701,3405, plane 3), south-east of the Ranging Guild.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Scorpion 1 is in a hidden room in Taverley Dungeon, unlocked by talking to a Seer in Seers' Village, reached past blue dragons and black demons/poison spiders using a dusty key from the jailer or a 70/80 Agility shortcut.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Scorpion 2 is upstairs in Edgeville Monastery, which requires Prayer 31 to enter (one-time).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Scorpion 3 is in the Barbarian Outpost Agility Course, accessible only after completing Alfred Grimhand's Barcrawl.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 6,625 Strength xp and 1 quest point.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Thormac can enchant a battlestaff into a mystic staff for 40,000 coins after completion.

notes: Thormac's three rare Kharid Scorpions have escaped; he gives a scorpion cage. Talk to a Seer in Seers' Village to unlock the hidden room in Taverley Dungeon holding scorpion 1 -- reach it past blue dragons (anti-dragon shield recommended) and black demons/poison spiders, using a dusty key from the jailer (or the 70/80 Agility shortcuts to skip). Scorpion 2 is upstairs in Edgeville Monastery (needs Prayer 31 to enter, one-time). Scorpion 3 is in the Barbarian Outpost Agility Course (needs Alfred Grimhand's Barcrawl completed). Catch each with the cage, then return the full cage to Thormac.

name: Scrambled

unlocks: Egg pet (from talking to the egg on the wall, can be placed in pet house)
Alan's bones lootable south of temple (auto-buried for 1.5 prayer xp if wearing bonecrusher/bonecrusher necklace)

start.location: Temple of Tal Teok, just north of Tal Teklan (1247,3166)

summary: Help Alan and three of King's men rebuild his egg-friend Humpy after repeated accidental drops, via fetch tasks and a puzzle.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Alan asks the player to mind his egg-friend Humpy while he cooks a feast, but gravity destroys it as soon as he leaves.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Recruit three of King's men from The King's Inn in Tal Teklan: Acatzin repairs a whetstone then an axe, Nezketi brews damiana tea, and Kauayotl repairs his cart.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: When the original egg can't be fixed, three replacement eggs are obtained by looting egg piles from a large chicken (level 16), a red dragon (level 106, safespottable), and a black jaguar (level 88).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: None of the painted replacement eggs survive judging, so the player and King's men solve a sliding/rotate puzzle to reassemble the original egg.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Wearing a bonecrusher or bonecrusher necklace auto-buries Alan's bones for 1.5 Prayer xp instead of leaving them lootable as a keepsake.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Alan at the Temple of Tal Teok, just north of Tal Teklan (1247,3166).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 5,000 xp each in Construction, Cooking, and Smithing.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants an egg pet (obtained by talking to the egg on the wall) that can be placed in a pet house.

notes: Alan asks you to mind his egg-friend Humpy while he cooks a feast; gravity kills it the moment he leaves. Recruit three of King's men from The King's Inn in Tal Teklan to help rebuild the egg: Acatzin (repair blacksmith's whetstone then his axe), Nezketi (brew damiana tea: pick damiana leaves + bowl of water from fountain, boil on oven, pour into cup), Kauayotl (repair his cart with 2 planks, nails from blacksmith's workbench, hammer, saw from Archery Supplies). Back at the temple the three can't fix the original egg so you fetch three replacement eggs by killing and looting egg piles: large chicken (lvl16, chicken farm south of temple), red dragon (lvl106, Dragon Nest SE across the river, safespottable), black jaguar (lvl88, campsite east, safespot behind tropical tree/fern). None of the painted eggs survive judging (three more great falls), so you and King's men solve a sliding/rotate puzzle (hover puzzle-piece icon for the solution) to reassemble the original egg. Cutscene resolves the plot; ironically Alan himself then has a great fall. Avoid having a bonecrusher equipped if you want his bones as a keepsake instead of auto-crushed prayer xp.

name: Sea Slug

skills: Firemaking 30 (boostable, not required at quest start -- only needed later to light the torch)
items: Swamp paste (made by cooking flour+swamp tar on a fire; also buyable from a Trader crewmember 75gp, Rasolo 60gp west of Fishing Guild, or Khazard General Store 42gp at full stock)

req_items.name: Swamp paste
req_items.note: made by cooking flour and swamp tar on a fire, or buyable from a Trader crewmember (75gp), Rasolo west of the Fishing Guild (60gp), or Khazard General Store (42gp at full stock)

unlocks: Access to the Fishing Platform
Oyster pearls

start.location: Coast just north of Witchaven, east of Ardougne (2716,3302)

summary: Fix Holgart's boat with swamp paste, rescue Kennith's father, then light a torch to save Kennith from platform machinery.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: First quest in the Sea Slug series and third in the Temple Knight series.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Give Holgart swamp paste to fix his boat and reach the Fishing Platform.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Find Bailey to learn sea slugs fear heat, then rescue Kennith's father Kent from a nearby island.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Light a torch, made from broken glass and damp sticks, and use it to save Kennith from the platform machinery (needs Firemaking 30, boostable, only at this step).

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Caroline on the coast just north of Witchaven, east of Ardougne (2716,3302).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 7,175 Fishing xp and 1 quest point.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants access to the Fishing Platform and Oyster pearls.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Gates The Slug Menace, the sequel quest.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The quest's own Rewards section grants access to the Fishing Platform, not the Fishing Guild -- a different location; some secondary sources conflate the two.

notes: First quest in the Sea Slug series and #3 in the Temple Knight series. Give Holgart swamp paste to fix his boat and reach the Fishing Platform; find Bailey to learn sea slugs fear heat, rescue Kennith's father Kent from a nearby island, then light a torch (needs Firemaking 30, boostable) using broken glass + damp sticks, and use it to save Kennith from the platform machinery. CORRECTION to the depth:04 queue payload: its rationale text calls this quest's unlock 'Fishing Guild access' -- the quest's own Rewards section states the unlock is 'Access to the Fishing Platform', not the Fishing Guild; these are different locations and the brief's phrasing appears to be an error. Gates The Slug Menace (sequel).

name: Secrets of the North

items: Lockpick (the Strange Old Lockpick does NOT work)

req_items.note: the Strange Old Lockpick does not work for this quest

unlocks: Mine ancient essence crystals in the Ghorrock Dungeon
Access to a stronger version of the Strange Creature: the Phantom Muspah

summary: Investigate Ceril's murder, track Evelot, infiltrate Weiss, and defeat the Strange Creature to unlock the Phantom Muspah.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Murder-mystery quest continuing the Hazeel Cult/General's Shadow storyline.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Desert Treasure I must be started (for the ring of visibility), though full completion is not required.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Investigate Ceril's murder at Carnillean Mansion (a broken window and a hidden room behind a false wall), then track and fight Evelot (level 148, drains prayer on her specials) near the Fight Arena bar.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Visit the Hazeel Cult hideout via an Ardougne sewers raft to enlist Hazeel's help, then lockpick a hidden chest behind the mansion's cooking shelves for a scroll (a Strange Old Lockpick does not work for this).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Travel to Weiss, question Big Fish and Snowflake, then fight a level-262 Assassin who lures with smoke bombs and throws poison vials.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Solve a multi-stage code-lock dungeon under Weiss (gate code, chest code, brazier-lighting order, arrow-direction gate code, combining two jewel shards) to reach the final boss, the Strange Creature.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Final boss is the Strange Creature under Weiss; ranged/magic gear is recommended before the fight.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to the guard outside Carnillean Mansion, south-west East Ardougne (2570,3276).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 60,000 Agility, 50,000 Thieving, and 40,000 Hunter xp.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks mining ancient essence crystals in the Ghorrock Dungeon and access to a stronger version of the Strange Creature, the Phantom Muspah.

notes: Also needs Desert Treasure I started (for the ring of visibility) though not a full completion requirement. Murder-mystery continuing the Hazeel Cult/General's Shadow storyline. Investigate Ceril's murder at Carnillean Mansion (broken window, hidden room behind a false wall), then track and fight Evelot (level 148, drains prayer on her specials) near the Fight Arena bar by following a clue trail. Visit the Hazeel Cult hideout via an Ardougne sewers raft to enlist Hazeel's help, lockpick a hidden chest behind the mansion's cooking shelves for a scroll. Travel to Weiss, question Big Fish and Snowflake, then fight a level-262 Assassin (lures you with smoke bombs, throws poison vials). Solve a multi-stage code-lock dungeon under Weiss (a 'BLOOD' gate code, a chest code, a brazier-lighting order, an arrow-direction gate code, combining two jewel shards) to reach the final boss, the Strange Creature -- resupply with ranged/magic gear first. Return to the mansion guard to finish; unlocks the Phantom Muspah boss.

req_items.name: Tarromin potion (unf)
req_items.note: or 2 vials of water + 2 grimy tarromin, both obtainable in-quest

req_items.note: or an axe to cut some

Shades of Mort'ton minigame teleport
Extra 335 Herblore xp for turning in the Diary of Herbi Flax to the Apothecary in Varrock (once only)

summary: Brew Serum 207, repair Mort'ton's ruined temple, then cure Razmire and Ulsquire with Serum 208.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Search a shelf in the ruined south-west house of Mort'ton and read the diary found there to start; this also unlocks making Serum 207.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Make 2 doses of Serum 207, use one to cure Razmire in the general store, kill 5 loar shades for their remains, and return the remains to Razmire.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Repair the temple north-east of Mort'ton to 100% with at least 20 sanctity (a minigame, safer with other players present), light the sacred altar, pour olive oil on the flame for sacred oil, and dip Serum 207 in the flame to make permanent-cure Serum 208.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Make a pyre log with the sacred oil and loar remains, light it at one of the stone funeral pyre groups west of the general store for 25 Prayer xp, then cure both Razmire and Ulsquire Shauncy with Serum 208 to finish.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: A regular hammer alone cannot finish the temple repair if the minigame world is empty; a Flamtaer hammer (buyable from Razmire for about 13,000 coins) is highly recommended.

facts.label: start
facts.value: No start NPC -- begin by searching the shelf in the ruined house, south-west Mort'ton (3481,3279).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 2,000 Herblore and 2,000 Crafting xp; turning in the Diary of Herbi Flax to the Apothecary in Varrock grants an extra 335 Herblore xp, once only.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks the Shades of Mort'ton minigame and its teleport.

notes: Search the shelf in the ruined SW house of Mort'ton and read the diary found there to start (this also unlocks making Serum 207). Make 2 doses of Serum 207 (clean tarromin + vial of water, then add ashes), use one on Razmire (afflicted) in the general store, kill 5 loar shades for their remains, return the remains to Razmire. Buy an olive oil from his general store and 5 timber beams/5 limestone bricks/25 swamp paste (+ optional Flamtaer hammer) from his builders' store. Serum the sick Ulsquire Shauncy east of the store and talk to him twice about the remains/temple. Head to the temple NE of Mort'ton and repair it to 100% with at least 20 sanctity (minigame, safer with other players present), light the sacred altar (or wait for someone with 5+ Firemaking), pour olive oil on the flame for sacred oil, and dip Serum 207 in the flame for permanent-cure Serum 208. Make a pyre log with the sacred oil, take it plus loar remains to one of the stone funeral pyre groups west of the general store, light the pyre (25 Prayer xp), collect the reward, then cure both Razmire and Ulsquire with Serum 208 to finish.

unlocks: 10,000xp in any combat skill other than Prayer (player's choice)

up to 6 cut gems (2 sapphire/2 ruby/2 emerald) from the demon's throne if not already taken during The Golem
Agrith-Naar accessible in Nightmare Zone

summary: Join Evil Dave's cult to gather demonic sigils, then defeat Agrith-Naar with Silverlight to upgrade it into Darklight.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Sequel to The Golem and Demon Slayer; must be able to defeat a level 100 demon, Agrith-Naar.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Talk to Father Reen, travel to the Ruins of Uzer, talk to Father Badden with Silverlight in inventory, dye Silverlight black with a black mushroom/dye, grab a strange implement, then wear 3 black outfit items plus the dyed Silverlight to join the cult with Evil Dave.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Through the portal: get a demonic sigil mould from Jennifer, smelt a demonic sigil from a silver bar, find a book hidden in one of 4 kilns and deliver it to Matthew, get an incantation from Denath, complete and chant a ritual circle, retrieve Denath's dropped sigil, then take Tanya's sigil (4 sigils total).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Rally the troops by convincing Father Badden and Father Reen, then reprogram the golem with the strange implement and send it into the dungeon.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Final fight: say the incantation backwards to Matthew, then kill Agrith-Naar (level 100, flinchable behind the north-west torch) with any weapon but land the final blow with Silverlight.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Do not flee mid-final-blow against Agrith-Naar or it heals.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Father Reen, south of Al Kharid bank.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: No flat xp reward; the player chooses 10,000 xp in any combat skill other than Prayer instead.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Silverlight upgrades into Darklight (a stronger special attack against demons, costlier to replace) and Agrith-Naar becomes accessible in the Nightmare Zone.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Up to 6 cut gems (2 sapphire/2 ruby/2 emerald) are available from the demon's throne if not already taken during The Golem.

notes: Must be able to defeat a level 100 demon (Agrith-Naar). Talk to Father Reen, travel to the Ruins of Uzer (magic carpet, necklace of passage, or fairy ring DLQ), talk to Father Badden (with Silverlight in inventory), dye Silverlight black with a black mushroom/dye, grab a strange implement, then talk to Evil Dave wearing 3 black items plus the dyed Silverlight to join the cult. Through the portal: talk to Denath, get a demonic sigil mould from Jennifer, ask Matthew about Josef, smelt a demonic sigil from a silver bar at a furnace, swap out the black outfit for combat gear (keep Silverlight), ask the clay golem about the prior night, find and read a book hidden in one of 4 kilns and deliver it to Matthew, get the incantation from Denath (write it down, used twice), complete the ritual circle and chant it, retrieve the demonic sigil Denath drops, exit through the portal and take Tanya's sigil, then tell Evil Dave to return to the lair (4 sigils total). Rally the troops: convince Father Badden and Father Reen, reprogram the golem with the strange implement and send it into the dungeon. Final fight: say the incantation backwards to Matthew, kill Agrith-Naar (level 100, flinchable behind the NW torch) with any weapon but land the final blow with Silverlight (don't flee mid-final-blow or it heals), then unequip Silverlight and choose a reward skill to finish.

summary: Investigate missing children in Auburnvale, rescue them, reinforce a house wall, and fight 3 Strange Creatures.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Missing-persons mystery in Auburnvale.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by reading the noticeboard at the Auburn Pub, Auburnvale (1396,3356).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Talk to Marcus, the Bartender, Ictus, and Captain Ariadna, then to Francis/Aemilia about their missing children.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Follow red marks west to the river, fish cloth from a floating plank, then follow a trail of puddles around the mountain to a cave holding an injured boy.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Bring 4 willow longbows to Captain Ariadna and reinforce the house's west wall with 4 maple logs plus a hammer, then talk to the rescued kids upstairs.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Return to the cave, talk to Antos, and fight 3 Strange Creatures (level 93); their bleed still ticks through Protect from Melee.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 10,000 Slayer, 4,000 Hunter, 3,000 Fishing, and 3,000 Construction xp.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks access to the Stalker Den (Custodian stalkers).

notes: Missing-persons mystery in Auburnvale. Read the pub noticeboard, talk to Marcus, the Bartender, Ictus, and Captain Ariadna, then to Francis/Aemilia about their missing children. Follow red marks west to the river, fish cloth from a floating plank, then follow a trail of puddles around the mountain to a cave holding an injured boy. Bring 4 willow longbows to Captain Ariadna and reinforce the house's west wall with 4 maple logs + a hammer, then talk to the now-rescued kids upstairs. Return to the cave, talk to Antos, and fight 3 Strange Creatures (level 93 -- their bleed still ticks through Protect from Melee). Report back to Captain Ariadna to finish and unlock the Stalker Den.

Fastest route is simply buying 20 balls of wool (tradeable) and handing them in; the 'intended' route -- shear sheep in the pen with shears, spin the wool at a spinning wheel -- is optional and grants +50 bonus Crafting xp over just buying. Same opener tier as Cook's Assistant, designed to walk new players through Lumbridge.

summary: Pick a side (Phoenix Gang or Black Arm Gang), retrieve your shield half, then combine it with a partner's to finish.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Talk to Reldo in the Varrock Palace library about quests, check and read the bright blue book on the top shelf of the bookcase, then talk to Reldo again to start.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Pick a side, Phoenix Gang or Black Arm Gang; a partner going the opposite gang is needed to finish (the official chat-channel 'OSRS SOA' helps pair players).

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Gang-specific inner steps are unresearched here -- the walkthrough branches into two separate subpages not fetched at quick-guide grain.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Take your retrieved shield half to Curator Haig Halen in Varrock Museum, trade certificate halves with your partner (ironmen use halves on partner), combine the two halves, then hand the completed certificate to King Roald to finish.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Reldo in the Varrock Palace library (3209,3495).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short

facts.label: unlock

notes: Talk to Reldo in the Varrock Palace library about quests, check the bright blue book on the top shelf of the bookcase, read it, then talk to Reldo again to start. You must then pick a side, Phoenix Gang or Black Arm Gang, and need a partner going the opposite gang to finish (official chat-channel 'OSRS SOA' helps pair players). Gang-specific inner steps ?? (walkthrough branches into two separate subpages, not fetched at quick-guide grain). Once your half of the shield is retrieved, take it to Curator Haig Halen in Varrock Museum, trade certificate halves with your partner (ironman: use halves on partner), combine the two halves, then hand the completed certificate to King Roald for the reward.

facts.label: mechanics

summary: Investigate Slepe's plague, trek to Darkmeyer, forge the Blisterwood flail, and defeat Vanstrom Klause.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Continues the Myreque/vampyre storyline after Ranis Drakan's death.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Investigate Slepe's sleeper plague: tail Carl by hiding in sequence to find his supplier, kill lab-assistant Kroy from behind cover with ranged, and destroy both lab tables.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Trek with Ivan Strom through the swamp (vine bridge, nail beasts, a log bridge repair, low-tier vampyres via the Ivandis flail) to Burgh de Rott, then boat to the Icyene Graveyard and solve a mausoleum-door puzzle.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Mine 15 Daeyalt ore in the Ver Sinhaza mines and help Safalaan retrieve a book past a mutated bloodveld.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Disguise as a Vyre noble (buy/upgrade the outfit via Polmafi Ferdygris) to enter Meiyerditch, then fight Damien Leucurte with the Ivandis flail, stomping out his periodic magic fires or hits do nothing.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Cut 8 blisterwood logs in Darkmeyer's Arboretum after solving a two-valve gallon puzzle, then forge the Blisterwood flail from an enchanted ruby sickle, a blisterwood log, and the Ivandis flail.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Endgame boss Vanstrom Klause fights in two phases: phase 1 dodge acid-bloodveld/blood-orb-heal/stare-into-darkness specials under Protect from Magic; phase 2 (after he self-heals 200hp and drops specials) dodge repeated lightning strikes since prayer no longer blocks the damage.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Veliaf Hurtz outside the church in Slepe (3729,3319).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Long

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks Darkmeyer city, including the Daeyalt Essence Mine, Hallowed Sepulchre, Vyre-noble shopping, and buildable long-rope Agility shortcuts.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants a Tome of experience (15,000 xp to any skill level 60+, reusable 6 times, recoverable from Ivan Strom if lost) and the Blisterwood flail.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants a Darkmeyer teleport via Drakan's medallion, boat travel between Burgh de Rott/Slepe/the Icyene Graveyard, access to the mutated-bloodveld section of Meiyerditch Laboratories, and True Blood Altar access with a blood talisman (including the Guardian of Blood in Guardians of the Rift).

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Promotes the player to Lieutenant of the Myreque.

notes: Continues the Myreque/vampyre storyline after Ranis Drakan's death. Investigate Slepe's sleeper plague (tail Carl by hiding in sequence to find his supplier, kill lab-assistant Kroy from behind cover with ranged, destroy both lab tables). Trek with Ivan Strom through the swamp (vine bridge, kill nail beasts, repair a log bridge, kill low-tier vampyres with the Ivandis flail) to Burgh de Rott. Boat to the Icyene Graveyard, solve a mausoleum-door puzzle, brief the Myreque council, then mine 15 Daeyalt ore in the Ver Sinhaza mines and help Safalaan retrieve a book past a mutated bloodveld. Disguise as a Vyre noble (buy/upgrade the outfit via Polmafi Ferdygris) to enter Meiyerditch, then fight Damien Leucurte (stomp out his periodic magic fires or your hits do nothing) with the Ivandis flail. Cut 8 blisterwood logs in Darkmeyer's Arboretum after solving a two-valve gallon puzzle, forge the Blisterwood flail (enchanted ruby sickle + blisterwood log + Ivandis flail), then fight the endgame boss Vanstrom Klause in two phases: phase 1 dodge his acid-bloodveld/blood-orb-heal/stare-into-darkness specials under Protect from Magic; phase 2 (after he self-heals 200hp and drops specials) dodge repeated lightning strikes since prayer no longer blocks his damage. Unlocks Darkmeyer city.

No combat required for the miniquest itself; the unlock (mogres as a valid Slayer task/kill) is used afterward, recommended with 32 Slayer + fishing explosives.

start.location: East Ardougne, north of the Flying Horse Inn (2569,3333)

summary: Free Elena, incite rebellion in Ardougne, solve the Grand Library puzzles, and defeat the Fragment of Seren.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Grandmaster capstone quest that unlocks Prifddinas.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Disguise as an Ardougne knight (dyed helm + tabard) to free Elena from Ardougne Castle's dungeon, then infiltrate West Ardougne as a mourner to stir unrest before switching back to knight gear to rile up East Ardougne merchants.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Fight two waves of guards ('Battle of West Ardougne' at Mourner HQ, 'Battle of East Ardougne' at the castle), triggering exile to Lletya.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Solve a six-pillar light puzzle at Baxtorian Falls (6 items keyed to per-pillar riddle clues) to reach the Well of Voyage, then grow a cadantine seed and get a crystal seed enchanted.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Track down Lord Amlodd and Lady Trahaearn, repairing an elderly elf's mechanism with a runite bar, to reach the Enchanted Valley and brew an inversion potion.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Solve the Grand Library's nine house-seal puzzles (a separate sub-guide), find Lord Crwys and Lady Hefin, retrieve an orb of light by killing two Iorwerth guards, and track Ysgawyn via a footprint/clue trail.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Survive the attack on Lletya, a bow fight against Arianwyn: dodge orange 1x1 bomb arrows and re-enable prayers after purple ones.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Solve a five-symbol clue chain across Lletya and outdoor dig sites to find Lady Ithell and Lady Meilyr, then defend a dwarf camp for 4 minutes inside the Underground Pass and beat Essyllt.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Finale is a magic-only boss fight against the Fragment of Seren with 4 recurring specials: teleport-to-boss, copy/split identification, spawned healing orbs to snipe, and a near-max-hit freeze nuke requiring overheal via Saradomin brews.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Edmond, East Ardougne, north of the Flying Horse Inn (2569,3333).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Very Long

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 40,000 xp each in Agility, Construction, Farming, Herblore, Hunter, Mining, Smithing, and Woodcutting.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks Prifddinas, including Zalcano, The Gauntlet, the Trahaearn Mine, the Iorwerth Dungeon, a respawn-point option, moving the POH there, a teleport crystal, a new spirit tree destination, divine potions, and crystal tools/armour/weapons.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks the Prifddinas port and Port Tyras noticeboard access.

refs.title: Song of the Elves/Warped Library quick guide

notes: Grandmaster capstone that unlocks Prifddinas. Disguise as an Ardougne knight (dyed helm + tabard) to free Elena from Ardougne Castle's dungeon, infiltrate West Ardougne as a mourner to stir unrest (burn grain sacks, talk to townsfolk), then switch back to knight gear to rile up East Ardougne merchants. Fight two waves of guards ('Battle of West Ardougne' at Mourner HQ, 'Battle of East Ardougne' at the castle), triggering exile to Lletya. Learn Arianwyn's rebellion plan, then solve a six-pillar light puzzle at Baxtorian Falls (6 items keyed to per-pillar riddle clues) to reach the Well of Voyage. Grow a cadantine seed, get a crystal seed enchanted, then track down Lord Amlodd and Lady Trahaearn (repairing an elderly elf's mechanism with a runite bar) to reach the Enchanted Valley and brew an inversion potion. Solve the Grand Library's nine house-seal puzzles (separate sub-guide), then find Lord Crwys and Lady Hefin, retrieve an orb of light by killing two Iorwerth guards, track Ysgawyn via a footprint/clue trail, and survive the attack on Lletya -- a bow fight against Arianwyn (dodge orange 1x1 bomb arrows, re-enable prayers after purple ones). Solve a five-symbol clue chain across Lletya and outdoor dig sites to find Lady Ithell and Lady Meilyr, then defend a dwarf camp for 4 minutes inside the Underground Pass and beat Essyllt, before the finale: a magic-only boss fight against the Fragment of Seren with 4 recurring specials (teleport-to-boss, copy/split identification, spawned healing orbs to snipe, and a near-max-hit freeze nuke requiring overheal via Saradomin brews). Completing it opens Prifddinas.

summary: Help ghost Bernina reunite with mountain climber Bernard atop the Proudspire by tracing her lost necklace.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Unnamed-in-game miniquest (config name spiritofadventure_*), released 23 July 2025 with Varlamore: The Final Dawn.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Wearing a ghostspeak amulet, talk to ghost Bernina, who died in the wreck that named Shipwreck Cove and lost her diamond necklace to a salvager.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Find salvager Jasper at Salvager Overlook to learn the necklace went to a mountain climber, grab logs and a tinderbox from houses south of Jasper, then climb the Proudspire to find climber Bernard.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Light a fire for Bernard to learn he's carrying the necklace up the mountain to honour his late mother, who turns out to be Bernina.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Return to Bernina to relay this (she teleports to join Bernard atop the Proudspire), then return to Bernard so the player can ghost-interpret for the reunion, and climb to the peak obelisk to speak to Bernard there to finish.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: No combat is required.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to ghost Bernina on the Auburn Valley road, north of the Darkfrost, Varlamore.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Very Short

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: No item or xp reward is granted.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: No unlocks -- lore-only, per both the quest's own Rewards section and the Miniquests page's Lore table.

notes: Unnamed-in-game miniquest (config name spiritofadventure_*), released 23 July 2025 with Varlamore: The Final Dawn. Wearing a ghostspeak amulet, talk to ghost Bernina, who died in the wreck that named Shipwreck Cove and lost her diamond necklace to a salvager. Find salvager Jasper at Salvager Overlook (he sold it on due to bad luck) to learn it went to a mountain climber. Grab logs+tinderbox from houses south of Jasper, climb the Proudspire to find climber Bernard, light a fire for him (with the logs/tinderbox) to learn he's carrying the necklace up the mountain to honour his late mother -- who turns out to be Bernina. Return to Bernina to relay this; she teleports to join Bernard atop the Proudspire. Return to Bernard so you can ghost-interpret for the reunion, then climb to the peak obelisk and speak to Bernard there to finish. No combat, no item/xp reward.

summary: Defeat 3 golems guarding water channels, then trade with a genie to unlock the Nardah shrine to Elidinis.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Must beat 3 level-75 golems (white/grey/black), each weak to a different melee style, or solve a ranged/magic water-channel puzzle instead.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Talk to Awusah then Ghaslor the Elder for the ballad, find torn robe top+bottom in a cupboard, repair with needle and thread and equip them as Robes of Elidinis, then telegrab an ancestral key.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Follow the river north to a waterfall cave, rope the overhanging root, and use the ancestral key while wearing the robes to open the door.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Kill the white golem (stab) then clear its water channel via Thieving; kill the grey golem (slash) then clear via pickaxe; kill the black golem (crush) then clear via Ranged/Magic on a target.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Separately: take a genie's shoes by Awusah's door, knife them for a sole, find a crevice west of the general store, rope it, then strike a deal with the Genie for a statuette and place it on the shrine plinth north of the fountain to finish.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Awusah the Mayor in Nardah (3434,2917).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Short

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 8,000 Prayer, 1,000 Thieving, and 1,000 Magic xp.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks Nardah fountain water access and the Nardah Shrine to Elidinis, a prayer that restores/boosts HP, cures poison and venom, and restores prayer, run energy, and special attack energy.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants the Robe of Elidinis.

notes: Must beat 3 level-75 golems (white/grey/black), each weak to a different melee style (stab/slash/crush respectively) or a ranged/magic water-channel puzzle. Talk Awusah then Ghaslor the Elder for the ballad, find torn robe top+bottom in a cupboard, repair with needle+thread and equip (Robes of Elidinis), telegrab an ancestral key. Follow the river north to a waterfall cave, rope the overhanging root, use the ancestral key wearing the robes to open the door. Kill white golem (stab) then clear its water channel via Thieving; kill grey golem (slash) then clear via pickaxe; kill black golem (crush) then clear via Ranged/Magic on a target. Talk to a spirit through the north door, return to Nardah. Separately: take a genie's shoes by Awusah's door, knife them for a sole, find a crevice west of the general store, rope it, talk to and strike a deal with the Genie for a statuette, then place the statuette on the shrine plinth north of the fountain to finish. Region-opener (Nardah/desert), no known deeper dependency; useful all-purpose restoration shrine unlock.

start.location: Piscatoris Fishing Colony (boat west of Tree Gnome Stronghold)

summary: Repair the Piscatoris Fishing Colony's defenses, raise an army, then defeat the Sea Troll Queen to unlock the colony.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Requires the flattened chains behind One Small Favour (Rune Mysteries, Shilo Village -> Jungle Potion -> Druidic Ritual) and Garden of Tranquillity (Creature of Fenkenstrain -> Priest in Peril, The Restless Ghost) as prerequisites.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Must be able to defeat a combat level 170 monster, the Sea Troll Queen (magic+melee, drains prayer).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Talk to Herman Caranos, then the Wise Old Man in Draynor, then return to the colony, fighting through level 79 sea trolls and collecting 7 bones.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Repair the colony: light the firebox, press iron bars into sheets to repair the perimeter wall, fish and cook 5 monkfish for Arnold Lydspor while fending off increasingly tough sea trolls.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Raise an army: visit Wizard Frumscone in the Wizards' Guild basement, then Malignius Mortifer south of Falador for a brown apron, make a pot+lid at the Crafting Guild into an airtight pot (avoid wearing Saradomin gear near Malignius), then teleport back to the colony.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Final battle: fight and kill the Sea Troll Queen, using Protect from Magic while meleeing, or ranged.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Starting this quest temporarily blocks the Sir Amik Varze RFD subquest until 'Repairing the Colony' is done; the colony is instanced during the quest so Treasure Trail unlocks only apply after full completion.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Herman Caranos at the Piscatoris Fishing Colony (boat west of Tree Gnome Stronghold).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 15,000 Magic, 10,000 Prayer, and 50,000 Fishing xp.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Rewards 25,000 coins and unlocks access to the Piscatoris Fishing Colony, the ability to fish monkfish, and fighting Skeleton Mages for training.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants free use of Kathy Corkat's boat and on-request brown apron/bone seeds from Malignius Mortifer.

notes: One Small Favour's chain (Rune Mysteries, Shilo Village -> Jungle Potion -> Druidic Ritual) and Garden of Tranquillity's chain (Creature of Fenkenstrain -> Priest in Peril, The Restless Ghost) are prerequisites, flattened. Must be able to defeat a combat level 170 monster (Sea Troll Queen: magic+melee, drains prayer). Talk to Herman Caranos, then the Wise Old Man in Draynor, then return to the colony (fight through level 79 sea trolls, collect 7 bones, enter and speak to Herman). Repair the colony: light the firebox, press iron bars into sheets to repair the perimeter wall, fish and cook 5 monkfish for Arnold Lydspor while fending off increasingly tough sea trolls, then talk to the Wise Old Man/Herman. Raise an army: visit Wizard Frumscone in the Wizards' Guild basement, then Malignius Mortifer south of Falador (get brown apron), make a pot+lid at the Crafting Guild into an airtight pot (don't wear Saradomin gear near Malignius), teleport back to the colony. Final battle: fight and kill the Sea Troll Queen (Protect from Magic while meleeing, or use ranged). Note: starting this quest temporarily blocks the Sir Amik Varze RFD subquest until 'Repairing the Colony' is done; the colony is instanced during the quest so Treasure Trail unlocks only apply after full completion.

summary: Prepare bait and a poisoned spear, help Lubufu and Tamayu fight the Shaikahan, then deliver supplies to Tiadeche.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Jungle Potion's own Druidic Ritual requirement is inherited/flattened into this quest's reqs.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Prep ahead: banana rum (slice a banana onto Karamjan rum), burnt jogre bones (burn jogre bones with a tinderbox, needs 30 Firemaking or a furnace), pasty jogre bones (grind paste from raw karambwanji and apply to burnt bones, then cook), and a karambwan-poisoned spear (cook a raw karambwan into a poison karambwan, grind it, and apply the paste to a spear).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Talk to Timfraku to start, then to Lubufu to help him fish and learn Karambwan fishing.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: At fairy ring DKP, give Tiadeche a baited karambwan vessel; at CKR, find Tamayu at the mine, give him the agility potion and poisoned spear, watch him fight the Shaikahan, then bring him a monkey corpse and a seaweed-monkey-skin sandwich.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Cross to Cairn Isle and give Tinsay the rum, sandwich, and marinated jogre bones for a crafting manual; give the manual to Tiadeche to get him to agree to return.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Return to Timfraku to finish; optionally talk to Tiadeche, Tinsay, and Tamayu afterward for extra xp/rewards.

notes: Jungle Potion's own Druidic Ritual requirement is inherited/flattened. Prep ahead: banana rum (slice a banana onto Karamjan rum bought from Zembo at Musa Point, quest-safe to teleport with), burnt jogre bones (burn jogre bones with a tinderbox, needs 30 Firemaking or use a furnace), pasty jogre bones (fish 45 raw karambwanji near fairy ring CKR, grind one into paste, apply to burnt bones, cook - don't bury), and a karambwan-poisoned spear (cook a raw karambwan into a poison karambwan, grind it, apply paste to spear). Talk to Timfraku to start, then to Lubufu (help him fish, learn Karambwan fishing, lose/regain a vessel for a spare). Head to fairy ring DKP: give Tiadeche a baited karambwan vessel, then go to CKR and find Tamayu at the mine - give him the agility potion and poisoned spear, watch him fight the Shaikahan, then bring him a monkey corpse and a seaweed-monkey-skin sandwich. Cross to Cairn Isle and give Tinsay the rum, sandwich, and marinated jogre bones for a crafting manual; give the manual to Tiadeche to get him to agree to return. Return to Timfraku to finish; optionally talk to Tiadeche, Tinsay, and Tamayu afterward for extra XP/rewards.

summary: Investigate a prison skeleton, solve a device puzzle, and defeat a Corrupt Lizardman atop Mount Quidamortem.

facts.label: overview

facts.label: combat

facts.label: mechanics

facts.label: mechanics

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Mine and push a boulder, kill the Corrupt Lizardman guarding a magic gate, then inspect an Unstable Altar.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: World-hop to leave, speak to Duffy and Gnosi together back at the altar, report to Lord Shiro Shayzien, then return to Phileas's now-empty house for dialogue before reporting to Lord Shiro Shayzien once more to finish.

Talk to Phileas Rimor and agree to help, then travel to the Arceuus Library (Archeio) to be teleported into the Tower of Magic prison via Pagida; solve a 3-attack-type puzzle on a strange device (push west, attack with magic from north, attack with melee from south, push east, attack with ranged from south), then inspect a skeleton in the northern cell. Report to Phileas, then to Lord Shiro Shayzien at the War Tent. Travel to the summit of Mount Quidamortem, speak to Historian Duffy, rope down a western crevice, mine and push a boulder, kill the Corrupt Lizardman guarding a magic gate, inspect an Unstable Altar, world-hop to leave, then speak to Duffy and Gnosi together back at the altar. Report to Lord Shiro Shayzien, then return to Phileas's now-empty house for dialogue, then back to Lord Shiro Shayzien once more to finish.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: 43 Quest Points gate makes this a mid-game unlock, not an early one.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Tell Juna stories to earn a stone bowl recipe, then use a light creature (attracted by the sapphire lantern) to cross the chasm to a rock vein.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Mine a magic stone, chisel it into a stone bowl, and hand it to Juna to finish.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: The sapphire lantern doubles as the light source needed to safely navigate the caves and is reusable later for While Guthix Sleeps.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Juna at the Chasm of Tears, deep in the Lumbridge Swamp Caves (3252,9516, plane 2).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Very Short

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 1,000 Crafting xp.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Gates While Guthix Sleeps and the Hard Lumbridge & Draynor Diary.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks playing the Tears of Guthix minigame once per week, a repeatable activity granting xp in the player's currently lowest skill each run.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: 'Tears of Guthix' the quest and 'Tears of Guthix' the repeatable minigame it unlocks are the same wiki title-root but two distinct pages.

notes: 43 QP gate makes this a mid-game unlock, not an early one. Tell Juna stories to earn a stone bowl recipe, then use a light creature (attracted by the sapphire lantern) to cross the chasm to a rock vein, mine a magic stone, chisel it into a stone bowl, and hand it to Juna to finish. The sapphire lantern doubles as the light source needed to safely navigate the caves and is reusable later for While Guthix Sleeps. Gates While Guthix Sleeps and the Hard Lumbridge & Draynor Diary. Note: 'Tears of Guthix' the quest and 'Tears of Guthix' the repeatable minigame it unlocks are the same wiki title-root but two distinct pages -- cited both above since the depth:04 brief's long-run-value framing refers to the minigame, not the quest itself.
