start.location: Flying Horse Inn, NW East Ardougne

summary: Choose a side between Lucien and the Guardians of Armadyl, then defeat the Fire Warrior of Lesarkus.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Choice quest with a good path (side with the Guardians of Armadyl, kill Lucien level 14) or an evil path (steal the Staff of Armadyl for Lucien, may need to kill a safespottable level 43 Guardian of Armadyl).

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Must be able to defeat a level 84 Fire Warrior of Lesarkus with Ranged.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Enter the temple dungeon south of the Ranging Guild, get boots of lightness from behind a web (needs a light source and knife), equip the Pendant of Lucien to pass the north gate, and cross a lava bridge at -1kg or less.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Take a lever, cross back, attach the lever to a bracket and pull it, then loot ice arrows from chests along an icy path (16 arrows is enough with Ava's + 60 Ranged).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Go back through the north gate and search-and-pull another lever (needs 42 Thieving), then fight through to the Fire Warrior of Lesarkus (level 84, kill with ice arrows; Protect from Magic negates its damage).

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Handing Winelda 20 unnoted limpwurt roots at once teleports the player across the lava one-way, with no going back -- caution for ironmen.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Optionally grab a shiny key for a dungeon shortcut, then push through to the Guardians of Armadyl room and pick a side to finish.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Start by talking to Lucien at the Flying Horse Inn, north-west East Ardougne.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Medium

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Rewards 10,500 Ranged and 8,000 Fletching xp.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Good path unlocks the Armadyl pendant (requires killing Guardians of Armadyl); evil path unlocks the Pendant of Lucien for door access in Temple of Ikov.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Also grants optional Boots of lightness, an optional shiny key for a McGrubor's Wood dungeon shortcut, and 5 kudos claimable from Historian Minas, Varrock Museum.

notes: Must be able to defeat a level 84 Fire Warrior of Lesarkus with Ranged. Choice quest with a good path (side with Guardians of Armadyl, kill Lucien level 14) or evil path (steal the Staff of Armadyl for Lucien, may need to kill a safespottable level 43 Guardian of Armadyl). Enter the temple dungeon south of the Ranging Guild; get boots of lightness from behind a web (needs light source+knife), equip Pendant of Lucien to pass the north gate, cross a lava bridge at -1kg or less, take a lever, cross back and attach the lever to a bracket then pull it, loot ice arrows from chests along an icy path (16 arrows enough with Ava's + 60 Ranged). Go back through the north gate, search-and-pull another lever (needs 42 Thieving), fight through to the Fire Warrior of Lesarkus (level 84, kill with ice arrows, Protect from Magic negates damage), then hand Winelda 20 unnoted limpwurt roots at once to be teleported across the lava (one-way, no going back - caution for ironmen). Optionally grab a shiny key for a dungeon shortcut. Push through to the Guardians of Armadyl room and pick a side to finish.

name: Temple of the Eye

xp.Runecraft: 5,000 direct + ~4,210 gained during the quest itself, ~9,210 total

unlocks: Access to the Guardians of the Rift minigame
Medium pouch (if not already owned)

start.location: north of Al Kharid, near the Lumbridge gate (3286,3232)

summary: Short intro quest unlocking Guardians of the Rift via an eye amulet, an Abyss rift puzzle, and apprentice riddles.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Short intro quest that gates access to the Guardians of the Rift minigame.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts with Wizard Persten, north of Al Kharid near the Lumbridge gate (3286,3232).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Length: Short.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The Abyss puzzle requires touching runic colours until all are white; puzzle order is randomised per player.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Traiborn's three apprentice riddles are solved with the answer 11.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Buy (do not steal) a strong tea from the Tea Seller to bribe the Mage of Zamorak for an Abyss teleport; stealing triggers a 10-minute lockout.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Completion unlocks the Guardians of the Rift minigame and grants a medium pouch if the player doesn't already own one.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Reported reward XP is 5,000 Runecraft direct, plus roughly 4,210 gained naturally during the quest, for roughly 9,210 total.

notes: Short intro quest gating Guardians of the Rift. Get the eye amulet from Wizard Persten, use it on the Mage of Zamorak at Varrock's Altar of Chaos, buy (don't steal, or a 10-minute lockout) a strong tea from the Tea Seller to bribe him into an Abyss teleport, then use the amulet on the Dark Mage and solve a colour-matching rift puzzle (touch runic colours until all are white; order is random per player) to receive an abyssal incantation. Return to Persten, teleport to the Wizards' Tower, hand the incantation to Archmage Sedridor, then solve three apprentice riddles with Traiborn (the answer is 11). Finish with a guided Guardians of the Rift tutorial: assemble/charge guardians, mine and grind guardian fragments into essence, and power the Great Guardian at the Mind and Water altars.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Length: Short.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Rewards include 2,000 coins, a Kharedst's memoirs page 'A dark disposition' (teleports to the centre of Arceuus), the ability to recolour the graceful outfit to Arceuus colours, and the ability to fight the Trapped Soul in the Nightmare Zone.

notes: The first death in Arceuus house in a thousand years needs investigating. Talk to Mori, report it to Councillor Andrews at Kourend Castle, then go to the Tower of Magic and fight through 5 Tormented Souls (level 16) to reach Asteros Arceuus upstairs. Travel to Mount Karuulm, talk to Kaal-Xil-Dar, then find an ancient grave SE of the mountain and follow a track (searching bushes/plants along the way) to a hidden Trapped Soul (level 30) and defeat it. Report back to Kaal-Xil-Dar, then search the rocks ringing the Dark Altar north of Arceuus to find and destroy a hidden device. Finish by talking to Lord Trobin Arceuus back at the Tower of Magic.

Player works through Castle Drakan's key-and-clock puzzles, a running Blood Hunt chase through the woods, defends Another Night at the Theatre, then fights a multi-phase final battle culminating in Wyrd and then Drakan himself across three phases before the sun rises.

summary: Short quest uncovering a traitor among Corsair Cove's NPCs, ending in a fight against Ithoi the Navigator.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Short quest uncovering which of four suspects on Corsair Cove is the traitor behind a string of curses.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Final instanced fight is against Ithoi the Navigator (level 35); Protect from Magic negates his damage.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Recommended 20+ Combat plus weapon, armour, and food for the final fight.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts with Captain Tock at the crossroads north of Port Sarim (3030,3273).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Length: Short.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Speaking to Ithoi the Navigator first on arrival at Corsair Cove is required or the other NPC dialogues break.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks access to Yusuf's bank in Corsair Cove, the ability to dock a player-owned boat at Corsair Cove Port, and the Corsair Traitor as a fightable Nightmare Zone target.

notes: Talk to Captain Tock at the crossroads north of Port Sarim, agree to help, then meet him again on the single dock west of Rimmington to sail to Corsair Cove. On arrival speak to Ithoi the Navigator first (west along the beach, upstairs) or the other NPC dialogues break. Talk to Arsen the Thief and Cabin Boy Colin (same upstairs room, first house west of docks) and Gnocci the Cook (upstairs NW building, grab a tinderbox and a spade nearby if needed) about their 'curses'. Get the ogre artefact from Captain Tock, return it to Chief Tess in the Corsair Cove Dungeon. Dig a sand lump near the tropical tree by the fishing spot, use the telescope on Ithoi's hut deck, then report findings back to Gnocci, Arsen, and Colin. Question the same four NPCs again about who's the real traitor, culminating in confronting Ithoi twice, torching driftwood under his hut with your tinderbox to catch him not-sick, and reporting to Captain Tock. Enter the instanced fight and kill Ithoi the Navigator (level 35, protect from magic negates his damage), then report the kill to Captain Tock to finish. Recommended 20+ Combat plus weapon/armour/food for the fight.

notes: Master-tier Mahjarrat-lore quest continuing the vampyre/Arrav storyline. Elias White helps you find a way to free Arrav from Zemouregal's necromantic control: raid the Uzer Mastaba tomb (lever/trap puzzle, golem guard, coloured-tile crossing) for heart-preservation knowledge, sneak through Trollweiss Mountain tunnels into Zemouregal's old fort and briefly meet a still-captive Arrav, then infiltrate Zemouregal's Base — insulated boots past energy surges, a 4-digit vault code puzzle, and a crossbow-grapple fire-trap crossing — to steal Arrav's heart, before fighting a mind-controlled Arrav while dodging venom pools left by armoured zombies.

start.location: Exam Centre, south of the Digsite (3361,3340)

summary: Medium quest solving three student exams and a chemical-compound puzzle to recover the Digsite's ancient talisman.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Medium quest at the Digsite: pass three student exams and mix a chemical compound to breach a sealed chamber and recover a stone tablet.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: An unidentified liquid used in the chemical puzzle deals 25 damage and breaks its vial if dropped.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: The sealed chamber contains level-22 skeletons.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts with an examiner at the Exam Centre, south of the Digsite (3361,3340).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Length: Medium.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Exams can be answered correctly (no failure) once the right dialogue options are known, but students must be revisited between exam attempts, and the female student demands an opal before the second exam.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Choosing a museum-curator reward (Fruit blast or chocolate cake) is lost if dialogue is exited after the curator takes the certificate but before a choice is made.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Rewards include 2 gold bars, a choice of Fruit blast (needed for Recipe for Disaster: Another Cook's Quest) or chocolate cake (needed for Desert Treasure I), access to the Varrock Museum specimen-cleaning minigame for a Digsite pendant, and a viewable ancient talisman in the Digsite Exhibit.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Reward XP: 15,300 Mining, 2,000 Herblore.

notes: Talk to an examiner to attempt (and auto-fail) a first exam, then get a sealed letter from Curator Haig Halen at Varrock Museum. North of the Exam Centre near the blue urn hilltop, search bushes for a teddy bear, take a panning tray, use a cup of tea on the panning guide, pan for a Special cup (and an opal if needed), and pickpocket the nearby workman for an animal skull and specimen brush. Deliver the teddy/cup/skull to the three named students around the dig (NW, E, S respectively, talk twice each). Retake all 3 exams at the Exam Centre picking the correct dialogue options (fail is not possible once you know the answers) to get Level 1/2/3 certificates, re-visiting the students between exams (the female student demands an opal for the 2nd). Save the certificates for the museum curator. Back at the Exam Centre's western room, get a rock pick and specimen jar from cupboards, use the trowel on soil in a level-3 dig site until you find an ancient talisman (can take a while), then talk to Terry Balando and use his invitation on any digsite workman except the one by the western winch. Rope down the western winch to grab arcenia root and search bricks, then rope down the northern winch to talk to Doug Deeping about the rocks. South of the site, unlock a chest for chemical powder, get an unidentified liquid from a barrel (careful — it deals 25 damage and breaks the vial if dropped), grind charcoal, then give the powder/liquid to Terry Balando and mix nitroglycerin+ammonium nitrate, then charcoal, then arcenia root into a chemical compound. Use the compound then a tinderbox on the bricks down the western winch, enter the chamber (watch for level-22 skeletons), grab the stone tablet, and hand it to Terry Balando to finish.

summary: Medium hot-and-cold treasure hunt across ~11 sites using the enchanted key from Making History.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Medium hot-and-cold treasure hunt across roughly 11 fixed sites Gielinor-wide, using the enchanted key received from Making History.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: 'Feel Enchanted key' gives an absolute distance band (freezing through steaming, based on Chebyshev distance ignoring obstacles) plus a relative warmer/colder/same hint; digging happens once the key reads 'steaming' (within 4 squares).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Length: Medium.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Not gated by combat.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: 3 of the sites hold a unique god mjolnir/spear (Saradomin, Guthix, or Zamorak) obtainable no other way besides trading; the remaining sites give a spread of ores, runes, and ammo.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The enchanted key is re-buyable for 500 coins from the silver merchant in East Ardougne if lost.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Reward XP not stated on the held page (??).

notes: Hot-and-cold treasure hunt across ~11 fixed sites Gielinor-wide (map pins include areas near Rellekka, Ardougne, Tree Gnome Stronghold, Falador, Varrock, Mudskipper Point, Lumbridge Swamp, Al Kharid). Use 'Feel Enchanted key' to get a two-part temperature reading: an absolute distance band (freezing/very cold/cold/warm/hot/very hot/burning hot/steaming, based on Chebyshev distance ignoring obstacles) plus a relative warmer/colder/same-as-last-reading hint; walk in a direction, compare readings, and triangulate N/S then E/W until 'the key is steaming' (within 4 squares), then dig with a spade. 3 of the sites hold a unique god mjolnir/spear (Saradomin/Guthix/Zamorak) obtainable no other way besides trading; the rest give a spread of ores/runes/ammo. Not gated by combat.

start.location: east of the Gem Trader, northern Al Kharid

summary: Medium Thieving quest infiltrating Pollnivneach's gangs, ending in fights against a bandit champion and tough guy.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Medium Thieving quest infiltrating Pollnivneach's bandit and Menaphite thug gangs to identify a traitor for Ali the Operator.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Must be able to kill a level 70 bandit champion and a level 75 tough guy, both safespottable.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Includes a disguise-and-gloves mayor's-house infiltration with a 1-1-2-3-5-8 dial safe, and a poison-brewing traitor-identification sequence (kebab sauce on a trough, snake charm/basket, desert snake, poison spike).

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts with Ali Morrisane, east of the Gem Trader in northern Al Kharid.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Length: Medium.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Rewards include 500 coins, an oak blackjack, a desert disguise, a willow blackjack (tough guy drop), an adamant scimitar (bandit champion drop), access to the Rogue Trader miniquest, and the ability to pickpocket villagers, bandits, and Menaphite thugs in Pollnivneach.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Reward XP: 15,000 Thieving.

notes: Must be able to kill a level 70 bandit champion and a level 75 tough guy (both safespottable). Buy a Kharidian headpiece and fake beard from Ali Morrisane and combine them into a desert disguise, then travel to Pollnivneach. Use 3 beers on Drunken Ali one at a time (talk through full dialogue between each) to get him to talk, then question bandits and Menaphite thugs. Buy two camels from Ali the Camel Man (500gp) and deliver receipts to a Menaphite and a bandit. Join Ali the Operator's gang, then complete three thieving tasks for him: pickpocket a villager (with street-urchin advice and later a blackjack knockout), infiltrate the mayor's house (disguise+gloves, sneak past guards, crack a 1-1-2-3-5-8 dial safe for jewels), and identify a traitor (buy special kebab sauce, use on a trough to check dung colour, get a snake charm/basket via the snake charmer's money pot, catch a desert snake, trade snake basket+dung to Ali the Hag for poison, spike a beer with it). Finally fight and kill the tough guy and bandit champion (each drops a blackjack tier or the scimitar), then report back to the mayor and to Ali Morrisane to finish.

Infiltrate the Twilight Emissary cult undercover to steal a scroll (guarded by a hard-hitting Enforcer, lockpick puzzle), help Queen Zyanyi capture and interrogate cult leader Forebearer Janus in a safehouse heist, travel to Cam Torum to expose a bribed dwarf official (drink-barrel puzzle) and race the Tullus twins to an Old One keystone in Neypotzli (fight Lucius+Chimalli), defend the Crypt of Tonali through six cultist waves, defeat Ennius Tullus in a two-phase enrage boss fight, then solve chamber puzzles in the Tonali Cavern en route to Mokhaiotl.

summary: Medium quest with four trials (Draugen hunt, Koschei fight, Sigmund/Swensen chase, Peer riddle) gating Fremennik Isles sea access.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Medium quest comprising four trials for the Fremennik chieftaincy, gating sea access to the Fremennik Isles.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Requires beating level-69 Draugen and an unarmed, unarmoured fight against the first three forms of Koschei the deathless; the fourth form is safe to 'die' to, even on HCIM.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Thorvald's trial requires banking everything (no weapons, armour, or runes) and crafting a dramen or celastrus staff underground before the Koschei fight.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Peer the Seer's trial requires banking all bankable items, solving a riddle (each line yields one letter of a 4-letter word), then a multi-step item-transformation puzzle to unlock a trapdoor exit.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The Sigmund/Swensen trial is a long NPC-chase-and-recall-order chain, ending with paying Askeladden 5,000 coins then repeating the NPC list in reverse order.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts with Brundt the Chieftain in the Rellekka longhall (2658,3668).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Length: Medium.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Rewards include boat access to Miscellania, Etceteria, Neitiznot, Jatizso, and Rellekka facilities; wearable Fremennik blade plus warrior/berserker/archer/farseer helms; Fossegrimen's lyre enchant; free Jarvald travel to Waterbirth Island; and a Fremennik clan name.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Reward XP: roughly 2,812 in each of Agility, Attack, Crafting, Defence, Fishing, Fletching, Hitpoints, Strength, Thieving, and Woodcutting.

notes: Requires beating level-69 Draugen and an unarmed/unarmoured fight vs Koschei the deathless (first 3 forms; 4th form is safe to "die" to, even on HCIM). Order: lose a drinking contest to Manni, get Hunters' Talisman from Sigli, talk Olaf, obtain a lyre (craft from swaying-tree branch via Lalli's stew quest-within-quest, or farm Lanzig's 1/17 drop). Spin golden wool in Seers' Village if crafting the lyre, buy low-alcohol keg from Poison Salesman. Back in Rellekka: use beer on council workman for strange object, enchant lyre with raw fish at a strange altar near the bridge, hunt the Draugen via talisman, light strange object and use on longhall pipe, play enchanted lyre on stage, then win the rematch drinking contest vs Manni with the low-alcohol keg swap. Sigmund/Swensen trial is a long NPC-chase-and-recall-order chain culminating in paying Askeladden 5,000 coins then repeating the NPC list in reverse order. Thorvald trial: bank everything (no weapons/armour/runes allowed), craft a dramen/celastrus staff underground, fight Koschei's first 3 forms unarmed. Peer the Seer trial: bank all bankable items, solve a riddle (each line gives one letter of a 4-letter word) then a multi-step item-transformation puzzle (bucket/jug/vase/disks/red herring) to unlock a trapdoor exit. Finish by reporting to Brundt. High spine value: gates Fremennik Isles (Neitiznot/Jatizso/Miscellania/Etceteria) sea access.

name: The Frozen Door

unlocks: Access to the Ancient Prison section of the God Wars Dungeon, including Nex

summary: Short miniquest collecting frozen key pieces from the four God Wars Dungeon generals to unlock the Ancient Prison.

facts.label: overview

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Requires one kill each of Kree'arra (armadyl, level 580), General Graardor (bandos, level 624), Commander Zilyana (saradomin, level 596), and K'ril Tsutsaroth (zamorak, level 650); their bodyguards/lieutenants are listed as acceptable alternative kill-credit sources.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts automatically via a messenger NPC after completing Desert Treasure I.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Length: Short.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocks permanent access to the Ancient Prison section of the God Wars Dungeon, including Nex.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The four key pieces are combined via 'assemble' into the frozen key, then used at the Frozen Door in the dungeon's southern part, east of Armadyl's Eyrie entrance.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Reward XP not stated on the held page (??).

notes: After finishing Desert Treasure I, a messenger eventually appears and hands over a letter; reading (then destroying) it starts the miniquest. Kill each of the four God Wars Dungeon generals once to collect their frozen key piece: Kree'arra (armadyl, level 580), General Graardor (bandos, level 624), Commander Zilyana (saradomin, level 596), K'ril Tsutsaroth (zamorak, level 650) -- their respective bodyguards/lieutenants are listed as acceptable alternative kill-credit sources per the page's Details template. Combine all 4 pieces via 'assemble' into the frozen key, travel to the Frozen Door in the dungeon's southern part (east of Armadyl's Eyrie entrance), unlock it, and speak to Ashuelot Reis to finish and gain permanent access to the Ancient Prison/Nex.

He sends you to pay Sin Seer 40 coins in Seers' Village (upstairs in the spinning-wheel house), then relay word to 4 ghost scouts scattered across the map (south of Shantay Pass, south of the Gnome Stronghold entrance, between Falador and Draynor Manor, east of Tai Bwo Wannai) -- each just needs a conversation, no combat. Report back to Khazard, then enter the Goblin Cave east of the Fishing Guild carrying the severed leg, run to the north-east crack in the southern wall (a no-Prayer zone), and fight the level-160 ghost of Bouncer to 0 hp, which auto-throws the leg to finish. facts.label: overview

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: No formal quest prerequisites, but recreating the battleaxe needs Thurgo (found via The Knight's Sword, or by asking the Keldagrim librarian then Reldo in the Varrock library -- no quest completion actually required).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Gathers exquisite clothes (costume book + cloth from Vermundi), boots (stolen/telegrabbed from Dromund), and a repaired battleaxe (from Santiri's shop, socketed with 3 sapphires, repaired by Thurgo) to deliver to Blasidar's model Riki.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The Consortium company-join grind uses a hidden score (20 points per successful ore delivery, 100 needed to progress; full H.A.M. robes give Purple Pewter a +20 head start), followed by bar-delivery tasks for the director.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Do not world-hop mid-consortium-grind or delivery progress is lost.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts with the Dwarven Boatman at Underground River Kelda, the Keldagrim entrance east of Rellekka (2842,10129).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Length: Medium.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Opens the Rise of the Red Axe storyline (Keldagrim quest series) and grants membership in the chosen Keldagrim mining consortium company.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Reward XP: 2,500 each of Mining, Smithing, Crafting; 1,500 each of Magic, Thieving, Firemaking.

notes: No formal quest prerequisites, but recreating King Alvis's battleaxe needs Thurgo (Mudskipper Point smith) which can be found either via The Knight's Sword or by asking the Keldagrim librarian then Reldo in Varrock library (no quest completion actually required). Introduces Keldagrim city. Flow: talk Dwarven Boatman, then Commander Veldaban, then Blasidar the sculptor (east Keldagrim) who needs exquisite clothes+boots+a repaired dwarven battleaxe for a statue. Clothes: get a costume book from a bookcase in the library (need <30kg weight to climb), buy coal/logs-spun cloth from Vermundi for 200gp. Boots: steal the left boot from Dromund when he's not looking, telegrab the right boot through his window. Axe: get it from Santiri's weapon shop, socket 3 sapphires, take to Thurgo at Mudskipper Point with an iron bar to repair (may need a redberry pie bribe). Deliver all 3 items to Riki (Blasidar's model) then talk Blasidar for a cutscene. Join the Consortium: pick one company (not Red Axe) upstairs in the market, run ore-delivery fetch-tasks for their secretary (hidden score, 20 points per success, need 100 to progress, wearing full H.A.M. robes gives Purple Pewter a +20 head start), then bar-delivery tasks for the director, then formally join the company and report back to Commander Veldaban to finish. Do not world-hop mid-consortium-grind or progress is lost.

notes: Short desert quest. Read a letter near the broken golem, repair it with 4 soft clay, ask about the portal. Grab a black mushroom (grind for black dye) and a strange implement from the ruins basement (both reusable for Shadow of the Storm if doing that soon after). Talk to Elissa at the Digsite, read Varmen's notes in the Exam Centre bookcase, then talk to Curator Haig Halen at Varrock Museum about the Uzer statuette, pickpocket him for the display cabinet key, and take the statuette from an upstairs case. Return to Uzer, place the statuette in an empty alcove, rotate 4 statues to all face north to open the portal north; optionally chisel the throne for 2 each of sapphire/emerald/ruby. Make a phoenix quill pen (feather + black dye) and write the golem program on papyrus, then use the strange implement on the golem followed immediately by the program to finish. Region-opener with no known deeper dependency; sets up the desert quest line (Uzer).

notes: Intermediate quest requiring defeat of a level 172 Black demon (safespottable). Talk to King Shareen for a translation book and bark sample, deliver the sample to Hazelmere on the island east of Yanille (fairy ring CLS) for a message. Report back and tell the king about the man with the King's seal and the Daconia rocks; talk to imprisoned Charlie upstairs, search Glough's house cupboard for his journal, confront Glough (he arrests you), get the shipyard password 'Ka-Lu-Min' from Charlie, and escape via the gnome glider. On Karamja: run past level 53 jogres to the Ship Yard, give the password, get a lumber order from the Foreman (fight him or answer his security questions), and deliver the order to the king. Talk to Charlie again, get a key from Anita north-west of the tree, take invasion plans from Glough's chest, deliver them for 4 sticks, spell TUZO on the watchtower pillars in Glough's house, then descend to fight a level 172 Black demon (lure it against the daconia rocks north of the ladder for a safespot). Find the last daconia rock by searching roots, then finish by talking to the king. Required for: Monkey Madness I, The Eyes of Glouphrie.

summary: Medium quest chain finale fighting the Barrelchest, whose attacks disable Prayer.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Medium finale to a long flattened prerequisite chain (Cook's Assistant to RFD subquests, Priest in Peril to Rum Deal, Big Chompy Bird Hunting plus Jungle Potion/Druidic Ritual to Zogre Flesh Eaters, converging into Cabin Fever and Creature of Fenkenstrain).

facts.value: Instanced final fight is against the Barrelchest (level 190); each of his attacks disables Prayer, so re-enabling Prayer right after each hit avoids damage, or the fight can be safespotted from the monastery entrance by leaving and re-entering.

facts.value: Requires killing 4 Sorebones (level 57) inside the monastery for a cranial clamp, brain tongs, 3 bell jars, and 30 skull staples.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Requires access to a POH workshop with a crafting table, or the Grand Exchange, to obtain wooden cats; and the ability to kill a level-190 monster that disables Prayer (safespottable).

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts with Brother Tranquility, near the dock on Mos Le'Harmless (3682,2963).

facts.label: length
facts.value: Length: Medium.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Building the false-bottomed crate for Rufus requires 10 wooden cats and roughly 38 nails minimum (bronze/iron nails may bend, so more may be needed).

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Rewards include a blessed lamp (5,000 xp in a chosen skill 30+, bankable), a repairable Barrelchest anchor (230,000 coins from 'Smith' at Mos Le'Harmless docks with a book o' piracy in inventory), the Prayer book, the Harmony Island Teleport spell (Arceuus spellbook), and the Barrelchest as a Nightmare Zone target.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Reward XP: 6,000 Prayer, 3,000 Crafting, 2,000 Construction.

notes: Long quest chain flattened for prereqs: Cook's Assistant -> Recipe for Disaster subquests Freeing Pirate Pete + Another Cook's Quest; Priest in Peril -> Rum Deal; Big Chompy Bird Hunting + (Jungle Potion -> Druidic Ritual) -> Zogre Flesh Eaters; Rum Deal + Zogre Flesh Eaters -> Cabin Fever; Creature of Fenkenstrain + Cabin Fever are the direct gates. Walkthrough: talk to Brother Tranquility on Mos Le'Harmless (no pets), head north into the windmill, pull the statue and climb down, don underwater gear and dive, repair stairs, climb a ladder, peer through a peephole, then return to Brother Tranquility to be shuttled back and forth to Harmony Island. At Edgeville Monastery search the SW bookcase on the ground floor for a book, read it, then equip a Holy symbol back on Harmony Island and recite the prayer inside the windmill. At Dr Fenkenstrain's jail (NE of Canifis, via east/west stairs then a ladder) equip a Ring of charos and talk to him; talk to Rufus in Canifis food store repeatedly for a wolf whistle + crate part; make/buy 10 wooden cats, build a crate with a false bottom next to the doctor, fill it with cats, blow the whistle to summon Rufus, and give him the shipping order on the crate. Back at Harmony Island, dive to the shipwreck for a fuse, tinderbox, and keg; use the keg+fuse on the nearby monastery door and light it; inside, kill 4 Sorebones (level 57) for a cranial clamp, brain tongs, 3 bell jars, and 30 skull staples; deliver these to Fenkenstrain and talk to Brother Tranquility to prep the final fight. Instanced fight: confront Mi-Gor to trigger the Barrelchest (level 190) battle — each of his attacks disables Prayer, re-enabling it right after each hit avoids damage, or safespot from the monastery entrance by leaving and re-entering. Report the kill to Brother Tranquility to finish.

summary: Help Bert recover his lost magical past by tracking down Sandy in Brimhaven and Zavistic Rarve at the Wizards' Guild.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Help Bert recover a lost magical past by investigating his former colleague Sandy in Brimhaven and consulting wizard Zavistic Rarve.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Requires mixing bottled water with redberries then white berries, in that order, to brew pink dye for a rose-tinted lens used to make a truth serum.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Progressing at the Wizards' Guild requires ringing the entrance bell multiple times across the quest, each time triggering a new item or scene from Zavistic Rarve.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The final item, a wizard's head, comes from Mazion at the Entrana sandpit near the end of the quest.

refs.title: The Hand in the Sand/Quick guide

notes: Talk to Bert in Yanille (house next to the POH portal) to get a sandy hand. Buy/bring a beer and give it to the Guard Captain at the Dragon Inn pub south of Bert's house. Ring the bell outside the Wizards' Guild (or enter with 66 Magic) and give the hand to Zavistic Rarve. Get Bert's rota from Bert, travel to Brimhaven, find Sandy south of the Shrimp and Parrot restaurant, search his desk for Sandy's rota, and pickpocket him for sand. Back in Yanille, get a magic scroll from Bert, ring the bell for a magical orb from Zavistic Rarve, who then offers to teleport you to Port Sarim. At Betty's magic shop get bottled water, mix in redberries then white berries (order matters) for pink dye, dye the lantern lens for a rose-tinted lens, then use the lens on the counter from the doorway for a truth serum, and hand over sand. In Brimhaven, distract Sandy with dialogue and use the truth serum on his coffee mug, then activate the magical orb and click through every dialogue option with him. Back in Yanille, ring the bell twice more at the Wizards' Guild (getting a cutscene on the second), sail to Entrana and talk to Mazion at the sandpit for a wizard's head, then return to Yanille and ring the bell/talk to Zavistic Rarve one last time to finish.

summary: Investigate an attack on Prince Itzla at the Teomat through trials at the Tower of Ascension, then pursue Twilight Emissaries into an ice dungeon.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Investigate an attack on Prince Itzla at the Teomat by completing trials at the Tower of Ascension, then pursue Twilight Emissaries into an ice dungeon beneath the ruins.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The First Trial requires pickpocketing a caged Emissary Ascended for a key, then solving a per-player word-decode puzzle from looted books to derive a passcode spoken to Forebearer Janus.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: The Second Trial is a wave-based combat encounter requiring the player to fight off melee and mage waves while healing Prince Itzla with bandages from medical boxes.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The Third Trial requires questioning every cultist and accusing whichever one mentions 'Mictl', a belief not part of standard Emissary teachings.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: The Final Trial is a fight against Prince Itzla himself, who is immune to special attacks; re-enable Protect from Melee immediately after his sword-swipe special and move behind him when he telegraphs an AOE special.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The Statue Puzzle requires collecting 3 coloured icons from urns guarded by continuously-spawning level-104 Frost Nagua, then touching statues in a player-specific order derived from corner murals.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: The final boss is Amoxliatl (level 263), fought while praying magic, avoiding ice pools and spikes, and meleeing the healing ice blocks she summons.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: The Ice Dungeon beneath the ruins requires pulling 4 levers while dodging Frost Nagua, frost crabs, and traps that each deal 10 damage.

notes: Talk to Prince Itzla Arkan at the Teomat, then again after the cutscene. Travel to Quetzacalli Gorge, rent the pub's basement room from the bartender for 30 coins, rest on the bed in the SE room, talk to the Prince, then ask the general store shopkeeper for help and return to talk to the Prince again in the room. Head north through the gate to the Tower of Ascension, talk to the Prince outside, chat with recruits Nova/Sergius/Felius/Caritta, talk to the Prince again (no pets following), then enter the tower and talk to Forebearer Janus to climb up. First Trial: pickpocket a caged Emissary Ascended for a tower key, loot two chests for a book/paper via a word-decode puzzle unique per player, read a resulting poem, and speak the derived passcode to Forebearer Janus on the same floor (don't go downstairs mid-trial or it resets). Second Trial: a wave-based combat encounter where you must heal Prince Itzla with bandages from medical boxes while fighting melee/mage waves. Third Trial: question every cultist and accuse whichever one mentions 'Mictl' (heretical, not part of Emissary beliefs). Final Trial: fight Prince Itzla himself (level ~unlisted) — pray melee against basics, re-enable prayer immediately after his sword-swipe special (which disables it), and run behind him when he telegraphs his AOE special; he's immune to special attacks (unequip a Scythe of Vitur and punch/kick instead). Loot Twilight Emissary robes from a chest, equip them, bring the Prince through the temple east, then descend into the ruins south into an Ice Dungeon: mine through rocks, pull 4 levers scattered through icy rooms while dodging Frost Nagua/frost crabs/traps (each trap deals 10 damage), then pull a chain for future shortcut access. Statue Puzzle: collect 3 coloured icons from urns (level-104 Frost Nagua spawn continuously, Protect from Magic negates their hits), fix broken statues with the icons, then read corner murals to learn a player-specific activation order (Zema/Oma/Yeya/Naui = 1-4) and touch statues in that order to open the frozen door. Final Boss: fight Amoxliatl (level 263) praying magic, avoiding ice pools/spikes, re-enabling Protect from Magic after it's disabled, and meleeing the healing ice blocks she summons (or ignoring them at high level and just maging her). Finish by speaking to Servius, Teokan of Ralos, at the Sunrise Palace.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Help Cassius investigate why Seth and Gillie Groats' cows are unusually productive on their farm near Lumbridge.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Progress requires delivering milk samples in turn to Cassius, Duke Horacio, and Gillie Groats before being granted a permit to access the bull's pen.

facts.label: kills

notes: Talk to Cassius by the Lumbridge pond to start. Talk to Gillie Groats then Seth Groats about their productive cows, search the shelves in Seth's farmhouse for 'The Groats principles' book, and bring it to Cassius for a milk sample. Drink the sample in front of Cassius to get a second sample for Duke Horacio; deliver it, then talk to Gillie Groats and drink the sample in front of her for a permit to deal with the bull. Open the pen in the NE corner of the cow farm and fight the bull (recommended 15+ Combat), then report back to Gillie Groats and Cassius, and finally talk to Gillie Groats again to collect the rewards.

summary: Help the Squire recover the White Knights' lost ceremonial sword by finding smith Thurgo and mining replacement blurite ore.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Help the Squire recover the White Knights' lost ceremonial sword by tracking down the Imcando smith Thurgo and mining replacement blurite ore.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Requires searching Sir Vyvin's bedroom cupboard for a portrait while he isn't watching, then giving it and iron bars to Thurgo so he agrees to reforge the sword.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Blurite ore is mined inside the Asgarnian Ice Dungeon, reached via a trapdoor east of Thurgo.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Talking to Reldo about the Imcando dwarves can be skipped if Thurgo was already met through The Giant Dwarf.

notes: Talk to the Squire in the White Knights' Castle courtyard about his lost ceremonial sword. Talk to Reldo in the Varrock Palace library about the Imcando dwarves (skip if Thurgo was already met via The Giant Dwarf). Find Thurgo south of Port Sarim near the anvil (north of fairy ring AIQ) and give him a redberry pie, talk to him again, then return to the Squire. Climb to the White Knights' Castle 1st floor, go west and up the east staircase, and search the cupboard in Sir Vyvin's bedroom for a portrait while he isn't looking. Bring the portrait (and iron bars) to Thurgo. Enter the Asgarnian Ice Dungeon via the trapdoor east of Thurgo, follow the path west/north/east, and mine blurite ore along the southern wall of the icy area (mine a second if doing the Falador easy diary). Give Thurgo the iron bars and blurite ore to forge the replacement sword, then deliver the sword to the Squire to finish.

summary: Help Duke Horacio investigate goblin activity beneath Lumbridge Castle and reconnect with the goblin Mistag.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Help Duke Horacio investigate goblin activity in the cellar caves beneath Lumbridge Castle and reconnect with the goblin Mistag.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Requires searching the Varrock Palace library's northern bookcase for a goblin symbol book, then mediating between generals at Goblin Village to learn the Goblin Bow and Goblin Salute emotes.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Travel through the Lumbridge cellar caves toward Mistag can be auto-pathed by clicking the minimap.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Pickpocketing Sigmund and looting his chest yields HAM robes worth keeping for the separate quest Death to the Dorgeshuun.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Straying off-route near swamp gas underground can extinguish a light source and risks an explosion.

notes: Talk to Sigmund on Lumbridge Castle's 1st floor to start. Ask around (Duke Horacio, the Cook, Hans, Father Aereck, Bob, Gee/Donie) about the collapsed cellar wall until someone mentions a goblin sighting, then report back to the Duke. Climb down the kitchen trapdoor, mine through rubble in the basement, light your light source, and grab a brooch about 10 steps into the cave; show it to the Duke. In Varrock Palace library, talk to Reldo and search the northern bookcase on the west wall for a goblin symbol book; read it. At Goblin Village, talk to the generals and mediate between them until you learn the Goblin Bow and Goblin Salute emotes. Return to the Lumbridge cellar caves and follow the mapped path (clicking the minimap auto-paths it) to reach Mistag; perform the Goblin Bow for him, then let him guide you back to Lumbridge. Report to the Duke (he won't believe you), then pickpocket Sigmund and unlock his chest in the other room for HAM robes (keep for Death to the Dorgeshuun). Head to the HAM lair west of Lumbridge Castle, pick the trapdoor lock, enter, and search a crate south of the ladder for stolen silverware. Return it to the Duke, who fires Sigmund and hands you a signed peace treaty. Go back to the cellar, follow Kazgar down to Mistag, and give him the treaty to trigger the finishing cutscene.

Expose King Bolren's new pet 'Dumpling' as another evil creature via Yewnock's value-matching disc machine, found through a monolith-pushing puzzle in the Tree Gnome Village Dungeon and Bolrie's diary cutscenes recounting gnome political history. Track down explorer Longramble in the Galarpos Mountains and heal the corrupted spirit tree Incomitatus with a crystal chime (unlocking it as a teleport), then race through the Warped Depths dungeon past aggressive Warped Terrorbirds and kill three of them before Hazelmere teleports you to safety from a tar-and-gas trap. Required for: While Guthix Sleeps.

notes: Intermediate quest investigating Port Piscarilius's uniting gang, the Saviours of Kourend. Bribe an informant with cooked stew to learn their hideout in The Warrens, fake-kill a rival gang leader to prove loyalty, then confront the titular Queen of Thieves — revealed to be Lady Shauna Piscarilius, using the gang to target the corrupt rather than terrorise the poor — and burgle a Council member's house for evidence tying him to the Disciples of Yama. summary: Infiltrate the Desert Mining Camp in disguise for Irena to rescue the trapped worker Ana.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Infiltrate the Desert Mining Camp in disguise for Irena to rescue the trapped worker Ana.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Requires goading the Mercenary Captain into attacking for a gate key, then trading with a slave for disguise clothes to enter the mine undetected.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Entering without full waterskins risks heat damage or jail; equipping weapons/armour, removing the disguise, or leaving through the gate or mine door while still in disguise all risk being jailed.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Smithing a bronze dart tip and fletching it with 10 feathers produces a prototype dart traded to Al Shabim for the Tenti pineapple needed to bait a mine guard.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: If the mine cart cutscene doesn't trigger when searched, it must be redone; teleporting while carrying a captive in a barrel drops the barrel instead.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for starting Desert Treasure I.

notes: Talk to Irena south of Shantay Pass to start. At the Desert Mining Camp entrance, goad the Mercenary Captain (level 47, safespottable/cannonable) into attacking via dialogue and kill him for the gate key — do not skip the dialogue or enter without full waterskins, both can get you jailed or killed by heat damage. Unequip all weapons/armour before entering the gate (graceful is fine if not worn over the required desert clothing) — equipping gear or removing the disguise inside gets you jailed. Trade with the male slave on the east wall for slave clothes when no guard is watching (getting caught = jail; escape by bending the window bars, climbing out over the wall/cliff, and re-entering the gate), equip the clothes, and enter the mine north. Follow the path to a guarded cave entrance and talk to a guard until he mentions a Tenti pineapple, then leave the mine, fully unequip the slave robes, and exit camp via the front gate (or fairy ring BIQ) — leaving in disguise through either the gate or the mine door also risks jail. At Bedabin Camp (west of the mining camp) talk to Al Shabim in the south tent for a Bedabin key. Back in the mining camp (slave outfit on again), climb to the jail building's upper floor, search the southernmost west-wall bookcase for a sailing-books clue, then talk to Captain Siad in the same room and open a chest for technical plans. Show the plans to Al Shabim, then use the northern tent's anvil to smith a bronze dart tip from a bar and fletch it with 10 feathers into a prototype dart; trade it to Al Shabim for the Tenti pineapple. Re-suit in slave clothes, give the pineapple to a mine guard, head east past a mine cart, search barrels for a spare barrel, then search the mine cart to trigger a cutscene ride (redo if it doesn't trigger). Do not talk to Ana — instead follow the west-north-west path and use the spare barrel on her (don't try to teleport with her in your inventory, it drops the barrel). Ride the cart back, put Ana in it then get in yourself, search barrels to find her again, place her on the north-west winch bucket, decline the guard's suspicion-raising offer of help, then surface, operate the winch (south-west of camp), search the barrel west of the winch for Ana, load her onto the wooden cart at camp centre, and talk your way past the mine cart driver. Board the cart and return to Irena for the reward. Downstream note: required for starting Desert Treasure I.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Help King Bolren defend the Tree Gnome Village from the Khazard Stronghold by disabling its ballista defences and recovering three orbs of protection.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Requires gathering a 4-digit ballista code from riddles told by 3 tracker gnomes around the Khazard Stronghold, then firing the south-west ballista with that code.

facts.label: kills
facts.value: Level 48 Khazard commanders inside the stronghold can be ignored or killed while retrieving an orb of protection from an upstairs chest.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Defeat the level 112 Khazard warlord (safespottable near the West Ardougne wall) for the other two orbs.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Intermediate quest.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for: Monkey Madness I, The Path of Glouphrie, Medium Western Provinces Diary, Medium Varrock Diary.

notes: Intermediate quest requiring defeat of a level 112 Khazard warlord (safespottable). Talk to King Bolren (teleports you to the north-west corner of the maze), give Commander Montai 6 logs on the battlefield's south-west edge, then agree to help further. Talk to all 3 tracker gnomes around the Khazard Stronghold to get 4-digit ballista coordinates from riddles, then fire the south-west ballista with that code. Climb into the stronghold over the crumbled wall, ignore or kill the level 48 Khazard commanders, take an orb of protection from the upstairs chest, exit, and report to Elkoy to be sent to the village centre. Track down and kill the level 112 Khazard warlord north-west of the battlefield (safespottable near the West Ardougne wall) for the other two orbs, then return all 3 orbs to King Bolren to finish. Required for: Monkey Madness I, The Path of Glouphrie, Medium Western Provinces Diary, Medium Varrock Diary.

name: Troll Stronghold

items: Climbing boots or 12 coins to buy them

req_items.note: or 12 coins to buy them from Tenzing along the route

unlocks: Law talisman (rebuyable from Dunstan for 1,000gp, or obtainable via Guardians of the Rift)
Ability to make law runes
Access to Trollheim, the Troll Stronghold, and the Law Altar
Access to the God Wars Dungeon
Dad fightable in the Nightmare Zone

summary: Help Denulth fight into the Troll Stronghold to rescue prisoners Twig and Berry from the trolls.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Help Denulth fight into the Troll Stronghold near Trollheim to rescue the imprisoned Twig and Berry.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Fight Dad in the arena (safespottable from the gate); the fight ends automatically before he dies.

facts.label: kills
facts.value: Kill a Troll General inside the stronghold (safespottable) for a prison key.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Freeing prisoners Twig and Berry can be done by pickpocketing (30+ Thieving) or by killing them while they sleep.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Thrower trolls on the path must be passed by sprinting, using Protect from Missiles, or taking agility shortcuts.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Intermediate quest.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for: Desert Treasure I, Devious Minds, Eadgar's Ruse, Monkey Madness II, Troll Romance.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: 15 Agility plus defeating Dad also gates access to the God Wars Dungeon (without Ghommal's hilt).

notes: Intermediate quest requiring defeat of a level 113 Troll General (safespottable). Talk to Denulth south of the Burthorpe teleport, follow the path to the stronghold buying climbing boots from Tenzing along the way, then fight Dad in the arena (safespottable from the gate) -- the fight ends automatically before he dies. Exit north, get past Thrower trolls (sprint, protect from missiles, or agility shortcuts), and enter the stronghold. Inside: kill a Troll General for a prison key (safespottable), pickpocket (30+ Thieving) or kill sleeping Twig and Berry to free both prisoners, escape via games necklace or the secret exit, and talk to Dunstan to finish. Required for: Desert Treasure I, Devious Minds, Eadgar's Ruse, Monkey Madness II, Troll Romance; 15 Agility plus defeating Dad also gates God Wars Dungeon entry (without Ghommal's hilt).

name: Troubled Tortugans

unlocks: Access to the Great Conch and its facilities (fairy ring code CJQ, notice board)
Ability to fight gryphons

start.location: Remote Island in the Unquiet Ocean, NW of the Great Conch, far south of the Pandemonium (2958,2606)

summary: Rescue the tortugan Floopa, repair storm damage at the Great Conch, and hunt down a threatening gryphon.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Rescue and bandage the injured tortugan Floopa, then help the Great Conch's elders repair storm damage and investigate a gryphon threat.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Fight the level-235 Shellbane Gryphon at The Little Pearl using Protect from Melee/Ranged; its charge knockback (up to 65 damage) is blocked by standing near a tree or eggs, and whirlwinds require stepping off warned tiles.

facts.label: kills
facts.value: Kill a level-95 gryphon found by tracking footprints from the market's central monument through a cave cleared of roots with an axe.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Repairing six structures around the Great Conch requires gathering a saw, hammer, 6 tortugan scutes, 10 jatoba logs chopped from jatoba trees, and 6 sea shells from the beach.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: A bandage for Floopa is made by combining seaweed with a palm leaf shaken from a palm tree.

notes: Bandage and rescue the injured tortugan Floopa (seaweed + palm leaf from a shaken palm tree = bandage); she boards your boat and directs you to the Great Conch. Speak to Elder Raley in the village for a repair list, then gather materials around town (saw, hammer, 6 tortugan scutes, 10 jatoba logs chopped from jatoba trees, 6 sea shells on the beach) and fix six structures (Elder Krill's stall + store wall, Elder Strom's store wall + crate, Elder Coco's stall + crate). Track a gryphon from footprints at the market's central monument, following the trail through inspectable objects (flowers, rockslides, plants) to a cave; clear roots with an axe and kill the level-95 gryphon inside. Elder Blunn then gives a Tortugan shield (cape slot, must stay equipped) before sailing to The Little Pearl to fight the level-235 Shellbane Gryphon — use Protect from Melee/Ranged, ranged/magic negates a mechanic, its charge knockback (up to 65 dmg) is blocked by standing near a tree/eggs, step off tiles when whirlwinds spawn on wing-flap warning. After the fight, talk to Korel about a mysterious ship before returning to Elder Raley to finish.

Quetzal Transport System

summary: Help Prince Itzla round up six Knights of Varlamore to investigate a plot against his father Servius.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Help Prince Itzla round up six Knights of Varlamore scattered around Civitas illa Fortis to investigate a plot against his father Servius.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The six knights are found via a pickpocket hunt in the bazaar, a smuggling-crate search on the docks, a sparring fight against knight Mezan, and reuniting drunk Azali with her partner.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Fight off 8 cultists at the Teomat after a forged letter gets an innocent knight killed by Ennius Tullus.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Servius is mysteriously wounded during the cultist fight despite no cultist reaching him, foreshadowing the Twilight Emissaries' doomsday belief.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Intermediate quest.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for: Perilous Moons, The Heart of Darkness.

notes: Intermediate quest, the proper introduction to Varlamore's capital. Help Prince Itzla investigate a plot against his father Servius by rounding up six Knights of Varlamore scattered around Civitas illa Fortis — a pickpocket hunt in the bazaar, a smuggling-crate search on the docks, a sparring fight against knight Mezan, and reuniting drunk Azali with her partner. A forged letter gets an innocent knight killed by Ennius Tullus; learn of the doomsday cult Twilight Emissaries and their belief in the Final Dawn, then fight off 8 cultists at the Teomat as Servius is mysteriously wounded despite no cultist reaching him. Required for: Perilous Moons, The Heart of Darkness.

name: Underground Pass

req_items.name: Non-poisoned metal-tipped arrows
req_items.note: 1-5 recommended; used to light and shoot the bridge guide-rope

unlocks: Iban's staff + Iban Blast spell
Klank's gauntlets

summary: Cross a one-way dungeon west of Ardougne to destroy the demon Iban for King Lathas.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Cross a one-way dungeon west of Ardougne toward Tirannwn for King Lathas, destroying the demon Iban at his temple well.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Lighting an arrow with Koftik's cloth and tinderbox lets the player shoot a bridge guide-rope to cross the grid maze.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Collecting 4 light orbs to fuel a furnace requires avoiding wall traps and disarming a trap on the fourth orb.

facts.label: kills
facts.value: Requires killing 3 paladins (safespottable) for badges, 3 demons for amulets, and Kalrag the spider (safespottable, ignores Protect from Melee) along the route.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Destroy Iban by repeatedly using the Doll of Iban on his temple well while enduring his unblockable spike attacks (~8 damage each).

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: The dungeon is a one-way point of no return once entered, reversible only via a lever/rope pull; HP must stay above 15 while crossing the grid maze bridge.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Gates access to the Tirannwn/Elf lands questline infrastructure.

notes: One-way point of no return dungeon crawl west of Ardougne toward Tirannwn. Talk King Lathas then Koftik at West Ardougne cave entrance, enter dungeon (no going back except via lever/rope pull). Light an arrow via Koftik's cloth+tinderbox, shoot the bridge guide-rope, cross grid maze carefully (keep HP>15), pull lever. Collect 4 light orbs (avoid wall traps, use plank on flat rocks, disarm trap on 4th), fuel furnace, descend well. Navigate slave-cage/pipe area (50 Thieving shortcut optional). Get unicorn horn via railing-on-boulder trick, kill 3 paladins (safespottable) for badges, use badges+horn on well to open door. Meet dwarves Niloof and Klank (get gauntlets - keep inv slot free), steal Witch's cat to enter her house for loot, kill 3 demons for amulets, brew dwarf beer + tinderbox on tomb. Kill Kalrag the spider (safespottable, ignores Protect Melee), get Iban's dove wearing Klank's gauntlets. Kill a Disciple of Iban for ceremonial robes (required to enter temple), use Doll of Iban on the well in Iban's temple (repeat through his attacks, ~8dmg unblockable spikes) to destroy him. Return to King Lathas via Koftik. Gates access to Tirannwn/Elf lands questline infrastructure.

name: Vale Totems (miniquest)

Four fletched items of the same wood type as the logs, e.g. unstrung longbows or shields (obtainable during the quest)

req_items.note: obtainable during the quest by chopping nearby trees with an axe

req_items.name: Fletched items
req_items.note: same wood type as the logs, e.g. unstrung longbows or shields; obtainable during the quest

summary: Help Ranulph and Isadora rebuild and decorate a totem damaged by local ents.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Help Ranulph and Isadora rebuild a totem damaged by local ents, then decorate it to unlock the Vale Totems Fletching minigame.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Requires laying a new totem base from chopped oak-or-better logs, then selecting carving options for a randomized set of spirit animals to decorate it.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Finishing the totem requires fletching 4 items, such as bows or shields, from the same wood type as the totem and using them on it.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: No combat is required.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Reward xp is not specified in the held source ("??").

notes: Introductory miniquest gating the Vale Totems Fletching minigame. Talk to Ranulph, who shows you a totem the local ents damaged; child NPC Isadora tells you to rebuild it. Chop a nearby tree for oak-or-better logs, click the totem site to lay the new base, then return to Ranulph/Isadora. Next, click the totem and select carving options for a set of nearby spirit animals (randomized per player) to decorate it. Return again, then fletch 4 items (bows/shields) from the same wood as the totem and use them on the totem to finish decorating it. The ents leave an offering to collect, then speak to either NPC to receive Sacraments of ent folk and complete the miniquest, unlocking the minigame proper. No combat.

notes: Very short early quest, beats level-34 Count Draynor (safespottable, high healing - garlic weakens him and is strongly recommended). Talk Morgan, grab garlic from his upstairs cupboard. Travel to the Blue Moon Inn in Varrock, buy a beer, talk Dr Harlow twice for a stake, buy a hammer from the general store just north of the inn. Head to Draynor Manor basement (east stairs, not the west room), open the coffin and kill Count Draynor with combat gear (hammer/stake just need to be in inventory, not actively used). Free F2P quest, low reqs, no known deeper dependency chain.

start.location: Watchtower north of Yanille, top floor via trellis (2546,3116, plane 2)

summary: Help the Watchtower Wizard investigate ogre unrest at Gu'Tanoth by gathering relics and elemental crystals.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Help the Watchtower Wizard investigate ogre unrest at Gu'Tanoth by gathering ogre relic pieces and elemental crystals.

facts.label: kills
facts.value: Kill the level-68 Gorad (safespottable) for a tooth needed to complete an ogre relic.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Must be able to run past level-111 blue dragons near the Ogre Enclave.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Requires solving a 4-cave skavid word-repeat puzzle using a skavid map obtained with a death rune, then using a gold bar to unlock the east gate.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Brewing the magic ogre potion requires combining guam potion (unf), jangerberries, and ground bat bones, in that order.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The four elemental crystals must be returned to pillars matching their colours (NE magenta, SE grey, SW yellow, NW cyan) before pulling the final lever.

notes: Must be able to beat level-68 Gorad (safespottable) and run past level-111 blue dragons. Flow: talk to Wizard in tower, get fingernails from bush below, talk to Og NW of Yanille for Toban's key, rope across to Jangerberry island (talk Grew), enter Tunnel to Toban south of Gu'Tanoth, kill Gorad for tooth, give Toban dragon bones for relic part 3, loot chest, return relics to Wizard for ogre relic. Enter Gu'Tanoth, steal rock cake from market, pay 20 coins/talk guards to reach Skavid caves, get skavid map (death rune), solve 4-cave skavid word-repeat puzzle, gold bar unlocks east gate, second nightshade in Mad Skavid cave, use nightshade on Enclave guard to trigger cutscene. Brew magic ogre potion (guam unf + jangerberries + ground bat bones, in that order) with Wizard. Return to Enclave, potion 6 ogre shamans for crystal 3, mine Rock of Dalgroth for crystal 4, return all 4 crystals to correct pillar colors (NE magenta/SE grey/SW yellow/NW cyan), pull lever. Key spine value: unlocks Ogre Enclave blue dragons + Watchtower teleport infrastructure.

name: Waterfall Quest

unlocks: 2 diamonds
2 gold bars
40 mithril seeds

start.location: her house north-east of Baxtorian Falls

summary: Retrieve Glarial's amulet and urn from her tomb for Almera to restore the Baxtorian Falls shrine.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Retrieve Glarial's amulet and urn from her tomb for Almera to restore the Baxtorian Falls shrine.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Glarial's Tomb bans all equipped gear; reaching it requires withstanding or evading level-86 Fire Giants and a level-84 Moss Guardian unarmed.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Requires banking everything except the pebble, food, and a teleport method before entering the tomb via Glarial's Tombstone.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Finishing requires placing one of each air/water/earth rune on 3 pillars, wearing the amulet at Glarial's statue to raise the ground, then pouring the urn into the chalice.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Glarial's pebble, obtained from Golrie in the Tree Gnome Village dungeon, is worth banking for the later quest Roving Elves.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Intermediate quest.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Required for: Desert Treasure I, Legends' Quest, Medium Kandarin Diary, Roving Elves, The Path of Glouphrie.

notes: Intermediate quest requiring the ability to withstand/evade level 86 Fire Giants and a level 84 Moss Guardian with no equipment (Glarial's Tomb bans gear). Talk to Almera, raft to the island, talk to Hudon then Hadley, find a Book on Baxtorian in an upstairs bookcase and read it. Enter the Tree Gnome Village maze/dungeon (worth starting Tree Gnome Village here too), find a key in a crate, unlock the west gate, and get Glarial's pebble from Golrie (keep it banked for Roving Elves later). Bank everything except the pebble, food, and a teleport method, use the pebble on Glarial's Tombstone east of the Waterfall to enter, and retrieve Glarial's amulet and urn from the tomb. Return with rope + 6 air/water/earth runes + amulet + urn: cross the raft/rope route into the waterfall dungeon, unlock the door past the Fire Giants, place one of each rune on 3 pillars, wear the amulet at Glarial's statue to raise the ground, then pour the urn into the chalice to finish. Required for: Desert Treasure I, Legends' Quest, Medium Kandarin Diary, Roving Elves, The Path of Glouphrie.

name: While Guthix Sleeps

skills: Warriors' Guild access (Attack+Strength >=130, or 99 Attack, or 99 Strength)

unlocks: Access to the Ancient Guthixian Temple and tormented demons
Duradel's notes (via Kuradal) — one-time Slayer xp = 15x current Slayer level, plus ability to craft emberlight/scorching bow/purging staff
Loot from Movario's base: 100 death runes, 100 fire runes, 100 coal, 100 magic logs
Elite black armour and Dark Squall's robes
Access to the Black Knight Catacombs
Adoptable broav pet if kept

summary: Investigate Movario's schemes and infiltrate the Black Knights' Fortress while chasing the Stone of Jas.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Grandmaster Mahjarrat-storyline quest chasing the Stone of Jas: investigate Movario's schemes, rally old allies, and infiltrate the Black Knights' Fortress.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Infiltrating Movario's underground base requires solving a rune-symbol door puzzle, a bookcase-lever/wire maze, a trapped bed chest, and a weight/pressure-gauge puzzle.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Face Surok Magis (magic-only, 450hp, Vorkath-style attack rotation) atop the Black Knights' Fortress: bind/weaken his summoned warriors, telegrab-and-alch his explosive orbs, and counter his elemental surges with the opposite element.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The Chasm of Tears requires collecting 4 elemental orbs from braziers, solving elemental-block puzzles in skull tunnels, and matching herblore-ingredient offerings (randomized per player) to 8 of 13 statues.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Final fight is the Balance Elemental (524hp, prayer-switches by attack style, drains stats every 3-4 hits, doubles up at 25% hp), followed by two Tormented Demons alongside the Guardians of Armadyl.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The two Tormented Demons alongside the Guardians of Armadyl can be cheesed by letting the Guardians finish them off from behind a pillar.

facts.label: difficulty
facts.value: Grandmaster-tier quest.

notes: Grandmaster Mahjarrat-storyline quest, sequel to Defender of Varrock, chasing the Stone of Jas. Thaerisk sends you to kill 2 assassins, then investigate Movario at the Fight Arena: bribe/charm the Khazard Launderer for info, train a broav at Feldip Hills to sniff out Movario's hideout, then break into his underground base solving a rune-symbol door puzzle, a bookcase-lever/wire maze, a trapped bed chest, and a weight/pressure-gauge puzzle to recover two volumes of notes. Rally allies: rescue Idria from mercenaries at McGrubor's Wood, plant a teleorb on a shady stranger near Draynor for Akrisae, get a snapdragon seed from Thaerisk and enrich it with pink dye + Betty's help, plant it at Falador castle. Use NPC Contact to summon Turael/Mazchna/Cyrisus/Duradel about Lucien, harvest the enriched snapdragon for a truth serum to interrogate a captured shady stranger, then recruit Ghommal/Harrallak/Sloane at the Warriors' Guild. Infiltrate Black Knights' Fortress in disguise, descend into the Black Knight Catacombs, farm elite black armour off Elite Black Knights, rescue Silif and swap armour with him, then face Surok Magis (magic-only, 450hp, Vorkath-style attack rotation: bind/weaken his summoned warriors, telegrab-and-alch his explosive orbs, counter his elemental surges with the opposite element) atop the fortress. Teleport to Lucien's camp via a strange teleorb, then descend the Chasm of Tears: collect 4 elemental orbs from braziers, solve elemental-block puzzles inside skull tunnels, and match herblore-ingredient offerings to 8 of 13 statues (offerings vary per player) to open the final door. Finish with the Balance Elemental (524, prayer-switches by its attack style, drains stats every 3-4 hits, doubles up at 25% hp) then two Tormented Demons alongside the Guardians of Armadyl (can be cheesed by letting the Guardians finish them off from behind a pillar) before returning to Idria to complete.

name: Witch's House

skills: Ability to defeat 4 monsters (levels 19-53) back-to-back with no break

unlocks: Witch's experiment (all four forms) accessible as a Nightmare Zone boss

start.location: near the members gate of the long garden, Taverley (2928,3456)

summary: Sneak past the Witch to rescue a boy's ball and defeat her shape-shifting experiment.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Starts with the Boy near the members gate of the long garden, Taverley.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Very Short quest length.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Requires the ability to defeat 4 monsters (levels 19-53) back to back with no break before starting.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: The Witch's experiment escalates through four forms (levels 19, 30, 42, 53) fought in sequence inside the shed; the shed corners are safespottable.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Being seen on the grass or by the Witch forces the break-in to restart, and getting caught mid-fight while carrying the ball can lower combat stats.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Completing the quest unlocks the Witch's experiment (all four forms) as a selectable Nightmare Zone boss.

notes: A boy's ball landed in a scary witch's garden. Sneak into her house (key under the potted plant by the front door), grab gloves and a magnet from the basement, then trap a mouse with cheese and the magnet. Cross the garden to the fountain and shed without the Witch seeing you (stick to hedges, never walk on grass, or you must repeat the break-in); getting caught mid-fight also drops your combat stats if you grab the ball too early. Open the shed and fight the Witch's experiment through all four escalating forms (levels 19/30/42/53, corners of the shed are safespottable), take the ball, then sneak/teleport back out and return it to the boy.

name: Witch's Potion

facts.label: start

facts.label: items-note

notes: Hetty the witch wants help brewing a potion. Gather four ingredients: kill a rat in the archery shop west of Hetty for a rat's tail, buy an eye of newt from Betty (3gp), kill a giant rat at the chapel south of Port Sarim for raw rat meat and burn it on Hetty's fireplace, and pick an onion from the field north of Rimmington. Bring all four to Hetty and drink from her cauldron to finish.
