facts.value: Completing the quest unlocks access to Uglug's shop, entered by giving Uglug a dose of Relicym's balm.

notes: Grish asks you to investigate zombified ogres (zogres) infesting the ogre ceremonial grounds. Head into the cave below Jiggig, being careful of disease from zogre/skogre attacks if under 89 combat (carry super restores). Loot a ruined backpack and a torn page from a skeleton/lectern, then pry open a coffin with a knife for a black prism. Head to Yanille, use the recovered Dragon Inn tankard on the bartender there, then visit Zavistic Rarve at the Wizards' Guild. Investigate the ogres' bedridden neighbor Sithik Ints: search his room for a portraiture book, necromancy book, papyrus/charcoal and a Book of 'H.A.M.', read and use all three on him, then sketch a portrait that reads as an honest "classic realist charcoal portrait" (higher Crafting speeds this up) and get it signed by the Dragon Inn bartender. Bring the necromancy book, H.A.M. book, tankard and signed portrait to Zavistic Rarve for a strange potion; spike Sithik's tea with it, then question him for the secrets to Relicym's balm, curing the disease, and removing the undead ogres. Return to Grish for an ogre gate key and to learn comp ogre bow use, then fight the level-111 Slash Bash (uses Ranged+Melee — safespot with Protect from Missiles, or use Crumble Undead) in the ceremonial chamber to finish.

name: Wintertodt

rewards.xp: Firemaking (level*100) on subdue with 500+ points, plus per-action xp during the fight: lighting braziers (FM 6x), cutting/fletching bruma roots (WC 0.3x / Fletching 0.6x), feeding roots/kindling (FM 3x/3.8x), fixing broken braziers (Construction 4x, needs a POH), brewing rejuvenation potions (Herblore 0.1x)
rewards.items: Reward Cart loot scaled to the searcher's skill levels (higher Herblore/Mining/Fishing/Crafting/Farming/Woodcutting = rarer pulls); every 500 points guarantees +1 extra reward beyond the base 2

summary: Wintertodt is an AFK-style Firemaking trainer at Doors of Dinh that also grants Woodcutting, Fletching, Construction, and Herblore xp as side effects.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: AFK-style Firemaking trainer with side xp in Woodcutting, Fletching, Construction, and Herblore during the same rotation.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Construction xp from fixing broken braziers requires the player to own a player-owned house (POH).

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Reward Cart loot quality scales with the player's other skill levels (Herblore, Mining, Fishing, Crafting, Farming, Woodcutting); every 500 points guarantees one extra reward beyond the base two.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Firemaking xp on subdue equals character level times 100, requiring 500+ points; additional per-action xp accrues throughout the fight from lighting braziers, cutting/fletching bruma roots, feeding roots/kindling, and fixing braziers.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Unlocks reward is unrecorded in the held text ('??').

notes: Low-req AFK-ish Firemaking trainer that also feeds Woodcutting/Fletching/Construction/Herblore xp as side effects of the same rotation -- a good early-game multi-skill xp source once FM 50 is hit, and the reward cart is a steady supply chain (herbs/ore/fish/gems/seeds/logs) gated by the player's OTHER skill levels, so it stays worth revisiting as those skills climb. Construction xp specifically requires owning a POH (brazier repair). Entry is cheap: no quest required, only FM 50 and basic tools/potions obtainable inside the minigame itself.

name: Tempoross

items: harpoon or barb-tail harpoon (skippable w/ barbarian fishing)
small or big fishing net (only needed to search the reward pool)

req_items.name: rope
req_items.note: skippable with the Spirit Angler outfit

req_items.name: small or big fishing net
req_items.note: only needed to search the reward pool

rewards.xp: Fishing xp gained continuously during the fight itself (no lump subdue bonus, unlike Wintertodt) -- rate scales up through 'Not cooking' vs 'Cooking' play styles per the page's xp-rate section
rewards.items: Reward pool permits (1 per 2000 pts, +1 per further 700) spent on: spirit flakes (bonus-catch buff), casket, soaked pages, fish barrel, tackle box, big harpoonfish, Tome of water (empty, needs Magic 50 to wield, infinite water runes + spell dmg/accuracy boost), dragon harpoon, Tiny tempor pet

summary: Tempoross at the Ruins of Unkah has zero entry requirements and grants continuous Fishing xp plus a reward-pool shop scaled by Fishing level.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: No combat or skill level gates participation; base Fishing level only affects reward-pool loot quality at the time of searching (skill boosts don't count).

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Fishing xp accrues continuously during the fight itself with no lump subdue bonus; the rate differs between the 'Not cooking' and 'Cooking' play styles.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Reward pool permits are earned at 1 per 2,000 points plus 1 per further 700 points, spent on spirit flakes, casket, soaked pages, fish barrel, tackle box, big harpoonfish, Tome of water, dragon harpoon, and the Tiny tempor pet.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: The Tome of water (needs Magic 50 to wield, grants infinite water runes plus a spell damage/accuracy boost) and the fish barrel/tackle box storage items are exclusive to this reward pool.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Coordinates for the activity location and the unlocks field are unrecorded in the held text ('??').

notes: Zero entry requirements -- no combat or skill level gates participation, only base Fishing level affects reward-pool loot quality at the time of searching (skill boosts don't count). Best pure Fishing trainer with no subdue bonus (all xp is per-action during the fight), and the reward pool is the only source of the Tome of water (magic dmg/accuracy utility item) and the fish barrel/tackle box QoL storage items. Good round-robin activity since it has no gear/skill investment barrier -- can be done from level 1 in every relevant skill.

name: Guardians of the Rift

rewards.xp: Runecraft level x 45 per game on reaching 300+ total energy gathered (elemental+catalytic combined); up to 1200 energy/game (1320 with abyssal lantern burning yew logs), capped 1000 per energy type
rewards.items: Elemental/catalytic talismans (altar access), Abyssal needle (merges essence pouches into a colossal pouch, needs Crafting 56), Abyssal lantern, Abyssal pearls (currency for Temple Supplies: raiments of the eye +60% runes full set, ring of the elements, Blood talisman exclusive here), intricate pouch (runes/dragon items/hard clue chance), Abyssal protector pet
rewards.unlocks: Ring of the elements teleport network; raiments of the eye passive Runecrafting rune-multiplier set

summary: Guardians of the Rift at the Wizards' Tower requires Temple of the Eye and Runecraft 27, rewarding scaling RC xp plus talismans and Abyssal gear.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Primary high-level Runecraft trainer/gear source once Temple of the Eye is complete; the RC 27 entry gate is trivially met via the quest's own xp.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Runecraft xp equals Runecraft level times 45 per game on reaching 300+ combined elemental+catalytic energy gathered; up to 1,200 energy per game (1,320 with an abyssal lantern burning yew logs), capped at 1,000 per energy type.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Abyssal pearls buy Temple Supplies rewards including raiments of the eye (+60% runes, full set), ring of the elements, and the Blood talisman, which is exclusive to this minigame.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The Abyssal needle merges essence pouches into a colossal pouch and needs Crafting 56 to use.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Grants the ring of the elements teleport network and the raiments of the eye passive Runecrafting rune-multiplier set.

notes: Primary high-level Runecraft trainer/gold-and-gear source once Temple of the Eye is done -- gates on RC 27 (trivially met via the quest's own xp) not a high skill investment, so it's accessible early but scales its xp reward with current RC level, making it worth returning to repeatedly as RC climbs. The exclusive Blood talisman and the raiments' passive rune-multiplier set make it the natural feeder loop for pushing RC further (buy talisman access -> craft more runes -> more RC xp elsewhere).

name: Barbarian Assault

rewards.items: Honour points -> Penance armour set (fighter hat/torso/skirt/gloves + runner boots, each ~2100 pts across all 4 roles + 95,000gp + 7 Penance Queen kills for the granite body); fighter torso is the headline item (Strength bonus, no stat reqs to wear); gambling honour points can yield dragon chainbody or the Penance Queen pet (1/1000 on high gamble)
rewards.unlocks: Elite Kandarin Diary requires level-5 upgrades in all 4 roles (5,600 honour points total, capped 5000/role)

summary: Barbarian Assault is a 5-player, 10-wave team minigame at the Barbarian Outpost with no skill or quest requirement beyond Captain Cain's tutorial.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: 5-player team minigame with attacker/defender/healer/collector roles across 10 waves; the whole team restarts a wave on any death or player leaving.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: The Elite Kandarin Diary requires level-5 upgrades in all 4 roles, totalling 5,600 honour points (capped at 5,000 per role).

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Honour points buy the Penance armour set (fighter hat/torso/skirt/gloves plus runner boots), each piece costing roughly 2,100 points across all 4 roles, plus 95,000gp and 7 Penance Queen kills for the granite body.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Points-gambling on honour points can yield a dragon chainbody or the Penance Queen pet at a 1/1000 chance on a high gamble.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: The fighter torso is a cheap, no-stat-requirement Strength-bonus body slot upgrade, the minigame's headline item.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Reward xp is unrecorded in the held text ('??').

notes: 5-player team minigame (attacker/defender/healer/collector roles, 10 waves, whole team restarts a wave on any death/leave) gated by nothing beyond forming a team and completing Captain Cain's tutorial -- no skill or quest requirement. Its main progression value is the fighter torso (cheap, no-stat-req strength-bonus body slot, a long-lived early/mid melee upgrade) and being a hard requirement for the Elite Kandarin Diary (all 4 roles to level 5). Points-gambling is also a source of the dragon chainbody and the rare Penance Queen pet.

name: Pest Control

reqs.combat_level: 40/70/100 for Novice/Intermediate/Veteran lander respectively -- the only entry gate

rewards.xp: Commendation points can be exchanged for Combat, Crafting, and Woodcutting xp
rewards.items: Void Knight equipment (regular + elite void, ~850-1910 commendation points per piece/set), plus coins per win (10x current combat level); commendation points capped at 4000 held at once

summary: Pest Control is a combat-level-gated (40/70/100) 5-25 player wave-defense minigame reached by boat from Port Sarim, rewarding Void Knight equipment and coin.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Entry gate is purely combat level (Novice 40 / Intermediate 70 / Veteran 100 lander); no skill or quest requirement.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Players must keep an 'activity bar' above 0% to earn points; points cannot be earned while a Dwarf multicannon or Alchemy spells are in use.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Commendation points (capped at 4,000 held) buy regular and elite Void Knight equipment, roughly 850-1,910 points per piece/set, plus coins per win equal to 10x current combat level.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Void Knight armour is a cheap, universal combat-bonus set with no stat requirements to wear, usable across melee/ranged/magic.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Commendation points can also be exchanged for Combat, Crafting, and Woodcutting xp.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Unlocks field is unrecorded in the held text ('??').

notes: Pure combat-level gated (40/70/100, no skill/quest reqs) 5-25 player wave-defense minigame; must keep an 'activity bar' above 0% to earn points, and points cannot be earned while Dwarf multicannon or Alchemy spells are used. Its main progression payoff is Void Knight armour, a cheap universal combat-bonus set (no stat requirements to wear) usable across melee/ranged/magic, making it a strong low-investment gear upgrade for any account, plus a modest side supply of Crafting/Woodcutting xp via point exchange.

name: Blast Furnace

skills.smithing: none to enter; 60 waives the 2500c/10min entry fee and unlocks the nearby anvil

quests: The Giant Dwarf (started only, for Keldagrim access)
items: ice gloves, smiths gloves (i), or a bucket of water (fast bar-cooling)
coal bag / goldsmith gauntlets recommended for inventory efficiency

req_items.name: ice gloves, smiths gloves (i), or a bucket of water

rewards.xp: Full Smithing xp per bar smelted at greatly boosted bars/hour vs a normal furnace -- e.g. steel bar 2,900-3,400 bars/h (50,800-95,400 xp/h with a coal bag), mithril up to 3,700 bars/h (up to 111,000 xp/h); scales by bar tier

summary: Blast Furnace at Keldagrim is the fastest bar-smelting method in the game, needing only started The Giant Dwarf; Smithing 60 waives the entry fee.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Entry costs 2,500 coins per 10 minutes, waived at Smithing 60, which also unlocks the nearby anvil.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: On a Blast Furnace themed world, dwarven workers auto-operate the furnace for 72,000 coins/hour paid into a shared coffer; non-themed worlds must be run manually and solo.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Keldagrim access requires only starting The Giant Dwarf, not completing it.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Steel bars smelt at 2,900-3,400 bars/hour (50,800-95,400 xp/hour with a coal bag); mithril bars reach up to 3,700 bars/hour (up to 111,000 xp/hour); rate scales by bar tier.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Its steel/mithril+ bar output feeds directly into cannonball and weapon/armour smithing chains elsewhere in progression.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Item and unlock rewards are unrecorded in the held text ('??').

notes: The fastest bar-smelting method in the game (steel/mithril+ especially) -- entry is cheap (2500c/10min, waivable at Smithing 60) but running it on a Blast Furnace themed world costs 72,000c/hour into a shared coffer to pay dwarven workers who auto-operate the furnace; on non-themed worlds it must be run manually (slower, solo). This is the standard high-volume Smithing xp/bar-production venue once a player has Keldagrim access via starting The Giant Dwarf, and its steel/mithril+ bar output feeds directly into cannonball/weapon-armour smithing chains elsewhere in progression.

name: Nightmare Zone

quests: any 5 of a long list including The Grand Tree, Dragon Slayer I, Monkey Madness I, Desert Treasure I, Lost City, Tree Gnome Village, Vampyre Slayer, What Lies Below, etc -- only 5 completions needed, not all

rewards.items: NMZ reward points spent on: Herblore-ingredient/rune resources (flax, sand, seaweed, runes, herb boxes capped 15/day, scrolls of redirection -- ironmen restricted to scrolls-only on this tab), combat-stat upgrades, and minigame-exclusive potions unusable outside NMZ (overload, absorption, super ranging/magic) stored in on-site barrels and drinkable without delaying attacks

summary: Nightmare Zone near Yanille is an AFK combat trainer needing any 5 completed quests from a long list, rewarding points for resources and exclusive potions.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Gate is shallow: any 5 completed quests from a long eligible list (e.g. The Grand Tree, Dragon Slayer I, Monkey Madness I, Desert Treasure I, Lost City, Tree Gnome Village, Vampyre Slayer, What Lies Below), not a fixed set, making it reachable early.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Historically the go-to AFK melee/ranged/magic combat trainer via a boss-rotation 'dream' using cannon and auto-retaliate.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: NMZ reward points buy Herblore-ingredient/rune resources (flax, sand, seaweed, runes, herb boxes capped at 15/day, scrolls of redirection), combat-stat upgrades, and minigame-exclusive potions (overload, absorption, super ranging/magic) drinkable from on-site barrels without delaying attacks.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Ironmen are restricted to scrolls-only on the resource tab of the reward shop.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: The only source of Overload and Absorption potions, which are strong utility for other high-level PvM.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Raw combat xp/hr is unrecorded in the held text ('??'); the source page directs to its own Bosses/Playing sections for that number.

notes: Gate is shallow (any 5 completed quests from a long list, not a specific set) making it reachable early despite the list containing some mid-game quests. Historically the go-to AFK melee/ranged/magic combat trainer via the boss-rotation 'dream' with cannon/auto-retaliate, though the wiki page itself doesn't state raw xp/hr here (see Bosses/Playing sections if xp numbers are needed downstream). Its progression value is largely as a combat xp treadmill plus a cheap secondary Herblore-ingredient/rune shop and the only source of Overload/Absorption potions, which are strong utility for other high-level PvM.

name: Pyramid Plunder

skills.thieving: 21 minimum to start; every +10 (unboostable) opens the next tier of rooms up to 91

quests: Icthlarin's Little Helper (started only, for Sophanem access)

rewards.items: Artefacts (urns/chests/sarcophagi) sellable for 50-1250gp each or used to recharge a Pharaoh's sceptre; the sceptre itself is a rare drop from golden chests/sarcophagi in later rooms, rate improving from 1/4200 at Thieving 21-30 up to ~1/63.5 cumulative at 91+ per full run
rewards.unlocks: Pharaoh's sceptre (Sophanem/Kalphite Cave/Menaphos/Nardah/Sophanem-Pyramid teleport network, useable as this minigame's own fast-entry item once charged)

summary: Pyramid Plunder in Sophanem is a Thieving-only minigame (21 to start) scaling room access up to Thieving 91, rewarding the Pharaoh's sceptre.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Room tiers unlock via unboostable Thieving levels in +10 steps up to 91, scaling it as a Thieving-training destination through mid-to-late game rather than a one-time unlock.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: City access requires only starting Icthlarin's Little Helper, not completing it.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Artefacts (urns/chests/sarcophagi) sell for 50-1,250gp each or recharge a Pharaoh's sceptre.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Sceptre drop rate improves from 1/4,200 at Thieving 21-30 up to roughly 1/63.5 cumulative at Thieving 91+ per full run.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: The Pharaoh's sceptre grants the Sophanem/Kalphite Cave/Menaphos/Nardah/Sophanem-Pyramid teleport network and can itself be used as a fast-entry item into this minigame once charged.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Raw xp/hr reward is unrecorded in the held text ('??').

notes: Thieving-only gate (21 to start, no quest requirement beyond starting Icthlarin's Little Helper for city access), with room tiers unlocked by unboostable Thieving levels up to 91 -- so it scales naturally as a Thieving-training destination through the mid-to-late game rather than being a one-time unlock. Primary progression payoff is the Pharaoh's sceptre, a teleport/utility item recharged by the very artefacts this minigame produces, making it a self-sustaining loop once obtained.

name: Volcanic Mine

items: 30 numulite per entry (or 3,000 numulite one-time for permanent access)

req_items.name: numulite
req_items.note: per entry; 3,000 one-time buys permanent access

rewards.xp: Mining xp scales with level: ~40,000 xp/h at Mining 50 (~7,200 pts), rising to 85,000-95,000 xp/h at Mining 99 (~15,400-17,200 pts) with good pickaxe + play; roughly 3.8 Mining xp granted per point at game end (3.895 with prospector kit), plus xp earned live during the game
rewards.items: Ore fragments (kept even if not cashed for points) plus calcite/pyrophosphite/Fossil Island fossils/numulite gathered along the way; points spent at Petrified Pete's Ore Shop, with runite ore fragments (unlocked Mining 85) worth the most per point

summary: Volcanic Mine under the Fossil Island Volcano is a high-intensity Mining 50+ trainer where death or teleporting out forfeits the run's fragments and points.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: A riskier, more active alternative to AFK Mining spots; dying or teleporting out forfeits all fragments/points for that run.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Mining xp scales with level: roughly 40,000 xp/hour at Mining 50 (~7,200 points), rising to 85,000-95,000 xp/hour at Mining 99 (~15,400-17,200 points) with a good pickaxe and play, plus roughly 3.8 Mining xp granted per point at game end (3.895 with a prospector kit).

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Ore fragments are kept even if not cashed for points; points are spent at Petrified Pete's Ore Shop, with runite ore fragments (unlocked at Mining 85) worth the most per point.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Also a source of numulite and Fossil Island fossils, useful for other Fossil Island unlocks (Fossil Storage, Ore Shop purchases).

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Unlocks field is unrecorded in the held text ('??').

notes: High-level (Mining 50+) high-intensity Mining trainer/moneymaker on Fossil Island -- dying or teleporting out forfeits all fragments/points for that run, so it's a riskier, more active alternative to AFK Mining spots. Runite-fragment access at Mining 85 makes it scale further into the late game, and it doubles as a numulite/fossil income source useful for other Fossil Island unlocks (Fossil Storage, Ore Shop purchases).

name: Motherlode Mine

skills.mining: 30 (unboostable) to mine ore veins; no level required merely to enter; upper floor needs Mining 57 + 100 golden nuggets one-time

rewards.xp: 60 Mining xp per ore vein mined (pay-dirt), continuous low-intensity training; golden nuggets drop 3.13% per pay-dirt cleaned (~1 every 32) regardless of Mining level
rewards.items: Golden nuggets spent at Prospector Percy's Nugget Shop on the Prospector outfit (helmet/jacket/legs/boots, each a small flat xp-boost %), coal bag, gem bag, plus in-mine upgrades: upper floor access (100 nuggets, needs Mining 57), Super Hopper (50), pay-dirt sack capacity 108->189 (200)
rewards.unlocks: Prospector helmet required for a hard Falador Diary task

summary: Motherlode Mine in the Dwarven Mine is a low-intensity Mining 30 trainer and the only source of the coal bag, gem bag, and Prospector outfit.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: No quest requirement; upper floor access needs Mining 57 plus a one-time 100 golden nuggets.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: 60 Mining xp per ore vein (pay-dirt) mined, continuous low-intensity training; golden nuggets drop at 3.13% per pay-dirt cleaned (~1 every 32) regardless of Mining level.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Golden nuggets buy the Prospector outfit (helmet/jacket/legs/boots, each a small flat xp-boost %), the coal bag, the gem bag, upper floor access (100 nuggets, needs Mining 57), the Super Hopper (50 nuggets), and pay-dirt sack capacity 108->189 (200 nuggets).

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: The Prospector helmet is required for a hard Falador Diary task; the coal bag is a major Blast Furnace xp/gp multiplier, so this grind feeds directly into Blast Furnace efficiency.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Recommended purchase order is upper-floor and hopper unlocks first, then outfit pieces by xp-bonus-per-nugget, then sack capacity last.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: A full outfit plus upper level plus bag upgrades costs roughly 17,500 pay-dirt (Mining ~30 to ~68) to fund via nuggets alone.

notes: Low-intensity, low-barrier (Mining 30, no quest) Mining trainer that doubles as the only source of the coal bag (a major Blast Furnace xp/gp multiplier) and gem bag -- so completing Motherlode Mine's nugget grind feeds directly into Blast Furnace efficiency later. Recommended purchase order is upper-floor+hopper unlocks first (best xp/attention), then outfit pieces by xp-bonus-per-nugget, sack capacity last. Full outfit + upper level + bag upgrades costs roughly 17,500 pay-dirt (Mining ~30 to ~68) to fund via nuggets alone.

name: Hallowed Sepulchre

skills.agility: 52 (unboostable) to enter floor 1, rising per floor: 62/72/82/92 for floors 2-5

quests: Sins of the Father (for Darkmeyer access)
items: recommended: Hitpoints 70+, a Saradomin-affiliated item to use the Magical Obelisks without draining run energy

req_items.name: Saradomin-affiliated item
req_items.note: recommended, avoids run energy drain when using Magical Obelisks

rewards.xp: Agility xp from traversing obstacles per floor (rate rises with floor difficulty); coffin-looting is optional side content gated by its own skill checks (Thieving 66 boostable, Construction 56 boostable for bridges, Prayer 54 for braziers, Magic 7 for portal frames, Ranged 62 for pillars)
rewards.items: Hallowed marks from coffins spend in the Mysterious Hallowed Goods shop: Hallowed crystal shard (lobby teleport), Hallowed token (+1 min per attempt), Hallowed grapple/focus/symbol/hammer (guaranteed obstacle-pass tools for pillars/portals/braziers/bridges), Hallowed ring (fail-safe teleport-back, cuts food need), Dark dye (recolours Graceful to Hallowed Graceful, 6 needed for full set), Hallowed sack (bonus loot rolls)
rewards.unlocks: Giant squirrel Agility pet (rate improves 1/35,000 on floor 1 up to 1/2,000 on floor 5)

summary: Hallowed Sepulchre in Darkmeyer is the premier late-game Agility trainer, gated Agility 52-92 by floor (unboostable), requiring Sins of the Father.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Unboostable per-floor Agility gates (52/62/72/82/92) mean it scales naturally alongside a player's Agility level rather than being a single unlock.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Coffin-looting is optional side content gated by its own skill checks: Thieving 66 (boostable), Construction 56 (boostable, for bridges), Prayer 54 (for braziers), Magic 7 (for portal frames), Ranged 62 (for pillars).

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Recommended (not required): Hitpoints 70+ for survivability.

facts.label: items-note

notes: The premier late-game Agility trainer/course once Sins of the Father is done -- unboostable per-floor Agility gates (52/62/72/82/92) mean it scales naturally alongside a player's Agility level rather than being a single unlock. Its reward-shop tools (grapple/focus/symbol/hammer) make later runs progressively faster/safer once bought, and it is the only source of the Hallowed Graceful recolour and (via coffins) supplementary Thieving/Construction/Prayer/Magic/Ranged practice tied to obstacle skill-checks.

name: Mahogany Homes

skills.construction: 1 for Beginner tier; Novice 20, Adept 50, Expert 70 to unlock higher (boostable) contract tiers

items: regular/oak/teak/mahogany planks depending on contract tier (nails not required)

req_items.name: regular/oak/teak/mahogany planks
req_items.note: depending on contract tier; nails not required

rewards.xp: Construction xp per contract scales sharply by tier: Beginner 500 (512.5 with carpenter's outfit), Novice 1,250, Adept 2,250, Expert 2,750 (2,818.7 with outfit); experienced players complete ~50-60 contracts/hour with a plank sack + efficient teleports/NPC Contact
rewards.items: Carpenter points -> Mahogany Homes Reward Shop: supply crates (Construction materials), Amy's saw (equipable, priority buy), plank sack (holds 28 planks, top priority for continued training efficiency), Hosidius blueprints (unlocks Hosidius Wall Kit), full Carpenter's outfit (helmet/shirt/trousers/boots, further boosts contract xp)

summary: Mahogany Homes is a repeatable Construction trainer (offices in Falador, Varrock, East Ardougne, Hosidius) gated by boostable Construction tiers up to 70.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Beginner tier needs only Construction 1; Novice 20, Adept 50, and Expert 70 unlock higher (boostable) contract tiers.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Construction xp per contract scales sharply by tier: Beginner 500 (512.5 with the carpenter's outfit), Novice 1,250, Adept 2,250, Expert 2,750 (2,818.7 with the outfit); experienced players complete roughly 50-60 contracts/hour with a plank sack plus efficient teleports/NPC Contact.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Carpenter points buy the Mahogany Homes Reward Shop: supply crates, Amy's saw, a 28-plank sack, Hosidius blueprints (unlocks the Hosidius Wall Kit), and the full Carpenter's outfit (further boosts contract xp).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The plank sack and Amy's saw are the priority early purchases since they most directly raise contracts/hour.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Full completion for the collection log requires 975 Expert contracts, a long-tail late-game grind.

notes: Repeatable Construction trainer with almost no entry barrier (Construction 1 for the lowest tier) that scales cleanly with level via 4 boostable tiers up to 70 -- unlike a player-owned house grind, materials are semi-consumed via contracts but return carpenter points and player-facing outfit bonuses that compound xp/hour. The plank sack and Amy's saw are the key early reward-shop purchases since they most directly raise contracts/hour; full completion (975 Expert contracts for the collection log) is a long-tail late-game grind.

name: Achievement Diary

rewards.effect: 492 tasks spread across 12 regions x 4 tiers (Easy/Medium/Hard/Elite). A tier's rewards can only be claimed from that diary's own taskmaster once every task in that tier AND every preceding tier of the same region is complete (tasks themselves can be done out of order). Every tier claim always includes an antique lamp + an equippable armour/cape piece, plus passive perks: xp-rate boosts, shop discounts, new teleport unlocks, and region access. Completing every diary at every tier unlocks purchase of the achievement diary cape (99,000gp, Twiggy O'Korn, Draynor Village), which itself lets the quest point cape be trimmed cyan and is one of two requirements for the music cape's black trim.

summary: The Achievement Diary spans 492 tasks across 12 regions x 4 tiers; each tier's rewards claim only after that tier and all preceding tiers are complete.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Tasks within a tier can be done out of order, but rewards claim from the region's own taskmaster only once every task in that tier and every preceding tier is complete.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Every tier claim always includes an antique lamp plus an equippable armour/cape piece, along with passive perks: xp-rate boosts, shop discounts, new teleport unlocks, and region access.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Completing every diary at every tier unlocks purchase of the achievement diary cape (99,000gp, Twiggy O'Korn, Draynor Village), which lets the quest point cape be trimmed cyan and is one of two requirements for the music cape's black trim.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: The aggregate 'All requirements' tables list 'Acquire a prospector helmet from the Motherlode Mine' as an Additional-requirement checkbox for both the Hard tier and the Elite tier bundles, across every region -- only one prospector helmet piece is needed, not the full 4-piece set, but it is a literal cross-dependency on the Prospector kit unlock (unlockdb:prospector-kit).

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Other Hard/Elite aggregate gates include Warriors' Guild access (130 combined Attack+Strength or 99 in either), Wilderness God Wars Dungeon access, at least one god spell, and (Elite only) full completion of Chambers of Xeric, TzHaar Fight Cave, and all quests -- chaining the diary system to the raid/quest-cape burndown.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Travel-hub grant field is unrecorded in the held text ('??').

notes: Own words, system-level rather than per-region: this is the requester's own named steer-point pattern (CONSOLIDATED.md pattern 12, 'QP/diary as progression metric & anchor') so it is marked hard rather than soft. Concretely non-obvious hard-gate found in this pass: the aggregate 'All requirements' tables (Achievement_Diary.s2, one per tier via a shared DiarySkillStats+quest-list tabber) list 'Acquire a prospector helmet from the Motherlode Mine' as an Additional-requirement checkbox for BOTH the Hard tier AND the Elite tier bundles (i.e. across every region's Hard/Elite diary, not one specific region) -- only ONE prospector helmet piece is needed to satisfy this box, not the full 4-piece set, but it means the Prospector kit unlock (unlockdb:prospector-kit, this same batch) is a literal cross-dependency baked into the diary system, not just a coincidentally-useful item. Other Hard/Elite aggregate gates worth flagging for planner interleaving: Warriors' Guild access (130 combined Attack+Strength or 99 in either), Wilderness God Wars Dungeon access, at least one god spell, and (Elite only) full completion of Chambers of Xeric, TzHaar Fight Cave, and 'all quests' -- these functionally chain the diary system to the raid/quest-cape burndown too.

Time-investment section on the source page explicitly warns the outfit is NOT worth farming purely for the xp bonus against faster training methods (3-tick barbarian fishing at 65, 3-tick fly fishing/Tempoross-with-crystal-harpoon at 80) -- own-words break-even is only worthwhile below ~66,129 xp/hr. Marked hard rather than soft specifically because of the minnow-fishing/clue-step/combat-achievement functional gates, which cannot be bypassed by training rate the way the xp bonus can.

name: Fairy ring

rewards.effect: Unlocks the fairy ring transportation network: 55 of 64 possible A/B/C/D letter-code combinations are active landing points spread across almost every region of the game, each reached by dialling a 3-letter code at any ring. This is the single densest point-to-point travel web in the game and is treated as the default transport layer in nearly every other route/guide already mined into this KB (herb-patch runs, Chambers of Xeric access, Barrows, Mort Myre, penguin/Miscellania errands).

facts.value: The unlock is itself a multi-quest requisite chain (5 full quests plus Fairytale II partial completion, 'up to granted permission'), not a single flag.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Treated as the densest point-to-point travel web in the game and the default transport layer assumed by most other already-mined routes/guides (herb-patch runs, Chambers of Xeric access, Barrows, Mort Myre, penguin/Miscellania errands).

notes: Own words: the unlock is a quest-chain burndown in itself (5 quests + a partial 6th), not a single flag, so it should be modelled as its own multi-quest requisite node rather than folded silently into whichever quest happens to finish it. Once live it becomes a hard dependency for a large share of downstream travel steps already contributed elsewhere in this KB (travel:ref-fairy-ring, bg:pps-03-withdraw-herb-run, travel:milestone-raids-cox all cite fairy-ring codes as a named routing option) -- it is the earliest 'hard' access anchor a fresh account can reach, and most later route synthesis assumes it is already unlocked. Downstream acceleration: turns multi-region herb runs, raid access, and quest-hub travel from multi-minute walks/teleport-chains into a single 2-click ring dial.

facts.label: mechanics
