notes: Own words: narrow QoL multiplier scoped entirely to pickpocket-based Thieving training/money-making, doesn't gate anything else in the KB. Storable in a POH costume room armour case (cross-references unlockdb:player-owned-house). Since 1 June 2017 the crates let players pick which piece to receive (previously fixed order until full set owned), which matters for Ultimate Ironmen who can no longer be RNG-locked out of the full set.

name: Spirit tree

rewards.effect: Unlocks the spirit-tree transportation network: 8 permanent stops (Tree Gnome Village, Tree Gnome Stronghold, Battlefield of Khazard, Grand Exchange, Feldip Hills, Poison Waste (partial Path of Glouphrie), Prifddinas (Song of the Elves), Laguna Aurorae (Pandemonium+58 Sailing)) plus 5 Farming-grown patches and a POH superior-garden tree at 75 Construction.

summary: Teleport network unlocked after Tree Gnome Village, with permanent stops plus Farming-grown and POH tree access.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Unlocked by completing the Tree Gnome Village quest.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Provides 8 permanent stops: Tree Gnome Village, Tree Gnome Stronghold, Battlefield of Khazard, Grand Exchange, Feldip Hills, Poison Waste (partial, requires Path of Glouphrie), Prifddinas (requires Song of the Elves), and Laguna Aurorae (requires Pandemonium and 58 Sailing).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Also reachable via 5 Farming-grown spirit tree patches and a player-owned house superior garden tree at 75 Construction.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Tree Gnome Village is a single, cheap early quest compared to the fairy ring network's 5-quest chain, making the spirit tree network the first regional teleport web a fresh account can unlock.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Nothing else researched in this KB requires spirit-tree access, so it functions as a soft/optional accelerant rather than a hard dependency.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Fastest pre-83-Farming access methods per the source page: the Ardougne cloak to the Khazard Battlefield tree (zero cost, low requirements) or the Grand Exchange (via ring of wealth/teletab plus the medium Varrock Diary); the Achievement Diary Cape plus Elder Gnome child route is called faster than even the player-owned-house tree despite being farther on foot (no load screen).

notes: Own words: gate is a single, cheap early quest (Tree Gnome Village, confirmed on Unlockable_content.s18's own table) versus fairy rings' 5-quest chain -- this makes spirit tree the FIRST regional teleport-web a fresh account can unlock, not merely a lesser alternative. It is not a hard dependency for anything else in the KB (nothing greps as requiring spirit-tree access), so it reads as a soft/optional accelerant, but a high-value one: source page's own text ranks fastest-access methods pre-83-Farming as Ardougne cloak -> Khazard Battlefield tree (zero cost, low reqs) or GE (ring of wealth/teletab + medium Varrock Diary), and calls the Achievement Diary Cape + Elder Gnome child route faster than even the POH tree despite being farther on foot (no POH load screen). Downstream acceleration: bridges the travel gap between tutorial-exit and the fairy-ring unlock chain being finished.

name: Xeric's talisman

items: No purchase/quest gate on the amulet itself -- it is a monster drop: 1/250 from lizardmen/lizardman brutes/lizardman shamans (improved to 1/125 after the easy Kourend & Kebos Diary), or 1/300 stealing from stone chests in the Lizardman Temple beneath Molch

req_items.name: Xeric's talisman (drop)
req_items.note: 1/250 from lizardmen/lizardman brutes/lizardman shamans (1/125 after the Kourend & Kebos easy diary), or 1/300 stealing stone chests in the Lizardman Temple beneath Molch

rewards.effect: 4 free teleports by default (Xeric's Lookout SE of Shayzien, Xeric's Glade NE of the Hosidius farm patch, Xeric's Inferno at the Lovakengj lovakite furnace, Xeric's Heart at Kourend Castle) plus a 5th, Xeric's Honour at the Chambers of Xeric summit on Mount Quidamortem, which permanently unlocks only after using an Ancient tablet (a 1/10 drop from the post-raid Ancient chest) on the talisman.

summary: Amulet dropped by lizardmen that grants free teleports to four Kourend locations plus Chambers of Xeric after first use.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Obtained as a monster drop from lizardmen, lizardman brutes, or lizardman shamans at 1/250 (improved to 1/125 after the easy Kourend & Kebos Diary), or 1/300 stealing from stone chests in the Lizardman Temple beneath Molch; no quest or skill gate applies to the drop itself.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Grants 4 free teleports by default: Xeric's Lookout (southeast of Shayzien), Xeric's Glade (northeast of the Hosidius farm patch), Xeric's Inferno (at the Lovakengj lovakite furnace), and Xeric's Heart (Kourend Castle).

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: A 5th teleport, Xeric's Honour at the Chambers of Xeric summit on Mount Quidamortem, permanently unlocks only after using an Ancient tablet (a 1/10 drop from the post-raid Ancient chest) on the talisman.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Teleporting to Xeric's Heart via the talisman is a listed Hard-tier task of the Kourend & Kebos Diary.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Listed as one of three named routes into the Chambers of Xeric, alongside the Lovakengj Minecart and the fairy ring code BLS.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Nothing else researched in this KB requires possessing the talisman, since the Chambers of Xeric has alternate access routes.

notes: Own words: no quest/skill gate to obtain the base item, only RNG (or the diary-boosted RNG); reaching a lizardman spawn to farm it does benefit from prior Kourend region quests for fast travel but that is convenience, not a hard requirement. Cross-references existing KB row travel:milestone-raids-cox, which already lists 'xerics-talisman-post-first-clear' as one of three named routes into Chambers of Xeric, and diarydb:kourend-and-kebos-hard, whose own Hard-tier task list includes 'teleport to Xeric's Heart via Xeric's talisman' as a literal diary requirement -- i.e. owning the talisman is itself gated INTO one diary tier while also accelerating repeat access to a raid. Anchor_hint is soft rather than hard because nothing else in the KB requires possessing the talisman to proceed (Chambers of Xeric itself has alternate access routes: Lovakengj Minecart, fairy ring BLS).

name: Fishing Trawler

skills.Fishing: 15 (only to catch/receive rewards -- the minigame can be boarded and played at any level)

items: Axe, hammer, several ropes, swamp paste, bailing bucket to do every activity (all buyable at the Khazard General Store; spare bronze axes/hammers/bailing buckets also spawn in boxes on the top deck)

req_items.name: Axe
req_items.note: buyable at the Khazard General Store; spare bronze axes also spawn in boxes on the top deck

req_items.name: Hammer
req_items.note: buyable at the Khazard General Store; spare bronze hammers also spawn in boxes on the top deck

req_items.name: Rope
req_items.note: several needed; buyable at the Khazard General Store

req_items.name: Swamp paste
req_items.note: buyable at the Khazard General Store

req_items.name: Bailing bucket
req_items.note: buyable at the Khazard General Store; spare bailing buckets also spawn in boxes on the top deck

rewards.xp: Fishing xp from the post-voyage net search (scaled by fish received); small Construction/Crafting/Woodcutting xp (5xp per successful action) from repairing leaks/net/kraken rail damage during the trip -- Construction xp specifically requires owning a POH
items: Raw shrimps/sardine/anchovies/oyster/tuna/lobster/swordfish/shark/sea turtle/manta ray (fish tier gated by own Fishing level, not contribution)
Junk items (old boot, broken arrow/glass/staff, buttons, damaged armour, pot, rusty sword) -- 0-2 per trip regardless of level
unlocks: Angler's outfit (hat/top/waders/boots), 1/12 per unowned piece per successful trip once 50+ contribution reached -- fastest known method to obtain it
Heron pet, 1/5000 base up to 1/2500 with a full 255-point contribution meter

summary: Co-op minigame at Port Khazard where players defend a fishing trawler to earn fish, xp, and a chance at the Angler's outfit and Heron pet.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Co-op boat-defense minigame based at the Port Khazard docks (2669, 3161): players board Murphy's trawler and defend it for a 5-minute trawl (about 6.5 minutes total including docking and cutscenes).

facts.label: start
facts.value: Requires Fishing 15 only to catch fish and receive rewards; the minigame itself can be boarded and played at any Fishing level.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Players patch hull leaks with swamp paste, bail water with a bailing bucket, re-rope torn fishing nets, and fight off a kraken tentacle with an axe while a hammer repairs the rail damage it causes.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: The boat sinks if water gets too high before time runs out; forfeiting or sinking both eject the player onto the beach near fairy ring code DJP for 1-2 damage.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Reward is driven by a 0-255 contribution meter earned through 4 actions (bailing = 2 points, leak repair = 5 points, net repair = 7 points, kraken chop = 10 points, rail fix = 10 points); 50+ points are needed to search the post-voyage net for fish, xp, and an angler-piece roll, and the meter never depletes.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Fishing xp comes from the post-voyage net search, scaled by fish received; small Construction, Crafting, and Woodcutting xp (5xp per successful action) come from repairing leaks, nets, and kraken rail damage during the trip, with the Construction xp specifically requiring an owned player-owned house.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: The Angler's outfit (hat, top, waders, boots) drops at 1/12 per unowned piece per successful trip once 50+ contribution is reached, the fastest known method to obtain it; the Heron pet drops at 1/5000 base, improving up to 1/2500 with a full 255-point contribution meter.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: A full 255-point contribution meter doubles fish yield and heron odds but does not increase angler-piece odds past the 50-point threshold.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Starting a voyage is an Ardougne Diary Easy task; catching and cooking a manta ray in Port Khazard is an Ardougne Diary Elite task.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Solo play yields more fish and xp per trip since server load is spread over fewer players, but official co-op worlds are the relaxed, faster route to the angler outfit.

notes: Co-op boat-defense minigame: board Murphy's trawler at Port Khazard, survive a 5-minute trawl (round is ~6.5 min total w/ docking+cutscenes) by patching hull leaks with swamp paste, bailing water with a bucket, re-roping torn fishing nets, and fighting off a kraken tentacle with an axe while a hammer fixes the rail damage it causes. Losing = boat sinks if water gets too high before time runs out; forfeiting or sinking both dump you on the beach near fairy ring DJP for 1-2 damage. Reward hinges on a 0-255 contribution meter earned via those 4 actions (bailing=2pts, leak=5pts, net=7pts, kraken chop=10pts, rail fix=10pts) -- need 50+ to search the net afterward for fish+xp+angler roll; the meter never depletes so you can coast once past 50. Full meter doubles fish yield and heron odds but does NOT increase angler-piece odds past the 50pt threshold. Starting a voyage is an Ardougne Diary Easy task; catching+cooking a manta ray in Port Khazard is an Elite task. Solo play yields more fish/xp per trip (server load spread over fewer players) but official co-op worlds are the relaxed/faster angler-outfit route per the wiki's own framing.

name: Trouble Brewing

skills.Cooking: 40 (hard gate to access the minigame at all)

items: Nothing carried in -- inventory, equipped head slot, and any followers must all be EMPTY to sign up (deposit at the bank chest near Honest Jimmy first); weight-reducing clothing and an m'speak amulet (unneeded once Monkey Madness II is done) are recommended but not required

req_items.name: M'speak amulet
req_items.note: recommended but not required; unneeded once Monkey Madness II is completed

xp.Crafting: 100 for contributing any non-water ingredient once per game, plus small per-action amounts (12.5-50) for repairing facilities
xp.Cooking: 250 for contributing an ingredient once per game, plus 100-200 per individual ingredient added to a hopper
xp.Firemaking: 150 for contributing an ingredient once per game, plus 50-100 per lit boiler log / doused fire / sabotage
xp.other: Hunter xp for gathering sweetgrubs (40) and bitternuts (50), Fletching xp for scrapey tree bark (50), Agility+Hunter xp for capturing the saboteur monkey (15 each), Strength xp for pumping boiler pressure (25)

items: 10 pieces of eight per bottle of rum the team brews (flat, regardless of individual contribution) plus up to 100 more scaled by your own contribution points -- halved if you auto-joined at the game's 10-minute midpoint
unlocks: Honest Jimmy's shop (39,590 total points to clear it): coloured naval shirts/tricorn hats/slacks in 7 colours, 6 pirate flags, 'the stuff' (boosts personal-brewing mature-ale odds), red/blue rum (consumable teleports back to the minigame), and a sellable medium clue scroll

summary: Co-op resource-gathering minigame at Mos Le'Harmless where teams brew rum for pieces of eight and Honest Jimmy's cosmetic shop.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: 2+ player co-op resource-gathering and crafting minigame on the east side of Mos Le'Harmless (3813, 2973); games run 20 minutes with a 3-minute gap between them on the dedicated world (304).

facts.label: start
facts.value: Requires Cooking 40 (hard gate to access the minigame at all) and completion of the Cabin Fever quest; inventory, equipped head slot, and any followers must be empty to sign up.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Sign up with San Fan (blue team), Fancy Dan (red team), or Honest Jimmy (auto-balances to the smaller team); team members split into gathering roles (fetch water, dye it at colour stations, hunt sweetgrubs/bitternuts, chop scrapey bark) and processing roles (feed the boiler, keep its fire lit and pressure up, repair sabotaged/broken equipment, fend off a saboteur monkey) to fill hoppers that convert into rum bottles.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Payout is two-part: a flat 10 pieces of eight per rum bottle split across the whole team regardless of individual effort, plus up to 100 more pieces of eight scaled to a player's own contribution points (1 point per resource deposited, capped at 100; water buckets are the fastest point source, capping in about 3-4 minutes).

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Joining exactly at the 10-minute midpoint halves a player's pieces of eight but not their xp.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Crafting xp: 100 for contributing any non-water ingredient once per game, plus 12.5-50 per repair action. Cooking xp: 250 for contributing an ingredient once per game, plus 100-200 per ingredient added to a hopper. Firemaking xp: 150 for contributing an ingredient once per game, plus 50-100 per lit boiler log, doused fire, or sabotage.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Additional xp: Hunter for gathering sweetgrubs (40) and bitternuts (50), Fletching for scrapey tree bark (50), Agility and Hunter for capturing the saboteur monkey (15 each), and Strength for pumping boiler pressure (25).

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Honest Jimmy's shop, cleared at 39,590 total points, sells coloured naval shirts/tricorn hats/slacks in 7 colours, 6 pirate flags, 'the stuff' (boosts personal-brewing mature-ale odds), red/blue rum (consumable teleports back to the minigame), and a sellable medium clue scroll.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: A completed game (a win is not required) is a Morytania Diary Medium task.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The reward economy is entirely sunk into Honest Jimmy's cosmetic/QoL shop -- no combat and no skilling xp rate competitive with dedicated training; this is a social/cosmetic-farming minigame.

notes: 2+ player co-op resource-gathering + crafting minigame: sign up with San Fan (blue team), Fancy Dan (red team) or Honest Jimmy (auto-balances to the smaller team) after banking everything, including your headgear. Games run 20 minutes with a 3-minute gap between them on the dedicated world (304). Team members split into gathering roles (fetch water buckets, dye it red/blue at colour stations, hunt sweetgrubs/bitternuts, chop scrapey bark) and processing roles (feed a boiler, keep its fire lit and pressure up, repair sabotaged/broken equipment, fend off a saboteur monkey) to fill hoppers that convert into finished rum bottles. Payout is two-part: a flat 10 pieces-of-eight per rum bottle split across the whole team regardless of your personal effort, plus up to 100 more pieces-of-eight scaled to your own contribution points (1 point per resource deposited, capped at 100; water buckets are the fastest point source at ~3-4 min to cap). Late-joining at exactly the 10-minute mark halves your pieces-of-eight (not your xp). A completed game (win not required) is a Morytania Diary Medium task. Reward economy is entirely sunk into Honest Jimmy's cosmetic/QoL shop -- no combat, no skilling-xp-rate competitive with dedicated training, this is a social/cosmetic-farming minigame.

name: Castle Wars

items: Only Castle Wars tickets, potions, runes, and combat armour/weapons may be brought in -- no food (bandages spawn inside instead), no scarves/capes, no dwarf multicannon; alchemy and jewellery-enchant spells and Lunar heal spells are disabled during a match

rewards.xp: None guaranteed by the minigame itself -- ironmen specifically get zero combat xp inside Castle Wars (only splash Magic xp if using their own runes, not a rune pouch)
items: Castle Wars tickets per completed 15+ minute game, scaled by result and by whether you're on a dedicated Castle Wars world (shut-out win 4, regular win 3, scoreless draw 2, drawn-with-points 3 on CW worlds; one fewer each on non-CW worlds) -- both winner and loser earn some
1 plaudit per ticket earned on dedicated CW worlds only, redeemable with Lanthus for a Castle Wars supply crate
unlocks: Full cosmetic Ticket Exchange via Lanthus: red/white/gold decorative armour sets (5-40+ tickets, F2P-usable), Saradomin/Zamorak hoods+cloaks+banners+halos and Guthix halo (10-100 tickets, halos are members-only), decorative magic/ranged armour sets (members-only, 20-40 tickets each)

summary: Free-to-play safe-PvP capture-the-flag minigame between Saradomin and Zamorak castles, rewarding tickets for cosmetic gear.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Free-to-play (since 20 July 2017) safe-PvP capture-the-flag minigame, Saradomin vs Zamorak, 20-minute matches with 2+ players per side; entered west of Yanille (P2P entrance) or via the Ferox Enclave portal (F2P entrance) at (2407, 3105).

facts.label: start
facts.value: No skill, quest, or combat-level gate; only Castle Wars tickets, potions, runes, and combat armour/weapons may be brought in -- no food (bandages spawn inside instead), scarves, capes, or dwarf multicannon; alchemy, jewellery-enchant, and Lunar heal spells are disabled during a match.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Goal is to steal the enemy standard from the top floor of their 4-level castle and carry it back to your own castle's standard stand for a point; defenders can block with barricades, locked doors, and collapsible underground rock tunnels, or kill attackers outright.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Each castle has ground-floor gates (a breakable/barricadeable big door and a pickable/lockable small door), an outer-wall catapult, a team-only second-floor spawn room stocked with bandages, a third-floor trapdoor to that spawn room, a top flag floor, and an underground passage linking both castles' basements through a central cavern used for ranged/mage skirmishes.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Deaths inside the game are free -- no item loss, and players respawn inside the game.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: No xp is guaranteed by the minigame itself; ironmen specifically get zero combat xp inside Castle Wars, earning only splash Magic xp if casting with their own carried runes (not a rune pouch).

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Castle Wars tickets are earned per completed 15+ minute game, scaled by result and world type: a shut-out win pays 4, a regular win 3, a scoreless draw 2, and a drawn-with-points result 3 on dedicated Castle Wars worlds, one fewer each on non-CW worlds; both winner and loser earn some tickets.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: 1 plaudit is earned per ticket on dedicated Castle Wars worlds only, redeemable with Lanthus for a Castle Wars supply crate.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Lanthus's Ticket Exchange sells red/white/gold decorative armour sets (5-40+ tickets, usable in F2P), Saradomin/Zamorak hoods, cloaks, banners, and halos plus a Guthix halo (10-100 tickets, halos members-only), and decorative magic/ranged armour sets (members-only, 20-40 tickets each).

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: A full inventory blocks ticket pickup, and at least 15 minutes of participation is required to earn any tickets.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Entirely a PvP/cosmetics minigame -- no skill-training xp of note; the reward economy is pure decorative armour/capes/halos plus the plaudit resource-crate currency on dedicated worlds.

notes: Free-to-play (since 20 Jul 2017) safe-PvP capture-the-flag minigame, Saradomin vs Zamorak, 20-minute matches, 2+ per side. Goal: steal the enemy standard from the top floor of their 4-level castle and carry it back to your own castle's standard stand for a point; defenders block with barricades, locked doors, and collapsible underground rock tunnels or just kill attackers (deaths are free -- no item loss, respawn inside the game). Each castle has ground-floor gates (big door can be broken/barricaded, small door can be picked/locked), a catapult on the outer wall, a team-only second-floor spawn room with bandages (the in-game food substitute), a third-floor trapdoor to that spawn room, a flag floor on top, and an underground passage connecting both castles' basements through a central cavern useful for ranged/mage skirmishes. 15+ minutes of participation is required to earn any tickets, and a full inventory blocks ticket pickup. Entirely a PvP/cosmetics minigame -- no skill training xp of note, reward economy is pure decorative armour/capes/halos plus a resource-crate currency (plaudits) on dedicated worlds.

name: Soul Wars

items: No food, potions, capes, followers or non-combat items allowed into the one-time Nomad tutorial (mandatory before joining a real team) -- must not be poisoned/envenomed/diseased to enter it either

rewards.xp: Any combat skill at level 30+, redeemable directly from Zeal Tokens via Nomad (own choice of skill/amount)
items: Soul cape (2500 zeal, untradeable cosmetic/imbuable cape)
Ectoplasmator (250 zeal, upgrades Ava's device/max cape for ammo-saving)
Spoils of war caskets (30 zeal each)

Blighted supply sacks -- entangle/teleport/vengeance/ancient ice runes, manta ray, anglerfish, karambwan, super restore(4) -- all 10 zeal each, usable only within Wilderness/blighted-restricted content
unlocks: Equipment imbue services and combat-xp redemption via Nomad, all gated purely behind Zeal Tokens, no other progression unlocks

summary: 10v10 members PvP minigame on the Isle of Souls where Zeal Tokens buy combat xp and Nomad-shop cosmetics.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: 10v10 members-only team PvP minigame (Avatar of Destruction vs Avatar of Creation), 15-minute games, on the Isle of Souls, reached via the Soul Wars portal in the Edgeville/Ferox Enclave Dungeon (southwest of Edgeville bank) at (2206, 2900).

facts.label: start
facts.value: Requires 500 total level and 40 combat level, plus a one-time Nomad tutorial completion; no food, potions, capes, followers, or non-combat items are allowed into the tutorial, and the player must not be poisoned, envenomed, or diseased to enter it.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The regular lobby auto-balances combat level across teams and needs 10+ players per side to start (20 players in the lobby starts the game in 4 minutes, scaling down to instant at 120); a separate east lobby handles clan-challenge games capped at 30 zeal.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Objective is to reduce the enemy Avatar's 100% starting damage reduction to 0% by controlling the Soul Obelisk, sacrificing soul fragments dropped by wandering monsters there (worth only 1/3 contribution if the enemy team controls it), while defending your own Avatar by burying bones at graveyards to raise its damage reduction (same 1/3 penalty if the enemy holds that graveyard).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Team capes are locked on and make enemies the default left-click attack target; friendly fire is blocked entirely, and the minigame is PvP-enabled on PvP worlds just like the main game.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Zeal Tokens, the only reward currency, require staying on an activity bar that drains after about 3 minutes idle; letting it fully deplete ejects the player from the game with a 10-minute rejoin cooldown.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Zeal is earned by killing enemy players (scaled by relative combat level, diminishing on repeat kills of the same target), healing teammates, burying bones, sacrificing fragments, damaging the enemy Avatar, and capturing graveyards or the obelisk; NPC kills and barricade destruction earn zeal-bar activity credit but not zeal itself.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Losers earn up to 40 zeal, winners up to 60, and draws up to 50, so losing is not a wasted game.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Any combat skill at level 30+ can be trained by redeeming Zeal Tokens directly through Nomad, choosing skill and amount.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Zeal Token rewards from Nomad: Soul cape (2500 zeal, untradeable cosmetic/imbuable cape), Ectoplasmator (250 zeal, upgrades Ava's device/max cape for ammo-saving), Spoils of war caskets (30 zeal each), and Blighted supply sacks (entangle/teleport/vengeance/ancient ice runes, manta ray, anglerfish, karambwan, or super restore(4), all 10 zeal each, usable only within Wilderness/blighted-restricted content).

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: All Nomad rewards are gated purely behind Zeal Tokens; Soul Wars grants no other progression unlocks.

notes: 10v10 members-only team PvP minigame (Avatar of Destruction vs Avatar of Creation), 15-minute games, needs 500 total level + combat 40 and a one-time Nomad tutorial completion (no food/potions/capes/followers allowed in the tutorial, and it's PvP-enabled on PvP worlds just like the main game). Regular-lobby games auto-balance combat level across teams and need 10+ per side to start (20 players in lobby -> game starts in 4 min, scaling down to instant at 120); a separate east lobby handles clan-challenge games capped at 30 zeal. Objective: reduce the enemy Avatar's 100% starting damage reduction to 0% by controlling the Soul Obelisk (sacrificing soul fragments dropped by wandering monsters there -- only worth 1/3 contribution if the enemy team controls it) while defending your own Avatar by burying bones at graveyards to raise its damage reduction (same 1/3 penalty if the enemy holds that graveyard). Team capes are locked on and make enemies the default left-click attack target; friendly fire is blocked entirely. Zeal Tokens (the only reward currency) require staying on an activity bar that drains after ~3 min idle -- letting it fully deplete ejects you from the game with a 10-minute rejoin cooldown. Zeal is earned by killing enemy players (scaled by relative combat level, diminishing on repeat kills of the same target), healing teammates, burying bones, sacrificing fragments, damaging the enemy Avatar, and capturing graveyards/the obelisk -- NPC kills and barricade destruction earn zeal-bar activity credit but not zeal itself. Losers still earn up to 40 zeal, winners up to 60, draws up to 50 -- so losing isn't a wasted game.

name: Last Man Standing

rewards.xp: None -- LMS grants no skill xp at all; it is F2P-accessible even though it hands out members-only gear/spells for the game's duration only
items: Competitive mode: performance points (1st=6, 2nd=5, 3rd/4th=4, 5th-9th=3, 10th-19th=1; +2 bonus for 3 kills or +3 for 5 kills, non-stacking) spent at Justine's reward shop -- tradeable rewards require having won at least 1 game
High Stakes mode: winner takes 7,500,000 coins, runner-up 2,500,000 coins (claimed anytime from a chest near Justine); 3rd-5th place get their next High Stakes entry fee waived

summary: Battle-royale PvP minigame with randomized max-gear loadouts, rewarding reward-shop points or High Stakes coin prizes.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Battle-royale PvP minigame, 4-24 players, started via Lisa at Ferox Enclave (3142, 3636); matches instance onto either a Desert Island or Wild Varrock map.

facts.label: start
facts.value: No skill, quest, or item requirement to enter Casual mode (4+ players, no payout); Competitive mode needs 24 players on members worlds and requires 750 total level plus 30 quest points (or 1500 total level with fewer quest points); High Stakes needs the same gate plus a 500,000gp buy-in, is disabled for ironmen, and is toggled on/off at Jagex's discretion.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Real inventory and equipment are confiscated for the match; every participant is handed one of three randomized max-gear loadouts (Max/Med 99/99/85/99/99/99, Zerker 80/99/50/99/99/99, or 1 Def Pure 75/99/1/99/99/99 across Attack/Strength/Defence/Magic/Range/Hitpoints) with a full Ancient Magicks/Lunar/Standard spellbook choice at a supply chest, with no quest or level prerequisites to cast any of it in-game.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Killing players fully heals and refills your own supplies and earns a bloody key (a bloodier key if fewer than 6 players remain) for a loot chest roll on offensive or defensive tables.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: A shrinking safe-zone fog mechanic forces engagements: light fog begins after 1 minute, greater fog after 2:30 deals up to 10 damage per 2 ticks outside 16 tiles of the zone, and damage escalates rapidly after 10 minutes for anyone still outside it.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Forfeiting (logging out or taking the Desert Island rowboat) forfeits all reward; 3 forfeits in one day bans the player from Competitive and High Stakes for the rest of that day.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: LMS grants no skill xp at all, despite being F2P-accessible and handing out members-only gear/spells for the game's duration only -- this is the only place F2P accounts get to use members' weapons, special attacks, and Ancient Magicks.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Competitive mode performance points: 6 for 1st, 5 for 2nd, 4 for 3rd/4th, 3 for 5th-9th, 1 for 10th-19th, plus a non-stacking bonus of +2 for 3 kills or +3 for 5 kills; points are spent at Justine's reward shop, and tradeable rewards require having won at least 1 game.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: High Stakes mode: the winner takes 7,500,000 coins and the runner-up 2,500,000 coins, claimable anytime from a chest near Justine; 3rd-5th place get their next High Stakes entry fee waived.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: A pure PvP-skill/gear-simulator minigame with a coin-sink High Stakes economy -- no skilling relevance to a progression route beyond its own reward-shop cosmetics and gp.

notes: Battle-royale PvP minigame, 4-24 players, entirely gear-agnostic: real inventory/equipment is confiscated for the match and every participant is handed one of three randomized max-gear loadouts (Max/Med 99/99/85/99/99/99, Zerker 80/99/50/99/99/99, or 1 Def Pure 75/99/1/99/99/99 across Atk/Str/Def/Magic/Range/HP) with a full Ancient Magicks/Lunar/Standard spellbook choice at a supply chest, no quest/level prerequisites needed to cast any of it in-game. This is the only place F2P accounts get to use members' weapons, special attacks, and Ancient Magicks. Casual mode needs only 4 players and pays nothing; Competitive needs 24 players, is members-worlds-only, and requires 750 total level + 30 QP (or 1500 total level with fewer QP) to enter; High Stakes is the same gate plus a 500,000gp buy-in and is disabled for ironmen and toggled on/off at Jagex's discretion. In-match: kill players to fully heal/refill your own supplies and to earn a bloody key (bloodier key if <6 players remain) for a loot chest roll on offensive/defensive tables; a shrinking-safe-zone fog mechanic (light fog after 1 min, greater fog after 2:30 dealing up to 10 dmg/2 ticks outside 16 tiles of it, then escalating rapid damage after 10 min for anyone still outside the zone) forces engagements. Forfeiting (logout or Desert Island rowboat) forfeits all reward and 3 forfeits/day bans you from Competitive/High Stakes for the rest of that day. Overall this is a pure PvP-skill/gear-simulator minigame with a coin-sink High Stakes economy -- no skilling relevance to a progression route beyond its own reward-shop cosmetics/gp.

name: Tithe Farm

skills.Farming: 34 minimum (boostable, but the boost must be active both when taking seeds AND when planting them -- watering/harvesting doesn't need it); 54 to use Bologano seeds, 74 for Logavano seeds, all yield identical points/gameplay but higher xp per plant

items: A seed dibber (or use bare-handed planting)
6-8 watering cans recommended (or one Gricoller's can, a 1000-dose reward-shop upgrade, once you have points)

req_items.name: Watering can
req_items.note: recommended; or one Gricoller's can (a 1000-dose reward-shop upgrade) once points are available

rewards.xp: Farming, scaled by seed tier -- deposit-based with a 250x-harvest-rate bonus on the 75th fruit (1500/3500/5750 xp for Golovanova/Bologano/Logavano) and double xp on fruits 75-100 of each 100-batch
unlocks: Farmer Gricoller's Rewards shop, spent from points earned depositing fruit (1 point per 3 fruit, +2 bonus per 100-fruit sack fill, capped at 16,000 held points): full farmer's outfit (400 pts total across strawhat/jacket-or-shirt/boro trousers/boots), compost/supercompost, grape seeds, Bologa's blessings, herb box, seed pack, seed box, herb sack (needs Herblore 58), Gricoller's can, and Auto-weed toggle -- 1150 points clears the whole shop

summary: Solo instanced Farming minigame in Hosidius where depositing fruit earns Farming xp and points for Farmer Gricoller's shop.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Solo instanced Farming minigame in Hosidius, Great Kourend (1819, 3500).

facts.label: start
facts.value: Requires Farming 34 minimum to enter (boostable, but the boost must be active both when taking seeds and when planting them; watering and harvesting don't need it); 54 Farming is needed for Bologano seeds and 74 for Logavano seeds, though all tiers give identical points and gameplay, just different xp per plant.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: One seed tier (Golovanova/Bologano/Logavano) is chosen at the entrance and used for the whole session, up to 10,000 seeds; each planted patch must be watered 3 times before the 1, 2, and 3-minute marks or it wilts and dies, but a plant fully grown at 3 minutes can no longer die.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Gricoller's fertiliser, auto-given on entry, doubles growth rate but is generally considered less point/xp-efficient than the standard watering cycle.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Leaving through the door fully resets the instance (score and xp are kept, but inventory seeds/fruit are cleared); logging out mid-session keeps points and inventory but loses any crop still in the ground.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Farming xp is deposit-based and scaled by seed tier, with a 250x-harvest-rate bonus on the 75th fruit deposited (1500/3500/5750 xp for Golovanova/Bologano/Logavano) and double xp on fruits 75-100 of each 100-fruit batch.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Farmer Gricoller's Rewards shop is spent from points earned by depositing fruit (1 point per 3 fruit, plus a 2-point bonus per 100-fruit sack fill, capped at 16,000 held points): full farmer's outfit (400 points total across strawhat, jacket-or-shirt, boro trousers, boots), compost, supercompost, grape seeds, Bologa's blessings, herb box, seed pack, seed box, herb sack (needs Herblore 58), Gricoller's can, and an Auto-weed toggle; 1150 points clears the entire shop.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: A well-run 25-patch, 4-cycle-per-hour session earns roughly 35 points per 100-fruit harvest in about 19 minutes, meaning the full 1150-point shop takes roughly 10.5 hours of consistent play.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: No combat and no risk -- a purely repetitive plant/water/harvest/deposit loop, best framed as a single long uninterrupted Farming-xp-and-outfit grind rather than something interleaved in short bursts.

notes: Solo instanced Farming minigame: pick one seed tier at the entrance (Golovanova/Bologano/Logavano, gated by Farming 34/54/74, only one tier per session, up to 10,000 seeds), then plant into empty patches inside and water each 3 times (before the 1, 2, and 3-minute marks) or it wilts and dies -- once fully grown at 3 minutes a plant can no longer die. Gricoller's fertiliser (auto-given on entry) doubles growth rate but is generally considered less point/xp-efficient than the standard cycle per the page's own Strategy note. Harvested fruit gets deposited into sacks around the farm in batches up to 100 for Farming xp + points; leaving through the door fully resets the instance (score/xp kept, but inventory seeds/fruit are cleared), while logging out mid-session keeps your points/inventory but loses any crop still in the ground. A well-run 25x4 (25 patches x 4 cycles/hr) session earns roughly 35 points per 100-fruit harvest in ~19 minutes, meaning the full 1150-point shop takes roughly 10.5 hours of consistent play. No combat, no risk, purely a repetitive plant/water/harvest/deposit loop -- best framed in a route as a single long uninterrupted Farming-xp+outfit grind rather than something to interleave in short bursts.

name: Gnome Restaurant

skills.Cooking: 29 minimum, needed to complete the mandatory one-time tutorial (gnome-cooking with Aluft Gianne snr. + gnome-cocktails with Blurberry)

quests: The Grand Tree strongly recommended (not hard-gated, but most delivery destinations and NPCs assume it) -- Monkey Madness II recommended for the royal seed pod fast-travel back to the Grand Tree
items: The specific gnome food/cocktail ordered must be player-made (premade restaurant/bar versions are rejected) -- GE-bought player-made items ARE accepted though, and pre-stocking a bank tab of common orders is the standard efficiency trick

rewards.xp: None directly from deliveries -- the minigame trains Cooking only via the one-time tutorial recipes, not per-delivery
items: Coins tip on every easy delivery (2-500gp, flat regardless of delivery speed as long as you're within the time limit)

Hard-delivery tips: gnome goggles/scarf (~23/384 each), gnomeball (10/128), grand seed pod x5 (23/512), mint cake (23/512), herbs, uncut gems, rare runes, mithril/adamant/runite bolts, bird nest, raw oomlie, pure essence, snake charm, half-keys, gold ore, coal
unlocks: Reward token system: 1 point/easy delivery, 3 points/hard delivery, every 12 points banks a free random gnome food item on your token (up to 10 items/120 points stored) -- a pure QoL/food-farming mechanic, not permanent unlocks

summary: Solo gnome food/cocktail delivery minigame at the Grand Tree paying coin tips and rare item tips for completed deliveries.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Solo delivery-running minigame themed as gnome food delivery, started via Gianne jnr on the 1st floor of the Grand Tree (2437, 3500).

facts.label: start
facts.value: Requires Cooking 29 to complete the mandatory one-time tutorial (gnome-cooking with Aluft Gianne snr, gnome-cocktails with Blurberry); The Grand Tree quest is not hard-gated but strongly assumed by most delivery destinations and NPCs, and Monkey Madness II is recommended for the royal seed pod fast-travel back to the Grand Tree.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The one-time tutorial teaches 4 gnome dishes (ingredients bought from Hudo) and 4 gnome cocktails (ingredients from Heckel Funch); afterward Gianne jnr hands out repeatable delivery jobs, either easy (in/around the Grand Tree, 6-minute limit, 1 point) or hard (anywhere in Gielinor including aggressive-NPC and desert-heat zones, 11-minute limit, 3 points).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The specific gnome food or cocktail ordered must be player-made -- premade restaurant/bar versions are rejected, though player-made items bought from the Grand Exchange are accepted; pre-stocking a bank tab of common orders is a standard efficiency trick.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Declining a hard delivery costs a 5:30 waiting penalty unless that time is spent on an easy job instead; tip value is independent of delivery speed.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Easy deliveries pay a flat 2-500gp coin tip regardless of delivery speed, as long as the time limit is met. Hard-delivery tips include gnome goggles/scarf (~23/384 each), gnomeball (10/128), 5 grand seed pods (23/512), mint cake (23/512), plus herbs, uncut gems, rare runes, mithril/adamant/runite bolts, bird nest, raw oomlie, pure essence, snake charm, half-keys, gold ore, and coal.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: A reward token system banks 1 point per easy delivery and 3 per hard delivery; every 12 points banks a free random gnome food item on the token (up to 10 items/120 points stored) as a pure QoL/food-farming mechanic, not a permanent unlock.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Once 10 unused reward tokens are banked, Gianne tells the player to go on vacation, letting them freely re-roll delivery targets by re-opening dialogue without completing the job and never triggering the 5:30 penalty -- this lets a player cherry-pick only the hard-delivery targets tied to rarer unique tips instead of grinding random jobs.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: A completed delivery is a Western Provinces Diary Medium requirement.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: No inherent xp gain from play itself beyond the one-time tutorial's Cooking xp -- value is purely in the tip-table loot and the food-reward-token QoL system, making this an intermittent side-loop for gnome goggles/scarf/grand seed pods rather than a skilling method.

notes: Solo delivery-running minigame themed as a gnome food delivery service. One-time tutorial (Cooking 29+) teaches 4 gnome dishes via Aluft Gianne snr. (ingredients bought from Hudo) and 4 gnome cocktails via Blurberry (ingredients from Heckel Funch), after which Gianne jnr. hands out repeatable delivery jobs: easy (in/around Grand Tree, 6-min limit, 1 point) or hard (anywhere in Gielinor including aggressive-NPC and desert-heat zones, 11-min limit, 3 points). Declining a hard job costs a 5:30 waiting penalty unless you burn that time on an easy job instead. Tip value is independent of delivery speed. Key farming loophole documented on the wiki: once you've banked 10 unused reward tokens (Gianne tells you to go on vacation), you can freely re-roll delivery targets by re-opening dialogue without completing it and never trigger the 5:30 penalty -- this lets you cherry-pick only the hard-delivery targets tied to the rarer unique tips (goggles/scarf/seed pods) instead of grinding random jobs. A completed delivery is a Western Provinces Diary Medium requirement. No inherent xp gain from play itself -- value is purely in the tip-table loot and the food-reward-token QoL system, so it slots into a route as an intermittent side-loop for gnome goggles/scarf/grand seed pods rather than a skilling method.

name: Rogues' Den

skills.Agility: 50, unboostable, to attempt the maze (higher Agility/Thieving both increase the trap-drain buffer and thus the effective safe-crack success rate)
skills.Thieving: 50, unboostable, to attempt the maze (99 Thieving alone also bypasses the 50/50 Agility+Thieving gate on Martin Thwait's shop)

items: Nothing -- you must enter with an EMPTY inventory, no equipment, and no follower; a mystic jewel to leave at will is auto-granted on entry
Recommended but optional: energy/stamina potions before repeat maze runs

req_items.name: Energy potion
req_items.note: recommended but optional, for repeat maze runs

req_items.name: Stamina potion
req_items.note: recommended but optional, for repeat maze runs

xp.Agility: 12 xp per stat level drained navigating traps (can be farmed up by intentionally jumping back and forth at the final pendulum to drain Agility down near 0-5 before cracking the safe -- up to ~22k Agility xp/hour at 99 Agility/80 Thieving per the wiki's own rate)
xp.Thieving: 12 xp per stat level drained the same way, in smaller amounts

items: Rogue kit (one-time convertible into a rope, chisel, tinderbox, or spade) OR a Rogue's equipment crate letting you pick one piece: rogue mask (+5 all defensive), top (+10), trousers (+7), gloves (+2), boots (+2) -- crate can alternatively be redeemed as a kit instead
unlocks: Full rogue outfit: 15% chance per piece worn (guaranteed at full set) to pickpocket double loot from any NPC -- xp is unaffected, loot quantity only

Martin Thwait's Lost and Found shop (50 Thieving+50 Agility or 99 Thieving) -- buys items at High Alch price rather than Low Alch, notable gp-per-item sink/source, plus sells the Thieving cape/hood at 99 Thieving
Storable in the POH costume room armour case once the full set is owned

summary: Solo trap-navigation maze under Burthorpe that drains Agility/Thieving for xp and rewards a rogue-outfit piece on a cracked safe.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Solo trap-navigation maze under Burthorpe, entered via a trapdoor near the bartender inside the Toad and Chicken inn (2906, 3537).

facts.label: start
facts.value: No entry requirement to walk into the Den itself (its shops, bank, and wall safes for uncut-gem cracking outside the maze are open at any level), but the maze proper requires unboostable 50 Agility and 50 Thieving; higher Agility/Thieving beyond that increase the trap-drain buffer and thus the effective safe-crack success rate. A player must enter with an empty inventory, no equipment, and no follower; a mystic jewel to leave at will is auto-granted on entry.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Route: run a fixed sequence past floor traps, pendulums, spinning blades, and wall traps using stand-then-run timing tricks (an 80-Thieving shortcut skips a long mid-maze detour), disable specific wall traps via right-click Search-wall, use flash powder on a guard near the end to stun and pass, then crack one of the exit wall safes to both claim a reward and teleport out.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Safe-crack success is a hidden roll against the player's current (drained) Thieving stat; failing has a 50% chance of ejection with no reward and a 50% chance of a further 1-point Thieving drain followed by a re-roll, so repeated attempts without resting Thieving compound the risk.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: A successful safe crack splits 3/8 into a rogue kit and 5/8 into an equipment crate; the rogue kit is a one-time convertible into a rope, chisel, tinderbox, or spade, while the equipment crate lets the player pick one piece (rogue mask +5 all defensive, top +10, trousers +7, gloves +2, boots +2) and can alternatively be redeemed as a kit instead.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Agility and Thieving each grant 12 xp per stat level drained while navigating traps; this can be farmed by intentionally jumping back and forth at the final pendulum to drain Agility down near 0-5 before cracking the safe, up to roughly 22,000 Agility xp/hour at 99 Agility/80 Thieving per the source page's own rate.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: The full rogue outfit gives a 15% chance per piece worn (guaranteed at a full set) to double pickpocket loot from any NPC; loot quantity only, xp is unaffected.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Martin Thwait's Lost and Found shop (50 Thieving + 50 Agility, or 99 Thieving alone) buys items at High Alch price rather than Low Alch and sells the Thieving cape/hood at 99 Thieving.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: The full rogue outfit is storable in a player-owned house costume room armour case once owned.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Expected time to a full rogue outfit set ranges from roughly 48 minutes at 50 Thieving down to roughly 22 minutes at 99 Thieving.

notes: Solo trap-navigation maze under Burthorpe. No entry requirement to walk into the Den itself (shops, bank, wall safes for uncut-gem cracking outside the maze are all open at any level), but the maze proper needs unboostable 50 Agility + 50 Thieving. Route: run a fixed sequence past floor traps, pendulums, spinning blades, and wall traps using stand-then-run timing tricks (with an 80-Thieving shortcut skipping a long mid-maze detour), disable specific wall traps via right-click Search-wall, use flash powder on a guard near the end to stun and pass, then crack one of the exit wall safes to both claim a reward AND teleport out. Crack success is a hidden roll against your CURRENT (drained) Thieving stat -- failing has a 50% chance of ejection with no reward and 50% chance of a further 1-point Thieving drain then a re-roll, so repeated attempts without resting Thieving compound the risk; success splits 3/8 into a rogue kit and 5/8 into an equipment crate. This dual-purposes as a fast, safe Agility/Thieving xp-drain farm (intentionally taking trap hits to drain stats near 0 maximizes the 12xp-per-level-drained reward) as well as the primary route to the rogue outfit's double-pickpocket-loot effect -- expected time to a full set ranges ~48 min at 50 Thieving down to ~22 min at 99 Thieving.

name: Brimhaven Agility Arena

skills.Agility: None to enter (any level can attempt some obstacles); 20 to pass the pressure-pad/floor-spike obstacles, 40 to pass spinning blades and darts -- higher Agility reaches more of the 25-pillar course's dispensers

items: 200 coins entry fee to Cap'n Izzy No-Beard, paid before each session

req_items.name: Coins
req_items.note: entry fee paid to Cap'n Izzy No-Beard before each session

xp.Agility: 30 xp per 10 Agility levels (including boosts/drains, capping at 300 xp if boosted to level 100) awarded directly for tagging an active dispenser, PLUS 345 xp per Agility arena ticket redeemed with Pirate Jackie the Fruit afterward (379.5 xp/ticket if wearing Karamja gloves 2/3/4 -- that +10% only applies to the ticket-exchange xp, not the per-tag xp or obstacle xp)

unlocks: Agility arena ticket + Brimhaven voucher per successfully-tagged active dispenser (10% chance of a double drop with Karamja Diary Elite completed)

Pirate Jackie the Fruit's voucher shop: toadflax (3), snapdragon (10), full graceful-outfit recolour (250, uncoloured pieces only -- won't recognise already-upgraded graceful), pirate's hook (800), amylase pack (60)

summary: Solo Agility minigame beneath Brimhaven where tagging the active ticket dispenser earns Agility xp and Pirate Jackie the Fruit vouchers.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Solo Agility minigame on an underground course beneath Brimhaven (2809, 3194), structurally different from lap-based courses: 25 pillars linked by agility obstacles, 24 of which hold a ticket dispenser, with exactly one dispenser 'active' at a time on a rotating 1-minute timer.

facts.label: start
facts.value: No Agility level is required to enter (any level can attempt some obstacles); 20 Agility is needed to pass the pressure-pad/floor-spike obstacles and 40 to pass spinning blades and darts, with higher Agility reaching more of the course's dispensers. A 200-coin entry fee is paid to Cap'n Izzy No-Beard before each session.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The player's task is to navigate obstacles to reach whichever dispenser just activated and tag it before it deactivates, earning direct Agility xp scaled to current Agility level (boosts included) plus a ticket and voucher pair banked for later exchange.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Best xp/hour comes from continuously running obstacles during the dead time between dispenser activations rather than standing idle waiting.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Tagging an active dispenser awards 30 xp per 10 Agility levels (including boosts/drains), capping at 300 xp if boosted to level 100; each Agility arena ticket separately converts to a flat, level-independent 345 Agility xp when redeemed with Pirate Jackie the Fruit (379.5 xp with Karamja gloves 2/3/4 -- the +10% applies only to this ticket-exchange xp, not the per-tag or obstacle xp).

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Each successfully-tagged active dispenser drops an Agility arena ticket and a Brimhaven voucher (10% chance of a double drop with the Karamja Diary Elite completed).

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Pirate Jackie the Fruit's voucher shop sells toadflax (3), snapdragon (10), a full graceful-outfit recolour (250, uncoloured pieces only -- won't recognise an already-upgraded graceful outfit), a pirate's hook (800), and amylase packs (60).

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: No combat xp or gear rewards -- purely an Agility-xp and cosmetic/herb sink.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Fastest known access is the Achievement Diary cape teleport directly to Pirate Jackie the Fruit; a player-owned house in Brimhaven with teleport-to-house is the next-best option if available.

notes: Solo Agility minigame, structurally different from lap-based courses: 25 pillars linked by agility obstacles, 24 holding a ticket dispenser, and exactly one dispenser is 'active' at a time on a rotating 1-minute timer. The player's job is to navigate obstacles to reach whichever dispenser just activated and tag it before it deactivates, earning both direct Agility xp (scaled to current Agility level, boosts included) and a ticket+voucher pair banked for later exchange. Best xp/hour comes from continuously running obstacles during the dead time between dispenser activations rather than standing idle waiting. Tickets convert to a flat, level-independent 345 Agility xp each (379.5 with Karamja gloves 2-4) via Pirate Jackie the Fruit, while vouchers buy herbs, the graceful-outfit recolour, a pirate's hook, and amylase packs -- no combat XP or gear rewards, purely an Agility-xp + cosmetic/herb sink. Fastest known access is the Achievement Diary cape teleport to Pirate Jackie the Fruit himself; POH-in-Brimhaven teleport-to-house is the next best if available.

name: Ava's device

items: Vorkath's head (assembler tier only, obtainable after Dragon Slayer II)

req_items.name: Vorkath's head
req_items.note: required only for the assembler tier; obtainable after Dragon Slayer II

rewards.effect: Worn backpack that auto-recovers a percentage of fired Ranged ammunition (arrows/bolts/darts/javelins/throwing knives/axes/toktz-xil-ul) each kill, scaling by tier: attractor (req 30 Ranged) lowest recovery, accumulator (50 Ranged) mid, assembler (70 Ranged + Vorkath's head) highest. Also usable in Castle Wars (on Lanthus) and Soul Wars (on Nomad) to recover ammo there.

summary: Ranged ammo-recovery backpack unlocked via Animal Magnetism, with three tiers gated by Ranged level.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Obtained from the NPC Ava after completing the Animal Magnetism quest; no additional gp cost beyond the quest.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Worn backpack that auto-recovers a percentage of fired Ranged ammunition (arrows, bolts, darts, javelins, throwing knives, axes, toktz-xil-ul) on each kill; recovery scales by tier: attractor (30 Ranged) lowest, accumulator (50 Ranged) mid, assembler (70 Ranged plus Vorkath's head) highest.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Also usable in Castle Wars (upgraded on Lanthus) and Soul Wars (upgraded on Nomad) to recover ammo in those minigames.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Does not recover ammo consumed on use (chinchompas, holy water) or weapon charges (blowpipe scales, webweaver ether) -- a passive cost-reduction accelerant for arrow/bolt/dart-based Ranged training rather than a blocking dependency.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The assembler tier is gated behind a second quest, Dragon Slayer II, for its Vorkath's head component; a fresh account may reach 70 Ranged well before finishing Dragon Slayer II.

notes: Own words: single quest gate (Animal Magnetism) plus a Ranged-level threshold per tier, no gp cost beyond the quest itself -- obtained directly from the NPC Ava. Does not recover consumed-on-use ammo (chinchompas, holy water) or weapon charges (blowpipe scales, webweaver ether), so it's a passive cost-reduction accelerant for arrow/bolt/dart-based Ranged training rather than a blocking dependency for anything else researched in this KB; the assembler tier is gated behind a second quest (Dragon Slayer II) for its head component, worth flagging since a fresh account may reach 70 Ranged well before finishing DS2.

name: Dramen staff

skills.crafting: 31 (to fashion the branch into a staff with a knife)

items: Dramen branch (cut from a Dramen tree on Entrana), knife

req_items.name: Dramen branch
req_items.note: cut from a Dramen tree on Entrana

rewards.effect: A weak blunt staff whose real value is enabling entry to Zanaris (via the Lumbridge Swamp shed) and activating the fairy-ring transport network -- required in-hand to use any fairy ring until the player has also finished the Elite Lumbridge & Draynor Diary, after which fairy rings work staff-free. Also usable during The Fremennik Trials to defeat Koschei the Deathless by smuggling in a branch+knife, and is superseded functionally (better stats, autocast) by the lunar staff upgrade from Lunar Diplomacy.

summary: Staff crafted from a Dramen branch after Lost City that enables Zanaris entry and fairy-ring transport.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Crafted at Crafting 31 by using a knife on a Dramen branch cut from a Dramen tree on Entrana, after completing the Lost City quest.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Enables entry to Zanaris via the Lumbridge Swamp shed and activates the fairy-ring transport network; required in-hand to use any fairy ring until the player has also completed the Elite Lumbridge & Draynor Diary, after which fairy rings work staff-free.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Also usable during the Fremennik Trials to defeat Koschei the Deathless by smuggling in a branch and knife.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Functionally superseded (better stats, autocast) by the lunar staff upgrade obtained from the Lunar Diplomacy quest.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Documents the item half of the fairy-ring transport network's item gate, which accepts either a Dramen staff or a Lunar staff.

facts.label: caveat

notes: Own words: this is the physical item half of unlockdb:fairy-ring's item gate -- that entry already lists 'Dramen staff or Lunar staff' as a requirement, so this row exists to document how the staff itself is obtained (cut a branch on Entrana post-Lost-City, fletch/knife it at Crafting 31) rather than to introduce a new hard dependency. Cheap to duplicate (advise crafting a spare immediately in case of loss) since Entrana access and the Dramen tree are both freely available after the quest.
