req_items.name: Slayer reward points
req_items.note: spent unlocking the 'Malevolent masquerade' combination ability

rewards.effect: A single headgear combining the special effects of all six Slayer-reward face/head items it's crafted from (nose peg smell immunity, facemask, earmuffs, spiny helmet, enchanted gem, black mask's 16.67% Attack/Strength boost vs assigned Slayer tasks), plus rune-full-helm-equivalent defensive stats. Doubles as warm clothing for Wintertodt and blocks Nex's infection virus attack. Can later be imbued (Soul Wars/Nightmare Zone/Emir's Arena) to add a 15% Ranged and Magic accuracy+damage boost on task, stacking the whole kit into one slot.

summary: Slayer-reward headgear that combines six face/head items' effects into one slot, craftable at Crafting 55.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Crafted at Crafting 55 (boostable) by combining a nose peg, facemask, earmuffs, spiny helmet, enchanted gem, and black mask; requires Defence 10 to equip.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Combines the special effects of all six Slayer-reward face/head items it's crafted from (nose peg smell immunity, facemask, earmuffs, spiny helmet, enchanted gem, and the black mask's 16.67% Attack/Strength boost versus assigned Slayer tasks), plus rune-full-helm-equivalent defensive stats.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Doubles as warm clothing for Wintertodt and blocks Nex's infection virus attack.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Can later be imbued (via Soul Wars, Nightmare Zone, or Emir's Arena) to add a 15% Ranged and Magic accuracy and damage boost on task, stacking the whole kit into one equipment slot.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: A gear-consolidation unlock, not a blocking one -- the underlying six items must already be owned, each with its own Slayer-point, quest, or drop gate; the helmet merges their perks into a single slot plus grants the task-only combat boost the black mask alone doesn't confer outside Slayer assignments.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Reinforced goggles (requiring the A Porcine of Interest quest) are only needed as one of the six component items and are not otherwise a hard gate on the base helmet.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The helmet's Attack/Strength bonus does not stack with Salve amulet variants against undead tasks -- a route should not recommend using both at once.

notes: Own words: this is a gear-consolidation unlock, not a blocking one -- the underlying six items must already be owned (each has its own Slayer-point/quest/drop gate), the helmet just merges their perks into a single equipment slot plus grants the task-only combat boost the black mask alone doesn't confer outside Slayer assignments. The A Porcine of Interest quest requirement for reinforced goggles is itself optional to the base helmet (only needed at the point that quest exists as a component-item source), and the helmet's Attack/Strength bonus does not stack with Salve amulet variants against undead tasks, worth flagging so a route doesn't recommend both at once.

name: Ring of dueling

skills.magic: Lvl-2 Enchant castable on an emerald ring (low-level, no quest gate on the spell itself)

rewards.effect: 8-charge teleport ring to Emir's Arena (Duel Arena), Castle Wars, Ferox Enclave, and (after reaching Hero rank / 12,000 glory at the Fortis Colosseum) the Colosseum itself -- crumbles to dust on the 8th use. Ferox Enclave teleport additionally grants a free full heal/prayer/run-energy restore via the Pool of Refreshment on arrival.

summary: Enchanted emerald ring giving 8 charges of teleport to Emir's Arena, Castle Wars, Ferox Enclave, and the Fortis Colosseum.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Made by casting Lvl-2 Enchant on an emerald ring; the spell itself has a low Magic level requirement and no quest gate.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Provides 8 charges of teleport to Emir's Arena (Duel Arena), Castle Wars, and Ferox Enclave, crumbling to dust on the 8th use; a 4th destination, the Fortis Colosseum, unlocks after reaching Hero rank (12,000 glory) at the Colosseum.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The Ferox Enclave teleport additionally grants a free full heal, prayer restore, and run-energy restore via the Pool of Refreshment on arrival.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The cheapest of the enchanted-ring travel items, needing no quest to make or use; primarily valued for the Ferox Enclave stop's free restore and proximity to Wilderness boss/skilling routes.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The Fortis Colosseum destination is a later unlock gated behind Colosseum glory rank, not available from a freshly made ring.

notes: Own words: cheapest of the enchanted-ring travel items (Lvl-2 Enchant, no quest needed to make or use one), primarily valued for the Ferox Enclave stop -- free full restore plus proximity to the Wilderness boss/skilling routes already documented elsewhere in this KB (bg: herb/compost runs). The Fortis Colosseum destination is a later unlock gated behind Colosseum glory rank, not available from a fresh ring, so route synthesis should not assume it's live from level 1.

name: Games necklace

skills.magic: Lvl-1 Enchant castable on a sapphire necklace (very low level, no quest gate)

rewards.effect: 8-charge teleport necklace to Burthorpe, Barbarian Outpost, Corporeal Beast, Tears of Guthix, and the Wintertodt Camp -- crumbles to dust on the 8th use. On the last charge it's recommended to alch it instead (alch value is charge-independent) rather than use the final teleport.

summary: Enchanted sapphire necklace giving 8 charges of teleport to Burthorpe, Barbarian Outpost, Corporeal Beast, Tears of Guthix, and Wintertodt Camp.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Made by casting Lvl-1 Enchant on a sapphire necklace; a very low Magic level with no quest gate.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Provides 8 charges of teleport to Burthorpe, Barbarian Outpost, Corporeal Beast, Tears of Guthix, and the Wintertodt Camp, crumbling to dust on the 8th use.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: On the last charge it's recommended to alch the necklace instead of using the final teleport, since alch value is charge-independent.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The cheapest enchant-tier travel jewellery alongside the ring of dueling; its destination spread is training/boss-access oriented rather than general travel, so its value is highly dependent on which of Wintertodt, Corporeal Beast, or the other listed activities the account is actually doing.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Tears of Guthix requires its own quest-count gate to be worth visiting.

notes: Own words: cheapest enchant-tier travel jewellery alongside the ring of dueling (Lvl-1 Enchant, trivial Magic level, no quest). Its destination spread is training/boss-access oriented rather than general travel -- Wintertodt Camp and Corporeal Beast are the two most-used stops for accounts already engaged with those activities, and Tears of Guthix requires its own quest-count gate to be worth visiting, so this ring's value is highly dependent on which of those five activities the account is actually doing.

name: Amulet of glory

skills.magic: Lvl-5 Enchant castable on a dragonstone amulet (no quest gate on the spell itself)

quests: Heroes' Quest (required to recharge at the Fountain of Heroes/Fountain of Rune; Dragon Slayer II additionally unlocks recharging at the Fountain of Uhld)

rewards.effect: 4-charge (6 if recharged at the Fountain of Rune) teleport amulet to Edgeville, Karamja, Draynor Village, and Al Kharid, rechargeable indefinitely once its quest gate is met, plus flat melee/ranged/magic damage-style combat bonuses while worn. Its dragonstone teleports still function up to level 30 Wilderness, unlike most teleports which cut off at level 20.

summary: Enchanted dragonstone amulet giving rechargeable teleports to Edgeville, Karamja, Draynor Village, and Al Kharid plus combat bonuses.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Made by casting Lvl-5 Enchant on a dragonstone amulet; the amulet can be made and worn immediately for its combat bonuses with no quest requirement, but can only be recharged after completing Heroes' Quest.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Provides 4 charges of teleport (6 if recharged at the Fountain of Rune) to Edgeville, Karamja, Draynor Village, and Al Kharid, plus flat melee/ranged/magic damage-style combat bonuses while worn; rechargeable indefinitely once the quest gate is met.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Its dragonstone teleports still function up to level 30 Wilderness, unlike most teleports which cut off at level 20.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Recharging requires Heroes' Quest, a mid-length quest with its own prerequisite chain (Shield of Arrav, Lost City, Merlin's Crystal, Dragon Slayer I); a fresh account effectively cannot sustain glory-teleport use until that chain is cleared. Dragon Slayer II additionally unlocks recharging at the Fountain of Uhld.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Popular for its combined Al Kharid/Draynor/Karamja/Edgeville destination spread (banking, GE-adjacent, minigame, and desert-quest travel in one item) and its up-to-30-Wilderness range, making it a strong candidate for an early steer point once Heroes' Quest is in reach.

notes: Own words: the amulet itself can be made and worn (for its combat bonuses) with zero quest requirement, but it can only be recharged after Heroes' Quest -- a mid-length quest with its own prerequisite chain (Shield of Arrav/Lost City/Merlin's Crystal/Dragon Slayer I) already flattened in this KB's questdb rows -- so a fresh account effectively cannot sustain glory-teleport use until that chain is cleared. Extremely popular for its Al Kharid/Draynor/Karamja/Edgeville spread (banking, GE-adjacent, minigame, and desert-quest travel in one item) and its up-to-30-Wilderness range, making it a strong candidate for an early 'soft' steer point once Heroes' Quest is in reach.

name: Skills necklace

skills.magic: Lvl-5 Enchant castable on a dragonstone necklace (no quest gate on the spell itself)

quests: Legends' Quest (required to recharge to 6 charges at the Fountain of Rune; base 4-charge recharge at the Legends' Guild totem pole / Fountain of Uhld / Recharge Dragonstone spell has no quest gate)

rewards.effect: 4-charge (6 at the Fountain of Rune post-Legends' Quest) teleport necklace to the Fishing, Mining, Crafting, Cooking, Woodcutting, and Farming Guilds -- a dedicated gathering-skill training hub network. Also up-to-30-Wilderness range like other dragonstone jewellery, and gives a small (+0.4 percentage point) casket bonus while worn/charged during big-net fishing.

summary: Enchanted dragonstone necklace giving rechargeable teleports to the six gathering-skill training guilds.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Made by casting Lvl-5 Enchant on a dragonstone necklace; base recharging (at the Legends' Guild totem pole, the Fountain of Uhld, or via the Recharge Dragonstone spell) needs no quest at all.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Provides 4 charges of teleport (6 at the Fountain of Rune after Legends' Quest) to the Fishing, Mining, Crafting, Cooking, Woodcutting, and Farming Guilds -- a dedicated gathering-skill training hub network.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Shares the up-to-30-Wilderness teleport range of other dragonstone jewellery and gives a small (+0.4 percentage point) casket bonus while worn/charged during big-net fishing.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Legends' Quest only adds 2 extra charges via the Fountain of Rune, making this a lighter-gated travel item than the amulet of glory despite sharing the same dragonstone-jewellery mechanic.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Its destination set is narrowly gathering-skill-guild-focused (no combat or quest-hub stops), making it a niche accelerant specifically for guild-gated training routes rather than a general-purpose hub.

notes: Own words: unlike the amulet of glory, base recharging (Legends' Guild totem pole or Fountain of Uhld or the Recharge Dragonstone spell) needs no quest at all -- Legends' Quest only adds the 2 extra charges via the Fountain of Rune, so this is a lighter-gated travel item than glory despite sharing the same dragonstone-jewellery mechanic. Its destination set is narrowly gathering-skill-guild-focused (no combat/quest-hub stops), making it a niche accelerant specifically for guild-gated training routes already synthesized elsewhere in this KB rather than a general-purpose hub.

name: Digsite pendant

skills.magic: 49 (or an enchant ruby or topaz tablet) to cast the pendant's own enchant spell, once learned

items: ruby necklace (enchant target); clean necklace found via Varrock Museum specimen-cleaning (~1/51 chance from an uncleaned find) to learn the enchant spell from a museum archaeologist

req_items.name: ruby necklace
req_items.note: enchant target used to create the pendant

req_items.name: clean necklace (from Varrock Museum specimen-cleaning)
req_items.note: roughly 1/51 chance from cleaning an uncleaned find; needed once to learn the enchant spell from a museum archaeologist

rewards.effect: 5-charge, non-rechargeable teleport necklace to north of the Digsite; two more destinations can be permanently unlocked (toggle on/off per pendant) by using any digsite pendant on strange machines -- House on the Hill on Fossil Island after Bone Voyage, and Lithkren near the rune dragons after Dragon Slayer II. A mounted version (needs 82 Construction + curator's medallion in a POH portal nexus) gives unlimited teleports to the same set.

summary: Digsite pendant is a 5-charge teleport necklace near the Digsite, made by casting an enchant spell learned via The Dig Site and a rare museum-cleaning drop.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: 5-charge, non-rechargeable teleport necklace to north of the Digsite, made by casting the pendant's own enchant spell on a ruby necklace.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: The Dig Site quest is required to access the Varrock Museum specimen-cleaning process that teaches the enchant spell; it does not guarantee the clean-necklace roll on any fixed timetable.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Casting the enchant spell needs Magic 49, or can be substituted with an enchant ruby or topaz tablet.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Two more permanent destinations toggle on/off per pendant when used on strange machines: House on the Hill on Fossil Island (needs Bone Voyage) and Lithkren near the rune dragons (needs Dragon Slayer II).

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: A mounted version giving unlimited teleports to the same destinations needs 82 Construction and a curator's medallion placed in a POH portal nexus.

notes: Own words: the enchant spell itself is a one-time learn gated behind a random museum-cleaning drop (~1/51) rather than a fixed quest step, which is worth flagging as an RNG-variable unlock rather than a deterministic one -- The Dig Site quest is required to access the museum/enchant process at all, but does not guarantee the clean-necklace roll on any fixed timetable. Its Fossil Island and Lithkren destination unlocks each layer an additional quest gate (Bone Voyage, Dragon Slayer II respectively) on top of the base pendant, so route synthesis should treat those two teleports as separately-gated bonus stops, not automatic.

name: Explorer's ring

items: Lumbridge & Draynor Diary, tier matching the ring number (1=easy..4=elite)

rewards.effect: Tiered ring (1-4) awarded by Hatius Cosaintus in Lumbridge for completing each Lumbridge & Draynor Diary tier; free to re-obtain if lost. The elite tier (ring 4) grants 30 free casts/day of Low or High Level Alchemy (no runes, no Magic xp, and since a 2025 QoL update items can be alched directly by using them on the ring) plus 3 free 100% run-energy restores/day and unlimited teleports to the Falador farm cabbage patch; lower tiers grant a subset of these plus other always-on diary-tier perks.

summary: Explorer's ring (tiers 1-4) is awarded by Hatius Cosaintus for each Lumbridge & Draynor Diary tier; the elite ring grants 30 free daily alchemy casts.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Tiered ring (1-4) awarded by Hatius Cosaintus in Lumbridge for completing the matching Lumbridge & Draynor Diary tier; free to re-obtain if lost.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The gate is Lumbridge & Draynor Diary tier completion rather than a craftable-item recipe; no separate quest or item requirement applies to the ring itself.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Elite tier (ring 4) grants 30 free casts/day of Low or High Level Alchemy with no runes or Magic xp cost, plus 3 free full run-energy restores/day and unlimited teleports to the Falador farm cabbage patch.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: A 2025 QoL update lets items be alched directly by using them on the elite ring instead of casting the spell manually.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Lower diary tiers grant a subset of the elite tier's perks plus other always-on diary-tier perks.

notes: Own words: unlike the other jewellery in this batch, the gate is entirely the Lumbridge & Draynor Achievement Diary tier system (own skill/quest reqs already out of scope for a per-item row, tracked separately if a diarydb: row exists for that diary), not a craftable-item recipe -- gate.quests/items are correctly empty here, the real prerequisite is diary-tier completion. The free daily high alchemy casts (elite tier) are a genuine gp-saving accelerant for any alching-heavy money-making or item-processing route already documented in this KB, worth noting as a recurring daily-reset resource rather than a one-time unlock.

name: Camulet

items: Ugthanki dung + bucket to recharge (dung obtained from certain camels after partial completion of My Arm's Big Adventure); optional 1,000,000 coins paid to Lazim for unlimited-charge upgrade

req_items.name: Ugthanki dung
req_items.note: obtained from certain camels after partial completion of My Arm's Big Adventure; used with a bucket to recharge the camulet

req_items.name: bucket
req_items.note: used with Ugthanki dung to recharge the camulet

req_items.name: coins
req_items.note: optional payment to Lazim for an unlimited-charge upgrade

rewards.effect: 4-charge (or unlimited, post gp-upgrade) teleport to Enakhra's Temple in the desert near Lazim; switchable to the temple entrance near the Bandit Camp Quarry once the Hard Desert Diary is complete. Also lets the wearer speak to certain camels (e.g. Al the Camel in Al Kharid) found across the Kharidian desert. Using it to reach Enakhra's Temple is itself a requirement of the Medium Desert Diary.

summary: Camulet is a 4-charge teleport necklace to Enakhra's Temple, rewarded from Enakhra's Lament and rechargeable with Ugthanki dung and a bucket.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: 4-charge teleport necklace to Enakhra's Temple in the desert near Lazim, awarded on completing Enakhra's Lament; free to reclaim from Lazim if lost.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Recharges with Ugthanki dung (from certain camels after partial completion of My Arm's Big Adventure) plus a bucket; an optional 1,000,000 coin payment to Lazim upgrades it to unlimited charges.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Switches to teleport near the Bandit Camp Quarry entrance instead, once the Hard Desert Diary is complete.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Using the camulet to reach Enakhra's Temple is itself a requirement of the Medium Desert Diary.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Also lets the wearer speak to certain camels (e.g. Al the Camel in Al Kharid) found across the Kharidian desert.

notes: Own words: direct quest reward (Enakhra's Lament), free to reclaim from Lazim if lost, and can be duplicated via the drop trick though all copies share one charge pool. It's a narrow desert-region teleport rather than a general hub, but it feeds two other systems already in this KB's scope: the Medium Desert Diary requires actually using it once, and the Hard Desert Diary upgrades its destination -- so its usefulness compounds with diary progress rather than being static.

name: Ectophial

rewards.effect: One-click, unlimited-use teleport to the Ectofuntus outside Port Phasmatys -- empties then auto-refills on use (player is immune to damage, though PvP-world attackers still gain xp on a landed hit during the refill animation). Free to replace from Velorina in Port Phasmatys (needs a ghostspeak amulet or better) or buy from Perdu for a flat fee if lost.

summary: Ectophial is an unlimited-use quest reward from Ghosts Ahoy that teleports to the Ectofuntus outside Port Phasmatys, auto-refilling after each use.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: One-click, unlimited-use teleport to the Ectofuntus outside Port Phasmatys, awarded on completing Ghosts Ahoy with no further gate.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Empties then auto-refills on use; the player is immune to damage during the refill animation, though PvP-world attackers still gain xp on a landed hit.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Free to replace from Velorina in Port Phasmatys (needs a ghostspeak amulet or better) or bought from Perdu for a flat fee if lost.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Like other teleports in this KB it stops working above level 20 Wilderness.

notes: Own words: unlimited free reuse (no charge count, unlike most teleport jewellery in this batch) makes it a strong standing Morytania/Slayer/Prayer-training access point and a cheap combat-escape button once Ghosts Ahoy is done -- a single quest reward with no further gate. Like other teleports in this KB it stops working above level 20 Wilderness, worth flagging since it's sometimes proposed as an emergency Wilderness escape.

name: Book of the dead

items: 9,500 coins to buy an extra copy from Perdu, if wanted

req_items.name: coins
req_items.note: buys an extra copy from Perdu, optional

rewards.effect: Equipable book required in inventory/worn to cast resurrection spells (Necromancy). Carries the same teleport set as Kharedst's memoirs (the five Kourend House teleports) plus 150 extra charge capacity (250 max once all 5 Kharedst pages are collected, vs. the memoirs' 100), making it a strict upgrade once obtained. Free to reclaim from Commander Fullore in Kourend Castle if lost.

summary: Book of the dead is required to cast Necromancy resurrection spells, awarded via A Kingdom Divided with Kharedst's memoirs teleports plus more charges.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Equipable book required to cast resurrection spells (Necromancy); awarded on completing A Kingdom Divided.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: A Kingdom Divided is a hard prerequisite for casting any resurrection spell, since the book is the item-form gate for that spell family.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Carries the same teleport destinations as Kharedst's memoirs (the five Kourend House teleports) with 150 extra charge capacity (250 max once all 5 Kharedst pages are collected, vs. the memoirs' 100).

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Free to reclaim from Commander Fullore in Kourend Castle if lost; an extra copy can be bought from Perdu for 9,500 coins.

notes: Own words: this is the item-form gate for casting any resurrection spell, so any future Necromancy-training research in this KB should treat A Kingdom Divided as a hard prerequisite for that specific spell family even though the book itself is only a soft travel/utility upgrade over the already-documented unlockdb:kharedsts-memoirs. Since it inherits the memoirs' exact destinations, no new travel-hub facts beyond 'same network, bigger charge pool' needed re-deriving here.

name: Rada's blessing

items: Kourend & Kebos Diary, tier matching the blessing number (1=easy..4=elite); 200gp each from Perdu to buy extra copies once one tier is unlocked

req_items.name: coins
req_items.note: buys an extra copy from Perdu once a tier is unlocked, optional

rewards.effect: Ammo-slot item awarded by Elise in the Kourend Castle courtyard for each Kourend & Kebos Diary tier; free to reclaim if lost. Elite tier (blessing 4) gives the best-in-slot ammo-slot Prayer bonus (+2, beats god blessings), unlimited teleports to both Kourend Woodland and Mount Karuulm, and an 8% chance per catch to land an extra fish everywhere (no bonus xp/bait). Lower tiers grant a subset (smaller Prayer bonus, fewer/no teleport destinations, lower fish-bonus chance).

summary: Rada's blessing (tiers 1-4) is an ammo-slot item from Elise per Kourend & Kebos Diary tier; the elite tier gives the best ammo-slot Prayer bonus plus teleports.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Ammo-slot item awarded by Elise in the Kourend Castle courtyard for each Kourend & Kebos Diary tier; free to reclaim if lost.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The gate is Kourend & Kebos Diary tier completion rather than a craftable-item recipe.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Elite tier (blessing 4) gives the best-in-slot ammo-slot Prayer bonus (+2, beats god blessings), unlimited teleports to both Kourend Woodland and Mount Karuulm, and an 8% chance per catch to land an extra fish everywhere (no bonus xp/bait).

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Lower tiers grant a subset: smaller Prayer bonus, fewer/no teleport destinations, lower fish-bonus chance.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Stackable in the ammo slot alongside main-hand/off-hand gear, so it does not conflict with weapon choice.

notes: Own words: like the explorer's ring, the real gate is Kourend & Kebos Diary tier completion rather than a craftable-item recipe, so gate.quests/items reflect the diary-tier requirement plus the trivial gp cost of extra copies rather than a quest chain. Stackable in the ammo slot alongside main-hand/off-hand gear (no weapon conflict), making it a low-opportunity-cost Prayer/fishing/travel accelerant for any Kourend-region route once diary progress supports it.

name: Barrows gloves

skills.defence: 35 minimum in practice (from quest-req/xp-reward accumulation across the RFD chain; no explicit Defence requirement on the gloves themselves)

quests: Recipe for Disaster (entire quest, all subquest chapters)
items: 130,000 coins paid to the Culinaromancer's Chest in the Lumbridge Castle cellar (104,000 after the Elite Lumbridge & Draynor Diary)

req_items.name: coins
req_items.note: paid to the Culinaromancer's Chest in the Lumbridge Castle cellar; 104,000 after the Elite Lumbridge & Draynor Diary

rewards.effect: Best-available gloves for most accounts: second-highest Melee offensive bonuses (behind ferocious gloves), second-highest Ranged offensive bonuses (behind zaryte vambraces), third-highest Magic offensive bonuses (behind tormented bracelet/confliction gauntlets) -- strong across all three combat styles at once, with no Defence requirement to equip.

summary: Barrows gloves are strong all-combat-style gloves bought for 130,000 coins after finishing the entire Recipe for Disaster quest chain.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Second-highest Melee offensive bonuses (behind ferocious gloves), second-highest Ranged offensive bonuses (behind zaryte vambraces), third-highest Magic offensive bonuses (behind tormented bracelet/confliction gauntlets), with no Defence requirement to equip.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Requires completing the entire Recipe for Disaster quest chain (10 subquest chapters plus the master quest), one of the largest single requisite bundles in the game.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: In practice needs roughly Defence 35, accumulated from RFD's own quest/xp requirements rather than a stated explicit Defence requirement on the gloves.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Bought for 130,000 coins from the Culinaromancer's Chest in the Lumbridge Castle cellar (104,000 after the Elite Lumbridge & Draynor Diary).

facts.label: hazard

notes: Own words: treated as hard because the entire Recipe for Disaster quest chain (10 subquests + master quest) is one of the largest single requisite bundles in the game and is explicitly framed by the wiki itself as a long-term goal 'every beginner should work toward' -- many later combat-training and boss routes in this KB implicitly assume best-in-slot-adjacent gloves are available. In PvP death without protection they revert to 78,000 coins rather than dropping the item itself, worth noting for risk-calc steps elsewhere in the KB.

name: Fire cape

items: completed run of the TzHaar Fight Cave, ending in a kill on TzTok-Jad (must be removed from the cave by the game recognising Jad's death, not the player's own death, even simultaneous deaths do not award it)

rewards.effect: Second-best melee cape in the game (behind only the infernal cape). Also functions as the access key to deeper Mor Ul Rek (shown to TzHaar-Ket guards, not consumed) and is the one-time entry fee sacrificed to TzHaar-Ket-Keh to unlock the Inferno, gateway to the infernal cape.

summary: Fire cape is the second-best melee cape, earned by killing TzTok-Jad at the end of the TzHaar Fight Cave; it is also the entry item for the Inferno.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Second-best melee cape in the game (behind only the infernal cape), earned by completing a run of the TzHaar Fight Cave ending in a kill on TzTok-Jad.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Award requires the game to recognise Jad's death, not the player's own death; even simultaneous deaths do not award the cape.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Sacrificed to TzHaar-Ket-Keh as the one-time entry fee to unlock the Inferno, gateway to the infernal cape.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Functions as the access key to deeper Mor Ul Rek, shown to TzHaar-Ket guards, not consumed by that use.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: No formal skill/quest/item prerequisite exists for entry -- the gate is purely a combat-performance check (survive every Fight Cave wave then kill Jad).

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: If lost unprotected in PvP it becomes a repairable broken cape (Perdu, 150,000 coins) rather than being destroyed outright.

notes: Own words: no formal skill/quest/item prerequisite exists on the wiki page itself -- the gate is purely a combat-performance check (survive every Fight Cave wave then kill Jad), making this the one unlock in this batch whose 'requirement' is proficiency rather than a checkable stat/quest. Treated as hard because it is both a widely-used mid-game melee-cape milestone in its own right and a literal, consumed prerequisite item for entering the Inferno -- any future inferno/infernal-cape research node in this KB has a hard dependency on this row. If lost unprotected in PvP it becomes a repairable broken cape (Perdu, 150,000 coins) rather than being destroyed outright.

name: Void Knight equipment

Elite Void's extra requirement (Hard Western Provinces Diary) is a second, separate gate layered on top of the base set, not needed for basic void.

name: Shades of Mort'ton (minigame)

quests: Shades of Mort'ton (the quest unlocks the repeatable minigame; using a Serum 208 dose on Razmire Keelgan during/after the quest is highly recommended for permanent shop access)
items: Hammer (imcando or Flamtaer hammer strongly preferred -- Flamtaer is significantly faster), building materials (timber beam/plank, limestone brick, swamp paste -- bought from Razmire or GE), a flamtaer bag recommended to hold up to 60 beams/60 bricks/500 paste, a tinderbox and appropriate-tier pyre logs + shade remains + sacred oil to cremate, and a key of the matching metal/colour to unlock catacomb chests (only obtained via cremating shades, not purchasable)

req_items.name: hammer
req_items.note: imcando or Flamtaer hammer; Flamtaer is significantly faster

req_items.name: building materials (timber beam/plank, limestone brick, swamp paste)
req_items.note: bought from Razmire or the Grand Exchange, used to repair the temple

req_items.name: flamtaer bag
req_items.note: holds up to 60 beams/60 bricks/500 paste

xp.Crafting: ~12 xp/second with a Flamtaer hammer from repairing the temple -- one of the cheapest Crafting-xp methods by material cost, though slow
xp.Prayer: Awarded per shade cremated, scaling with shade tier (Loar/Phrin lowest through Urium highest) and slightly with pyre-log tier; +50% with Morytania Hard Diary
xp.Firemaking: Small amount per cremation; +50% with Morytania Elite Diary
xp.Combat: From optionally killing shades directly (Loar shades wander freely; higher-tier shades live in the Shade Catacombs behind keyed doors)

items: Sacred oil itself is a sellable/tradeable byproduct many players farm the minigame purely for

Chest loot scaled by key tier: Bronze chests (Steel-Mithril items + easy clues), Steel (Steel-Adamant + medium clues), Black (Black-Adamant + medium clues), Silver (Black-Rune + hard clues), Gold (Adamant-Dragon + elite clues, plus Zealot's robes) -- key loop colour (Red<Brown<Crimson<Black<Purple) shifts odds toward the chest's better rewards without changing which rewards are possible
Coin drops scaled inversely to main-loot value per a documented RandomRoll-minus-MainLootValue/3 formula, bounded per chest tier (Bronze 400-600 up to Gold 4500-9500)
unlocks: Amulet of the damned (glory-tier stats + barrows-equipment buffs)
Flamtaer bag, Fine cloth (for splitbark armour)
Coffin locks (bronze-gold) traded to Dampe outside the catacombs to upgrade a broken coffin into an equippable cape-slot shade-remains container, holding 3 (bronze) up to 28 (gold)
Runescroll of swampbark and Runescroll of bloodbark (unlock crafting swampbark/bloodbark armour from splitbark armour)
Zealot's robes (helm/top/bottom/boots -- high Prayer bonus + a small chance to save remains while training Prayer)

summary: Shades of Mort'ton is a repeatable multi-skill minigame from its own quest, cremating shade remains for Firemaking/Prayer xp, keys, coins, oil, catacomb loot.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Repeatable minigame unlocked by completing the Shades of Mort'ton quest, which teaches the core loop: brew Serum 207/208 from Herblore, repair the temple with Crafting, cremate shade remains into keys/coins/xp with Firemaking+Prayer.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: A Serum 208 dose used on Razmire Keelgan during or after the quest grants permanent shop access.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Repeat play runs on a personal Sanctity resource (0-100%, decays 1%/min, resets to 0 on logout), raised by spending building resources on temple repair (~5% Sanctity per 1% resources spent) or by killing nearby shades (2% per kill).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Sanctity unlocks sanctifying olive oil into sacred oil (10%+ Sanctity, 0.9% cost per dose) or brewing Serum 208 at the temple's fire altar (20%+ Sanctity, 5% cost per dose).

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Loar shades (level 40, no key needed) wander Mort'ton freely and can aggro/chase from long range in this single-way combat zone.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The five higher shade tiers (Phrin/Riyl/Asyn/Fiyr/Urium, level 60-140) live behind keyed doors in the Shade Catacombs, requiring progressively rarer bronze-to-gold keys obtained only by cremating shade remains of the matching or adjacent tier -- each cremation is a probabilistic key-or-coins roll, roughly 21% coins otherwise a key.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Repairing the temple with a Flamtaer hammer gives roughly 12 Crafting xp/second, one of the cheapest Crafting-xp methods by material cost though slow.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Chest loot scales by key tier: Bronze (Steel-Mithril items + easy clues), Steel (Steel-Adamant + medium clues), Black (Black-Adamant + medium clues), Silver (Black-Rune + hard clues), Gold (Adamant-Dragon + elite clues, plus Zealot's robes).

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Coffin locks (bronze-gold) traded to Dampe outside the catacombs upgrade a broken coffin into an equippable cape-slot shade-remains container, holding 3 (bronze) up to 28 (gold); Runescroll of swampbark and Runescroll of bloodbark unlock crafting swampbark/bloodbark armour from splitbark armour.

notes: Combat + multi-skill minigame unlocked by completing the Shades of Mort'ton quest (which itself teaches the core loop: brew Serum 207/208 from Herblore, repair the temple with Crafting, cremate shade remains into keys/coins/xp with Firemaking+Prayer). Repeat play centers on a personal 'Sanctity' resource (0-100%, decays 1%/min, resets to 0 on logout) earned by spending building Resources on temple repair (~5% Sanctity per 1% resources spent) or by killing nearby shades (2% per kill, only worth it at high combat since kill-rate must outpace the drain) -- Sanctity is what lets you sanctify olive oil into sacred oil (10%+ Sanctity, 0.9% cost per dose) or brew Serum 208 (20%+ Sanctity, 5% cost per dose) at the temple's fire altar. Loar shades (level 40, no key needed) wander Mort'ton freely and can aggro/chase from long range in this single-way combat zone; the five higher shade tiers (Phrin/Riyl/Asyn/Fiyr/Urium, level 60-140) live behind keyed doors in the Shade Catacombs and require progressively rarer bronze->gold keys, which are themselves obtained ONLY by cremating shade remains of the matching or adjacent tier (each cremation is a probabilistic key-or-coins roll, ~21% coins otherwise a key, with documented per-remains-type key-tier fractions). This creates a bootstrapping loop: burn Loar remains for a shot at bronze keys, use bronze keys to reach Phrin shades in the catacombs for steel keys, and so on up the chain toward Gold/Urium. World 377 is the dedicated minigame world where temple-repair crowding also usefully disperses shade aggro away from lower-combat players. Overall this is a mid-length grind best framed as a repeated-visit steer point (sacred oil/Crafting xp on early visits, catacomb key-chain climbing and Zealot's robes/coffin on later, higher-combat visits) rather than a single-session activity.

name: Rat Pits

quests: Ratcatchers, partially -- each of the 4 pits unlocks individually as you help that pit's owner during the quest (not an all-or-nothing gate)

A catspeak amulet unless Dragon Slayer II is completed (DS2 grants catspeak innately) -- without any strategy control the cat fights to the death by default
100-20,000 coins to bet (both players must match stakes)

req_items.name: cat (growth stage matching the pit)
req_items.note: kitten for Ardougne, cat for Varrock, overgrown cat for Keldagrim, wily cat for Port Sarim; lazy cats are banned everywhere and must be trained up to wily via Felkrash at the Port Sarim pit first

Ardougne and Varrock pits additionally advance your cat's growth timer by ~15 minutes on a win, ~7.5 minutes on a loss

summary: Rat Pits is a 2-player PvP cat-fighting gambling minigame across 4 city pits, staking 100-20,000 coins with no xp reward; ironmen cannot play.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: 2-player-only PvP gambling minigame across four separate underground pits (Ardougne, Varrock, Keldagrim, Port Sarim), each accepting only one specific cat growth stage.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Ironmen are hard-locked out entirely.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Each pit unlocks individually as the player helps that pit's owner during the Ratcatchers quest, not an all-or-nothing gate.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: A catspeak amulet (or Dragon Slayer II's innate catspeak) lets the owner pick one of 4 tactical dialogue options trading Attack/Strength for Defence/Hitpoints and whether the cat flees at low health vs fights to the death; without any strategy control the cat fights to the death by default.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Winner takes the matched coin bet (100-20,000gp) from the loser; a draw (both cats tire out at equal rat-kills) returns both bets untouched.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Ardougne and Varrock pit wins additionally advance the cat's growth timer by roughly 15 minutes on a win, 7.5 minutes on a loss.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: No xp reward for playing.

notes: 2-player-only PvP gambling minigame explicitly flagged by the wiki as one of the least popular/most dead pieces of content in the game -- finding a second live player is the main practical obstacle, not any skill/level barrier. Ironmen are hard-locked out entirely. Each of the 4 pits accepts only one specific cat growth stage (kitten/cat/overgrown cat/wily cat respectively), unlocked individually as you progress through Ratcatchers rather than needing the full quest. Both players stake an equal coin bet (100-20k), then optionally use a catspeak amulet (or Dragon Slayer II's innate catspeak) to pick one of 4 tactical dialogue options trading Attack/Strength for Defence/Hitpoints (and whether the cat flees at low health vs fights to the death) before the cats duke it out against rats until one owner's cat dies/flees (loss) or both simultaneously tire out (draw, bets refunded). Reward is purely the opponent's coin stake plus a modest cat-growth-timer boost at the Ardougne/Varrock pits only (~15 min win / ~7.5 min loss) -- no xp, no unlocks, no gear. Given the dead-content status and pure coinflip-adjacent nature, this is a low-priority novelty stop in any progression route, useful only as a cat-growth accelerant if a partner happens to be present.

name: Gnome Ball

xp.Ranged: 4-7 xp per goal scored, plus a flat 25 xp bonus for winning

summary: Gnome Ball is a free-for-all Agility/Ranged sports minigame at the Tree Gnome Stronghold pitch, first to 5 goals, with no entry requirement.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Free-for-all sports minigame with no entry requirement; weapons are auto-unequipped into inventory on entry.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Players Pass (to teammates or the two green gnome wingers), Tackle (success scaled by Agility; being tackled while holding the ball transfers it to the tackler plus 1-2 damage), or Shoot (success scaled by Ranged level and proximity to goal) to score; first to 5 points wins and their score resets.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: 4-7 xp per goal scored in both Agility and Ranged, plus a flat 25 xp bonus in each skill for winning.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: One tradeable, GE-priced gnomeball is awarded on winning a game, capped at one per game; players who brought their own gnomeball onto the pitch don't get a second one from winning.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Scoring one goal is an Easy Western Provinces Diary requirement.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Being tackled deals 1-2 chip damage, a consideration for players with low Hitpoints.

notes: Free-for-all sports minigame: get a gnomeball from the referee on entry, then Pass (to teammates or the two green gnome wingers, who pass it back after a delay), Tackle (opposing gnomes/players -- success chance scaled by Agility, and being tackled while holding the ball transfers it to the tackler plus 1-2 damage), or Shoot (success scaled by Ranged level and proximity to goal) to score. First to 5 points wins and their score resets. Any number of players can join simultaneously (no team-size requirement). Xp gain is small and per-goal (not per-attempt), so this is a low-intensity Ranged/Agility trickle rather than a training method, with scoring one goal being an Easy Western Provinces Diary requirement -- best framed as a quick diary-task stop rather than a repeatable grind target.

Magic butterfly net (higher catch chance, purchasable from Elnock once unlocked) recommended over the base net
Weight-reducing clothing, magic armour pieces (boosts entangle/dark lure success), ring of endurance or stamina potions all recommended but optional

req_items.name: butterfly net

summary: Impetuous Impulses is the concentrated impling-catching minigame at Puro-Puro, Hunter-gated per impling tier, reached via the Zanaris crop circle.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: A maze of magical wheat rows at Puro-Puro (pushable through with high Strength) where implings of many tiers spawn and must be caught, functionally the concentrated version of impling hunting otherwise done across the overworld.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Hunter 17 minimum to enter the maze and catch the lowest tier (baby impling) with a net and jar; 27 to catch it barehanded without a net (the barehanded threshold is +10 over the netted minimum for every tier), though a jar is still needed to carry a barehanded catch out.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Lost City is needed only for the permanent Zanaris crop-circle portal shortcut -- no quest is required to reach Puro-Puro via the migratory surface crop circles found by talking to the wandering impling in Zanaris' wheat field.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Elnock Inquisitor at the entrance gives a free butterfly net, 7 starter impling jars, and an impling scroll that tracks catches per type on first entry.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Elnock's Exchange barter shop converts caught jars into catching aids: 3 baby+2 young+1 gourmet jars -> Imp repellent; 3 gourmet+2 earth+1 essence jars -> Magic butterfly net; 3 essence+2 eclectic+1 nature jars -> Jar generator; 1 jar of any type -> 3 empty impling jars.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Nearly every impling type in the game (baby through lucky) spawns here; the sole exception is the crystal impling, which is Prifddinas-only.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: A daily gold-coin jar purchase cap of 10 applies at Elnock's Exchange.

notes: Solo Hunter minigame: a maze of magical wheat rows (pushable through with high Strength) inside which implings of many tiers spawn and must be net-caught then jarred (or, at higher Hunter, caught barehanded for an on-the-spot catch without needing a jar at all -- though jars are still needed to carry a barehanded catch out, per the wiki's own catch-mechanics caveat). This is functionally the concentrated version of impling hunting that's otherwise done ambiently across the overworld -- nearly every impling type in the game (baby through lucky) spawns here, the sole exception being the crystal impling which is Prifddinas-only. Elnock Inquisitor at the entrance both onboards new players (free net + 7 jars + tracking scroll) and runs a barter exchange converting caught jars into catching aids (repellent, magic net, jar generator) rather than gp, plus a daily gold-coin jar purchase cap of 10. Reward value is almost entirely in what's inside the jars (trinkets/treasures/gp scaling with tier) plus the Hunter xp for the catch itself -- there's no separate minigame-specific gear/unlock track beyond the catching-aid exchange.

summary: Sorceress's Garden is a stealth Thieving/Farming/Cooking minigame in four seasonal instanced gardens near Al Kharid, Thieving-gated 25/45/65 by season.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Sneak past guardian elementals in one of four seasonal instanced gardens to steal either one sq'irk fruit or 2 random herbs per entry, then get auto-teleported back to the central fountain-garden hub.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Thieving 25 for Spring, 45 for Autumn, 65 for Summer gates each seasonal garden's sq'irk tree and herb patch (Winter needs none); boosts, including from the sq'irkjuice reward itself, count toward the gate.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Getting spotted by an elemental, which can only detect/teleport the player within 2 tiles, ejects the player empty-handed.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Started via Prince Ali Rescue (talk to Osman); Beneath Cursed Sands additionally unlocks Selim as an alternate quest-giver for the same minigame.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Herb picks give a flat 50 Farming xp (25 xp/herb, always 2 clean herbs) independent of season; crushing a sq'irk into juice gives a flat 5 Cooking xp independent of season.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Turning sq'irkjuice in to Osman/Selim gives 350 (Winter) / 1,350 (Spring) / 2,350 (Autumn) / 3,000 (Summer) Thieving xp per juice.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Random clean herbs per garden tier: Winter (guam/marrentill/tarromin/harralander only), Spring adds irit+avantoe, Autumn adds ranarr+kwuarm+cadantine, Summer adds lantadyme+dwarf weed -- higher seasons strictly superset lower ones.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Sq'irkjuice can alternatively be drunk instead of turned in, for a temporary Thieving boost (0/+1/+2/+3 by season) plus a run-energy restore (5%/10%/15%/20% by season).

notes: Solo (or crowded-mass-world) Thieving/Farming/Cooking minigame: sneak past guardian elementals in one of four seasonal instanced gardens (each gated by a Thieving level) to steal either one sq'irk fruit from that season's tree or 2 random herbs, then you're auto-teleported back out to the central fountain-garden hub -- so each 'run' is a single fast in-and-out. Getting spotted by an elemental (which can only detect/teleport you within 2 tiles) ejects you empty-handed. The wiki documents two efficiency strategies: solo speed-running with run energy maximized via low weight/Agility/stamina potions, or a now largely-dead 'mass worlds' method (historically World 471) where enough simultaneous thieves diffuse elemental attention so almost everyone reaches the tree unclicked-at, though the wiki notes this is rarely populated enough to work anymore except at peak times for the Summer garden. Reward loop is dual-purpose: crushed sq'irkjuice turned in to Osman/Selim gives large flat Thieving xp chunks scaled steeply by season (350 up to 3000 per juice), or can instead be drunk on the spot for a temporary Thieving level boost + run energy refill, letting higher-tier gardens bootstrap access to themselves. Herb picks are a flat, season-independent 50 Farming xp with the herb pool simply widening at higher seasons. No combat, no gp cost, purely a stealth-timing minigame best suited to short repeated bursts for Thieving xp injections or bulk herb collection.
