facts.value: Xerician cape accolades unlock from Captain Rimor at 100/500/1000/1500/2000 Challenge Mode completions (Xeric's guard/warrior/sentinel/general/champion); an ancient tablet reward unlocks a direct Xeric's talisman teleport to the mountain.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Challenge Mode is a harder fixed-layout variant and the only route to the cosmetic ancestral colour kit; it counts separately toward the Xerician cape ladder.

notes: OSRS's first raid (Raids 1) -- a scaling 1-100 player PvM+skilling raid with no hard entry gate (no required quest or skill level), procedurally laid out from a pool of resource/puzzle/boss rooms culminating in the Great Olm. Reward quality is driven entirely by team points banked during the run (combat kills + skilling actions), not by a fixed completion bonus, so higher-level accounts and more thorough resource-gathering both push loot chances up. Challenge Mode (a harder fixed-layout variant) is the only route to the cosmetic ancestral colour kit and counts separately toward the Xerician cape ladder.

name: Theatre of Blood

quests: Priest in Peril (only hard requirement -- unlocks Morytania access)

rewards.xp: no direct completion xp beyond normal combat xp from killing the room bosses
rewards.items: Monumental Chest common table (3 rolls if no unique) plus a unique-roll chance per player per completion: Avernic defender hilt, Justiciar faceguard/chestguard/legguards, Ghrazi rapier, Sanguinesti staff, Scythe of vitur, plus Vials of blood as a common uniques-adjacent drop (charges Scythe of Vitur, makes bastion/battlemage potions); 6 lootable books drop from the named room bosses and the treasure vault bookshelves
rewards.unlocks: Sinhaza shroud tiers 1-5 from the Mysterious Stranger at 100/500/1000/1500/2000 Normal/Hard Mode completions

summary: Theatre of Blood (Raids 2) is a fixed 1-5 player raid beneath Ver Sinhaza needing only Priest in Peril, with per-player loot from up to 5 chests.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: A fixed 1-5 player raid (unlike Chambers of Xeric's scaling layout), with the lowest entry bar of the three raids: no combat/skill level is enforced, only Priest in Peril for Morytania access.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Loot is per-player (up to 5 large chests spawn, one per party member) rather than team-pooled; a unique roll glows the chest purple, the same signal as Chambers of Xeric's beam.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: No direct completion xp beyond normal combat xp from killing the room bosses.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Monumental Chest common table rolls 3 times if no unique, plus a unique-roll chance per player per completion: Avernic defender hilt, Justiciar faceguard/chestguard/legguards, Ghrazi rapier, Sanguinesti staff, Scythe of vitur, plus Vials of blood as a common uniques-adjacent drop (charges Scythe of Vitur, makes bastion/battlemage potions).

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Sinhaza shroud tiers 1-5 unlock from the Mysterious Stranger at 100/500/1000/1500/2000 Normal/Hard Mode completions.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Hard Mode is a separate, harder scaled variant of the same room sequence rather than a distinct raid.

notes: Raids 2 -- a fixed 1-5 player raid (unlike CoX's scaling layout) with genuinely the lowest entry bar of the three raids: no combat/skill level is enforced, only Priest in Peril for Morytania access. Loot is per-player (up to 5 large chests spawn, one per party member) rather than team-pooled like CoX, and a unique roll glows the chest purple the same way CoX's beam does. Hard Mode is a separate, harder scaled variant of the same room sequence rather than a distinct raid.

name: Tombs of Amascut

quests: Beneath Cursed Sands (hard requirement to enter)

rewards.xp: no direct completion xp beyond normal combat xp from the four path bosses and the Wardens
rewards.items: Sarcophagus unique roll (one unique max per raid, else 3 common-table rolls): Masori mask/body/chaps, Lightbearer, Osmumten's fang, Elidinis' ward, Tumeken's shadow; independent tertiary rolls for Thread of elidinis (divine rune pouch component), keris partisan jewels (Breach of the scarab, Eye of the corruptor, Jewel of the sun, Jewel of amascut) and the Tumeken's guardian pet; under 1,500 points yields fossilised dung instead; 6 lootable books drop from the path bosses/Wardens/burial-chamber bags
rewards.unlocks: Icthlarin's shroud tiers 1-5 (tier 5 also grants Icthlarin's hood) from a lobby chest at 100/500/1000/1500/2000 Normal/Expert Mode completions

summary: Tombs of Amascut (Raids 3) is a scalable-difficulty 1-8 player raid in the Jaltevas Pyramid necropolis, requiring Beneath Cursed Sands.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Up to 8 players per party, the largest of the three raids, set in the Jaltevas Pyramid necropolis in the Kharidian Desert.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Beneath Cursed Sands is a hard requirement to enter.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Players tune raid difficulty/length pre-entry with an Invocation system that toggles modifier tiers, scaling both danger and the raid-level number used for loot-table weighting -- difficulty is a player-selected point on a curve rather than a single fixed tier like Chambers of Xeric/Theatre of Blood.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: No direct completion xp beyond normal combat xp from the four path bosses and the Wardens.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Sarcophagus allows one unique roll max per raid (else 3 common-table rolls): Masori mask/body/chaps, Lightbearer, Osmumten's fang, Elidinis' ward, Tumeken's shadow; independent tertiary rolls for Thread of elidinis, keris partisan jewels (Breach of the scarab, Eye of the corruptor, Jewel of the sun, Jewel of amascut) and the Tumeken's guardian pet; under 1,500 points yields fossilised dung instead.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Icthlarin's shroud tiers 1-5 (tier 5 also grants Icthlarin's hood) unlock from a lobby chest at 100/500/1000/1500/2000 Normal/Expert Mode completions.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: A post-raid Raid Summary interface hands out flavour titles based on performance stats, purely cosmetic with no mechanical reward attached.

notes: Raids 3 -- unique among the three raids for its Invocation system: players tune raid difficulty/length pre-entry by toggling modifier tiers, which scales both danger and the raid-level number used for loot-table weighting, so 'the raid' isn't a single fixed difficulty like CoX/ToB but a player-selected point on a difficulty curve. Up to 8 players per party (largest of the three raids). A post-raid Raid Summary interface hands out flavour titles (The Leech/The Carry/etc.) based on performance stats, purely cosmetic bragging-rights info with no mechanical reward attached.

name: Fight Caves

items: combat gear/prayer for level-360 Ket-Zek and level-702 TzTok-Jad

req_items.name: combat gear and prayer supplies (for level-360 Ket-Zek and level-702 TzTok-Jad)

rewards.xp: no bonus xp beyond normal combat xp from the 63 waves
rewards.items: 8,032 Tokkul on defeating TzTok-Jad plus 2 additional Tokkul per wave cleared (running total N*(N+1) for N waves) with a further 4,000 Tokkul bonus for the Jad kill itself; 1/200 chance of the TzRek-jad pet on the Jad kill (1/100 on a Slayer task), plus a second independent 1/200 roll if a spare fire cape is exchanged with TzHaar-Mej-Jal afterward
rewards.unlocks: fire cape (untradeable) on defeating TzTok-Jad -- this is the sole entry ticket into the Inferno; Elite Karamja Diary doubles Tokkul earned here and grants one free resurrection per day inside the Fight Caves

summary: Fight Caves is a solo 63-wave combat gauntlet in Mor Ul Rek with no entry requirement, ending in TzTok-Jad and rewarding the fire cape.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Solo 63-wave combat gauntlet with zero entry requirements (no quest, no level, open from level 1), culminating in the level-702 TzTok-Jad.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: No restocking mid-run and only one life, barring the Elite Karamja Diary's one free daily resurrection inside the Fight Caves.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: 8,032 Tokkul on defeating TzTok-Jad plus 2 additional Tokkul per wave cleared (running total N*(N+1) for N waves), with a further 4,000 Tokkul bonus for the Jad kill itself.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: 1/200 chance of the TzRek-jad pet on the Jad kill (1/100 on a Slayer task), plus a second independent 1/200 roll if a spare fire cape is exchanged with TzHaar-Mej-Jal afterward.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Fire cape (untradeable) drops on defeating TzTok-Jad -- the sole entry ticket into the Inferno.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: The Elite Karamja Diary doubles Tokkul earned here and grants one free resurrection per day inside the Fight Caves.

notes: Solo 63-wave combat gauntlet with zero entry requirements (no quest, no level, open from level 1) -- the classic early-to-mid-game major challenge. Because there's no restocking and only one life (barring the diary's free resurrection), the run is entirely about surviving the escalating waves up to TzTok-Jad, the level-702 final boss. It exists in the same TzHaar volcano complex as the harder Inferno, which explicitly requires sacrificing a fire cape earned here to even attempt.

name: Inferno

items: fire cape (one-time entry fee, consumed by TzHaar-Ket-Keh)
top-tier combat gear/prayer for the 69-wave gauntlet and the level-1400 TzKal-Zuk

req_items.name: fire cape
req_items.note: one-time entry fee, consumed by TzHaar-Ket-Keh

req_items.name: top-tier combat gear and prayer supplies (for the 69-wave gauntlet and the level-1400 TzKal-Zuk)

rewards.xp: no bonus xp beyond normal combat xp from the 69 waves
rewards.items: Tokkul scaled to the wave reached, 16,440 max (doubled with the elite Karamja Diary); 1/100 chance of the Jal-nib-rek pet on defeating TzKal-Zuk (1/75 on a Slayer task), plus a second independent 1/100 roll by exchanging a spare infernal cape with TzHaar-Ket-Keh afterward
rewards.unlocks: infernal cape (untradeable, the strict best melee cape in the game per the wiki's own Fight Caves comparison) on defeating TzKal-Zuk; a defeated TzKal-Zuk can later be assigned as a Slayer task, applying to all Inferno monsters on that task

summary: Inferno is a solo 69-wave gauntlet against TzKal-Zuk in Mor Ul Rek, entered by sacrificing a fire cape, rewarding the infernal cape.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Solo 69-wave gauntlet in the same TzHaar volcano complex as the Fight Caves, entered by handing a fire cape to TzHaar-Ket-Keh; no requirement beyond that sacrifice.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: A death sends the player all the way back to wave 1 with no checkpoint or restock; the run can only be paused, not saved, by logging out between waves; purple sweets are disabled inside.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Tokkul scales to the wave reached, 16,440 max, doubled with the Elite Karamja Diary.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: 1/100 chance of the Jal-nib-rek pet on defeating TzKal-Zuk (1/75 on a Slayer task), plus a second independent 1/100 roll by exchanging a spare infernal cape with TzHaar-Ket-Keh afterward.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Infernal cape (untradeable) drops on defeating TzKal-Zuk -- described by the wiki's cited Jagex quote as the strict best melee cape in the game.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: A defeated TzKal-Zuk can later be assigned as a Slayer task, applying to all Inferno monsters on that task.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Restricted to unrestricted worlds only since 2018 to stop free unlimited practice, later loosened again from 2021.

notes: The successor solo challenge to the Fight Caves and, per the wiki's own cited Jagex quote, 'the greatest solo PvM challenge ever' at release -- explicitly no requirement beyond sacrificing a fire cape. A death sends the player all the way back to wave 1 (no checkpoint/restock), and the run can only be paused (not saved) by logging out between waves; purple sweets are disabled inside. Restricted to unrestricted worlds only since 2018 to stop free unlimited practice, later loosened again from 2021.

name: Fortis Colosseum

quests: Children of the Sun (required for all of Varlamore, including the Colosseum)
items: combat gear/prayer for the 12 waves and final boss Sol Heredit
food + potions (all accumulated wave loot is lost on death/teleport/logout mid-run)

req_items.name: combat gear and prayer supplies (for the 12 waves and final boss Sol Heredit)

rewards.xp: no bonus xp beyond normal combat xp from the 12 waves
rewards.items: Rewards Chest loot banked wave-by-wave (bankable any time by ending the run, or lost entirely on death/logout); unique rolls open from wave 4 onward with rising odds: Sunfire fanatic helm/cuirass/chausses, Tonalztics of ralos (wave 7+ only), Echo crystal (combines with guardian boots into echo boots), plus Sunfire splinters as a common uniques-adjacent drop (charges Dizana's quiver/Tonalztics, crafts sunfire runes/searing pages/sunfire wine)
rewards.unlocks: Dizana's quiver + Smol heredit pet guaranteed on beating Sol Heredit in wave 12; Glory-tier citizen perks at 2,000/5,000/8,000/12,000/16,000/20,000 personal-best Glory: Colosseum bank chest access, Civitas illa Fortis as respawn point, Oriana buying valuables at 65gp each, ring of dueling teleport to the Colosseum, disease-free Outer Fortis herb patch, and the Fortis Salute emote

summary: Fortis Colosseum is a solo 12-wave gauntlet east of Civitas illa Fortis with a bank-or-push-your-luck loot mechanic, requiring Children of the Sun.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Solo 12-wave combat gauntlet (2024, Varlamore: Part One), structurally like the Fight Caves/Inferno but with a bank-or-push-your-luck loot mechanic instead of a single end-of-run roll.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Children of the Sun is required for all of Varlamore, including the Colosseum.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Players choose each wave whether to bank current+next-wave loot or press on for better odds; any death, teleport, or logout forfeits everything unbanked.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: All accumulated wave loot is lost on death/teleport/logout mid-run.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Unique rolls open from wave 4 onward with rising odds: Sunfire fanatic helm/cuirass/chausses, Tonalztics of ralos (wave 7+ only), Echo crystal (combines with guardian boots into echo boots), plus Sunfire splinters as a common uniques-adjacent drop.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Dizana's quiver and the Smol heredit pet are guaranteed on beating Sol Heredit in wave 12.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Glory-tier citizen perks unlock at 2,000/5,000/8,000/12,000/16,000/20,000 personal-best Glory: Colosseum bank chest access, Civitas illa Fortis as respawn point, Oriana buying valuables at 65gp each, ring of dueling teleport to the Colosseum, disease-free Outer Fortis herb patch, and the Fortis Salute emote.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Glory (the performance score from clearing waves) is a personal-best ladder, not an accumulating currency.

notes: Solo 12-wave combat gauntlet (2024, Varlamore: Part One) structurally like the Fight Caves/Inferno but with a bank-or-push-your-luck loot mechanic instead of a single end-of-run roll -- players choose each wave whether to bank current+next-wave loot or press on for better odds, and any death/teleport/logout forfeits everything unbanked. Glory (the performance score from clearing waves) is a personal-best ladder, not an accumulating currency, and unlocks account-wide QoL perks separate from the item rewards.

name: The Gauntlet

quests: Song of the Elves (required for Prifddinas access)

rewards.xp: no direct completion xp bonus; all skilling done while gathering supplies (Mining/Woodcutting/Fishing/Herblore/Smithing/Cooking-style crafting of gear) grants its normal skill xp during the run
rewards.items: two rolls on the Reward Chest common table plus several crystal shards and a unique roll every completion: crystal armour seed (1/120, -> crystal armour), crystal weapon seed (1/120), enhanced crystal weapon seed (1/2000, -> Blade of Saeldor/Bow of Faerdhinen), Youngllef pet (1/2000); a smaller consolation reward drops on failure/early exit but with no unique chance
rewards.unlocks: unlocked after completing The Gauntlet once, players may attempt the harder Corrupted Gauntlet variant (see minigamedb:corrupted-gauntlet)

summary: Solo procedural Prifddinas dungeon: gather resources to craft gear, then fight the Crystalline Hunllef for chest rolls and crystal seeds.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Solo procedurally-generated dungeon: players enter with no items and gather resource nodes to craft weapons, armour, food and potions before fighting a boss.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Boss is the Crystalline Hunllef, fought in a timed encounter after the preparation phase.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Entered via the Gauntlet Portal in north-western Prifddinas; requires Song of the Elves for Prifddinas access.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: All items are auto-banked and boosts stripped on entry; gear must be gathered and crafted inside the dungeon.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: No direct completion xp bonus; skilling actions during preparation (Mining/Woodcutting/Fishing/Herblore/Smithing/Cooking) grant normal skill xp.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Completing the Gauntlet once unlocks the ability to attempt the harder Corrupted Gauntlet variant.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Rewards: two Reward Chest common-table rolls plus crystal shards every completion, plus a unique roll: crystal armour seed (1/120), crystal weapon seed (1/120), enhanced crystal weapon seed (1/2000, upgrades to Blade of Saeldor/Bow of Faerdhinen), or the Youngllef pet (1/2000).

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Dangerous minigame: hardcore ironmen lose HC status and ultimate ironmen can lose instanced-bank items on death here.

notes: Solo procedurally-generated dungeon: players enter with zero items (everything is auto-banked on entry, boosts stripped) and must gather resource nodes/herbs/fish within the dungeon to craft their own weapons, armour, food and potions before a timed fight against the Crystalline Hunllef in the boss room. It's a dangerous minigame -- hardcore ironmen lose HC status and ultimate ironmen can lose instanced-bank items on death here. Built as the Amlodd Clan's warrior-training ground per its own in-game lore.

name: The Gauntlet

reqs.notes: must complete the regular Gauntlet at least once before the Corrupted variant becomes accessible (progression gate, not a quest/skill/item)

rewards.xp: no direct completion xp bonus; skilling actions during preparation grant normal skill xp same as the regular Gauntlet
rewards.items: three rolls on an upgraded Reward Chest table plus 7-12 crystal shards and a higher-odds unique roll every completion: crystal armour seed (1/50), crystal weapon seed (1/50), enhanced crystal weapon seed (1/400), Youngllef pet (1/800); Gauntlet cape is a guaranteed drop every completion
rewards.unlocks: Gauntlet cape guaranteed on every completion (cosmetic accolade cape)

summary: Harder Gauntlet mode: shorter prep, Corrupted-tier monsters/items, 1000 HP Hunllef, guaranteed Gauntlet cape.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Harder mode of the Gauntlet using the same procedurally-generated dungeon shell, with shorter prep time and Corrupted-tier monsters and items.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Requires completing the regular Gauntlet at least once as a progression gate before this variant becomes accessible.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Entered via the same Gauntlet Portal in north-western Prifddinas, offered as a harder mode after first clearing the regular Gauntlet; requires Song of the Elves for Prifddinas access.

facts.label: length
facts.value: Preparation time is 7:30, versus 10:00 for the regular Gauntlet.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Boss is the Corrupted Hunllef with 1000 HP.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The Corrupted Hunllef's max hit scales down by the summed armour tier worn (0/3/6/9) while the correct protection prayer is active, so crafted Corrupted armour materially affects survivability.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: The inner 3x3 ring around the boss room drops its usual resource-randomness mitigation, except one room with guaranteed 3 fishing spots.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: No direct completion xp bonus; skilling actions during preparation grant normal skill xp, same as the regular Gauntlet.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Rewards: three upgraded Reward Chest rolls plus 7-12 crystal shards every completion, plus a higher-odds unique roll: crystal armour seed (1/50), crystal weapon seed (1/50), enhanced crystal weapon seed (1/400), or Youngllef pet (1/800); the Gauntlet cape is a guaranteed drop every completion.

notes: Harder mode of The Gauntlet: same procedurally-generated dungeon shell but only 7:30 to prepare (vs 10:00 normal), all monsters/items are Corrupted-tier with increased stats, and the inner 3x3 ring around the boss room drops its usual resource-randomness mitigation (except one room guaranteed 3 fishing spots). The boss room's Corrupted Hunllef has 1000 HP with a max-hit table that scales down by the summed armour tier worn (0/3/6/9) while running the correct protection prayer -- gearing up through crafted Corrupted armour tiers materially changes survivability, not just cosmetic.

name: Zalcano

quests: Song of the Elves (Prifddinas access, prerequisite to the level gate)
items: graceful outfit and Varrock armour recommended for movement/pickpocket-style resource speed

rewards.xp: Mining xp OR item drops, player's choice (ask Rhiannon to switch) -- xp scales with contribution points (~points_main + points_tertiary*5) and is boosted by the Prospector kit; the unique/pet roll always happens regardless of which mode is chosen
rewards.items: main + tertiary drop tables scale with points (damage-to-health + 2x damage-to-shield, capped 400/300 respectively for tertiary); guaranteed 1-3 crystal shards + infernal ashes (MVP-only ashes) every eligible kill; runes (blood/cosmic/death/law/soul/nature) and noted ore/bar/gem materials (silver/coal/mithril/adamantite/runite/gold, steel/mithril/adamantite/runite bars, uncut diamond/dragonstone, onyx bolt tips, pure essence) on the main table; tertiary-only 1/200 roll for the crystal tool seed table (39/8000 seed, 1/8000 uncut onyx), Zalcano shard (1/750-1/1500 by contribution), Smolcano pet (flat 1/2250)

summary: Skilling boss beneath Prifddinas: mine/smith/runecraft to strip Zalcano's armour, then finish her with a pickaxe for Mining xp or drops.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Skilling boss encounter: Zalcano is immune to normal damage, so players mine ore, smith it, and runecraft it into imbued tephra to strip her stone armour before finishing her with a pickaxe.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Boss is Zalcano, located in volcanic caverns beneath Prifddinas.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Reached via the teleport platform west of the south-eastern Prifddinas bank, teleport crystal/eternal teleport crystal, spirit tree, or a POH teleport with a Prifddinas house; requires Song of the Elves for Prifddinas access plus 70 Mining and 70 Smithing.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: All of Zalcano's own attacks are typeless, so prayer cannot reduce them.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Must do at least 5 damage to her shield to be loot-eligible at all, and 31 combined damage to be eligible for uniques/pet; the MVP (top damage) gets a bonus 10% of their own non-unique loot.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Players choose Mining xp or item drops (ask Rhiannon to switch); xp scales with contribution points and is boosted by the Prospector kit, while the unique/pet roll happens regardless of mode chosen.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Guaranteed 1-3 crystal shards plus infernal ashes (MVP-only) every eligible kill; main/tertiary drop tables include runes, noted ore/bar/gem materials, and a tertiary-only 1/200 roll for the crystal tool seed table, Zalcano shard (1/750-1/1500 by contribution), or the Smolcano pet (flat 1/2250).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Effectively a group activity even though solo-eligible, since ironmen need to join others' instances to receive rewards.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Unlocks from this activity are unknown ("??") -- not stated in the held source text.

notes: Not a conventional combat boss: Zalcano is immune to normal damage, so the fight is a skilling encounter -- players mine ore, smith it, and runecraft it into imbued tephra to strip her stone armour, then finish her off by mining her directly with a pickaxe (all her own attacks are typeless, so prayer can't reduce them). Must do at least 5 damage to her shield to be loot-eligible at all, and 31 combined damage to be eligible for uniques/pet; the MVP (top damage) gets a bonus 10% of their own non-unique loot on top. A group activity in practice even though it's fought solo-eligible, since ironmen need to join others' instances to get rewards.

name: Giants' Foundry

items: metal bars, or metal equipment worth at least 2 bars, to feed each commission
a bucket (obtainable on-site) or Ice gloves to lift moulded weapons

req_items.name: metal bars (or metal equipment worth at least 2 bars)
bucket
req_items.note: obtainable on-site, or use Ice gloves to lift moulded weapons

req_items.name: Ice gloves
req_items.note: alternative to bucket for lifting moulded weapons

rewards.xp: Smithing xp per sword forged, scaled by final quality (metal score + mould score, minus 5 per mistake)
rewards.items: a gold payout per sword equal to double the Smithing xp reward (bars/mould choices, e.g. steel or a 14/14 steel-mithril blend, can make forging net-profitable at high quality); Foundry Reputation earned 1:1 with final quality, spendable on extra moulds per preform section or in the Reward Shop for Double ammo mould (2,000 rep, needs Dwarf Cannon quest), Kovac's grog (300), Smithing catalyst (15), Ore pack (200), Smiths tunic/trousers (4,000 each), Smiths boots/gloves (3,500 each, gloves combine with ice gloves into smiths gloves (i)), Colossal blade (5,000)

summary: Smithing minigame: forge giant-sized commissions for Kovac via metal blend, mould choice, and a heat/hammer minigame for Smithing xp and rep.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Smithing minigame: players fulfil giant-sized weapon commissions for Kovac by choosing a metal blend and preform mould, then a heat/hammer minigame to maximise sword quality.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Located in a cave beneath the Giants' Plateau at the Kharidian Desert edge; requires the Sleeping Giants quest to participate.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: No Smithing level is directly enforced by the activity itself, but the Sleeping Giants entry quest has its own Smithing requirement, so the activity is gated by that quest's prerequisites.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Smithing xp is granted per sword forged, scaled by final quality (metal score + mould score, minus 5 per mistake).

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Gold payout per sword equals double the Smithing xp reward; certain bar choices can make forging net-profitable at high quality.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Foundry Reputation is earned 1:1 with final quality, spendable on extra moulds per preform section or in the Reward Shop for items like Double ammo mould (2,000 rep, needs Dwarf Cannon quest), Kovac's grog (300), Smithing catalyst (15), Ore pack (200), Smiths tunic/trousers (4,000 each), Smiths boots/gloves (3,500 each), or the Colossal blade (5,000).

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Unlocks beyond the Reward Shop list are unknown ("??") -- not stated in the held source text.

notes: A Smithing minigame, not a combat one: players fulfil giant-sized weapon commissions for Kovac by choosing a metal blend and preform mould combination, then working a heat/hammer minigame ('sweet spot' timing during Refinement) to maximise sword quality before handing it in. No Smithing level is directly enforced by the activity itself, but Sleeping Giants (the entry quest) has its own Smithing requirement, so in practice it's gated by that quest's prerequisites rather than a level check at the door.

name: Shooting Stars

items: a telescope in a POH study room to locate the next star (oak=24min window, teak=9min, mahogany=2min) -- optional, not required to mine a star once found

req_items.name: telescope (POH study room)
req_items.note: locates the next star; oak=24min window, teak=9min, mahogany=2min

rewards.xp: Mining xp while mining the crashed star, idle-friendly (only needs input roughly every 7 minutes); F2P players get half xp and no shop access; ~30,000 xp/hour achievable at 90+ Mining with a dragon pickaxe
rewards.items: stardust, spent at Dusuri's Star Shop by the Mining Guild entrance in Falador on: celestial ring (uncharged, 1600 to buy/2000 to sell), star fragment (2400/3000), bag full of gems -stardust variant- (240/300), soft clay pack -stardust variant- (120/150)

summary: F2P-eligible D&D: a star crashes roughly every 90 minutes per server at a random site; mine it for idle-friendly Mining xp and stardust.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Free-to-play-eligible Distraction and Diversion: a star crashes roughly every 90 minutes (+/-15) per server at a random landing site, and players mine it for Mining xp and stardust.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Landing sites span nearly every major region (Asgarnia, Crandor/Karamja, Feldip Hills/Isle of Souls, Fossil Island/Mos Le'Harmless, Fremennik Lands/Lunar Isle, Great Kourend, Kandarin, Kebos Lowlands, Kharidian Desert, Misthalin, Morytania, Piscatoris/Gnome Stronghold, Tirannwn, Varlamore, Wilderness).

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: F2P players get half xp and no shop access; roughly 30,000 xp/hour achievable at 90+ Mining with a dragon pickaxe.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Stardust is spent at Dusuri's Star Shop by the Mining Guild entrance in Falador on: celestial ring uncharged (1600 buy/2000 sell), star fragment (2400/3000), bag full of gems -stardust variant- (240/300), soft clay pack -stardust variant- (120/150).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: A player-owned house telescope narrows the search window (better wood tier = tighter window), and the in-game Shooting Stars grouping chat crowdsources sightings; without one, discovery is essentially luck-of-passing-by.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: Exact coordinates are unknown ("??") since landing sites are randomized across a fixed pool.

notes: A free-to-play-eligible Distraction and Diversion, not a members-only minigame -- F2P players can mine stars but can't spend stardust at the shop (half xp too). Locating a star well requires a player-owned house telescope (better wood tier = tighter time window) and the in-game Shooting Stars grouping chat to crowdsource sightings; without one, discovery is essentially luck-of-passing-by. The activity is notable in the guide-burndown model as a near-zero-babysitting Mining xp source once a star is found, unlike most other Mining training methods.

name: Champion's Challenge

items: 32+ quest points (to be eligible to receive champion's scrolls from monster drops at all)
combat gear appropriate to the champion being fought, respecting that champion's specific rule restrictions (e.g. melee-only, no prayer, no armour)

req_items.name: 32+ quest points
req_items.note: eligibility to receive champion's scrolls from monster drops at all

req_items.name: combat gear appropriate to the champion being fought
req_items.note: must respect that champion's rule restrictions (e.g. melee-only, no prayer, no armour)

rewards.xp: Hitpoints and Slayer xp per champion defeated (160 Imp up to 592 Lesser Demon, 492 for Leon d'Cour); a Champion's lamp claimable after each win grants 10x that xp again on top; 40,348 combined Hitpoints+Slayer xp total from all 11 champions + all 11 lamps
rewards.items: no tradeable item drops -- purely an xp + cosmetic activity
rewards.unlocks: a banner per defeated champion hung on the Champions' Guild basement wall; the cosmetic (no stat bonus) champion's cape from Leon d'Cour after all 11 champions are defeated

summary: D&D built on rare champion's scroll drops, redeemed to fight that race's champion in the Champions' Guild basement for xp and a cosmetic cape.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Distraction and Diversion built around an extremely rare (1/5,000) champion's scroll drop from a race's regular monsters, redeemable to fight that race's champion.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Entered via a trapdoor to the Champions' Guild basement in Misthalin; players cannot teleport out, only exit through the portcullis then up the ladder.

facts.label: boss
facts.value: Only 1 of the 6 'Champions of Champions' seated in the arena (the human, Leon d'Cour) is actually fightable; the other 5 (Dwarf/TzHaar/Werewolf/Elf/Gnome) are lore-only NPCs.

facts.label: xp-note
facts.value: Hitpoints and Slayer xp per champion defeated ranges from 160 (Imp) up to 592 (Lesser Demon) and 492 (Leon d'Cour); a Champion's lamp claimable after each win grants 10x that xp again; total is 40,348 combined Hitpoints+Slayer xp from all 11 champions plus all 11 lamps.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: No tradeable item drops -- purely an xp and cosmetic activity.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: A banner is hung on the Champions' Guild basement wall per defeated champion; the cosmetic (no stat bonus) champion's cape is granted from Leon d'Cour after all 11 champions are defeated.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Scrolls survive death (recoverable from the player's gravestone) but are lost once the corresponding champion is beaten; each champion enforces its own combat-style/gear restriction, so full completion requires 11 different loadouts across melee/range/magic/no-armour/no-prayer builds.

notes: A Distraction and Diversion built around an extremely rare (1/5,000) champion's scroll drop from that race's regular monsters -- e.g. an Imp champion scroll can drop from any imp, a Skeleton champion scroll from Calvar'ion/Vet'ion/skeleton hellhounds/etc. Only 1 of the 6 'Champions of Champions' seated in the arena (the human, Leon d'Cour) is actually fightable; the other 5 (Dwarf/TzHaar/Werewolf/Elf/Gnome) are lore-only NPCs. Scrolls survive death (recoverable from the player's gravestone) but are lost once the corresponding champion is beaten. Each champion enforces its own combat-style/gear restriction, so full completion effectively requires 11 different loadouts across melee/range/magic/no-armour/no-prayer builds.

name: Abyssal whip

skills.Slayer: 85 to damage abyssal demons, its main drop source (no active-task requirement stated on the demon's own page)

rewards.effect: One-handed slash melee weapon at the fast 2.4s/hit speed of daggers and scimitars; a long-standing default Slayer/training weapon until upgrades like the ghrazi rapier or blade of saeldor are reached. Attaching a kraken tentacle (itself a Kraken-boss drop) upgrades it into an abyssal tentacle (75 Attack, +8 slash/+4 Strength bonus), consuming the whip.

summary: One-handed slash weapon at fast dagger/scimitar speed; requires 70 Attack, gathered from 85 Slayer abyssal demons or Abyssal Sire unsired drops.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: One-handed slash melee weapon at the fast 2.4s/hit speed of daggers and scimitars.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Requires 70 Attack to wield.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Requires 85 Slayer to damage abyssal demons, its main drop source (no active-task requirement stated on the demon's own page).

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Attaching a kraken tentacle (a Kraken-boss drop) upgrades it into an abyssal tentacle (75 Attack, +8 slash/+4 Strength bonus), consuming the whip.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Gather, not buy: dropped by abyssal demons (Slayer Tower, Catacombs of Kourend, or the Wilderness Slayer Cave, 85 Slayer), or via the Abyssal Sire boss (87 Slayer) unsired-drop sacrifice for a chance among several possible melee items.

facts.label: hazard
facts.value: Abyssal demons can teleport themselves or the player mid-fight (breaks safespotting, up to 20 tiles), so Auto Retaliate or a teleport anchoring scroll helps maintain uptime.

notes: Gather, not buy: kill abyssal demons (Slayer Tower, Catacombs of Kourend, or the Wilderness Slayer Cave, 85 Slayer) for a direct whip drop, or farm the Abyssal Sire boss (87 Slayer) for unsired drops and sacrifice one for a chance at a whip among several possible melee items. Abyssal demons can teleport themselves or the player mid-fight (breaks safespotting, up to 20 tiles), so Auto Retaliate or a teleport anchoring scroll helps maintain uptime.

name: Dragon scimitar

rewards.effect: Strongest scimitar in the game; fast 4-tick slash weapon and one of the definitive early-mid melee power spikes, widely used for Slayer and general training. As dragon equipment it cannot be smithed.

summary: Strongest scimitar in the game; 60 Attack, bought from Daga on Ape Atoll for 100,000 coins after Monkey Madness I, or a rare boss drop.

facts.value: Strongest scimitar in the game; fast 4-tick slash weapon and a definitive early-mid melee power spike.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Alternatively obtained as a rare drop from the Wilderness boss Scorpia or the Zombie Pirate's Locker, bypassing the quest/purchase route entirely.

facts.label: items-note

notes: Earn, not GE: after Monkey Madness I, buy directly from Daga on Ape Atoll for 100,000 coins gathered/earned through play (must be in monkey form via greegree unless Monkey Madness II is also done, or King Awowogei was freed via Recipe for Disaster to reach Marim by boat). Alternatively obtained as a rare drop from the Wilderness boss Scorpia or the Zombie Pirate's Locker, bypassing the quest/purchase route entirely. The Arkan blade (The Final Dawn quest reward) is a near-identical free alternative.

name: Rune crossbow

rewards.effect: One-handed crossbow firing up to runite bolts; pairs with a shield or god book for extra defensive bonuses, a common Slayer/TzHaar Fight Cave choice for players who want a shield slot rather than a two-handed bow.

summary: One-handed crossbow firing up to runite bolts; 61 Ranged, fletched at 69 Fletching from yew stock, runite limbs, and a crossbow string.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: One-handed crossbow firing up to runite bolts.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Requires 61 Ranged to wield and 69 Fletching to craft from raw materials (boostable).

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Fletch from gathered materials: cut a yew log into a yew stock (50 Fletching xp), attach runite limbs with a hammer (100 xp) for an unstrung crossbow, then string it with a crossbow string (50 xp), 200 xp total.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: As an ironman, the level requirement can be skipped entirely by farming the crazy archaeologist, who reliably drops a finished rune crossbow.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Crafting the whole chain within Witchaven/Yanille counts toward the elite Ardougne Diary.

facts.label: combat
facts.value: Pairs with a shield or god book for extra defensive bonuses, a common Slayer/TzHaar Fight Cave choice for players who want a shield slot rather than a two-handed bow.

Crafting the whole chain within Witchaven/Yanille counts toward the elite Ardougne Diary.

name: Magic shortbow

rewards.effect: Fast one-handed shortbow firing up to amethyst arrows with its own special attack; the strongest ranged weapon commonly available to mid-level players, a Slayer-task staple, and popular in PvP for stacking a knockout hit alongside melee. Imbuing it (magic shortbow scroll) raises ranged attack bonus to +75 and halves the special-attack energy cost.

facts.value: Rare unique drop from Verzik Vitur's treasure vault at the end of the Theatre of Blood raid, dropping uncharged.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Charging it (+20 stab/crush, +50 slash attack, +4 slash/+10 crush defence, +25 Strength bonus) is what makes it viable -- uncharged it is drastically weaker.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Charging uses vials of blood and blood runes deposited at the vyre well next to the bank in Ver Sinhaza (or, after Sins of the Father, the one in Darkmeyer); uncharging at a well returns the runes/vials, but an unprotected PvP death loses all charges with no refund.

notes: Rare unique drop from Verzik Vitur's treasure vault at the end of the Theatre of Blood raid (drops uncharged). Charging uses vials of blood and blood runes gathered/farmed rather than bought outright per this KB's no-GE convention, deposited at the vyre well next to the bank in Ver Sinhaza (or, after Sins of the Father, the one in Darkmeyer); uncharging at a well returns the runes/vials, but an unprotected PvP death loses all charges with no refund.

name: Ancient Magicks

skills.Magic: 50 minimum to cast any Ancient Magicks spell (Desert Treasure I's own req plus prerequisite-quest xp)

quests: Desert Treasure I -- see questdb:desert-treasure-i in this KB for its own flattened chain (The Dig Site, Temple of Ikov, The Tourist Trap, Troll Stronghold, Death Plateau, Priest in Peril, Waterfall Quest)

rewards.effect: Combat-heavy alternate spellbook (16 combat spells, 9 teleports) using expensive runes like death and blood; includes some of the strongest combat spells in the game and is especially popular in PvP. Swapped in at the pyramid's altar after finishing the quest; the pyramid's back-door shortcut also unlocks after the first run.

summary: Combat-heavy alternate spellbook (16 combat spells, 9 teleports); requires 50 Magic and Desert Treasure I completion, swapped in at the pyramid altar.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Combat-heavy alternate spellbook (16 combat spells, 9 teleports) using expensive runes like death and blood.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Requires 50 Magic minimum to cast any Ancient Magicks spell (Desert Treasure I's own requirement plus prerequisite-quest xp), and completion of Desert Treasure I.

facts.label: required-for
facts.value: Desert Treasure I is a master-tier quest chain (The Dig Site, Temple of Ikov, The Tourist Trap, Troll Stronghold, Death Plateau, Priest in Peril, Waterfall Quest); already fully detailed under questdb:desert-treasure-i in this KB.

facts.label: mechanics
facts.value: Swapped in at the pyramid's altar after finishing the quest; the pyramid's back-door shortcut also unlocks after the first run.

facts.label: items-note
facts.value: Includes some of the strongest combat spells in the game and is especially popular in PvP.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: The same quest also grants the ring of visibility, the ability to buy an ancient staff from Eblis for 80,000 coins, and access to the Smoke Dungeon.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The equipment slot for this spellbook is unknown/not applicable ("??") in the held source -- spellbooks don't occupy an equipment slot.

notes: Desert Treasure I is a master-tier quest chain, already fully detailed under questdb:desert-treasure-i in this KB; this row documents the spellbook-specific payoff. The same quest also grants the ring of visibility, the ability to buy an ancient staff from Eblis for 80,000 coins, and access to the Smoke Dungeon.

name: Lunar spellbook

quests: Lunar Diplomacy -- see questdb:lunar-diplomacy in this KB for its full prerequisite chain and walkthrough

rewards.effect: Utility/combat-support spellbook (uses every rune type except wrath) focused on reducing or removing the need for certain items/equipment for various tasks -- teleports, item-creation spells (e.g. Superglass Make, Tan Leather), and stat-restore/boost spells. A larger spell set unlocks after Dream Mentor, plus two more spells from the Hard Fremennik Diary.

summary: Utility/combat-support spellbook unlocked via Lunar Diplomacy (Magic 65, Defence 40 to cast), expanded after Dream Mentor and the Hard Fremennik Diary.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Lunar spellbook is a utility/combat-support spellbook that uses every rune type except wrath, offering teleports, item-creation spells (such as Superglass Make and Tan Leather), and stat-restore/boost spells.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: Completing Dream Mentor unlocks a larger Lunar spell set, and the Hard Fremennik Diary unlocks two further Lunar spells.

facts.label: caveat
facts.value: The 65 Magic / 40 Defence casting requirement can remain unmet even after Lunar Diplomacy is completed, if the quest was finished via a Fremennik quest lamp or if Magic/Defence experience was lost on death in an old Deadman Mode season.

notes: Lunar Diplomacy is already fully detailed under questdb:lunar-diplomacy; this row documents the spellbook payoff specifically. Note the page's own caveat: if the quest was auto-completed via a Fremennik quest lamp, or Magic/Defence xp was lost on death in old Deadman Mode seasons, the 65 Magic/40 Defence stat floor can end up unmet even with the quest already done, blocking spellcasting until levels catch up.

name: Arceuus spellbook

rewards.effect: Combat/utility spellbook (a mix, like the standard spellbook) focused on necromancy, prayer support, demon-slaying, and combat augmentation; heavy use of blood/nature/soul runes, notable for reanimation and offering spells used in Prayer training and a wide net of teleports to death-associated locations (graveyards, undead dungeons). A Kingdom Divided (already detailed under questdb:a-kingdom-divided) unlocks 24 further combat/utility spells beyond the base set.

summary: Arceuus spellbook is a necromancy/prayer/demon-slaying combat-utility spellbook with no unlock requirement; A Kingdom Divided adds 24 more spells.

facts.label: overview
facts.value: Arceuus spellbook is a combat/utility spellbook (a mix, like the standard spellbook) focused on necromancy, prayer support, demon-slaying, and combat augmentation, using mostly blood/nature/soul runes for reanimation, offering, and death-location teleport spells.

facts.label: start
facts.value: Base Arceuus spellbook access has no skill, quest, or item requirement -- speak to Tyss at the Dark Altar north of Arceuus (reachable via fairy ring code CIS, a games necklace to Wintertodt Camp then travel, or an Arceuus Library Teleport POH portal) to swap in immediately.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: 80 Construction lets a player build a dark altar in their POH Achievement Gallery, letting them swap spellbooks without traveling.

facts.label: unlock
facts.value: A Kingdom Divided unlocks 24 further combat/utility spells beyond the base Arceuus spell set.

notes: Unlike the other two spellbooks, base Arceuus access has zero skill/quest/item gate: simply speak to Tyss near the Dark Altar north of Arceuus (reachable via fairy ring code CIS, games necklace to Wintertodt Camp then travel, or an Arceuus Library Teleport POH portal) to swap in immediately, per the page's own text. 80 Construction lets a player build a dark altar in their POH Achievement Gallery to swap spellbooks without traveling at all. The expanded 24-spell version is gated behind A Kingdom Divided, already captured separately under questdb:a-kingdom-divided.
